Review_Example: So disorganized and very awkward. She's an easy grader and the material should have been interesting, but she does not know how to lead a discussion. The class was such a waste of time.

Gerner is one of the worst/worthless professors in Cornell. Everyday she comes to class with a word document of like 5 pages that she just reads off while looking up from time to time. The least engaging teacher I've ever had. Still, her class is easy cause people all bomb the assignments/test
Very caring professor! His lecture is very engaging.

Wait...what?  She is by far the worst professor I have had so far in my career as a student.  The material she includes in her course can be literally covered in a crash course, which prompts me to quesiton why her grading and course is ultimately so difficult to do well in.  In terms of comments/grading,**** "surprises" are what you should expect
hes rude and arrogant, youll learn, but youll be dealing with a condescending guy with no tact or ability to see outside his own ego
She knows a great deal about early modern Spain and she is funny. However, when she does not know what to say, she just ask students to read aloud (sometimes for half an hour!)
always has extra time to explain something that isn't quite clear.

Overall, material was understandable and assignments were not too difficult. Make sure (for any class of his) you watch each lecture (within a week or points off) and participate in each discussion even if it's not applicable to you. Any you miss will deduct 1 point from 10, it's not over total number of discussions + lectures.

Great professor, great class. He's new to the teaching game, so he no doubt has a lot to learn, but whatever you may think he lacks in clarity or conciseness he makes up for with enthusiasm, helpfulness, and raw knowledge on his subject material.
Miller was a wonderful professor! Very interesting lectures and material. Easy class - grade was primarily based on iClicker questions (readings required)

This is a very challenging course. Loads of reading, but it is crystal clear what is expected from day one and I learned more than in any other class. Professor Miller makes the material come alive and pushes you to go the extra mile. His classroom manner helps a lot. Funny, attentive, and hot too.

I loved PLSCI1101 so much. Dr. Miller made it fun and is a hilarious guy. Reading is somewhat heavy in the beginning of the semester, late semester is mostly guest lectures. He is very flexible with due dates and lets you submit late assignments. Gets back to email fast. By far my favorite class this semester!
Prof. Miller is a great professor and a genuinely caring person. Made lectures engaging and always open to questions and re-explaining concepts.

plays AKB48 in front of class, singing in class with baritone voice
lets you explore your own interest and guide your research, overall pretty good

Miller is a little all-over-the-place, he speaks fast and jumps from topic to topic during lecture and it can be confusing. But he's a really good guy, went out of his way to add extra office hours at night and is always willing to meet and talk about the material. Psets take forever and there were too many, prelims were difficult but scaled.
Boxing is fun, Seaman is interesting, a little hard on you. Early time sucked though. I suggest taking his Filipino martial arts class instead, it looked alot cooler.

Hallock is a definite superstar faculty member. He's a leader in his field and is very well respected. Charming and entertaining guy who keeps class interesting. Unfortunately, he is a VERY busy guy and it's hard to get a hold of him. Expectations on exams are not well defined, which makes it hard to do well. Good class, hard grade.
As they said not a hard grader. Also provides a lot of useful comments. too many to read

Clermont is a brilliant scholar but unable to make the material understandable for his students.  Lectures go off on irrelevant tangents, leaving less time for important material.  The exam was absolutely ridiculous and does not measure how well the students learned the semester's material.  Sorry Cornell 1Ls, you're stuck with him one semester.
I would not recommend Professor Ernste to anyone. He is extremely unresponsive to student's emails and concerns. Really disappointing experience overall.

Professor Packard is the worst professor I have had at Cornell. He overcomplicates content to the extreme. He did not have a single review session all semester before the prelim or final (despite promising one). Exam content is not at all similar to homework or class material. The median on the final was a 58% for context. Avoid at all costs.

Quin does an amazing job at teaching this FWS. The readings and seminars are really engaging with class topics. Hes understanding with his students, and he grades fairly. I highly recommend this course. The content will stick with you.

Amazing guy and engaging professor. Highly highly recommend taking herpetology with Harry if you get the chance; it is a once in a lifetime opportunity! Extremely helpful and interested in students personally outside of class too. TAKE THE LAB. Hard, but so worth it. It will give you another dimension to the course.

I've taken a lot of great classes at Cornell, but this beats them all by a mile. The material is SO interesting, and the small class size helps you get to know him really well. Greenwood is incredibly knowledgeable and extremely helpful in office hours. If you are interested and engaged, you will do well in this class. Recommended to EVERYONE!

Prof. Greenberg cares about his students, and runs the class in a way where your participation is both required and respected. Definitely the major theorist in his field, he knows and is ready to tell all about his speciality. He is funny, interesting, and very recommended.

He covers a wide range of material very quickly, and the lectures can be very technical and complicated at times. However, he is incredibly inspirational. His passion for learning and teaching is amazing, and he wants to instill this passion in his students. His knowledge and experiences are incredible. Take a class with him if you can!
She goes out of her way to seem caring but at the end of the day does not listen to students. She does not like questions and is quick to correct you with her opinion

I hated this class. In lectures, she just speaks and says so much and so little at the same time. She calls out people talking in class and does not even cover everything she says she will. I truly cannot believe this is the only required course for a fashion studies minor. I am so sorry for everyone who has to take this.
Great class, easy grading. The content was always interesting, and Professor Greenlee is very fair about grades, assignments, and attendance. Super kind and caring professor.
Very nice professor. If you are interested in restaurants, he is great. Exams can be a bit tough, but if you study and know the material, it shouldn't be too bad.

This class was straight up terrible.  There was bound to be at least on catastrophic math error in every lecture that put the entire class into a tailspin.  We were never given any sort of practice in what we were actually supposed to retain.  Our problem sets consisted of a laundry list of c-level MWG questions.  And the final...WTF

I really loved Prof. Rock-Singer's lectures because they cleared up anything I didn't understand in the pre class readings. His class definitely packed a lot of information about pre-modern Islamic civilization, but coming from no previous knowledge on this topic, it was easy to succeed if you do the readings and attend lecture.

I took Wagner for ECE 2720 and initially didn't like him.  I matured a bit since then in terms of school preparation and took ECE 4670 with him recently. Wagner will not hold your hand --- you need to go to lecture, office hours, and understand the material well. But if you do, he's a fair guy, great lecturer, and the coursework is very doable.
Loved this class. Def long/annoying readings to do every week for discussion, and also coming to lecture is important as he doesn't do slides. Would recommend this class to anyone.

Had him as an FWS prof, and he did a good job directing the class. Good incorporation of book and essays. He taught lessons on how to improve writing, but he did not give specific details on how to improve certain aspects of an essay unless you asked. Pretty chill dude and is understanding of special circumstances if you make him aware of them.

Aaron has devoted a number of years of his life to the subject matter he teaches. Having said this, he is very passionate and intuitive about his work. His sincere care about philosophy reflects a deep concern about human nature, and in a larger sense, the nature of nature. He is a wonderful instructor and I recommend taking his class.
A arch history teacher that will temper and refine your architectural knowledge from merely fashionable and abstract into reasoned and articulate.
condescending and slams you with way too much homework at the beginning of the semester. not a nice person when you talk to him

This class is super time consuming and hard. The instructors expect you to know most about calculus before this class. If you don't know what you're doing they expect you to learn on your own through the textbook. The Math Learning Center was really helpful because Brock and the other instructors were not.

Professor Klosin was amazing! This class is really enjoyable and he teaches a lot of modern mathematics that is very interesting! There is only one project and a final but that shouldn't scare you because he is a very fair and easy grader! You can tell that he loves teaching and is always open for you to ask questions in class.
GREAT TEACHER! LOVED EVERY MINUTE

Probably the most pathetic professor I've ever met.  He can't teach anything or explain anything.  He assigned an incomprehensible book with too many errors.  If you're willing to turn in a paper every class that looks like the homework you should get an A.  (You'll notice I'm didn't care enough about the class to do that).
None

The structure of the class worked really well for me. The lectures were interesting for the most part. Subject matter is very relevant, which is a nice change from some of my other classes. Also, the debates he held allowed people the chance to express their opinions and learn from another. Overall, I would definitely recommend this class.
Very strict professor and content in lecture does not match that of exams.

PHYS2217 was the biggest mistake of my freshman year. Shvets gives unclear lectures which often left me more confused than when I entered the room. Exams were unreasonably difficult even considering this course is classified as "honors," evidenced by extremely low exam averages. The curve was a saving grace but not worth the misery of this class.
Very clear, exercises are really helpful, would take again 10/10! Best at Cornell!

Zifeng is a nice guy and its clear how interested he is and how much knowledge he has, but his class is so overwhelming. he assigned so many papers and assignments, and for about half of them hes only now submitting grades (on the last day of the semester). his comments arent helpful but are confusing and frustrating sometimes

Out of all of the classes I took at Cornell, this class has been the most rewarding and eye-opening. It is a fairly easy 2-credit class, but it has significantly challenged my opinions on pesticides. He's truly HILARIOUS even when he doesn't try to be, so his lectures are always engaging. Very good at explaining concepts and organized. TAKE IT!

Kelly was super enthusiastic about the material, which made going to class easy for me. I do think she is one of the more difficult professors, though. I would do many practice tests, which were always substantially easier than her exams. There were questions on the final I had never heard of.

Oh, Crocker. Think of this class as the real estate minor weed-out course. We started the semester with 65 students and ended it with 16. You'll probably get a 25 on a test but don't worry... that'll be the median (heavy curve). Tedious psets & opaque grading. Rambling, nonsensical lectures. If you really want the minor, just get thru this class.

Liu Laoshi is really likable and easy to reach outside of class. You'll probably spend at least 5 hours a week outside of class preparing for weekly quizzes, preview homework, and journals. But overall, her classes are really enjoyable; if you put in the effort, you should be able to get an A.

I was genuinely excited to take HADM 3210 and learn about Real Estate investing, yet was disappointed with Professor Liu's course. The "flipped" format was highly ineffective, and the pre-class videos did little to educate me on the content. His lectures are dry, all over the place, and the mid-term exam was poorly executed. Take someone else.

Dr. Allmon is without a doubt one of the best, most engaging lecturers at Cornell. He has a tendency to put you on the spot and likes to give pop quizzes. He's a harsh grader, but usually his TA is much nicer about it than he is. He's very empathetic and if you show interest in the material, he is very easy to approach. (Attendance recommended)

Amazing professor. His assignments complement his lecture and are very practical. He makes you think about real world problems. This is one of the best courses I have taken. I have a very good understanding of finance after this. Not a very heavy subject.

She is a motivated professor, you can tell she loves to teach and that makes me more motivated.  There is online homework 4 days/week and a quiz every other week.  Definitely read the textbook, study 30 minutes a day, do all online homework, and you'll be good. Oh yeah,participation is not even optional so you will either sink like Titanic or float

You get to do ten barn chores that take about an hour and fifteen minutes to do. Lectures are necessary. Quizzes cover random facts and not so much the actual useful stuff or even the material focused on. Quizzes are harder than prelims. TA's are generally not nice or all that available. Office hours do not exist. Horse experience is necessary!!!

Worst professor I have ever experienced.  Lectures are rushed and questions are treated as an interruption to class.  Grading affords no constructive feedback that provides solutions, only aggressive comments stating the answers are incorrect.  Lacks personable skills when interacting outside of class.  If your goal is to learn , avoid this one.

Brooks is probably the best professor I have had so far at Cornell. He genuinely cares about all his students and wants to see everyone succeed. He puts an emphasis on understanding why things happen rather than just memorizing stuff which is nice for a biology class. I had him for 3350.

Took him for labor relations. Lectures are scattered and  unclear. Bargaining sim, two tests, participation papers. group work heavy. Minimal work for the actual class, and Riddell is a cool and very nice guy. You probably won't learn more than the basics of labor relations. Significantly less work than Aleks.

Super boring lecturer. Extremely unresponsive to emails. Tests were unrelated to material on Psets. 

All of these would be excusable and I would not give him too much of a hard time, but for the first time in my life I got curved down in a class. Received a RAW score well above a 92 and was given a B+ in the class. Absolutely inexcusable.

Schaffer is quite temperamental and unhelpful--never knew what was good enough for him even though TAs said ideas were good. Otherwise, he plays almost no role in the class... Would advise against taking it.
Chris is a great conductor, he's extremely helpful and is always open to suggestions. I really enjoyed being in orchestra, and his (sometimes random) metaphors always keep rehearsals fun.

I LOVED THIS CLASS!!! Made me really excited about Civil Engineering. The class was easy and interesting. Earls is really passionate about the material. Very helpful in office hours. No textbook, a few short homeworks, 1 paper, and a group project in the place of a final. The project was fun and a great way to apply the material. My favorite class.

Favorite class at Cornell. Chris is funny and knows his stuff, especially poetry. My favorite part of the class is that he treated us not like kids and students, but like people who actually wanted to create quality writing and improve. Not recommended for people looking for an easy pass; you have to try. Worth it if you do.

Not a great class but need to take if you're pre-med or anything related. Tests are very specific and difficult. My tips: pay attention to wording on tests - the answer is almost never all of the above; memorize EVERYTHING on summary pages; use quizlet/flashcards for little details because they WILL be asked. No need to really read textbook.

Nice man but unorganized in his lectures, sometimes unclear about what you were learning, and he doesn't grade until the end of semester. His lectures are open to student opinion and discussion led. The good thing is that meant that the essay prompts on homeworks and exams were always subjective which led to easier grading.
Highly knowledgeable, caring professor. I learned a lot in her class. Don't be afraid to approach for help or advice.
Prof. Garcia was one of the best professors I ever had.  May he Rest-in-Peace.  He managed to make Mechatronics a fun course that taught theory and practice.
Not the best and certainly not the worst

Pablo the legend! He does have a strict attendance policy, but you won't want to miss class with him. Pablo's classes are high-tempo and there's A LOT of talking. He explains grammar in a clear way and he's pretty understanding. Pablo's really funny, in a sort of snarky way. I truly miss him!

Has written a few good papers - without doing real research I might add.  Overall pretty pretentious, unorganized, and an idiot.  Some people are tricked into liking him because he plays the cool card.

I loved taking PHIL 1440 with Professor starr. I signed up for this class because I was really interested in the topic, and professor starr definitely made the class worth it. They showed a lot of passion in the subject and also graded the papers super clearly. Gave good feedback and made it easy to learn. The topics were super interesting too.
Great!
openly mocks struggling students.
not helpful at all

An amazing professor and musician.  Bill is extremely knowledgeable about all kinds of music, although he can be prone to going off on a tangent in class.  Very reasonable workload, and always willing to help.  Exams are a joke.  Definitely take any class you can with Bill!

Really passionate about the subject matter. Quizzes and exams require you to memorize small details to do well on them. Lots of extra credit is offered.  Exams are curved in a way that everyone benefits.

disrespectful. uncomfortable during tough subjects. if your question varies even the slightest from what is predictable,he wont answer you during class but will emailyou later with the answer. we wrote stories with 7 year olds all semester and after we turned them in he CHANGED THEM! my 7 year old buddy was very mad it wasnt his story his anymore
Incredibly good professor. He goes beyond a professor; he is a mentor.

Phenomenal Prof. By making us interact/learn from the guest speakers -- all of whom are VCs/Entrepreneurs themselves, Dr. BenDaniel teaches one to think creatively, out of the box and like an entrepreneur.

Professor Widom is one of the best teachers ive ever had in any subject at any institution. He uses no notes because of his ritual, perhaps learned from his advisor(?), of practicing lectures the day before. Clarity and deep insight into the subject matter are the norm. To learn from this master is to be lucky indeed. Always most interesting.

Prof is professional, knowledgeable and logical. He assigns a lot of reading, but each passage is carefully chosen and therefore meaningful. The lectures are clear and, if you actually follow him attentively, you will find your questions predicted and answered by him. The essays are fun to write.

Prof. Rissing is truly a gem of a professor. His lectures were all super engaging, and he marked all important vocab terms in bold. His grading criteria was super clear, and it was fairly easy to get 100s on both of the papers. He is super approachable and really cares about each of his students. He is a really great professor and all around guy.

let me be clear
1. INSANE WORKLOAD (hw + lab + project + ae + exam + extra credit... should be 8+ credit course)
2. AMBIGUOUS grading criteria (most TAs don't know what they mean either
3. 50% OF WORK COMPLETELY OUT OF CLASS MATERIAL 
4. DISORGANIZED PROFESSOR (speak too fast with no meaningful info)
5. 0 HELP FROM EXAM ("just review the material")

Note: I took multi at an extension school and retook it.
Lectures uninteresting and exactly follows textbook examples instead of his own material. HW not bad, but office hours necessary and carried by goat TA. Prelims + tests have some tricky problems, but overall fair if you study well. Didn't provide practice for prelims, but did so for final.

A lot less reading than the other labor history profs, and grades a little bit easier.  Tough TA made assignments tougher, so a lot of work has to be put in at times, but reasonable effort can get you a good grade.
He is not very well respected and is known to be non-feasible. A truly awful professor.
He taught the material in an interesting way but his exams are too hard for what he taught in lecture, at least make them a bit more parallel.

He really knows his stuff.  The class was a lot of fun to take.  It is highly recommended that you know your way around a dairy cow.  If not, it will be a struggle at the beginning.   Everything in his class is conceptual, so go to lecture.  There is no good way to study for the few tests other than to now the concepts.  Memorization not required.

I took the class because it sounded interesting, but I didnt really care much about the topic. Turns out I really enjoyed the class. The material is difficult, but he really does work hard to help you get it. He gives lots of feedback on your writing, too.

Professor Koch is absolutely fantastic! He's really great at keeping class discussions going in the right direction by asking incisive questions, but is never overbearing. Incredibly positive, constructive comments in private meetings. Excellent readings, and his story-reading voice is second to none. Highly recommended!
Really good dude. I took his marketing class and it was really nice and super easy. I still learned a lot.
Very encouraging professor, one of the best teachers in the department.
Great teacher, Cornell's loss
One of the best educators I have ever had. She's very accommodating and makes psychology the most interesting subject you'll ever learn.

Do not take anything w Tennyson! Sometimes I doubted if she knew the material at all. Did the bare minimum, she would zoom in whenever she wanted. She made mistakes about basic concepts and yes she is easy but you learn nothing. read off slides and wrote confusing questions in exams. never again
Difficult Grader, Very vague about assignments and work. Difficult tests with open ended not specific questions. Many long and challenging homeworks. Stay away
Very timely and organized just as a lecturer should be. The material is useful and applicable to the real world.

I would never take this class but I had to in order to stay in public policy. i tried to switch majors because of this class. it is impossible to do well. no matter what you do the TAs/professor will find a way to take 20% off your grade for no reason. stay away.
Loved 4740 a great class

AMAZING PROF! I can't imagine having learned biochem from anyone else. Clearly covers difficult concepts, so helpful and welcoming during office hours even just to chat about non biochem related topics. So sad he is retiring from teaching, one of the best!

Prof Escalona Torres is amazing! Honestly he might be my favorite professor of all time. I took SPAN 1210 + 1220 with him. He's super caring, he was great at explaining the material, and he really made every class fun. Cannot praise this man enough. So sad that I can't take more classes with him, I think he's at a different university now :(

Not the most interesting subject matter, but I enjoyed this class. Discussions were straightforward, assignments were very clearly and returned promptly. Seemed relaxed about teaching a freshman writing seminar, and seemed to treat students with same relaxed approach. Would recommend.

I recently discovered that 3152 has an Inclusiveness Statement on the course website which apparently course staff need to follow.

She should read that.

She is a caring and nice professor, but not the best professor in terms of teaching. Lectures disorganized, most of grade reliant upon group assignments that are very unclear. You learn the basics and self-explanatory concepts. Not worth taking.

Pamela consistently is distracting from the Professor's lecture, many students find her unapproachable due to her stubborn nature, and I frequently find her explanations to be incorrect. Pamela seems to append rubrics while grading which makes it impossible to properly complete assignments.

I did not read the textbook but would say her lectures are a must-attend. Most amazing professor in terms of clarity when lecturing, and if you pay attention you are very well prepared for the exams esp with all the practice. She may have her quirks & be super strict but at the end of the day is respected. WRITE DOWN ALL NOTES IN CLASS
She is amazing !

Professor Chang is AMAZING. Even if you are not interested in gender studies, you should take this class. Her lectures are not opinionated at all and she allows for class discussion. The TA was amazing too.
Quite possibly the most fun I've had in a class freshman year, if not ever. Stress is unheard of, as long as you pitch in maybe once a week

Seminar-style class with a lot of readings, but is easy to do well in even if you don't read all the books. I appreciated prof bringing in guest lecturers (Google employee, other profs) to explain technical topics. Sometimes disorganized lectures but if you participate in every 2 lectures, you'll get full credit. Attending OH helped with essays.

Incredibly knowledgeable and passionate about her subject. The course was definitely reading heavy and you are expected to participate regularly. Gives good, detailed feedback on papers, always happy to meet during office hours, and accommodating regarding extensions. Overall, a phenomenal professor who I highly recommend taking classes with.

Professor Barclay is one of the most interesting professors I've had at Cornell. He always fills his lectures with fascinating discussions of current research. He presents the material very logically and provides good explanations for everything he says. I am not a science major, but his explanations made perfect sense even without a bio background

My favorite professor at Cornell. He is incredibly intelligent and knowledgeable but unlike many other professors, he truly wants to see you succeed. He goes out of his way to set up meeting times and help you understand complex material. He does not make students compete against each other for grades. He is just great!

Dr Johnson is great!!! She's so nice and if you pay attention in class you'll do well.  The posters are a nice touch to a lecture based class and allow for grade improvements.  Definitely recommend the class and getting to know Dr Johnson!  You won't find a more motherly professor at Cornell
Pat is so awesome. He's a great lecturer and a good person. I learned the most from this class out of any I've taken at cornell but it taught me the most
Unclear powerpoint slides, way too much textbook readings every class, doesn't seem to know how to teach all the material. Wouldn't take the class again if it wasn't a requirement.
So trash, hard to follow along in lectures, made many mistakes. Class material is hard enough but just reading the textbook is better than trying to listen in class

Matt is by far one of the smartest and most capable faculty members in the CS department. He is kind, caring, and genuinely cares about his students. His course is really satisfying to take because he is so passionate and good at explaining things.

Nice guy, seems like he's always high. On a few occasions the lecture notes weren't clear enough to answer the homework problem accurately but if you go to the textbook afterwards there wasn't any doubt.
Loved this class! Professor Reid is very helpful during lab.

It is clear that McGranaghan has no experience with Marketing for a real company. You might as well read the textbook: it will be more concise and probably more interesting than him. Also, none of our class assignments are related to the lectures. He thinks there is only one correct answer to cases, so you better say exactly what he wants you to.
I really enjoyed the field trips to farms and guest speakers in this class.
No Comments

Professor Delisa is probably the best teacher I've had. His lectures are really engaging. He encourages discussions in class. Even the assignments are graded based on how much you tried rather than on how correct the answers are. The very atmosphere in the class promotes learning. The TA- Natalia was also really great and prompt in grading.

Doug is really funny and nice. He cracks some of the best brilliant jokes. Hes classes are very fun to go to. Materials, though, are very tough. But you dont really have to be able to work out everything. Physically based computer animation requires advanced physics knowledge, but you dont need everything to do well in this class though.
James is very very nice, caring, and is not a tough grader. I love this class because he invites lecturers/ performers from different fields to give presentations in class. They are truly amazing.
Terrible!Stay away from him. You will not regret this advice.

I didn't read the textbooks, just did my best to memorize the glossary terms from the assigned readings. I took extremely detailed notes of everything he SAYS. Memorize everything you can. His delivery of the lecture was most important. I learned a lot in this class and appreciate Prof Cutting's dedication and passion for these topics :D

You will learn useful, applicable skills in this class if you pay attention and do the work. A lot of people claim that Stapp can be confusing, and while the instruction to assignments can at times be a little confusing, he is more than willing to clarify at anyone's request. If you come into this course wanting to learn, you'll enjoy it.
Lectures are a tad boring and can be awkward at times. Overall, he's just average.

I think that most of the unhappy reviews are because he taught intro stats (I can see him being difficult to learn from in intro stat). But for an advanced class he was good. Taught the theory in class and gave us examples of how to use R to actually apply the theory in the real world. Met with me 1 on 1 when I was behind and helped me catch up.

I loved Nick's class! It gives you good insight about starting a startup through case studies and learning about Nick's own experience with his startup Rosie! In the class you are assigned a group where you start a startup together. I really enjoyed this coming from a STEM background and this being my first AEM class.

Very fun intro class! Quizzes are very straightforward and the short responses aren't too difficult. You get a plethora of resources to learn about Chinese history and it's not limited to boring textbook readings (documentaries, short stories, fictional movies, etc). Prof is super funny, kind, and cares about making the class accessible :)

I took Television with him (AMST 2160) and he was such a great teacher, the class clapped for him after the first and last lecture. We watched films throughout the lectures and talked about them. We were also assigned to watch these for homework and sometimes they'd be a little long. Essay grading was a little tough but doable if you put in effort
cool guy who likes to teach. pretty sure he's leaving cornell, but wherever he ends up they'll love him

Professor Taylor is one of the nicest profs I have met at Cornell. From meeting with me even at late hours of the day to discuss my performance, to being able to explain in depth concepts in a digestible way, he is the best. Physics is plain and simple not an easy subject, but this is the best professor I could have asked for for this material.
Amazing man, super interesting and kind if you get to know him. That being said, the essay questions are broad and vague and the grading is extremely harsh. A LOT of reading.

Great professor and great class. Is probably the best econ professor in the dept. He knows the subject very well and grades are based on the understanding of the material from an essay standpoint. A must take for any econ major attempting to go into finance.

Great professor who knows alot. Nice guy and very approachable and helpful. The only problem is the class. The material can be pretty dry and instead of abstract thought, the class focuses mainly on plotting the arguments. Just wasn't my style of learning.
Classes are always entertaining and even include guest speakers at times. However, students are graded on very few things and the professor can be a harsh grader.

Amazing prof. Went in hating physics but he completely changed my mind. Flipped classroom structure was a good amount of work but ultimately beneficial, with lectures clearing up confusion and including relevant demonstrations. Exams are fair and plenty of outside resources (office hours, etc) provided. Krasicky wants you to succeed, loved him.
he has a mean-streak.  in class he waste time and picks on students.  I did learn my probablitly and stats but it had little to do with him.  basically he comes off an an entitled academic.

This class seems like it was created to be an ego boost for the professor. He only promotes his ideals with his textbook (that is hard to follow because it's poorly written). I loved my TA and he made the class bearable but overall McMichael just rambles and is not responsive to students making the class more difficult than it needs to be

I took ENGL 3240 with Lorenz (I don't know why it isn't an option for the course code), and I liked his class enough to review him on RMP (this is my first review) -- so hopefully that says something! I found his insights interesting throughout the course, and he's surprisingly funny. Lean into the class, it'll make the experience much better!!
Funny kind and clear. Skim through the longer readings, concentrate on her lectures. Easy grader too
Really really nice guy who cared more about his students than making the class hard. Psets were reasonable but challenging and the class was really fun. Would take it again if I could.

I really appreciate how he made things easy to understand. The professors Ive had at Cornell have made me feel lost bc they yap a whole lot abt nothing during class and then test you on material that ur not prepared for, but Terry has been one of the only professors who made me feel very confident in the material I was learning. Thank you Terry!

Grade weighed heavily on prelims and final exam (25+25+15 --> 65%) Lab session twice per week, assignment once per week. Generous on general marking of works, just submit the things on time and you'd be fine

His lectures were interesting and engaging, he clearly cares a lot about the material and that always makes for the best professors. My TA was great and the grading was really not that hard at all. You'll do best if you're keeping up with the readings, which are substantial but not excessive. Would definitely take another class with him
Even though I struggled a lot in 2940, I love Alex Townsend!!
Took Prof. Gaeta's Quantum and Nonlinear Optics class as a masters student. He grades grad students and undergrad separately so that takes care of grading worries for undergrads.

Take this class!  Labs consist of field trips and hands-on experience, tests are fair, and lectures are both useful and fun.  Her emphasis is on analysis, not rote memorization--she teaches you not how to be a student, but a scientist.
Good class but not an EASY A. Do the readings + study and you'll do well.

Lectures are well-organized. After explaining the concepts, she leaves time for us to practice questions and always walk around to answer any questions with patience. She brings cookies and chocolates to class on Halloween and after prelims. She is really good at making difficult concepts easy to understand and provides general solving strategy.

I absolutely love Hannah! She completely changed the way I approach literature and writing, and she's the reason I'm considering declaring a comparative lit minor. She is incredibly intelligent, which shines through when she presents course materials and leads class discussions. You should definitely take this class! Hands downs best FWS at CU.

Hannah was great. The class content she focused on was fascinating (in short: French cultural exchange). She always cultivated interesting class discussions based on the assigned readings/movies/songs, and created a welcoming class environment in general. The class felt "homey", as opposed to a space that was stressful or difficult to speak up in.

Loved this class, despite not being a huge Shakespeare fan. He tends to ramble a bit, but his asides are usually very interesting. The essays and texts we read were great and assignments are graded fairly with extensive feedback. The final project is also awesome. Definitely take this class!

The level of difficulty of the three exams were all very different. The two prelims were easy but the final was very hard. Lecture was not very helpful - you can teach yourself the course just fine by reading the textbook. Lectures were often more confusing than they were insightful. Average amount of homework at an average level of difficulty.

I did not find his teaching much helpful. He seemed to be a highly knowledgeable person, but his lectures were just like him blandly throwing information at you. I could not absorb like 80% of what he said in the lectures. Speaks too fast. Does not connect things together to help you see the big picture. Was a really depressing experience.

Recommend to any STEM major as alternative to 2214 (if Davis is teaching). His lectures were very thorough and he takes care to explain everything in detail, answering all questions. Throughout the semester you gain a solid understanding of everything from EM waves to fermi statistics and none of it is rushed. One of the best professors I've had.

Lectures were very informative and held my attention. Did demos every class. Homework was reasonable and lecture slides are posted at least the day before lecture. Overall a great professor who is passionate about what he does.

The most irresponsible professor I have ever seen. Barely got any useful comments from him to move forward. Materials are confusing, a lot of the time, students can't understand what he wants for homework. I can almost never find him when I need him, especially in class time. He claims that will stay around but no, can't find him anywhere.

Professor Davis does a good job explaining the conceptual matter behind concepts in the lecture, focusing heavily on understanding as opposed to focusing on memorization. However, Abe was quite unprepared for lectures this semester, because he was loaded with tons of paper deadlines.
He obviously cares a lot about students and has very reasonable expectations.

Although the class is generally low effort and there are few assignments, so as long as you do well on those you can get a good grade. She barely knows how to use canvas so the site was a mess. All the assignments and exams were CANVAS MULTIPLE CHOICE QUIZZES. There were calculations in the questions but no chance for partial credit

Rudest professor I've met here, made me quit my project team because she didn't support any students. Always loved hearing herself talk and brainstorm, and bored me every meeting. I don't know how she is in class, but she's an insufferable person
This class is easy and interesting! The teaching team is so caring and passionate about the subject. Overall, such a great class.

She makes lectures as interesting as possible and always makes sure to answer students' questions throughout class, thoughtfully. She grades with mildly high expectations of what students will submit (specifically final paper draft and other materials). Readings discussion posts twice a week are assigned.
Super clear and enthusiastic lectures. Exams are really easy

Professor Schneider is wonderful; her class isn't difficult and at all and she genuinely cares about students and wants us to learn the material.  Overall just a really lovely prof and I had a good experience in the class.
She only teaches you the basic mineral and expects you know a lot of stuff for the exam. Her exams are 11 pages long.

Erin is very kind, but her teaching style fell flat for me. Seminars were boring. She could've ended class (2 hours) early most weeks. Grading was inconsistent and not based on a rubric, so it was difficult to know how to improve. That being said, she was more than willing to meet about an assignment or discuss concerns if you had them.
Squyres is LEGENDARY. Awesome lecturer, hilarious, AND he worked on the flippin' Mars Rovers. What's not to love????? Definitely one of my favorite professors here.
Best professor I have ever taken.  I have learned more in this class than all others.

Generally interesting material covered but Prof. Wolf has a "my way or the highway" approach to the subject matter, all while claiming that debate and discussion is an important facet of the course. Grading is fair, but be prepared to say what Prof. Wolf wants to you to hear rather than your own opinions about the subject matter.

Great Lecturer. Very nice professor. My only complaint is of great personal bias which is that he used too many "2"'s in his exams and homeworks on complex analysis. The amount of confusion I got when trying to differentiate between if what I wrote down was a 2 or z. Not his fault of course, completely mine however that is my greatest gripe.

Professor Paling is clearly a capable web developer with a lot of field experience, but he has a lot of difficulty teaching the concepts. I was fortunate to come in with web development experience, and that allowed me to do very well in the class, but my peers who did not have experience struggled because of his teaching ability.

I really enjoyed this class a lot! Prof. Son is very caring and approachable, and she does a nice job keeping everyone engaged. There's not too many assignments for this class- 1 short writing most weeks, a midterm, and a final project + short essay. The class is pretty discussion heavy. I feel like all the topics are super interesting though!

Prof. Song is still a great professor. KOREA 1102 is a little harder than 1101 though. You have to learn a lot more grammatical structures and vocabulary words. An A is still achievable, but you will have to put more work in. Attendance policy is the same. The final project is a group skit.
Super interesting material, although lectures can be somewhat dry. As close to an easy A as you can get at Cornell.
Clear organized lecture.  Unproblematic guy. Tests were very fair and representative of the material.

It's hard to make an extended lecture starting at 8am engaging. Dr. Huson is very kind and includes just about everything you need to know on the slides. Communication started to dwindle by the end of the semester and there wasn't a lot of preparation before the final... class ended very abruptly. The discussion sections are relatively unhelpful.
She is so understanding and caring. Thought some of the assignments were unnecessary but overall such a fun class.

Steve's INFO 1200 was so entertaining. However, this class has a TON of reading, and a tests that really grills you on your comprehension. If you're good at reading and understanding nuanced points, you will thrive in this class, it literally made me switch my major to info sci. But, my friends who hate reading and writing struggled- be warned!
Very tedious material, a decent amount of work for a FWS.
A hard class, and prepared to work for your grade.  Good content, lectures and labs.  Just a lot of work if you want to score decent in the lab.
Extremely helpful, explains readings really well. Also one of the nicest professors out there. This class could have been awful with a different professor but Jose made it interesting.
Best lab TA ever! He grades pretty easily and he really knows his stuff. But more importantly, he plays Lady Gaga during labs and blew up a gummy bear in our last lab session. Really nice guy.

Terrible professor. Save yourself and do not take an FWS with him. He made a class that should be an easy A a horrible and dreadful experience. Not supportive to students, a stickler for citations, and rude. I never looked forward to this class. I saw my peers in other FWS's doing less work and receiving easy A's for simply doing the work.
Lovely professor! She is very motivating and truly cares about her students. Her lectures are meaningful and she teaches things you can use throughout your life not just in her class.
Very caring prof. Class works backwards so you do classwork and learning for hw and do the assignments in class.

I love his lecture because he try to explain why you should learn concepts, properties, and theorems in the graduate students' point of view. Especially, he tried to make connection between probability and your research area, how to adopt what you learned from this course for your research. I got A, but I heard that he had tough grading policy.

RVR is one of my favorite professors I have had at Cornell. Knowing that you're being taught OS by someone who worked at Bell Labs with the creators of Unix and C is very compelling and inspirational. He is hilarious and his office hours are immensely helpful, more so than TAs. He is also very active on Ed. His demos in class are also insightful.

Professor Merritt was overall a great lecturer, and prioritized learning Greek over assignments and exams, which was clearly reflected in how he graded. While quizzes and midterms could be tough, and grades were not posted on canvas, he was adamant that our final grade reflected our knowledge of the language. Very passionate, I would recommend.
Very good lecturer. He teaches the first half of the course but honestly wish he taught the entire course
I took Latin with him and did not learn much. Not only was the class boring, but the reading was very dull. He's also very egotistical. He rarely responded to my e-mails.

The textbook is very useful since the homework assignments come from the textbook. Lectures are useful as well, and the professor gives a 5-minute break during lectures. The course itself is not too difficult and is really straight-forward.

Logistically a mess due to Zoom. But he's a good, very old-school lecturer. I wish I had more time to spend on this course, because it was extremely rigorous and the hardest I've worked for 3 credits. I didn't enjoy it, but I appreciated what he was trying to do. I'm not a MechE so I wish I took it with someone else with a more streamlined approach

Interesting class on Ethics of Eating. Quizzes in class, but can drop one. There are 2 papers, and no exam! Controversial subject matter, so is a stimulating class. Active participations from students makes it a lively atmosphere to be in.

lectures are incredibly boring. i never paid attention in lecture and crammed in the last 24-48 hours and ended up pulling a B/B+ on every exam. there is a very heavy curve in the class (an 82% was an A-) which makes it okay, but can confidently say that i am not coming out of this class understanding even one small thing about gen chem.

The neurobiology of love and attraction is a great senior seminar. The student presentations are great and the short essays are thought provoking. Definitely a class you should check out in the future!

Professor Joo does not care about his student. I many times reach out to him through email asking about program related information and asking for help, he would never reply those emails. The way I look at this him is that, he only cares about who/what brings him value, and does not want to spend any time on any that could 'waste' his time

AWESOME PROF. Prof Ault understands most people are taking this class for distribution and doesn't try to make the class too hard. Genuinely, he just wants to make people understand the threat of climate change and need for action. Rewards people who come to class with extra credit opportunities. Tries to make lectures engaging and class not hard.

Lectures are dense, but the material is really interesting if you like the subject. Dr. Barre is extremely knowledgeable and kind. Tests are difficult, but a note sheet was allowed in the hybrid version of the class!

Professor Brown is an absolute delight. She managed to create a comfortable and inviting environment for all students to participate. The readings can sometimes be a lot at once but she is willing to adjust the deadlines if you ask. She gives very clear feedback and wants to see all her students succeed. Wonderful inside and outside the classroom.
This teacher is one of the best I've had at Cornell. There is a workload and lectures are mandatory... but it's all worth it.

Prof. Tach is the sweetest. TAs grade all of the essays and pretty fairly. Quizzes were very easy and took 15 minutes max. Very light workload for a 4 credit class and a great introduction for kids interested in policy; not intended to weed out kids at all. However, can be redundant for policy majors taking second semester freshman year.
I had her for NS 1400 Intro to HBHS and her lectures were very boring but the material was easy to understand; exams did match lecture content, which is always helpful
Holy smokes she is hot. Anyways I had a great time in the class. She is very open minded and it is very easy to get a good grade in the class provided you put in a reasonable effort.
Great professor and very engaging!
He's the Skipper. Enough Said.

I did really like Williams. He was great as a person, but I'm not sure learned much in regard to writing. I spent most of the time not sure how to write something he would like. Very much so a lofty English person. Picky grader, and very picky about the rules of his class/syllabus. One of the nicest, funniest professors I've had at Cornell though.
Passionate about computational biology, and tries her best to make lectures engaging. Cares about her students, and responds to both questions on Piazza or via email.

Williamson co-taught with David Shmoys. Although he was not as engaging as Shmoys, he was a very clear and concise lecturer. Remembers our names, and cares to explain if someone needs help. Overall, a solid professor that I would love to take another class with

Professor Williams' class was an exact copy of APUSH from high school. She randomly quit with one month left in the semester with no notice, leaving the class in the dark and having to get a new professor for the last 4 weeks. However, her grading criteria is clear and her tendency to cancel class at least once a week makes this easy to pass.

This was supposed to be a Women in Management class but she just turned it into a class of feminist rantings .  Nothing irritates a woman more than seeing a woman of authority espouse your gender as nothing but helpless victims of circumstances.   Offered nothing in terms of actual Women in Management. She just used this class to vent her ideology.
Jim is such an amazing Individual and an awesome professor. He made such a positive impact on my life as an artist. I highly recommend him.

Professor was insightful and clearly knowledgeable on the content but was very very bland. Lectures were repetitive and the slides were confusing to look back on. Information was overall super disorganized. But the workload is light and content is interesting, so not all bad.

hands down the best professor i've had so far. i loved lectures--he's entertaining & his notes were very helpful. he demands a lot from his students because of the nature of the material, but he's very supportive and kind. prelims were open note/book but challenging... fair overall. he's brilliant, really cool, and cornell is lucky to have him.

Professor Villenas is probably the sweetest professor I've had at Cornell. She always makes an effort to accommodate to students' needs and you can tell she loves what she does. Lectures can be a little tedious from time to time, but overall, Villenas is an amazing person and I'd definitely recommend taking this course!

Great Class! Learned a lot about the three authors examined, and she guided us really well through comprehending, interpreting, and discussing the material. She gave good feedback on ideas of your essay, but was minimal on the mechanics of writing.
Extremely disorganized and repetitive. Support are really poor as well, no responds on Ed and Hw are poorly structured. Do not recommend
Quizzes you a lot. He didn't help me personally but he may for you.
Tom was one of my favorite professors at Cornell. His grading and content deliver style was student centric. I have long remembered the hands-on projects we worked on.
Prof. Avedisian is really nice. Do your part; you get good grade.

He is funny and tries to be engaging although some of what he says can be offensive. Class is made up of only two exams, a final, and a paper. People usually do very poorly but the class is curved and there is extra credit. I would try to find other options and avoid this class if you care about getting high grades, otherwise might be interesting.

Took both his American Econ History Courses (3310 & 3320). Classes are full of info and Lyons is a great lecturer. You ABSOLUTELY HAVE TO GO TO CLASS and TAKE GOOD NOTES, if you skip lectures you will be screwed since there are no powerpoint slides and the class is graded on two prelims+final with pretty much all the questions coming from lecture.
Tom Lyson was the best professor Cornell has ever had. He was dedicated, dynamic and brilliant.We will never see the likes of him again. He's in Professor Heaven! Look down on us kindly!

This guy is a living legend.  He has over 500 published papers, and has been the pioneer for the field of chemical ecology.  His story-telling is fantastic, and all of the stuff he talks about is just so interesting.  One of my favorite professors that I have ever had, I stand in awe of his accomplishments as a researcher and a teacher.

Homeworks are kind of difficult and really really long, but they are all due by the end of the semester. There are no practice exams provided, so taking notes on the slides will be the best bet. I avoided lectures since he spends most of the time going over impractical examples. Class is not hard, but it is TONS of boring work.

He is very strict with students attending lecture ON TIME WITH ICLICKER. If you show up 2min late you might as well go back to bed because you would have missed the iclicker. His lectures are extremely boring and homework are harder than what he expects in the lecture. His instructions are confusing and his jokes are lame too....

Made real analysis super interesting yet built our foundation of the subject. It is his first time teaching as a professor yet he is highly organized and willing to help. Difficulty of course is due to nature of content, but he tries to show the motivation for every result. He taught many topics normally not taught, such as Lebesgue integration.
It's a class that is worth waking up early for... He also gives you tons of extra credit opportunities so getting an A is not too difficult.
Great guy

Absolutely amazing professor. He's clearly passionate about what he teaches and is extremely interesting in and outside of class. He is very good at leading engaging discussions and is not a harsh grader at all. Very entertaining. And the amount of work is fair.

He is an absolutely wonderful professor! He is helpful and his lectures are always interesting. Very open and thoughtful. The one draw back is that the exams are all over the place in what is considered important material. On a side note, I took this course for writing in the majors and it was amazing! Would definitely take a course of his again!
Amazing! Loved it.

Content is valuable, but the structure could be a lot more student-friendly. Grading is petty and inconsistent, with hidden criteria. "Nothing a TA or professor tells you is guaranteed correct". Relies heavily on pre-recorded videos, actual class time is redundant. Weird 9 P.M. deadlines. Prof's lack of enthusiasm affects learning atmosphere.
He presents the material very clearly and lectures are organized and interesting.

HD1130/PSYCH1131, awesome class. Prof. Hoffman is funny and a breath of fresh air, clearly cares about what he teaches. Even though he reads off of slides, he applies useful scenarios, and relays the content in an interesting way. I recommend going to lecture but its not necessary. 3 prelims, final non-cum, 2 papers. Textbook not needed :)
Super unreasonable when dealing with issues in the course.
Comically nerdy guy, but nice enough. As someone who knew nothing about CS I learned a lot. Lectures are recorded so there's no reason to attend. Everything's curved heavily. Median was a B, I think.
Great professor. She is a tough grader but really values her students and wants to see them succeed and grow as writers.

Lyrae is one of the most incredible teachers, and inspiring people I have ever met. I came into her class hating poetry and left after every class understanding something knew about poetry, literature, and life. She cares about her students, engages the class and facilitates great discussions. Take her class. She's amazing.

Harold was a fantastic professor and I absolutely enjoyed his class. It is definitely in my top 5 classes that I've taken at Cornell. If you're interested in soil or ag, you will not be disappointed. We did have to read a short book as an assignment but it was worth the read. Overall an excellent experience.

My advice, use the textbook, lecture slides were made off the textbook. Anke was enthusiastic and caring, but sometimes skimmed through topics. The key is to visit office hours, as long as you do HW well, you are set for the exam. Discrete math may require some creativity but once you notice the patterns its manageable. Poll for attendance.

This was a new course this semester based on a grad course. The class was incredibly interesting, well taught, and had no tests. Grade comprised problem sets, peer grading, and a final group project/presentation. The teaching team was always accessible. I wish they gave A+'s, but otherwise this was my favorite class at Cornell.
Excellent professor.
By far the best professor I have had.

Caleb was a great teacher and always made class super engaging even in the online setting. Grading criteria were always clear and homework assignments were simple yet interesting. You could skim the readings but if you really want to get the most out of this class you should do the readings and pay attention to lectures. Highly recommended.
For once I enjoyed going to class, can't recommend strongly enough. I wasn't always sure what to make of the writing prompts but they were never boring

First the first couple of weeks, his lectures were clear, and verbatim of the textbook. He really did make the textbook extremely straightforward. However, as time progressed, his lectures became extremely confusing, and the majority of the stuff he was teaching was nowhere to be found in the textbook. Read the textbook and you should be fine.

I don't know why Cornell is hiring professors that can't teach at all. This guy has no teaching skills whatsoever. Also, he's never present in class during exams. He gives out the exams and leaves the room... he developed a new method to give exams I guess. Stay away if you can! you will learn more by reading Hull's book on derivatives.

Yo f*ck this class. Literally go in, listen to her say "sigma wah wah" twenty times in a lecture,  then end up asking yourself, 'Da f*ck did I just learn?' TAs don't know the material; claim the class is messed up. Pass a prelim, do some projects, have your ears bleed two times a week, done.
Really nice prof.

If there is definition for an average class, this class is it.  Everything is predictable; do the homework, go to lecture, put in some work for the class and you get an A.  He's not the most exciting professor however and will talk quietly while he basically reads the textbook to you.  But the class is not overly difficult.

This class was okay, it gives you a sampling of knowledge you need for future classes. She didn't really help anyone understand anything, so thankfully the material is pretty easy. Projects/HW is usually easy but time consuming.

Professor Fahey is incredible and easily one of my favorite professors I've had at Cornell. He's super passionate, and his deep wealth of knowledge is clearly built from years of staying up-to-date with the literature, not to mention his own research. Definitely go to lecture, you won't get much from the slides alone. The lab portion was super fun.
Best professor ever!!!
Really great guy. Classes are always very interesting.

I really enjoyed taking differential geometry with Professor Healey. I He very much cares about his students a lot. His lectures were very clear and informative.His exams were fair and had the potential of being very challenging, the killer questions were the true/false where he would take off points if you marked the incorrect answer.

Professor Riley is a gem. I took this class to explore proof based math and was enchanted by his enthusiasm and course. Lectures are engaging and rewarding. Homework can be challenging but also rewarding. The two TAs were great. Exams were challenging but the course was curved a bit. Perfect course for intro real analysis and fair rigor.

Professor Devoogd class is the reason why im pursuing neuroscience as a career. His class was really interesting to me and I genuinely learned alot. The content is heavy and you need to go to class to pass as it is mostly graded by weekly quizzes. However his teaching style was very clear and he tries his best to make the class engaging

graded completely on discussion section participation and three 25 question MC canvas quizzes, super light work if you show up to lecture but easy to tank your grade with a few questions wrong. would recommend if you can stay organized and on top of the big lecture topics

Great instructor, so understanding and sensitive to the student experience and stressors. Does not assign a crazy amount of work and wants students to do well. The assignment descriptions/criteria can be a bit unclear/confusing, but just ask any questions you have about it. Easy class, just do the work(mostly papers and readings for class).

Don Koch taught Heat and Mass Transfer Fall 09. He had good lecture notes that you can follow. He presented the material well and explained any confusion. However, the homework and prelims tend to be tedious and hard. However office hours were very useful.

Great professor! I decided to take another class with him because I liked the first one so much; he really tries to engage students and create thought provoking discussions. Assigns quite a bit of reading but a very chill and caring professor overall.
Dr. Campbell was a joy to be taught by and really cares for her students. I don't recommend taking this class as a freshman. Dr. Campbell is a really good professor and the TAs are very helpful!
The lectures are structured very well. Homeworks are crisp and clear. Support is provided at anytime. Professor is ready to help in academic matters outside of course as well.

Interesting class Makes you buy two books which are a waste of our time Tough and unclear grader Cancelled 4 of our classes and had us attend a 2 hour make up class Gave us final essay prompt 9 days before due date (gave us the Tuesday of finals week (12/6) and due last day of finals (12/15). Very unfair thing to do as many of us had 3 or 4 finals

Simply put, Clete is the man. Dresses well, great facial hair, the first teacher I was inspired by at Cornell. He's an unbelievable speaker, and you'll be writing 4 pages of notes per class.  They call him D-minus Daniel, but it's an overstatement. Just do most of the reading and go to class. I got an A. Love this guy, really nice out of class too.
Had her in undergrad. Horrible teaching methods. Cruel to students. Only cares about herself. Does have good knowledge of subject matter but should not be teaching.

I really wish I hadn't taken his class.  He grades a full letter grade off from the other lecture of the same course and doesn't have as good of a grasp as most of his students.  Even winning the course competition (judged by actual consultants) does not positively affect how simon grades you will not get you an A.  Those that can't do teach?

Professor Lichter is a really nice guy and genuinely wants you to do well. The topics are pretty interesting. There is a lot of reading. Tests were multiple choice but the averages were pretty low (he curves). The class median ended up being a B.

Wonderful professor. He is kind & takes great care to create a community in the classroom- always inviting students to dinners & campus events/activities. Class might seem daunting at first, but if you try, show up, read, & most of all, care, you will gain a lot. Highly recommend making the most of your class with him. Plus, great sense of humor :)

Absolutely fantastic class that taught me so much. Prof. Haynes is such a great lecturer and I would highly recommend ASTRO 2201. Plus, she makes herself so accessible and welcoming. I also really appreciated the writing-based and spread-out grading structure!
Hard class, heavy workload, don't recommend taking it
She is the best! Take any class you can with her.
Lectures meander and seem more like a recitation of the casebook than actually analyzing the issues within the cases. I can't imagine how her exams will be.  May the Creator have mercy on our grades.

Incredible teacher! She was always great at answering questions and clearing up any confusion. Gave a lot of great feedback on papers and was very understanding of student's workload. I can definitely say I am a better writer because of her!

Do not ever take classes with her. Took ENGRD 2700 with her and she was the most inexperienced and unhelpful professor ever. She went through 3-4 lectures worth of material in one lecture, and sometimes finished lectures in 25 mins. Whenever I left her class, I felt like I did not learn anything and had too much material. Worst core class ever.

Take PASS/FAIL if you have the option.  It's way more work than 2 credits should be. Tough projects and a very hard exam.  Class itself is pretty high stress, you cook every day and its a rush to get it all done by the end. It can be fun if you take with friends but aside form learning very few cooking tips and some bad recipes  it's not worth it

One of the harder PAM classes. She's actually nicer than she seems during lecture. What she says during class in terms of current policy reforms, data, etc. are very accurate. Doesn't conform to one political ideology. Maybe sounds like a libertarian but maybe that's because the course is focused on utilitarianism, efficiency, opp. to paternalism

Believe me no matter what you do your grade at the end of the day could be a grade lower because of your teammates scoring. The professor makes your original A total a B with an extremely unfair peer grading (despite the fact that you did the appropriate work in your group). Obviously this class is rife with injustice
Very kind and helpful professor, happy to meet outside of class, but very easy to understand in class.
I took his night class. He was always chill and understood that students have a lot of other things on their plate. He brought in snacks sometimes and was really open to hearing everyone's opinions.
Great professor. Very respectful of the diversity of students in class discussions. Papers are straightforward and flexible.

Great, caring lecturer, she is interesting and understanding when it comes to attendance; this class is a super easy A, as there are no prelims, and no hw except for weekly take-home discussion posts and quizzes; attendance is "mandatory", there are many extra credit opportunities, and you don't need to read the textbook. TAKE THIS CLASS!!
One of the best professors you could ever asked for in life.

Very good Prof and interesting subject matter. Be ready to read though as the class is very reading heavy (thought not more than any other in the Gov department) and relies heavily on these readings for class discussions. Grading is fair and there aren't too many essays to write throughout the course.

One of my favorite professors and has the most wonderful manner. You read a new work for each class and length varies (short stories, poems, plays). Reading is always manageable. Class consisted of weekly discussion posts of 300 words and 2 5-page essays. There is a lot of freedom to explore and write about the readings you prefer, easy grading.

Professor Giannella was an okay lecturer, important to pay full attention and take good notes in class. Readings are not too bad to go through, however it is important to memorize key facts and cases. I would not recommend this class to anyone who is not interested in Ancient Roman terminology and society.

Bruce is honestly the best professor I've had at Cornell. I'm not gonna sugarcoat and pretend like his class was easy; the lab reports did take a lot of work. However, I truly truly mean it when I say he wants you to succeed.  He was incredibly supportive and kind when I was struggling. As long as you are actively trying, you'll get an A.
Very good lecturer, helpful with homeworks. Tests were hard but reasonable. Learned a lot. Definitely do the readings.
I love the course! It tell us a lot new things!

Professor Johnson was extremely rigorous, and homework and exams were difficult.  He was extremely willing to help students learn the material, however, and had a good grasp of how well the class was understanding his lectures.
Amazing lecturer, definitely one of my favorite classes at Cornell so far. Take it if you like history, food, Amstud, even if you're not in those majors

Professor Katz confuses being "tough" with being arrogant and rude towards students. I've heard many accounts from interactions with her that are nothing short of unprofessional to downright offensive. It shocks me that someone with 35+ years of teaching experience would choose to engage in verbal flame wars with ~20 year old college kids.

Professor Nyrop was my favorite professor during my first semester. He is an amazing lecturer and truly cares about his students. Honestly, the class is super easy, but he makes it interesting and makes you care about the class in terms of other things beyond just an easy A or A+.

I truly enjoyed this class. I'm not sure the lectures were terribly useful (are they ever?), but I kept going to class anyway because this guy is geeky and hilarious, and his love of what he does is infectious. I didn't think it was that hard, but CS100 is a weed out class so some people will find it way too hard.
You might start crying during his exams. Bring tissues or a sense of humor.

Lectures are boring, talks about one main culture in class and leaves it up to the TA's to discuss the readings in section. Lots of reading, but overall not a difficult class. Pop quizzes have really specific information on them.

Awesome teacher. Was my favorite class last year. He goes through the material pretty quickly so make sure to pay attention. If you don't want to rapidly write down notes, I would recommend buying the Take Note. Overall hilarious guy though.

Professor Muller is abrasive and arrogant towards his students and colleagues alike. He calls out students in class for asking clarifying questions. He also makes students feel bad when they are struggling in his class and ask for help. He is an obviously accomplished researcher, and he never hesitates to let students know this or feel his power.

Dr. Putnam's class was tough but also my favorite. The literature discussions were the low point of the class, but he's not out to humiliate anyone by calling on them. My best advice would be to study hard for the exams and put in the work to write the term paper at the end of the semester, and you'll learn a ton!
One of the funniest professors and very caring. Not the best lecturer

There's very little work and no exams. The lectures are a little boring but he clearly knows what he's talking about. I wouldn't consider this an "easy A" class though (median grade is B+). Most people think they can ace the quizzes without paying attention to the lecture, which isn't the case

I was in his freshmen writing seminar called critical surfing. He seemed kind of off topic sometimes but his assignments are reasonalbly distributed and not so overwhelming. He is generally nice and easy grading. His video demonstrations are interesting yet disgusting time to time. He is generally a good professor and I liked his class.

Phil 1112: American philosophy. Took this for FWS. Discussion-based class, but sometimes Kwiatek steps in to guide us in the discussion. 4-5 Papers, weekly 500 word annotations on readings. Gives good feedback and grades generously. Went through Emerson, William James, Du Bois, Jane Addams. Very nice guy, genuinely interested in philosophy.

The best class I've taken at Cornell by a long shot. It is very obvious Yunhao is passionate about teaching and cares about students' outcomes. I found myself often going to office hours to go beyond the course material, which Yunhao made exciting and approachable.
Hard to communicate with

Dr. Zhang is a really great professor with clear slides and plenty of resources for success. She's usually strict on assignments and extensions, and her grading can feel unpredictable unless you're 100% clear with the content. Visiting office hours is honestly the best way to clarify content and the best way to get a high grade.

It's his first time teaching the course but he is always available and ready to work with students as needed. His exams are fair and you can do well as long as you study! He provides practice exams and the homework is a good way to make sure you know the concepts. Overall, he's a fair grader and gives chances to earn points back on exams

Dr. Zhang is a very nice man, however, he does not understand that students have other classes besides his class. He also has very poor course organization as he doesn't give us rubrics and has unrealistic deadlines.
Gives amazing lectures, always fast to respond to emails and shows great understanding for students. One of the best organized class I've taken

Natalie deserves to get her PhD revoked for how badly she runs this course. Lectures consist of her mumbling through slides and "solving" practice problems by copying the answers she has right next to her. She has no idea how to solve the problems herself. You won't learn anything in this class. Terrible introduction to the EnvE major.

Natalie is an amazing FWS professor who really cares about her students' success. She always made an effort to accommodate students during the pandemic. I loved taking this course and would definitely recommend her to future students!!

Prof is nice and accommodating; lecture content pretty interesting, can sometimes be repetitive. Overall, engaging class to think about technology and society, and not too difficult. Exams were a little challenging, but homework, essay, and participation hold enough weight to boost your grade. Liked the TAs and would recommend this class!
Professor Melas is great! Super sweet, super passionate! You're going to have to read and participate, but a great time nonetheless!

Definitely more more than I expected, but that's probably because Ms. Soto actually read my writing and really worked with me to help improve my writing skills. I hope to take other classes with her in the future. Great class if you want someone who will invest in you - both with her time and attention (slackers beware!).

I took Mindful Intercultural Communication with her for a few weeks before I dropped the course. We did not mention the topic of communication once, and largely focused on tangents and themes that were not described in the syllabus. The course description did a terrible job at describing what actually went on in the course.
Tough but good professor; takes forever to grade things

I took Professor Alleynes Intro to Poetry class followed by Advanced Poetry because I loved being in her class. Passionate about what she teaches and a beautiful writer! Ive never felt more creatively challenged and rewarded. If you have any genuine interest in poetry or creative writing, shes a great choice!
One of the best professors Cornell has! I loved her courses!! Highly recommend
No Comments
Lauren's class helped me become more confident in my writing! Her assignments make writing feel much more accessible and have inspired me to write more. Great class!
He explains things very clearly, and cares very much. Amazing teacher.

Awesome class (comic theatre), super interesting subject and Rusten teaches it exceptionally, very enthusiastic about the subject and very conversational teaching style. GPA saving class but also just an enjoyable part of my week.

He's a pretty straightforward lecturer, and the class, despite seeming like a lot of work at first, wasn't too bad! Just keep up with the assignments, and the concepts are easy to pick up. Lots of research papers to read, but Jeff is pretty chill in this class and gives good hints on what to pay attention to for quizzes, during lectures.

Rachlinski is a funny guy, but his lectures are confusing. I found myself having to lean heavily on the readings to understand concepts because of this. I think that he needs to slow down and keep focused. He also seemed to be a more intense question-maker for the tests than Chatrchyan (co-prof), treating undergrads like actual law students.
incredible class about financial engineering & hands down the best professor I have had at Cornell.
He is the best. no need to say more. If you're looking for an FWS, look no further.

Out of all of the classes I took at Cornell, this class has been the most rewarding and eye-opening. It is a fairly easy 2-credit class, but it has significantly challenged my opinions on pesticides. He's truly HILARIOUS even when he doesn't try to be, so his lectures are always engaging. Very good at explaining concepts and organized. TAKE IT!

Released final project details two weeks before the final exam and decided to have a it due on the day of exam (with a presentation). Also absent and very irresponsive throughout the semester like bro u good
