Good class where you read texts, watch films, and then discuss them in lecture. There are some group presentations about those things and then a final project. Very nice prof.

Sara is one of the best people to learn from within PMA at Cornell.

She has so much knowledge about the history of the craft its a performance in itself to watch her give a lecture or present to the class.

Her projects are incredibly fun and allow you to be as creative as you want.

If you can take a class with her, do it. You won't regret it.

This class was a lot of work but Dr. Pryor is a really awesome professor! Really good lectures and you can tell she cares a lot about her students. Definitely don't be afraid to ask questions, she's really good at answering them and is very willing to help. Exams were very fair based on the course content and she used a free online textbook.

I took this class SP20, and Professor Pritchard was by far the most empathetic instructor that I had when the world tilted. The class can be a lot of reading and is discussion-heavy, but it's very relevant, and I ended up rethinking many of my own assumptions. I definitely recommend this course (and Professor Pritchard as an instructor).

I had Jonathan for the FWS CLASS 1531- Greek Myth. We read directly from the Iliad, the Odyssey, and the Aeneid. There were 5 essays total, and I found them fairly easy and straight forward. I enjoyed Jonathan, as he was a nice person, but the actual in class discussions were a little dry sometimes.
Professor Warner is quick-minded and can always bring you brilliant ideas. She may be intimidating at first, but you will find out how awesome she is afterward!

Warner is among the best lecturers in CEE. He really cares about his students, and all of his lectures are extremely well done. I feel like I learned a lot of valuable stuff. The one TA for this course was spread really thin though. Really needed to go to office hours to do well, but few office hours were available.

Doesn't know how to teach. Doesn't know how to support you in office hours (her positive reviews are obviously written by her if you just read them). Assignment instructions are vague but graded as if you know exactly what she wants. Has a very fragile ego.

at most 3 absence in lecture or you will fail. lots of readings for an intro 1000-level course. barely any homework besides readings and two papers. HEAVY accent and low voice! yet the worst part is she does not give any slides and notes for every lecture are required to upload. gives very little information about the course and doesnt communicate
This guy is the best! Not just in class but also during office hours. Amazing person to talk to, really encourages youth development. I'm gonna miss taking your class Papa Ditti!

She was always prepared for lectures and had an engaging teaching style. I gave her a low rating because she did not stand by her syllabus or rubrics. If she disagrees with the way in which you handled her class, she will fail you, regardless of your grades. She failed me based on attendance, even though it only counted for 15% based on syllabus.

She has trouble with actually giving specific advice to people. When I was confused about a topic, all she did was send me word for word the assignment details again. Also, a lot of her examples just straight up don't follow the guidelines at all and make the assignments more confusing.
I took Professor Vaugn's Narrative Writing Class. We read really great things, she had brilliant insights, she fostered an easy environment to engage in, and she provided good writing feedback. 10/10

I wish I had listened to the site/friends who said not to take her class. Even if you understand the lecture materials which are relatively easy, her grading and timing is very harsh, so that the test averages are all Cs and less than 5 students actually get As in the class and majority get Bs/Cs. There also is no curve which is unfortunate.

She has high standards but she is very honest and fair. The class is very intimidating at first but eventually you become accustomed to the workload, which is definitely very heavy. I went from a background of 0 chinese to being able to hold basic conversations and keep up with chinese friends

I wouldn't recommend taking his class if you want to understand anthropological theory. He seems to believe that because we are undergraduate students, we can only understand very superficial things; he doesn't seem interested in engaging in any intellectual discussion or listen to your opinion. I feel like that defeats the purpose of seminar.
Iris watched TV drama while students were working hard beside her.

Professor Gu is one of the best professors I have had at Cornell. He really makes the material engaging and interesting, which I am sure was difficult considering the class was taught through Zoom this semester. Great class and great professor!

Dr. Thonney is an excellent teacher and highly respected within the sheep industry. Labs are practical and useful, as are chores, and lectures are informative. He is sometimes hard to hear and is not great at explaining concepts, but if you pay attention you will have no trouble in this class. Practically no homework.

Very very easy to do well without going to lecture. The only hard thing is that everyone does well, so the prelims being easy are not really that helpful. Honestly if you just understand the concepts, a lot of the tests is just figuring it out right there and then and not that much of studying hard.

All this comments are a lie. I am currently an Econ major and took this.  Grading criteria is purely based on your exam (45% for 2 prelim 45% for final, 10% hw which is completion). DO NOT expect helpful lectures. He skips a lot of fundamental ideas and he is not clear. TA makes the exam so your test depends on the TA, and the TA changed this year.

Awesome class. Johnston is super easy going, canceled one of the papers for the class. The lectures are pretty interesting, lots of videos and visual material, and all of them are online word for word. I went to most of the lectures, read over my notes before exams, got an A+. Exams are 100% essays and graded fairly.

Rob was the toughest Honors College instructor that I've had.  He assigned an entire book to read, plus a paper.  Weekly.  He was quite condescending, and was really too obsessed with the term:  Design Principle.  My class should have been titled: Introduction to Design Principles.  Avoid taking his class if you're all about grades.

Prof. Hockett just skips couples of his classes. No PPT, no syllabus, and no homework. His lecture is more like chatting with someone, which is not favored by me. Very open text problems and you cannot miss it.
Prof. Travers is superb! He will change your view of history for the better!

just a reminder to anybody looking at this page that the math department professors rate themselves to raise their scores. Strichartz is a failure as a professor and will literally not write notes on the board. Also has bordeline pedophilic tendencies and you should avoid him if possible.

If you wanna actually learn about healthcare and business take US Healthcare Systems, you'll actually learn about it more and with better lectures. Karpman kinda sucks with lectures, the slides are boring and he could be dry. Its 1 credit though so its not that deep, there were only like 3 papers which weren't bad.

I actually took 2095 with Menard. The class was interesting, and she is super friendly. It is mainly discussion focused which gave it a chill atmosphere. She is a very fair grader and will give helpful feedback on papers as well as being very relaxed and understanding about deadlines.

Although this class was hard work at times, I enjoyed to discussions she lead in class. The essays were graded fairly harshly but she gave us the chance to meet with her before they were do and discuss our progress- you MUST do this. Overall she was pretty good, although she could have been more understanding at times.
I took this course in Spring 2018. One of the best classes I have ever taken at Cornell. I learned a lot of useful and transferrable skills from the course.

Honestly kind of mediocre. She seems nice, and she brings her dogs to class, but I dont think shes the best teacher, per se. Class is decent, moderate in difficulty (more bothersome than difficult, since there are lengthy projects that must be done in pairs as well as exams and written assignments and a pretty involved participation grade).
Professor as extraordinarily unprofessional, took far too long to get back to us when asking questions, and all in all provided for an overly stressful semester. Would not recommend.

Great professor, great guy. Had a panic attack one day and he sat down to reassure me outside of class. He genuinely cares about the success of each of his students. He expects a lot because he knows his students can deliver. Loves to meet outside of class to help you succeed.
Best class I've taken at Cornell. Prof Geddes teaches such an interesting topic in a way that will def leaves students very engaged. Super funny guy, def recommend taking! You'll learn a lot!

very informative and a great class, that being said you gotta attend class. supposedly an "easy" class, but that's only true if you take notes during lectures. i didn't pay attention in class thinking everything would be on blackboard. i got rocked.

Students who aren't that good at economics will have a difficult time in Rick's class. He's extremely smart and goes very fast, but you can easily approach him for extra help. Weekly problem sets can be challenging. His tests' means/medians normally gravitate around the 70s, but he later adjusts grades to be more fair in the end.

Prof. Hurd is the greatest. He has decades of experience with unions. He appears strict and has expectations, but if you work hard it'll pay off. His banter with Prof. Sherwyn is not just entertaining and educational - it is arguably the most productive dialogue between labor/management in the country. Shoutout his TA's, you have to take this class
He is a remarkably sage man who very willingly hands out A's. There isn't much not to like.

He's a really sweet old guy. He cares a lot about his students. His lectures can be a little boring sometimes, but the class is overall quite enjoyable. Also, if you ask him about his garden or his garage, the remainder of the lecture will be about his garden, garage, or something else entirely. Enjoy!

great lecturer with years of real-world experience as a clinical psychologist. Good lectures, a lot of reading not busy work; worth it. Only small issues with him are he seems to not really understand the content in the neurobiology section of the course (still teaches it fine) and tests on specific Hamlet quotes, so just make sure you've read it.

The material was complex but De Gorter grades generously. We were graded on attendance, two pop quizzes (he warned us the class prior for both), two five page papers, a midterm, and a final. The final was an online take home exam.

Amazing and respected professor. No one better with this much experience to teach this class. Doesn't overcomplicate anything and provides great understanding with real life examples. Exams are pretty easy and writing assignments are fair. Listens well, all past reviews are talking about harsh grading from TA's, writing assignments are now P/F.

Most boring lectures I've ever witnessed. Spends 75% of class showing modelssimulations with numbers like 0.345323423 throughout the slides. Just do the homework and reference the book if you need help. If you can do the homework, you should have no problem scoring in the AA+ range on exams

Best math class with the best math professor I've ever had. Is a god at teaching combinatorics; explains everything so well and in such an unpedantic manner. Am currently crying that he's about to leave Cornell and would loved to have taken another class with him.
It's a ton of work, but my Chinese did improve a lot, both over CHIN 2201 and 2202. I wasn't on her bad side, but if she doesn't like you, it gets a lot harder for you to finish out the class.

Do not take his class. it's not worth it. He baited me and many other of my classmates into taking his class, The first paper I handed in before the deadline he graded very generously, after the deadline however he started to grade harshly. He also showed no enthusiasm. His class become extremely boring. I dislike every second I was in his class.

Prof. Flanagan taught the advanced undergrad quantum class. 4443 is a mathematically sophisticated and theoretical course. He's a caring professor and 4443 is awesome. He covers topics in graduate quantum including density matrix, tensor products, path integrals and more . Lectures are important because the content isn't encapsulated by one book.

Overall the math classes at Cornell really aren't the best. This was one of those courses were the professors teaches a minimal amount of stuff in class and then the hw is crazy hard. The tests were ok I guess. The professor was decent I guess I skipped the vast majority of classes but still got a satisfactory.

Professor Tang's lectures are decent at best, but her grading system is unclear and terrible. She does not make it clear how much participation/attendance counts for a grade, and your grade can be SEVERELY deflated at the end of the semester.

Professor Tang's lectures are a bit hit or miss. He's better at explaining fundamental concepts than applications. Homework problems range from being very difficult to straight forward, but are worth a large percentage of your grade, so make sure you have friends in the class to work with. Exams are not similar to homework, so they are difficult.

I thought Prof Tang was a great professor. He was a really clear lecturer and went over things multiple times if we asked. He made difficult concepts seem more manageable. Recommend it for anyone who wants to go into banking or finance. It was hard work to study for the second midterm but well worth it. All homework is for completion and oyo
Very nice person. Very accommodating.

Teaches Worldly repertoire, Personally ambitious and knowledgeable.  High charisma as a conductor.  Passionate about the work.  Effective pedagogy through setting clear expectations and communication skills. Some of my best college years spent learning from this conductor. Has tough standards, but keeps it fun all the same.
Extremely hard tests. When you go to office hours and ask questions, he just makes it more confusing. Very very strict in lecturing. All the jokes he makes is kinda sad.
inspirational professor
Best professor you'll have. He is the nicest person and truly cares about his students' well being. He makes sure his class does not become another burden for students, he is fun! 10/10!!
Not my favorite professor but thorough slides and content.
Although a lot of work outside of class and learning on your own, a great course
Always willing to help out, answer questions, and get to know students. You can tell he truly cares about his students and their learning. Exams were a good reflection of homeworks/practice prelims
Boring at times; though makes a difficult subject easy. Great person - invited the class over to dinner at his house!
Great guy good teacher

All exams are True/False and are very subjective and up to professor's interpretation. Not interested in returning points even if you prove him wrong, "that's what the curve is for". All lecture materials are old and half the time he just plays videos instead of teaching. Avoid at all costs.

Awesome professor. Very helpful in office hours. She uses videos to illustrate points and uses exercises to break up class. This was my first class at Cornell, and I am so happy to have had Professor Vincent. Make sure that you apply concepts to situations or you will lose points on exams.

Prof. Taylor's lectures are engaging and like a story. He's very accessible by email and the fastest grader I've ever seen. He gives good feedback and allows for revisions if you take his suggestions into account. For a 3000 level history class, the work load is standard, but as long as you do it and form your own opinions, you should do well.

There's really not much wrong with the course or the professor's ability to repeat the text. The issue is his method: most of the grade is in a massive stack of problems with a due date of "the end of the semester." If you're like me, that means "don't pay attention and don't bother" which doesn't work out well. Be wary if you lack self-motivation.
He's not a professor, he only does recitation. He's pretty good though. he sometimes goes too fast, but I liked him this semester. Very chill guy / decent teacher.

Wasn't a fan of him. Lectures are long and extremely boring. He loves to talk but also makes you work in small groups. Attendance is taken every day - there is a sign in sheet. He emails you to ask where you were if you don't sign in. Readings and comments due every class - make sure you do them. Wiki pages require a lot of research but aren't hard

absolute worst!Made ECE4670 a nightmare! Has no idea what's going on in labs,didn't cover the materials required for labs in lectures; HWs,labs left ungraded(has been 60 days), converted in-class prelim to take-home exam DURING the last 5 min of the exam! 9 min late to class on average, unresponsive on email/Piazza,Messed up commcloud several times

Professor Hitchcock has been my favorite professor; he makes the class feel personal, knowing the students' names by the second class, and takes the time to explain concepts thoroughly if you don't understand them... so participate! EAS3420 is a difficult class, but his chalkboard-style lectures and expertise with the material make it much easier.

There is so much detail in all of his lectures that it is almost impossible to gauge what will be put on the test and to the extent of detail you need to know. He is also quite snarky when you respond to his questions in class. It seems he expects you to understand what he has learned in the past 60 years within 4 months.

Great open-ended class where you get to work in a team on a real project that Prof. Jackson actually worked on before. There are no clear guidelines and answers, just like real life, but Prof. Jackson is very helpful and more willing to go the extra mile to get you in the right direction. One of the best classes at Cornell (and ORIE).

I don't know what happened last year, but he literally implies he could give extra credit in return of a good review. I think he needs to focus on the quality of his course rather than finding a way around.

Tamra is AMAZING. Such an entertaining person, genuinely cares about all of the students. The kind of instructor you would take your personal, not just academic, problems to. Highly recommend her for an FWS. Also a very fair grader, this class was easy.
The most boring lectures of any prof I've had and is an unnecessarily harsh grader.

Erik is a great guy and is very intelligent. He will help you understand the material and has a dry sense of humor. I thought he was hysterical although it seems as if many of his jokes go above peoples' heads.
i failed finance w another professor. ukhov makes info clear, practical, & understandable. it's almost like having a good professor can make a subject feel easy!

Very knowledgeable, the course definitely provides a good preview for prospective matscis. Baker has this idea that the ideal test mean is a 50, so the tests are correspondingly difficult, but the class is curved so don't be scared. Labs and homework were pretty substantial, but they did help me learn the material better.

I believe he visited my school(UC Riverside).Homework is online (Webwork) with unlimited tries. He is more than willing to stay after class or extend office hours to clarify anything. Very good professor
Such a caring and enthusiastic professor! The readings for the class were all very interesting and you can tell she is very passionate about the topic.

By far the most disorganized class at Cornell. The only good part about it was the tour of the particle accelerator that Prof. Hoffstaetter organized. Everything else was unsalvageable. On top of the weekly PSets, weekly lab reports, prelim and final exam, he added on a final project. Lectures were consistently 1-1.5 weeks behind homework.
He's fantastic.  Cares about what he's teaching, cares about whether or not we're learning.  The class is interesting and not too demanding.  Go to lectures, it's worth your time.

Extremely kind and sympathetic; gets if the whole class is sleep deprived and dying of overwork. Material is really engaging and gives a more rigorous understanding of how to analyze visual media. Fair warning though, it's still incredibly hard to learn and write about for those without much prior study in philosophy, literature, or fine arts.

I love Professor Rivera. She is intelligent and witty, and covers so many different and interesting concepts. Spans from disabilities in love to BDSM to Plato to online dating. Readings for homework are mandatory for discussion but never too long. Peer reviews for most essays, and Profs feedback is good. Such a good class, would recommend.

He's a really great prof!! He wants students to do well - if you are struggling/need help/want something to be different, tell him. Super accessible outside of class. Make sure you find a friend to do the end-of-class quizzes with (it's allowed). He is so nice and enthusiastic. Applies concepts to specific examples really well.
Genuinely enjoyed almost every lecture. Covered a good range of material, and the problem sets and programming assignments did a decent job of forcing you to understand it.

Essays are self-reflections about your personality. Other grades are group assignments that are online game simulations. One group receives 100, one 80, 60, 40, and 20.

If you are in failing groups both times, you won't do well in the class (3/5 groups fail each time).He is comfortable giving you 20% on essays that are fully completed and on time.

Adds some humor and excitement to otherwise dull material.  Class is relatively hard, but the tests are a fair assessment of what you know.  No textbook for class and no graded assignments beyond a pair of in-class and take-home tests.  Lab period is used for extra-long lectures more often than for looking at embryos under a microscope.

He's a really nice and funny guy. The grading is sometimes unclear, but he is always willing to help explain anything. Read the course packet notes he gives, you won't learn anything from the lectures. The lecture material was always way harder than anything tested on, the course packet is more accurate on what to expect. I believe he is retiring.
She's great

Although I barely went to lectures, I loved her energy. Lectures were great and interactive. I'm a visual learner and it helps that she draws a lot of diagrams. She is also very accommodating as long as you ask for it either in-person after lecture or through emails. Her TA's are all rly great too.

Her class is very lecture heavy (you cant miss em) and her tests are heavily based on them and on the readings. a lot to read as well, but if you read the week readings before the classes they help balance all the information well. hard grader but everything is found on the syllabus.
Definitely a great professor for Financial Accounting course !

One of my favorite professors.  She takes these incredibly complex philosophy writings and explains them so simply, yet so fully.  She doesn't water down the philosophers, she explains their points better.  She's liberal , and she felicitously attacks conservatism.  You will never be discriminated against for being a conservative. She loves debate
Organized and helpful.
KINDEST PROF IN ALL OF ILR!!! Take him!!!

*BIONB 3240 & PSYCH 4230. David is a very laid back prof- sometimes to a fault when he's late sending things out, etc, but it's def worth it bc it makes his classes v not stressful. He grades easy. In the lab course the grade was based off 3 papers, a short test, and participation, all graded reasonably as long as you can write a scientific paper.
If you like history you'll love frug. Great man, great teacher.

Dr. Vider taught a fantastic course on mental health and mental illness. This course introduces a lot of historical background of medical injustices in mental health care and Dr. Vider clearly has command over the content. He is very accessible and understanding of students' circumstances and wants to see you succeed. Highly recommend.

Dr. Jesch was absolutely one of the best profs I've had at Cornell; his course is very laid back, he is lenient with deadlines, and I learned an incredible amount about biochemistry lab techniques. He is caring and kind, and I would highly recommend this class, even if it is not required.

No class structure, his lectures have nothing in the way of purpose and he barely manages to extrapolate a few themes for the course. Thoroughly unhelpful in relating his ideas coherently or making sure you are understanding them. Boring and monotone as well, I couldnt help falling asleep in class. Only grade was the final, feedback is terrible.

While it is true that you can tell how much he cares about his students, and the class itself is an amazing help and made me a much better math student, the classroom environment was nearly always very toxic. Everyone, including the TAs, is scared of him. He frequently became explosive and has no problem belittling you in front of a class.

Professor Newman is very very knowledgeable about feminist theory and does a great job of teaching it to growing feminists. The majority of your class will be spent reading. These readings do matter as they are how you are tested. That being said, there is only 1 short quiz and one final, so not a lot of exams. I recommend

As a 2nd semester senior, this was the worst course I have taken at Cornell. The material is dull and the professor seems to enjoy it that way. Slides are paragraphs, no points, just walls of single-space text that are read verbatim to the class slowly. Miss more than 2 classes and your grade drops from A to A- and so on for each day after that.
Amazing professor. Take Comparative Foreign Policy with her. It was the best govt course I took at Cornell.

I took this FWS in the fall as a freshman. Although there is some reading, and there are papers, its a really good course, you only need to get one book, and the readings are mostly online. She does expect more from people, but in general, she tries to help you out. If you do the readings and essays, there is no reason you shouldn't get an A.

I loved Jenny! She was very shy and soft-spoken, but extremely sweet. You could tell she really cared about the students in the writing seminar, and was really flexible with class scheduling. The books we read were interesting for the most part, too. Not work-intensive, and a very fair grader. I highly recommend her.

Jennifer is all around my favorite professor from my first semester. I took her Disney FWS, and it was amazing. Her energy is infectious, and she is so understanding of the stress her students undergo throughout the semester. She is always willing to make time to chat - about life, her class, anything. Take her FWS! You won't regret it.
A lot of papers to write for a class where you learn so little. Hard to know what will be on exams.

Professor Wissink seems like a great person and was very accommodating but omg this class was crazy difficult. If you're considering taking the 3 week summer course with her please don't. I think her style of teaching just made it even more difficult than it needed to be. But def be prepared to study like crazy bc the summer class moves fast.

Very sweet and extremely willing to help. Strict about attendance and only allows one extension, but can be lenient with deadlines if you talk to her. Has not been known to give out As on essays (she said she does not really), but not hard to maintain a B in the course.

She made it really hard to enjoy the class, since she either moved through the material too quickly, or brought in guest lecturers who didn't know what they were doing either. That being said, this class is not difficult if you do the readings in the textbook and look through and memorize notes before exams.
The material itself was interesting but her lectures were unbelievably boring. She is really nice though.
Intro to MechE was the main reason I did not quit engineering first semester. The class was interesting and enjoyable, and the prelims did not feel like they were designed to make you fail.
It's asynchronous, so it's easy to go at your own pace, and the lectures are pretty easy. The group assignments are slow and annoying though, so hope that you have a good group.
Enjoyed Dr Soberon! She's passionate and her class is easy.

Hall is very kind, fair, and understanding. Gives extensions + lots of extra credit opportunities. However, this class had a TON of busywork. 2 group projects/presentations, 3 or 4 writing assignments/essays, a very long midterm, graded participation. Everything was take home and he gives a lot of time though. Lectures are very in depth and good!

Loved this class. Learned about leadership in a military setting that was clear and engaging. However, class badly organized: tested word-for-word on exams (annoying especially since lots of students weren't in ROTC), never kept us up-to-date on grades, and Blackboard was never updated. That being said, worth the annoying stuff to take this class.

Great for an introduction to Materials Science, but also a good engri for any major. There were weekly quizzes every Friday which were pretty hard and I hated them. Psets were difficult but not time-consuming and plenty of office hours. For the prelims, we got one page of notes and there was a group project instead of a final exam which was great.

Class was a lot of plug and chug with some algebraic manipulation. If you don't understand something, Bean will usually clear everything up during office hours. You'll also learn more about the fielddepartment by going if you're interested and she's likely to cut you some slack on HW if you tell her your weaknesses. Also very personable.

I like Riedl a lot but feel like its hard to get to know her. class was interesting but we didn't get a single grade until the last day of class. No tests. Creative project with vague guidelines and a big research paper with a lot of advance notice. Co-taught by several professors who were all knowledgeable but not very available. God tier TA
Rachel is an AMAZING teacher, she cares and really helps you grow in your writing and prose in general!

Does not provide any rubrics for exams or papers. Will deduct points on exams with no explanation. Dismissive and condescending in conversation. Overall horrible class and Graduate TA's do not know what is going on either. Never take!

I had professor Weil for my first history class at Cornell, and thanks to her I'm a history major. She's passionate, intelligent, and well versed on the material, but she also clearly shows an interest and care in her student's wellbeing. She's a fair grader w comments like "I don't agree with your point, but you defended it well." Love her!!

She was a tough grader on assignments without giving any helpful feedback. Argues constantly with her co-professor to the point where class is uncomfortable. Additionally, dismissive toward me during office hours.

This guy talks a lot but it's because he's interested in his work. Great lecturer, but if you don't show up to class good luck surviving. Despite this, very accessible and understanding outside of class.

One of the most frustrating classes I've taken. Complete lack of rubric for the 3 essays that comprise your grade. You can fix every mistake the TA cites and then get the exact same grade on the next essay. Professor is arrogant and condescending when you reach out for help.
Prof. Billera is very friendly and willing to help. Do come to his office hours and it is of great help for the tests.

Heavily biased grading in History of Economic Analysis. The structure is that you essentially get 2 grades and they are weighted in some ambiguous way (not listed on syllabus) for your final grade. 8:40 class, provides useless feedback on papers, and is downright cold . Take anything else  Received a grade much lower than expected with no warning.

Very unclear lectures, although his jokes are sometimes amusing. If you don't do well on the midterm, then 70% of your final grade depends on your final exam score, which is crazy thinking that your entire semester comes down to a 2.5 hour test. The curve must have been good though since somehow I did fairly well while not knowing anything.

Propaganda was an incredible course. This professor is a tough grader, but she is a very sharp, critical reader. She will tear your essay apart, but if you're willing, you will learn so much about expository writing, and how to craft watertight arguments. The essays aren't easy, but you will stretch your critical thinking and writing skills.

I STRONGLY discourage anyone from taking Wind Power. The lectures are disorganized and uninteresting. I thought the class was going to bestow practical knowledge concerning wind turbines on me, but it was the complete opposite. It is all off-the-deep-end wind turbine theory. Unless you get off to derivations and fluid theory, stay away from MAE 402
He likes to interrupt lectures to play his trumpet and have the class "Name that Tune". Not much learning goes on, but it is nonetheless the best part of my day
It was a one-to-one class, and he really cared about me as a person, and wanted me to succeed. however, he hates bell-curves, so watch out!

Was excited to take this class because of prof's reputation but was a bit disappointed. Class structure changed from prelims to exams. Prof also gave the option to not take the final because of "stress on campus." Appreciate the prof's flexibility but rigor of the course felt compromised. Felt like the second half of the course was a bit lost.
He is a really great professor and told us a lot on how to use assemble the Raspberry Pi to build an autonomous vehicle. He is so nice to help us about programming problems.

You just have to show and do the work to get A, but too much work for a 1 cred class. The prof does research relating to greenhouse gases and animal/environmental topics, so entire course was geared towards public misconceptions/a lack of education about those topics. The prof is a nice guy, but very blunt and often all over the place.
The best professor of my life.

Professor Halpern is a great lecturer and is very eager to answer questions outside of lectures. The class is quite hard conceptually, but especially workload wise. Personally I spend +10 h/ week on the course, majority on the HW.
Horrible lecturer and really boring content. Sits there on one slide and speaks for the entire class. No engagement.

Professor Joseph Conrad was THE WORST PROFESSOR I HAVE EVER HAD in any class. Throughout the course he was unclear, dishonest, prone to arbitrary grudges, and focused on the 10% of the students who already understood the material. He copies the same lessons from his extremely poorly written book year after year, without adapt to student needs.

Classes with Professor McComas are worth it because she's such a nice woman. They can be boring but once you get to know her she can be fun, and by the end of the semester you realize you actually learned quite a bit.

Katherine is AWESOME! She is a great masseuse that really understands the stresses of being a Cornell student. She shows you step-by-step the different techniques. This class, Swedish Massage, is a fantastic class to take, it really helps relieve stress, it feels great, and it gives you some new massage techniques to impress your significant other.

As a psych major, I can confidently say Kinzler's class has been the best psych course I've taken at Cornell. Her lectures are engaging, build clearly off each other, and if you take notes and pay attention in class you will ace the tests; she isn't out to get you, just testing knowledge. Attendance is taken with clickers, so show up!

Most of grade based on weekly quizzes, first taken individually then with you group- the grades for the two are combined.  I prefer this to tests because you just have to know the content from the previous lecture and have read the assigned paper vs weeks of content.  She's also a kind prof, seems to really know and care abt the content.
Great FWS and great professor. He was always available to help us with our writing and he did a great job of explaining the lecture concepts.

This class was really interesting. The prof went above and beyond to make the class accessible and flexible in the online learning environment. While the lectures were dense, learning goals were clearly identified and the assessments were firmly focused around these. Also assessment was flexible and tailored to each student's preferred method.

Merwin is awesome. he is so willing to help students, and is very flexible in terms of deadlines and due dates.  He obviously really loves teaching and is very knowledgeable.  I'm sad to be out of classes to take with him!

Amazing professor, doesn't assign work for the sake of it and is passionate about the topic. Well accomplished (Harvard, Princeton, UCLA), but very humble. If the opportunity to take this class arises, I would 100% sign up.
Loved the course. Required for the MS degree. Everyone attends.

She was a great lecturer who forced you to participate and read the material. Your grade is dependent somewhat on your participation and 2 pop quizzes, but it's almost solely based on 2 papers in the semester. I left the class with a new perspective and reverence for people. Take it!!!
She is awesome.
Asis is a great professor. You will learn a lot.
She is so sweet to all students and she knows her stuff so well! I really enjoyed reading literature with her in classes. rewarding experience

I'd only take a class with this guy again if I got hit with a shovel and forgot about the first time, but I learned a ton. By far hardest class I've ever taken and likely ever will take. He's super nice, very easygoing about late submissions, and wants you to succeed. He just really likes math. He may be unaware if everyone is struggling, TELL HIM.
Clearly cares about the students, knows his stuff. Make sure to take the notes on the slides before lectures because he goes very fast.

Lectures:  poor, condescending, boring.  Homeworks:  lengthy, tedious, uninformative, nit-picking.  TAs: variable.  HW sneaker-net was demoralizing.  Exams:  races against the clock.  Instructors demeanor:  arrogant, unhelpful.  Chewed out the class because someone's phone rang and he didn't look at his watch.  Lame.  Always late for class.  Awful.

Firstly- we had to write a gigantic paper on calculus.. CALCULUS!!!! The final was the worst thing, homeworks are graded so harshly that NO ONE DOES WELL, and not a thing is clear. I learned nothing from Steve, and if not for my amazing TA, Im 90% sure I would have shaved all my hair off from the stress of this class. NOT WORTH IT!!!! BOOO!!!!!!!
This class was extremely disorganized.

Professor Ichikawa is very funny but some say that she asks more difficult questions during discussion. Format of the class is the same as JAPAN1101 - daily discussion, two lectures a week, and the occasional quiz/test. Frequently you'll have to memorize whole dialogues to act out during class.

Great class with interesting readings and a professor that clearly knows what he is talking about. However he will spend most of lecture discussing things that are only tangentially related to the readings. Grading feels tough during the semester but isn't actually that bad by the end. Would recommend.
charismatic, easy to follow, makes u wanna go to lecture, super passionate, simplifies complex concepts, explicitly says what he'd ask on a test
Too hard to get a good grade

All you need to know is dont take this guys class if you see his name. He will count off a whole letter grade if you dont show up to class 3 times. Newbie professor who shouldnt be a professor. I put 5 difficulty because if you want to make anything above a B with how he grades it is gonna be borderline impossible. An A+? FORGET ABOUT IT

The worst professor I've ever had. She's horrible at communicating information, often confuses herself with content, and ends up teaching incorrect info. Then she'll quiz you on information she hasn't taught you or taught you incorrectly and stubbornly convinces herself that she didn't. Never seen so many students dislike a professor this much.

Really great professor and lecturer. Lectures were detailed and clear, content roughly correlated with the textbook, and homework was pretty easy if done after reading the book. Lecture slides directly correlated to test questions, and plenty of flexibility with choosing paper topic (there was 1 big paper when I took this class).

A horrible human being. First class on the first day, this guy had the audacity to say that there is a "genocide in Gaza" out of nowhere. This made me and other students who have family in Israel extremely uncomfortable and I hope no one takes him in any classes.
Absolutely love her.. ILR has some hidden gems and shes one of them.
difficult class but loved the professor
Very interesting and great class. Highly recommend taking this class. It is not terribly difficult and half of the class is participatory discussion. Loved it!

Very enthusiastic lecturer but course content was not at the level of an introductory course. Very little foundational info was given to build a basic understanding of complicated concepts. Grading guidelines for homeworks and exams were unclear, but revisions were allowed for exams for up to 50% of points back.

Pretty easy Spanish class if you are confident with your Spanish. Class is almost completely taught in Spanish and students are asked to actively participate (in Spanish only). Not a heavy homework load, and we spend a lot of time going over grammar rules in class, but they aren't taught very well. Watch a lot of videos and listen to songs.

I came into this class hating Spanish, and I now love Spanish, so I guess that goes to show what a great professor Diego is. He will make you do the work to understand Spanish, but I think at the end of the day he wants everyone to do well in his class and go on to actually use their Spanish. Cool guy with a very cool background

Professor Gillespie is easily one of my favorite professors at Cornell. He is very dedicated to the topics he teaches and his lectures are always interesting. He gives great feedback on assignments and his TA's were incredibly helpful.
No Comments
Very clear lecturer, if you go to class and put in some degree of effort, youll learn the material and do well in his courses. He wants everyone to succeed.

Probably the most applicable and interesting class that I've taken at Cornell. Prof. Geisler is also fantastic and   one of my favorite professors by far. Everyone should take this class! Class is easy if you're willing to put in the work and do the readings. Most of the material is discussed in depth in class.

Lectures can be long and pretty boring, but it is clear that prof Brainerd is an expert in his field and passionate about the subject. Lots of textbook reading, but if you stay on top of the readings, attend lectures, and memorize key concepts, the MC exams are pretty manageable (and usually graded on a curve too).
I took Kozen for FSAD 3200 and loved her. She is the best dean ever and is so sweet. The class was pretty easy if you did the homework. Love Kozen with all my heart.

Li Laoshi is a wonderful teacher, I would recommend that anyone taking her classes be well versed in mandarin however, she does not ever speak english in class and this is important. she grades fair, she has a loving personality and is really caring about her students even though it may not seem so. she is very sarcastic in class keep this in mind

Great Class. A lot of reading required though. However it is quite easy to get an A. Participation really counts. There is an activity for every class and it counts towards final grade. Do not waste the extra credit opportunities.  No textbook Just pdfs lots of pdfs uploaded online. There can be like 4 readings for just one lecture.

If you really hone in on his lectures, you're pretty much guaranteed at least an A-. Basically, just PARROT back his ideas and points in your papers and quizzes, and you're all set. Heck, you don't even need to bother with the readings. It's an easy A if that's all you're after, but don't expect to develop any original thoughts of your own.
Boring. Annoying. Made me rethink my major.
Interesting, witty teacher who uses provocative remarks to make you remember and pay attention! Her class was a lot of fun, but the essays she assigned were pretty long.

Professor Balance creates a class environment for open discussion and participation. Even though the class was only lectures of 40 people, she treated them like discussions. While she did cold call at times, she is a really nice person and is willing to discuss anything and help her students in any way she can.

She is the sweetest and I have learned so much from her. She is extremely helpful and makes learning fun! Even though we had class every morning at 8am I was always excited to come to French class because of Madame Sparfel.

Grading was very straightforward, this was one of my most organized classes. Homework was reading 1-4 sections of an online textbook with questions embedded. A lot of it was too detailed for tests, though. Tests were more lecture-focused. Lecture notes were posted and Prof Goodale was a good lecturer. Clear office hours and appointments if needed.
she's a nice lady and really cares about students and has a lot of positive energy, which is hard to come by at cornell.

This is the worst course of Cornell. Not only the lectures, but exams and its solutions also have typos and errors. The TA grades extremely harshly and in most of the cases, she herself has her solutions wrong. Her replies to piazza is extremely delayed and often confusing. I would rate this course D+.

Dr. Leuenberger is truly passionate about what she teaches. She's super accommodating and the class itself isn't too much work. Readings and discussion posts twice a week plus some response papers. The lectures sometimes got boring but the content was super interesting to learn!

Professor Enz is a great professor.  She may not be the easiest, but she will go out of her way to help you.  You will learn a lot from her course.  HADM 4143 is another great course to take from her.
Is there anything this professor can't teach?

The professor wasted half of lecture either talking about people's pets or playing MOBLAB games which is great until the prelim shows up and its nothing like the homework and requires luck and guessing...
Really enjoyed this class! Definitely recommend Professor Chan :)
One of the very best professors at Cornell and thoroughly cares about his students!
This is really good professor. Recommend taking his course.

Although he sometimes goes on rambles, you can see he is good at taichi. His movements are very delicate and I could not replicate them as a beginner, which shows how good he is at it. Overall, the class was quite slow in the content, and I wish we could have learned more moves. But I learned quite a bit and I enjoyed the class.

Guckenheimer gets a lot of hate. He's actually a very knowledgable guy, and insanely helpful. The lectures will make you sleepy and the homework sets frustrated: but if you actually enjoy math, you'll love them both. If you only are here because you HAVE to(but hate math): the prelims and exams are easy, but you'll hate it all coz HW and lectures.
very interesting guy with a unique(in other words, odd)sense of humor. if you fufill or exceed his expectations, then you will be in good shape. old school cornell pedagogy to the max.
what a Ph.D candidate

Honestly, he gets a lot of trash talk but he's seriously a really nice professor. He is so enthusiastic in his subject and it's unfortunate that many people don't take his class seriously. If you feel like you are doing poorly, try and talk to him! He won't bite :). There's also a huge curve at the end so no need to stress!

I absolutely recommend this class! He was an amazing lecturer and made the class super interesting. Super easy assignments (even let us skip one if we asked a question during a guest lecture), attendance not mandatory (but you should go he loves connecting with class), very little work, and he was super accommodating and brought in guest lectures.

I had her for The American Presidency FA2018 and had a great experience. She assigns too much reading, but I did barely any of it and did just fine from listening to the lectures. She does go on tangents a lot, but they're interesting and don't make the lectures hard to follow. The final isn't hard, and she gives you past finals to study from.

Unsupportive 
Condescending

For what its worth, the class doesn't seem hard on the surface. However, why should I take a class where I have to put in so much effort only to get an A-. There are so many other easier available first year writing seminars that give free A's and A-'s. This class just isn't worth taking.
Good lecturer. Only thing were her replies on ed discussion or in office hours which were not helpful or sarcastic , otherwise a good professor

Extremely energetic. Like its insane. She'll be all revved and ready to Arabic the second she bursts through the class door (literally). Overall she is nice, receptive to questions&explains concepts (especially grammar) well. Attendance&homework are a must; she  makes you work for it. Isn't lenient at all in terms of grading, but you'll learn.

I had so much fun in Professor Goold's class, all of the content was based around comedy and humor. Goold is very understanding and in my opinion a fairly easy grader. She reads between the lines and sees what students are trying to say in their work. Laid back class, she was easy to talk to.

Was drawn in by the topic in the beginning but this class wasn't engaging at all. Very United Nations-centered course to the point I would rename it "International Advocacy through the UN". Readings were not necessary and assignments are fairly generously graded. However, class was not organized or engaging at all and almost no one paid attention.

A mess. Syllabus was wrong in several places and was confusing, wasn't clear at all about what he wanted, wouldn't even mention some assignments, activities suck and we often didn't understand them, rushed through the slides. Very nice, but doesn't understand what he's doing (and practically admits this at end of course.)
Ask him for help and go to the writing center.
Worst teacher I have ever had. She dictates from a piece of paper each class and bores everyone to death. Physically impossible to pay attention in this class. AVOID AT ALL COSTS.
Great prof!
Best professor ever

One of my favorite professors at SHA. He gets really annoyed by students who do not put in effort, however. He is more than willing to help out students with assignments and discuss "bumping up" grades, but you really have to put in the effort beforehand. The class itself isn't hard; just become familiar with the nuances of MS Office.
Great class highly reccomend. Attend lectures and take notes on everything, no work outside class aside from studying for prelims

Amazing prof. Had him my fourth year and turned my education around. He's a productive and insightful teacher with incredible credentials. An incredible sense of humor helps reinvigorate the students and promotes an environment where the student and professor are equals.  10 out of 10.
Absolutely fantastic. He is an expert in the area (wrote a textbook). Very clear delivery in lecture. The course involves a lot of work, so make sure to attend lectures and do practice exams.

Difficult class, although the expected student product is well advertised. Open book exam, but this only slows you down. Lab quizzes are a nuisance. Very helpful in office hours, and make sure you do the "challenge" problem for the hw if you want to be in his good graces.
No Comments

Jerry is tough, but there is an incredible wealth of knowledge to learn from him. The pace in his studio is often intense, but it all winds up paying off at the end of the semester, when you look at how much you've improved your work. He is genuinely interested in students learning. Outside of studio, his stories are often very funny.

The main thing  is the lack of clarity surrounding R. Earlier, I wasn't clear on things and I asked both a TA and Marty if I should consider taking stats at an easier level  and I was told to stick with it. The concepts I mostly get but w R, I just don't get it. Way more explanations would help (he is v accessible by office hours and is super nice

I really enjoyed Development with Wellstead. This class made me want to pursue RE development. His exams are a little unfair in my opinion, but his lectures are really interesting and he is very passionate about the subject matter. He's always available to his students and happy to help or answer questions!

Piyawat is an amazing professor. I have never met anyone as inspiring as he is. Nevertheless, I have never had any music education before in my life, but he made everything sound so easy to comprehend!

Professor Weiner Heinemann is one of the most flexible, understanding, and caring teachers I've ever had. She truly cares about the subject and her engaging teaching style makes students care as well. I've learned so much  in her class, and my writing skills have improved so much.

Professor Koenecke is very caring and has a lot of knowledge from her experiences in the industry. Her lectures are awesome and her guest lectures are great as well. The final project is cool and you learn a lot in INFO 4390. Everyone who is interested in ML or data science should take the course with prof Koenecke.

This was my second class with PK, and if you can take a class with him DO IT! 1817 and 2817 are two of my favorite classes at Cornell. Learning from him is a joy, class related or on life. He values learning from students, even with his mountains of expertise. He pushes students to think critically, and challenges us. Cornell is lucky to have him.
I didn't actually take Professor Arias's class for 3316, but I pretty much did, considering I substituted all of Jian's lectures for Arias's lectures.

Rodrigo is pretty great for only being a grad student, definitely definitely take him over Cecilia Lawless. Hes very chill in class and wont push you too hard to participate. Essays and assignments are actually pretty easy compared to other spanish classes, hes just not quite as easy a grader as you would expect.

Professor Kim was amazing! She definitely is always there for her students and I loved taking her class. She uses real businesses like Amazon, Wal Mart, H&M to provide examples on difficult concepts which is extremely helpful. She's also extremely good looking as well, so that's a plus if that stuff matter to you lol.
Very organized and clear lectures and seems very knowledgable about the course content. Would recommend to take CS 4820 with!

He is a nice guy but he glazes over most concepts. I really did not benefit much from going to his lectures and often had to return to the material in the textbook to reteach myself the basic concepts. Nice guy just not effective as an instructor in my opinion.

If you want to learn how to use Adobe and useful FSAD modeling technology - this is not the course for you. There are only a few major assignments and your grade hinges entirely on what TAs you get and what mood they're in that day. Be prepared to get blindsided by a B- or lower if you make a single mistake on an assignment.

I found her class extremely frustrating.  No matter what I did, I still got the exact same grade on every assignment. She gives students an overwhelming amount of work (6 "small" group projects, 3 individual decks, 3 large group projects, TONS of participation) and consistently failed to explain what she wanted on assignments in order to do well.
Very strict in grading. Has an opinion and sees it as the only possible one. Horrible decision to take this class.

Her lectures were really good and I enjoyed the class. My only complaints are how long it took to get grades back and the way there were very few assignments worth so much. But it wasnt hard to do well on the essays, and if you study a bit you'll ace the final

BEST PROFESSOR I HAD AT CORNELL SO FAR
Great explanations, can be fast sometimes though so you have to be concentrating in class. But absolutely amazing, never wastes time but somehow still manages to not make lectures boring.

Good lectures and helpful demonstrations. Very fair exams and generously curved (I think). Accessible outside of class. Having a strong command of multivariate calc will help a lot. Purcell (the textbook) is quite good and should be read thoroughly to reinforce the lectures.
Just watching Johns tight body while lecturing about the most interesting mathematical theories turns me on to a level beyond the Delorian Principal.  The way he Pi's is a pleasure to my eyes.
I felt like professor Murray really cared about how we were doing and she was very interesting and knowledgeable. I really enjoyed the class and would highly recommend the professor.

He teaches some pretty complex material but explains it well. His midterm was difficult and you have to attend extra homework sessions to know how to do the problem sets. He has a really great dry sense of humor that made classes entertaining. :) I loved him as a professor but he was tough... you're expected to know everything on the slides!
Simply phenomenal

McBride is a great prof! Always kind, caring, and understanding. He makes sure his class isn't conflicting with any other classes and is very flexible with extensions. Make sure to stay in communication with him if you can't meet a deadline - communication is key! The syllabus guarantees an A if all work is turned in (regardless of quality).

Though I meant to write this review at the end of the class, I am writing now because the most recent one is incredibly wrong.
There have been no bad reviews since February of this year.
This professor is so kind and their grading system allows for so much flexibility, as long as you put in an ounce of daily work his grading policy GUARANTEES an A.
"I wish I could make you sorry."

I personally find statistic an inherently boring subject to begin with having taken the AP class. That said, Turnbull did nothing to improve the subject for me. He monotoned perfectly for entire 75-minute lectures. I held out longer than most and stopped showing up to lecture for the last two weeks of the semester and I don't gong would have helped

This was my all time FAVORITE class!! Halpern is the man. He's very energetic and knowledgeable...and pretty darn funny, too. LOTS of readings, but you learn time management and what you can get away with/without reading. Highly recommended!

The way the class is structured is that lectures mostly cover pots and paintings. The professor seems really helpful and nice, and he's pretty funny sometimes. But if you're not interested, it's kind of a hard class to stay awake in. Don't listen to the rumors-- you DO need to study for quizzes and put work into the essays. It's not a guaranteed A.

I also had Professor Fontaine for MATH 2210 (lin alg). He is very nice, and if you look up his credentials, you can see that he's brilliant. His handwriting is sometimes hard to read, and sometimes hard to understand him because his voice sounds kind of muffled.

A really great professor who genuinely cares about his students. He is very understanding and grades on your progress (not natural talent for writing). The writing labs were interesting and a great way to show participation. I am a shy student but would work hard during the lab and add to discussion boards and got credit for participation.

Bruce is wonderfully aware of student dynamics in his classroom. He states his expectations clearly and reiterates often. His homework assignments are not too demanding. He is the "cool dad" type who is down-to-earth and very kind.
Shes just rude! Don't take this class unless you have to.

I really enjoyed class with Prof Bailey. I loved lecture, and enjoyed the somewhat open ended problems, since he graded them fairly according to how open ended they were. I loved the final project, and found him helpful when I chose to wait to talk to him instead of a TA. Was reasonably understanding about asking for extension.

The worst lecturer I have had at Cornell. He was not engaging and described material like he didn't know physics himself. The tests were hard and NOTHING like the pre lecture videos or homework's. Largest standard deviations on each test that I have ever had at Cornell.
Field Ecology was very fun and very interactive, this guy is knowledgeable and he's worth a listen.  While he says statistics are optional, they're really not (know them).
Gave us inspirational classes, great translational classes for future Engineering studies.

For PSYCH 1101. So random and completely out of rubric. Definitely not something should be taught in a PSYCH 1101 class. He is also using slides for HD 3660/ 1200 or whatever it is for this class. I haven't learn psychology at all before so everything does not make sense at all. use the same word as definition for the word. Writing before final day

Best professor I have had at Cornell thus far. Professor Litwin really cares about his students on both an academic and personal level. He will give you an entire page letter of constructive feedback on essays. Always kept class positive and tried to involve everyone in discussions. Readings sometimes felt long and repetitive, but nothing too bad.

His lecture notes are messy and full of typos. His lecture in class is confusing as he seems like to talk to himself and ignore if students can understand or not. He couldn't explain theories straightforward and clear.
Adam Levine is one of the best professors I have had at Cornell. You have to go to class everyday, but you don't have to do the readings. The tests are pretty easy if you review your notes

Bias towards atheletes, changes answers on a whim if athletes said so, doesn't like to use Mic, can't hear him, can't listen to recordings, doesn't have concrete reasonings as to why his quiz answers are correct, multiple correct answers to one question. Trick questions.

Basu doesn't teach the best, even he acknowledges that, however: open book exams (canvas, lecture notes, notes),  ALL MCQ, weekly quizzes, 3 grading schemes to give you the best grade. Discussions were super helpful, lectures not so much. Compared to the other prof teaching econ, I'd take easy exams over teaching quality for this intro couse

Alaka is an incredibly amazing professor. She doesn't expect you to reinvent the wheel but she does expect you to think critically. If you go to lectures (take good notes), do the readings, and make connections this will be more than enough to do well. Alaka really cares for her students so make sure you go to office hours for any clarifications!

Basu is an amazing professor -- he uses anecdotes from his experiences at the World Bank and at the Gov't of India! I loved his lectures and it really forced me to think differently about problems as well as more strategically. I found myself reading the optional material. Great class and prof; one of Cornell's best. The right amount of challenge.

Snow is the best professor I've ever taken for at least three reasons, but his high standards are difficult for some students.  1. Great content: Snow developed and honed his content over 30 years. Great stuff. 2. Detailed, frequent feedback: He provides detailed written feedback & one-on-one tutorials. 3. Accountability: He holds us to standards.
super awesome way to quickly learn python for non CS majors!!! very sweet guy, brings his dog scooby to class
The worst professor that I have ever had in the entirety of my Cornell career. So disorganized, I completely wasted an entire semester taking his course.
prof is very passionate and knowledgable about what she teaches. assignments are straight from lectures
Class subjects are interesting, unfortunately he is exhilarated by the deaths of innocent civilians.
My favorite stats professor of all time. Russ' lectures are hilarious, and he gives you a great impression of what it's like to be a statistician in the real world. Office hours were really helpful.
Love Jane! A wonderful person, professor, and friend. Thank you!!
Absolute best professor I've ever had. Take a class with her, you will not regret it.
One of my favorite professors, she is a great lecturer. If you are interested in psych you will gain a lot from her course. Extremely organized and knowledgable.
Love Prof. Berger! She's a really approachable, nice professor. It was a fun class

Took Intermediate Fluids with her in Spring 11. Her first time teaching the class, so some of the flaws can be given to growing pains. But on the whole, her lectures were very general and did not really help me do the homework (arbitrarily graded because the TAs didn't have a good way of scoring them), and her tests were utterly impossible.

Keep up with the readings, lectures are boring, but I thought the material was decently interesting (maybe not to everyone). Class isn't too hard if you keep up, and exams just require you to regurgitate material.

Jane takes FWS too seriously. Papers are not only marked harshly, she gives them later than the subsequent paper is due which doesn't allow students to improve on their papers effectively. Lectures are boring and films are VERY long.

5 essays + a presentation (ungraded). Average workload except Essay 5 is 4000 words.... why? Avoid her.

Attending Cornell and not taking a class with Professor Law would be a mistake. Her lectures are eye-opening and can change your entire perspective on the world, religion, conflict, stress, and reading for the better. She cares deeply for her students and it shows in her teaching. Everyone should take a class with Prof Law, regardless of major!

Took his 70's film class at BC. Unfortunately his class was way too hard. He expects so much on exams without giving clear guidelines and there is so much reading. Its a shame because his lectures are spectacular and he's so passionate, and his content is super interesting but I can't recommend his class unless you plan on pass/failing it.
Clear knowledge and has great control on the subject matter. Great lectures and good learning.

Means well but lectures degrade into conceptual nonsense. FWS is very philosophy heavy and class is not based on traditional graphic novels but on informational graphic novels. It is hard to decipher what he wants in class at first, but become clear that you have to roll with his opinions. If you want an easy or interesting FWS this is not it.

Professor Culler, though a genuine sweet-heart, is not a great teacher. I love poetry but his lectures made me (and many others) fall asleep. His voice drones and is way too quiet. Lecture information isn't helpful for graded material. Also, he doesn't give clear grade criteria so I never felt confident handing in any of my assignments.

Flipped class- lectures were online (1-3 hr per week) but they didn't always make sense. Class was meant to be used for homework, but half the time it was more lecturing. Bad class to take if you don't have friends taking it. Everything turned in on paper and he lost our first prelims. Goes over time on labs. However, really cares about students.

spring 2019, median A. Took it s/u great lecturer, entertaining. Gave fairly interesting readings. Im not a humanities person, not very analytical about history/lit  I just wrote down everything he said. I had trouble following sometimes bc I didnt try very hard, but I think his analyses were cool. Class is too big for his discussion format

Prof. Boyarin is a top quality teacher whose expertise spans a wide range of topics. His lectures threaded diverse perspectives and subjects into one cohesive presentation. Anyone with a modicum of motivation and a brain between their shoulders can get an A in his class. And those who engage and listen will learn a lot from him.

The syllabus wasnt really clear. I was expecting he would have a course evaluation survey. The course is in ansynchronized type, he used recorded videos of past lectures. He will not respond to email immediately even if he seemed active on canvas discussion. I really like the materials and the course in general, and he is really advanced in R.
She is a nice woman but a little annoying. The tests are basically straight from the book and she gives ppts and handouts that are helpful.  Shes a solid teacher

This class is tough. I'm an A student, and I have gotten B's on every essay. She might have let my personal beliefs affect her grading, which I don't think is right. If you are willing to take a "credit", this class is for you, because she won't give you an A unless you get a 93 or above.

This class was the easiest class I have taken at Cornell. She treats students like they have no knowledge of basic biology or genetics. All you have to do is show up and look through your notes the night before prelims to get an A. 1 short paper and an easy take-home final. Worth taking for the interesting articles and easy GPA booster.

The tests are the same every year, basically, but the material is still hard if you've never taken AP and if you don't keep up with lecture/reading. I found it hard to follow lecture, but that's just me. i fell asleep a lot.
Prof Schnieder is great!  I learned so much very quickly.
Sweetest professor ever
David is a cool guy, but not the best professor. His teaching style is very messy and it is easy to get lost. The class is so obnoxiously easy, however, that it doesn't end up mattering.

In Byron Suber's ballet class you will get an amazing work out, spiced up with tidbits of dance history. He makes you reevaluate the Ballet aesthetic and think about its socio-cultural implications. His style is based on classical ballet, however, be prepared for unusual combinations that will challenge you to ditch your habits.

Despite being easy, this is the only course that I regret taking. Dr. Drinkwater is passive-aggressive, a poor communicator, fails to facilitate discussion but blames the class, and makes many errors but offers little grace. With scatterbrained lectures, redundant field trips, and elementary content, even the most motivated will learn very little.
Good class

Professor Berger is absolutely outstanding. She really engages with the material and inspires her students to do the same. If you're willing to work, you will get a lot out of her classes. Also, it must be said that she is a very kind and thoughtful person who really cares about her students' success.

Great, encouraging professor with clear, easy-to-follow lectures. Holds helpful office hours and review sessions. Projects in the class are a great balance of challenging and engaging. Learned a ton in the class and would recommend to anyone who is trying to get into CS!

ASTRO 3334 is a great chance to get hands-on experience with Python techniques in Astronomy. Lloyd is helpful in class, although difficult to reach outside of it. The class consisted of 8 projects that ranged from the very easy (apply to research programs online) to finding exoplanets and other difficult tasks. You need Python experience.

I love Professor Maralani. She is intelligent, cool, and a great lecturer. She clearly cares so much about her students and wants to get to know them individually, even in a large class. She's transparent about her process as a professor and genuinely wants feedback to improve, even with a lot of teaching experience under her belt. Great class!
Ridiculously hard content with very little explanation. Inhuman amount of homework and tough grading.

I had no idea what to expect when signing up for this class, but I can honestly say that it completely changed my outlook on the idea of parenting. She's brilliant, and yet still seems happy to be teaching us. The 4 quizzes shouldn't be a problem if you go to class and take notes. Take this class, it will change your views on everything.
Would take a class with her again

Dr. Lane was incredibly good considering she teaches ENGRC courses which engineering students notoriously hate. She was respectful of our time and tailored the class to our future careers. Generous with extensions, kind, tolerant of slang/swearing during discussions, and baked cupcakes for us! Would love to get to know her if I weren't graduating.

This class was okay, it gives you a sampling of knowledge you need for future classes. She didn't really help anyone understand anything, so thankfully the material is pretty easy. Projects/HW is usually easy but time consuming.

Amazing professor. Really organized + engaging lectures. There's a manageable amount of readings per week plus textbook chapters, however you don't actually need to read the textbook in order to do well in the class. The assignments are tedious but doable and are graded pretty fairly.
Great course.

What everyone's said is true. She might first seem a little scary bc she's brilliant but she's not scary. She's hands down one of, if not the BEST professor I've ever had so far at Cornell. She listens to every question and answers, is seriously prepared for every lecture, and makes all her tests fair. 10/10 would take another class just for her.

Prof. Orr is super nice and knowledgeable. Despite his amazing educational and professional background (Princeton, Yale Law, New York Times), he is humble, listens to you, and stays after class however late if you have any questions. Course materials are carefully chosen. Totally recommend any class with him.
Great lecturer. Was happy to go to class every week. Entertaining, loves to answer questions. Good demos in class. Super nice and curved reasonably. Just an awesome dude.

Class itself is not hard and I would it's an easy A if you put in the work (which is a lot). However, there's a lot of reading so if you don't want to read a LOT, do not take this class. Attendance is mandatory for lecture and discussion sections. Lecture is incredibly annoying because the assigned readings tell you the same stuff in more detail.

Wonderful teacher. There to help. Subject is rather difficult so going to office hours is essential. Tests are tough, but doable. Homework assignments are fairly easy and serve to solidify knowledge of the subjects taught in class. Did an excellent job teaching her first semester of Labor Econ. Textbook not mandatory but helps.

Had Professor Cao for 1920 but there was no option for that choice. His lectures aren't organized very well, making it very difficult to discern what's important and what isn't in his lectures. Had to learn everything from reading the textbook. To be fair, it looks like it was his first semester teaching 1920 and he tried his best to be a good prof

Professor Xiadong is a very caring and compassionate professor. He inspired me to learn about Ricci flow and alleviated some of my insecurities about applying for graduate school. Only suggestion I would have is to encourage more collaboration among students. If multiple students worked together on a single paper, it would more likely be published.
boring class, but good professor

Not a bad professor for lack of ability but lack of care about anything related to the course or its students. I don't know why she's in the field, because she could make anything associated with it more boring than simply watch the clock tick on. Also, her grading system is quite honestly atrocious.
Although Professor Verhoeven is very knowledgeable and a good lecturer, the class involved overly complicated paper assignments that are harshly graded and a lot of reading.
Good prof, great class, but not a spectacular lecturer.

Some people were frustrated with some of his lectures in the genetics section of Block II, but he also had the hardest topics (e.g. some other professors had "This is a gene" or "Simple modes of inheritance").

The BEST lecturer I've had in CSCE department. Notes are clear enough to get you an A without attending anything, but attending the lecture would probably inspire you to pursue more in his field. Very patient, helpful, and responsive both in and out of classroom. Well organized and developed course structure, definitely have him if you can.

Really great guy, he is really passionate about what he is teaching. Lectures can be rambling and confusing, but they didn't really matter for your grade. No tests just short papers every few weeks and two longer ones. Not an easy A but an easy B. It does take extra effort to get those final points, but average work will get you a B.

The problem sets are pretty difficult, so you basically have to go to office hours with the TA to get the answers, since it's a pretty significant chunk of your grade. The midterm is in class and you can bring a cheat-sheet and the final paper can be on anything you want. Overall a pretty solid class.

She did a good job at explaining how to use the formulas, but she didn't explain the theory behind them very well. Expect a lot of simple computation and some short proofs, which aren't difficult compared to proofs from other classes, like CS 2800.

Very kind professor and engaging during lecture. He allowed problems sets to be worked on with a partner but they were still very hard and sometimes not covered in lecture. I would take another course by him though!
He really doesn't know how to explain things and his office hours are such a waste of time. Highly recommend having ChatGPT replace him.
Well respected and esteemed professor. Don't try to cut any corners in his class, he is very good at picking out slackers.

Extremely good teacher, very dry (hilarious) sense of humor. Covers material fast, but you learn to keep up. Very passionate about chemistry. Teaches so you really understand where your electrons go. Hard to get by if you try to memorize without understanding the chemistry. All in all, one of the best professors I've had at Cornell.

Prof was very knowledgeable and interested in what he is teaching about. Syllabus is clear and Chad is open to listen to and learn from students. Writing assignments helped me practice writing often and class was engaging. I felt lucky to take his class and would again if I could.

Best professor I've had at Cornell. Period. Explains things more clearly than any other instructor - truly interested in your learning the material. I can't say enough good things. Only bad thing is, I learned recently he's retiring from teaching soon. Take his classes while you can. Not sure what the major will look like when he hangs them up.
Professor Riehl has been my favorite teacher at Cornell and his class has been excellent. Must take for all ILRies, Inequality in higher education. Tough class but very worth it.
