Great professor, passionate lectures, very project heavy and quizzes directly came from lectures

As a graduate student, I really love him even though the materials were extremely hard for me. His passion for mechanics and teaching really push you to another level. He makes me realize that I'm far from claiming I'm good at mechanics.

Ridiculously sarcastic and he's the only one that finds his jokes funny. He frequently picks on one student at a time and makes rude jokes towards them to make fun of them. Man comm should be an easy class, but he made it as difficult as possible with his grading scale and unimportant lectures.
Best prof ever.

Karl cares immensely about his undergraduate students, and is incredibly distinguished in this field. He goes out of his way to help and mentor students in their careers. When grading, he will taken into account your commitment to the class, so expect to attend lectures. Hands down, the best professor I've had during my time at Cornell.

One of the easiest classes at Cornell if you just show up. Minimal studying is needed if you go to lectures, and pay close attention during labs. Field Trips are engaging and tie back into exams. Lots of small projects, reading, and quizzes throughout the semester so just keep up with all the work. Watch the syllabus and you'll be fine.
He will literally go out of his way to help you on literally anything. He is super chill. Easy A, and you actually learn valuable skills and subject material.

My favorite class at Cornell so far. Lectures were very engaging and just a ton of fun. Essay prompts were enjoyable and the research process was straightforward. Ryan is understanding and clearly cares for her students very much. Just show up and try your best, and your efforts will be recognized.

Best professor I've had so far at Cornell. Very patient, encourages questions and makes sure you really understand the content in office hours. I loved attending office hours and thought they were extremely helpful. E&M itself is a hard subject but he explains it well in bare terms. He's very humble and open to feedback. Overall great guy.

Literally the easiest A+ you could possibly get at Cornell. Honestly, I felt a little bad throughout the course because since it was so beyond easy, nobody took the course seriously. A few extra mandatory assignments could make the course better in my opinion to keep students engaged. Lectures and professor were great otherwise.
Genuinely one of the laziest professors I have had. Every class was exactly the same and he just liked to listen to himself talk. He was often condescending and grading was completely arbitrary.

Mezey is a fantastic professor. Lectures are well-paced, clear, and he records them to make them accessible. He's accessible outside of class and hosts great OH. He wants everyone to do well and cares about engagement. This was my favorite class at Cornell, I wish he taught a part 2. The class will not be easy if you don't know R, but still doable.

He's a fine teacher and while I would not choose to take him again he is definitely better than other math professors. His handwriting on the board was a little hard to read but not impossible. Fine professor but there are better.

Doesn't really know how to teach a course and gives virtually no guidance on what he expects from you. Took course over summer and he tried to pack too much into the six week session. Be prepared to take a lot of photos and read the syllabus because he won't answer a question if it is on the syllabus.
Great professor.  great class
Just an all round gem. Whatta boyyyy
This class was annoying busy work. Moses lectures well and cares about his students. Exams are really easy. Lab is boring and annoying.
Cares about his students, but the class was often very boring.
Best professor ever. Love him to death.

Very good lecturer. Grading is spread evenly between homework, prelims and a final. Workload is fairly light. Lectures follow the textbook, but I would recommend reading the textbook before every lecture. Grading was lenient on exams and class was slightly curved. Would highly recommend this class for an engineering distribution as well.

Overall, the class was interesting and fairly enjoyable. However, some of the lecturers were boring and lectured beyond our knowledge. Also, the HW focused only on a few guest lecturers, and ignored the rest. The project wasn't too bad, but wasn't well organized and the directions were vague. Decent class overall, but could be better organized.

I WOULD NOT PREFER. I RECEIVING A 50 ON THE FIRST ESSAY, I ASKED FOR GUIDANCE. WE EMAILED BACK AND FORWARDS ABOUT 3TIMES CONCERNING THE 2ND ESSAY. I MADE ALL CORRECTION AND TWEEKS SHE ASKED ME TO MAKE THREEEE TIMESSSS, AND I STILL RECEIVED A 'C' ON THE ESSAY. don't do it unless you want to drop your gpa!

Fabulous woman. Knows her stuff (she co-authored the text). Approachable, engaging, & funny. If you do the work, the class is not difficult. The TAs are nice law students. A few short papers & a final exam (law school style) which turns out to be the most fun (yes a fun exam) exam I have ever had that legit. tested my understanding of the material
I was in Wind Symphony and loved every minute of it. Bet that dude didn't practice. Professor Turner is an amazing teacher and musician.

It was the easiest Class I took at Cornell!
The professor is really nice and gives out free points from time to time. Even though the class is 3 hours long, she always finishes early. She also hands out handmade fortune cookies! She's a very caring professor. It was the best class I had! :)
Apparently Kinsland in 1570 is totally different than Kinsland in 2510. She's honestly a great lecturer and makes learning orgo somewhat fun. However the class is curved to a B which is pretty harsh

The online format made the class 90% straight up lecture, 5% asking students to participate, and 5% about rabbits. Professor Robinson is definitely very knowledgeable about what she's teaching, especially since she is considered a leading scholar in the field. However, it can be quite abstract, and lectures are VERY important.

Dr. Leifer was an amazing lecturer. She covers the innate immunity portion of the course, which she knows inside out like the back of her hand. Her explanations are abundantly clear and her lectures are thoroughly engaging. She also occasionally says witty things to keep you entertained. She's my favorite lecturer from the course.

Very smart prof with a passion for the subject.
He has high standards - I did not reach them, but I learned so much. Psets are darn-near impossible for us mere mortals. Prelims graded very fairly and give so much feedback. You can get sub-30 on a prelim and still get a B.
The mathematica nightmares will go away, but your knowledge won't.
Take 3890.

Professor Londe is a great teacher. His lectures were engaging and fun. The grading criteria were as clear as can be, and if there was any confusion, he is attentative to anybody with any questions. Readings were enjoyable, and the amount was reasonable. Take this class if it seems interesting, and you won't be dissapointed.

He is extremely knowledgable about microeconomics and is genuinely interested in helping students, especially during office hours (which he conducted even over weekends during exam season!) Sections with the TA are really helpful but skipping lectures is not an option. His exams are slightly long but the curve in this course is generous.
Great class. He does a good job at reinforcing which concepts are important while keeping lectures fun and informative.
Take this class if you can, but it's difficult to get in.  Useful, fun, but biweekly quizzes can be tough.
Very interesting prof if you are among those who wish to read fiction about climate change.

She is really passionate about her subject which is great! we would receive long and hard readings every other class and she gave us A LOT of papers to write. In this class you will have a reading and 1-2 people in your class will be discussion leaders for the reading, and the next time in class you discuss the reading. we did not have slide tests.

Fantastic teacher. Fostered interest about the subject, provided timely and extensive feedback, taught a lot. Liberal towards different perspectives, and compassionate at times of trouble (midterms oh no). Hit the spot with reading assignments as well! Marx, Nietzsche, Freud is definitely a class I'd take again.
Very confusing
Good professor but not very rich class, more as memorization and that's it. Not very inspirational.

Anna is a decorated researcher, though falls short as a professor. Lectures consist of linearly reading slides adapted from another prof without context or student interaction. Assignments are very difficult and aren't motivated by lectures. Compared to other inspiring classes at Cornell Tech, this class feels haphazard and unnecessarily difficult.
Wouldn't round a 99.8% to a 100% for an A+. That's pretty petty. Other than that, she is a good lecturer and the coursework is very managable

She is really passionate about her subject which is great! we would receive long and hard readings every other class and she gave us A LOT of papers to write. In this class you will have a reading and 1-2 people in your class will be discussion leaders for the reading, and the next time in class you discuss the reading. we did not have slide tests.
Fairly easy class, difficult to jump from A- to A though

Perry is great! Took two classes with him, Art of teaching and Youth Organizations. There was reading and writing for both classes but it really was not bad at all. Great guy. Taking another class with him next semester. Make him your BFF if your in the education minor.
If you're good at math take Maria Terrell, if not find another professor.
Maria is as caring and experienced as Stan Taft, and provides significantly more usable feedback. Her grading is lenient and she provides more materials than any other teacher I've had in AAP.

Professor Wolfe was very inspiring in many different ways as she was exactly who I aspired to become both professionally and personally. I was really surprised and hurt at her lack of compassion when she decided to take half the credit off of my final reflections paper simply because I submitted it to the wrong folder on canvas.

Very passionate professor. Great class for student truly interested in understanding the mechanism behind time series model, with some technical details omitted. Cover a wide range of topics. A little European accent, but quite clear.

The professor, in general, is interesting and she tries to be like a mother to most of her students. She cares about her students, and in Tag3302 there are no exams but there are a lot of essays. During discussions, it would be best if you really know the language or else you will have a hard time. Grading is based on your progress.

Keith have been my foremost helpful advisor and professor I have met at Cornell. His advice on my research was inspiring and every direction was meaningful. I enjoyed meeting with him and receive his clear guidance to my further research. I would recommend other students to take his class. Thank you!
very interesting class and interesting projects. highly recommended!! excellent professor!
He is pretty easy. He isn't afraid to hurt your feelings on essay corrections, but it pays off because your essays get that much better. Easy, definitely a manageable 3 credit class too.

The class was okay, with prelims averaging 90%. It had two prelims, a final project instead of an exam, and some group case write-ups. Studying for the prelims was confusing, and lectures were a blur. Professor wasn't around much for help, and TAs just directed you to the slides. Consider taking it with Professor Nasser instead. Heard he's better.

Mike is one of the most underrated professors in the university. He might not be good presenting a material for the first time, but he wants the students to learn and is ready to help at any time. He is really flexible with deadlines , PSets and homeworks. As long as you show him that you are putting effort into the course, you are in a safe place.

Pretty good professor. All assignments have, individually rather low weights - that way if you screw on thing up, your entire grade for the class won't tank. 3 flex days are really nice. Prelims are all online take-home - but require effort to prepare for. If you put in just the smallest amount of effort into the class, you will pass.
Dr. Hammer is the best of the best. She changed my life by teaching me to write, to think, and to be better.

BEST professor I've had at Cornell. She's very caring, and gives many good suggestions both in and outside the classroom. I also work in her lab and participate in interesting projects. 4380 is also a very interesting class - students will have their own vr set to experience different virtual worlds. Multiple opportunities for extra credit as well.
very innovative & underprepared

He is one of the best professor in Cornell! I enjoyed his class! Very organized, very interesting, and he is simply just a great guy! I would highly recommend taking his class :) He is very very helpful outside of class so ask for help if you need it! He will definitely more than happy to help you!

Simitch is the most vitriolic personality I have ever witnessed. She is generally disrespectful, mean, and full of herself. Takes pride in making people feel small. Narcissistic. Bitter. Some people like it. Do not allow her to take the joy out of architecture because if she can, she will.

This professor gave amazing lectures and made somewhat mundane topics interesting and understandable. . Exams are very fair and check your knowledge rather than being out to "get you".  Problem sets could be designed better, but office hours help significantly with homework and I recommend attending all of them.
Self important opinionated professor.  No appreciation for other views.  Makes up statistics to suit her views - even when her own reference materials disagree.
Carmichael was an awesome professor. If you're interested in the subject material and keep an open mind, you will definitely learn so much information. I look at the world differently thanks to him.

Professor Turvey seemed nice, but he's not a great professor. His syllabus promised 5 equally weighted assignments, but we only had 3 due to his lack of preparation and poor spacing. He then released course grades without releasing the final assignment grades. He also announced he wouldn't curve the class, even though all AEM classes should be.

I LOVE PROFESSOR MCCRAY. 

Cornell needs more people like her here. She genuinely cares about her students and wants them to succeed in her course and beyond in a way that is so rare. 

She was always willing to meet to help you improve and also to check in with how you were doing in general. 

My writing definitely improved in her class.
She's nice, but lectures are boring and messy, and I got nothing after taking the class.

I felt that he made very complicated material extremely simple.  I left both classes (230 & 314) feeling like I knew everything those classes covered absolutely.  He was so good at explaining in lecture that the pace even felt slow even though a lot was covered.  So far, my favorite courses in ECE.

Doug Kriner is by far one of the best Government professors at Cornell. It's clear he is passionate what he teaches and is always happen to listen what students have to say. Discussion sections matter! Make sure to do readings as they are vital for prelims and writing assignments. Overall, this was a fantastic course!
Very tough

Professor Douglas is the most encouraging and guiding artist I've studied with. He is a "yes, and..." professor, building on your ideas with his wealth of knowledge and acute perception. I've made some of my most boundary-pushing work under Douglas. It's also really easy to get along with him and he has great style.
This guy pays just too much attention on coding style and will deduct your marks unwarned because of that. I got a C... Though good teaching anyway.
Great Professor, super accommodating about deadlines and always availabe to answer questions. Very interesting class material too.
He is extremely boring and everyone is asleep. He talks forever in a monotone voice.

Found it very hard to stay engaged with his bland, text heavy, lectures. Tests were based off of the many readings & were very specific to policy. I felt that even though I read and studied, I was unable to do well. Very disappointed by this class and professor. For a class on education, his teaching style did not work well with my learning style.

Only about half of what he teaches us is about actual film music. The rest of his class is about discussing the problematic history of hollywood where he goes on tirades about marxism, male gaze, and the evils of whiteness. You're gonna learn a few things about film music, but don't be fooled, he strays away from the actual subject a lot!
Amazing professor and made learning German fun and easy!

Although I would not necessarily call the class easy, I absolutely loved it.  Professor Hull is one of a kind, and the class is one I will never forget.  She has an incredible knowledge of German History, and the class (Post-War German History) is a great one.  If you have the opportunity, take her class!  You will not regret it.

This professor graded very harshly and it seemed impossible to do well on an assignment. The class environment made me nervous to participate because I have seen her single students out passive-aggressively. If possible avoid taking a class with this professor as you will put a lot of time and hard work into your assignments for mediocre grades.

she is nice, no busywork which is nice, accessible outside of class, my only issues were the 10 minute quizzes which gave you like 2 minutes to answer each question and how strict grading short response answers was in the class.
amazing professor!
Robin is awesome. His class is interesting and inspiring! Strongly recommend!!!!

Terrible guy. Went to him for help on suggesting academic programs but was replied that my grades suck and thus it would be impossible for me pursue any further study. Is this really what a professor should say? Can't believe such  a man could secure a place at Cornell!!
Very complex topics but Ron truly wants to see everyone succeed. He always makes an effort to get to know students on a more personal level.
Ronald is a great teacher. Despite the fact the class is pass/fail, he gives students a lot of attention and wants them to both pass and understand the content. He also gives great life advice.
An extremely knowledgeable and respected professor

Not enough space here to describe how horribly unorganized this professor is. Its like they poured researched gathered online and put it on random powerpoint presentations that were full of errors and messy. No clarity or explanations on assignments, very dry course teaching, no interactive experience on such an importance study of a course. Awful
Tough grader but not impossible. Attend lectures, go to discussion, you'll be fine. Essays aren't that bad, do the readings

pretty fair grader, people got through the class with B+/A- without doing the readings. really nice man.  tries to crack jokes.  very accessable. says the words "essentially" "striking" and "emphasize" a lot.  a really great teacher
Easily the worst professor I've had at Cornell.

Considering I had Professor Chen for his first time lecturing CHEM 2090, I think he did reasonably well. His lectures got better over the course, and often were relevant for exams so I don't recommend skipping them. Note that because the chem department reuses exams, he doesn't release the answer keys for prelims. Make sure you go to office hours!

One of the worst courses I've taken in Cornell partly because of the lecturers and partly because the tediousness of lab. Chen conducts the class with professionalism and with an iron fist. She is very strict but don't be afraid because she's like that with everyone. As a lecturer, she was monotonous. Wished she  made the course more interesting.

At first glance, Professor Chen seems like an awkward and strict guy. But once you get him talking, he is super friendly, accomodating, and funny. I can tell he puts a lot of thought into his lectures and is very prepared for any questions you could possibly throw at him.

Tests are fair. He does not give the most interesting lectures (partly because of his accent), but if you pay enough attention you will learn a lot. Office hour can be super helpful since he only guides you through the problems and makes you do them by yourself. He is also very caring and offers extra office hours before exams.

Her lectures are extremely boring and often run past when the class ends. However, there is really no reason to go to lectures since attendance isn't mandatory and the entirety of the course is graded based on 3 papers, which you can all write on the same thing.

Very hyped professor who mostly lives up to it. His grading scheme is designed for everyone in the class to get an A. DSP sort of bounces around between image processing (not that interested) and cooler stuff like multirate filters. Projects make no sense but are essentially graded on completion. Overall, an easy if slightly disappointing class.

I only went to class a few times, but the times that I did were always great. Oftentimes he would write something silly on the board, or if you were lucky he would make fun of a student for asking a bad question. It's also always a fun challenge to try to understand what he's saying. If you actually want to learn, however, you have to read.
I don't like her. gives discussion posts too late. like two days before they're due.

She is a great professor. Very knowledgeable. It is a fun class, you learn a lot of relevant things and a new perspective on examining scientific work. The midterm was graded very fairly and if you completed the study guide it was easy to do very well. The papers are more difficult. Weekly reflections are a major grade boost. I enjoyed the class.

Prof. Long is overall an awesome person and very passionate about what she teaches. While being an easy grader, she gives great constructive feedback on writing assignments. She also gives you some free rein on writing topics and usually supports whatever you are enthusiastic about.
Professor Arnink was great! Wines and Vines is a great class, and Arnink really cares about her students and teaching them about wine.

She's a very kind woman, but often seemed confused by what she was teaching. Her lectures consisted of her reading off the slides verbatim and then repeating the exact same thing in a slightly different way. The class often felt discombobulated. She's nice and understanding, but not a particularly helpful instructor.

Barbara was one of my favorite professors at Cornell. Her experience with wetlands before becoming a professor is one of her biggest assets when it comes to her teaching style. She is also very willing to provide extra credit on an individual basis, if you approach her. Barbara has continued to be a mentor of mine since my graduation in 2013.

She's an incredibly smart and opinionated person in the best way possible. She challenges you to look at neurobio in a new way, and I loved her papers, assignments, and lectures. If you can take a class with her, do it!

Her lectures are really disorganized, and she frequently forgets to bring her own notes and then tells the whole class about it. Her tests are tedious and some of the questions are impossible to answer correctly unless you memorize all of the textbook. Overall not such a great class.

some professors know how to lecture, prof C knows how to teach. She will uplift you and inspire you to work harder, do better, and not care what others think. She has such zany ideas about these classic texts, shell really surprise you! Shell expose you to several of the trickier dialects of academese along the way.
One of the best, most practical classes I've taken, taught by one of the best professors at Cornell.
Amazing professor. I strongly recommend this class. It's mostly based on understanding experiments and studies. There is a lot of discussion.

Although I was able to make up for this fact, her lectures and notes were entirely disorganized and her tests were poorly worded. It was easy to see why my classmates had difficulty drawing larger themes out of what she presented in class. Her research has lagged of late, making her frustrated and difficult to work with. Avoid joining her lab.
Not just a great professor, but a great man with a virtuous character.

The lectures were minimally helpful, and Attell is at best a merely average reader of poetry. The main problem was his lack of enthusiasm. I was very excited about the course, but Attell did not make the experience worthwhile. The readings are hard (DON'T BUY the packet; ALL of it is free through the library or online) but the grading was very easy
he is nice but so disorganized, and doesn't know how to teach at an intro level. would avoid the class if you don't have a good bit of astronomy knowledge already
If you attend his lectures, you will do fine and easily keep up with what's going on. Very nice guy, pretty funny as well and makes class interesting. I've learned so much from his class.

Very engaging professor, with plenty of guest lectures. Get ready to do lots of readings and reflections for each class but once you have a template for those assignments, it gets pretty easy. Big final group project can be annoying if you get stuck with a bad group. The TA you have makes a big difference!

Professor Sun is a phenomenal lecturer. He is incredibly organised which makes the course not only run very smoothly, but also that we learn a large amount of content in a short period of time. The labs and assignments are a suitable level of challenging, and you are rewarded for the amount of time you put into the work. Tough but rewarding class.

Deadlines were a little weird but they were forgiving at the end of the semester. Content was interesting and taught well most of the time. Homework was a little obscure feeling at times but the coding homework was always relevant and fun. Exams were easier than expected but still tested important content. Overall great prof and would recommend.
Seems like prof didn't care about the class too much.  He taught from PowerPoint, which made class extremely boring.  Also didn't cover all class material--didn't even touch antennas!
Dr. Cote is the best. I took her class at A.U. and it was the only class I actually enjoyed that semester. She's super smart and she really cares.
great.

Professor McGowan is really sweet, inspiring, and wants you to succeed.
However, if you're taking this class to have an easy ride like I did, think again.
The number of assignments is fairly high and they are not easy. 
Not the most difficult arts class, but certainly not the easiest either as others are trying to paint it as such.

I absolutely loved having Professor McAdams as a teacher! Class didn't feel like a chore, and I definitely improved my Spanish skills though the course. Her lectures were very easy to understand and in class activities were fun, engaging, and helpful. She is very kind and clearly cares about all of her students and their success in the class.

No discussion section when you can barely do well in the class without one. Everyone I know was struggling and if you don't understand, it's always your fault even if there are virtually no resources. There are TA's, but they move their office hours every single week so it's inconsistent. Take this class or this teacher if you want a headache.

The lectures are very clear and this course covers interesting content. Saikia does a good job of explaining her slides, making them interesting and applicable, and making materials very accessible to students. She tries to make your life easier as a student and is very reasonable.
Very inspiring class and professor. One of my two favorite professors up until now!

Great teacher. About 1/4 of each lecture is review of the previous class. Tests were moderately easy but long. Going to class is a must but extremely enjoyable. Homework assignments are easy and very open; a few times I played World of Warcraft as homework.

He's a very entertaining professor with bizarre personal anecdotes to adorn each of the many possible questions you have about coding in Python. Classes move quickly, but he sets an appropriate pace and is flexible. If you ask for help, you'll get it.

He sucks. Had 3010 last semester w/ him. He was ok, but i lost it in 3020. Makes simple content confusing. Prelims despite being open note were worded terribly and I really struggled with (hence my first B). The labs are good, but are tought by a TA. Most of the class content is about regression and at least useful. But Entner himself is atrocious.
We read really great things, workload was totally manageable, Professor clearly cared about students + course

The class content, separate from the class, is super interesting. Prof Wallace is an unmistakably kind man who cares about his work/research/field. Yet, lectures tend to be ramble-heavy: there are no clear words on slides so it is hard to take accurate notes that prepare you for exams.  He is also super disorganized with grading.

Best class I ever took at Cornell. Tests are exactly what you study. The more you study, the higher your grade. No ridiculous amount of studying required, but you'll still need to study. Lectures are full of pictures and cool videos of animal behavior, & are really enjoyable. Jeremy Searle is a super nice and caring British guy. Everybody loves him
By far my favorite professor at Cornell. This class was much easier than domestic but still a challenge. Would recommend him to anyone!!!!
No Comments
She's a really good professor. She mostly talked about the systems part of the class. Really enjoyed it. Teaches another class on datacenters but I think that's graduate.

Professor McDowell is one of my favorite professors I've had in the hotel school. Yes, she is a tough grader, but that is to be expected for any 3650 instructor. Professor McDowell genuinely cares about her students, and comes to class every day prepared and with enthusiasm. I would highly recommend selecting her as your HADM 3650 instructor.
best teacher ever

If you want a professor who cares, Theo is your man. He brings incredible enthusiasm to the classroom and not only cares about you as a student but as a person.

I HIGHLY recommend taking any classes you can with him. There will be lots of reading, but the pay off is well worth your time, he always brings out the best in you!
boring
Good person but the not very interesting class.
A lot of examples and demonstrations in lectures, medium sized homework every week. Labs aren't bad, especially compared to Chem 2090 labs. Julia is very sweet and funny and always willing to help.

Class is terrible unless you have already been a financial analyst/investment banker beforehand. Not designed for those trying to learn financial modeling for the first time. You will bust your butt off and do mediocre at best. Oh and you won't learn much either.

This class includes many guest speakers. Dr. Felice herself is such a sweet person. She is very understanding and willing to help if you have an issue. The class itself isn't hard at all. Just show up and listen. There are also two projects which aren't bad.

Readings were really interesting but she is a very harsh grader. I recommend taking an easier fws where u can get an A for just submitting something decent. It will make life easier especially if youre taking other hard classes.
Lectures were boring. Grading was a little tougher than expected (but that was done by the TAs, not the prof). Easy A-, had to put in a little extra work for that A.

Professor Pohland is an awesome professor and cares about the academic and holistic success of her students. While MATH1110 posed to be a huge struggle for me, she truly went above and beyond to give me leeway in order to succeed in the course. She is the best out of all the Calc 1 Professors.

VERY good professor and a very good course. Clear and relatively entertaining lecturer. Did a great job of designing such a lard course in my opinion. Well qualified and taught lots of interesting information.
Do not take her class!! Absurd workload

Super cool professor! I had her FWS on poetry (specifically loving and hating it) and as someone who doesnt love poetry, i still loved this class. Discussion is super tuned into to real life and not just old white british men. Fair grader and really just wants to help you be a better writer. One of the few online classes that i genuinely enjoyed

Amazing lecturer, really cares about students, and is very accessible. Entertains all questions without making anyone feel silly. Final kicked my butt but course was otherwise pretty fair if you read the textbook outside of class

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. Shefter is horribly boring and has no personality; moreover, he systemically goes on unintelligible tangents which only detract from this course's non existent value. He's a poor teacher, has no identifiable grading structure and its over-dependent on his TA's and the textbooks. The TA was neurotic, formalistic and semantic.

Boyer is a great guy and he keeps lectures interesting. Highly recommend taking this class! Material is interesting if you like history. Graded by 2 exams and optional paper only. Exams are straightforward if you prepare well. Review sessions before each exam and comprehensive slides posted. You don't really have to read or attend class.
One of the best Professor at Cornell!

She is a great career advisor and an okay teacher, but this class does have a lot of work. With at least 1-2 things to every other day, there is a lot of time and effort needed to pass. You can get ahead and complete assignments in advance if you can. Lectures are boring, but this class provides a lot of time for personal reflection which was nice

She is actually the coolest person on the planet. I want to be Dr. Rayor when I grow up. Beyond that, she's an AMAZING professor. She doesn't assign busy work; everything she expects you to do will help you grow as a student. She expects a high quality of work because she believes her students are capable of it. Cannot recommend enough.

nicholson was so kind and reasonable-- while biochem can be a lot, nicholson was super clear on what to expect for the weekly quizzes and exams (1 midterm + final). plus, we got 4 drops for quizzes and had other straightforward assignments that made exams weigh a little less
overall i actually really enjoyed the class and biochem as a whole now :-)

She knows her stuff, but the course has a lot of reading and some could be difficult to understand. One paper is graded by two peers, and she grades the other one very harshly. She would send a bunch of emails about the course evaluation for her own promotion but won't give an extra credit or round-up final grade.
She is wonderful! Very helpful, and the class is super interesting...one of my favorites! She keeps it interesting and fun, and is a really nice and approachable person.

He goes through lectures so quickly and doesn't explain what's really important. The lab goes so slowly, and the questions in weekly homework ask for details that aren't intuitive. I basically just had to go to TAs every week to have them explain the answers to me.
It's orgo, so of course it's really hard, but he does his best to make it manageable. He's also really available for help, and was really nice during the transition to the pandemic.
Quizzes on almost every lecture. Papers aren't bad. I needed this class for my major and Ann was the only one teaching it. Not good, but not terrible either.

Great Professor, great class, and extremely fair. The work load is extremely reasonable. He doesn't expect you to be an expert of Chinese Film, but he does expect you to watch the films, go to class, and have something intelligent to say. If you pay attention to what he says, you will find him to be extremely witty and intelligent.

Swartz is a good professor in general. The class itself is quite easy; to do well in the class, all you have to do is read a couple textbook sections/do the homework once a week, and you can skip the lectures completely. The lectures themselves are a bit dry. The TA's for this class were fantastic - all knowledgeable grad students.
great teacher

Readings were incredibly dense and lengthy. Lectures were genuinely unrelated to the readings. Baptist advertised his own merchandise and book repeatedly. Grading varied wildly across TAs. Grossly incompetent lecturer.

He's great at explaining things and will get you through the semester, but his grading can be harsh. The work is different from anything I've ever done but interesting. It was a great experience taking the class and he was willing to work with me when there were discrepancies. Highly enjoyed.

Professor Bond has easily become one of my favorite professors at Cornell. All the readings were interesting and relevant. The skills we learned in class based on the assigned cases reflect real problems we may face as professionals and I think this class has prepared us well. This class was interesting and fun and I would definitely recommend!
RUSSL 3333 is a very opening, welcoming and caring environment. Nancy Pollak is a very kind and one of a kind soul. Am excited to take another of her classes next semester!
Highly recommend professor Cathles. Climate 102 was such an interesting easy class and you can join class on zoom to get credit.

Class is unorganized. There's barely any structure to the materials. He basically teaches whatever comes to his mind. No quiz answers were given and problem set solutions were not followable. The TA does all the work for the class and all he does is show up at lecture. The majority of the final was materials NOT discussed in previous assessments.
terrible lecturer, hard tests, boring class, do not take
The nicest teacher you'll have at this school. Incredibly genuine and kind. The readings are excellent, and there are many different options for each essay. Gives great feedback on all essays.
Awesome Professor! Always there to help give guidance and support. Take this class...Amazing class...
Tough class but very interesting and Estroff is more than willing to help. One of my favorite professors!

:) Nicest guy ever. Grades essays kind of hard, but discussion is always very animated, and the students really get to lead it. He's so into the material, it kind of gets you to be interested, too. And he's really, really helpful. Hopefully, he'll get professorship here (he's a grad student now)...

One of the best professor I had. Super passionate and caring. A lot of emphasis on hands-on learning. He's the kind of professor that really cares about your growth and would always try their best to help you. Be ready to use a lot of Python for his assignments.

Very out of touch professor. Frequently went on tangents about his business not in a real world example way more of a disconnected interjection. No feedback or grades are given back to students on HW despite this being one of the grading factors ? Midterm had an average at about 45%. Very disorganized course.
Engaging lecturer, but sometimes disorganized. Only two papers, a midterm, and take home final. Exams were very easy.
The grade on the final was literally a D, but he did not curve it. Very unclear instructions for every essay with no fair rubric and pretty subjective grading as well.

One of my favorite professor! He is so helpful with my class, and I was so impressed by his way of solving problems. Other students and I always ask him problems right after the class, and he always stays 20 minutes or 30 minutes later to answer our problems. His homework is tough, but his tests is not hard at all.

I was really grateful for this class and am glad I found out about Roger. I was so busy this last sem and his class allowed me to get so much done! There really are no expectations. Just take it and get your A. The benefit for you is extra breathing room in your schedule to focus on actual subjects and a free Gpa boost. Works for me!
Easy to get into contact with. Such an interesting and intellectual guy. You know you're in the presence of a great person and scholar.

Professor Loring was a really good lecturer and the material he went over in class matched up nicely with the topics on exams. The workload was manageable, and although the material was hard, he taught well. He was really helpful during office hours and I enjoyed this class.

I learned so much from Dr. B this past semester and would recommend this class to anyone interested in the US Health Care System.  TA's wrote Takenote, as well as the quizes, midterm and final, and graded all assignments.  Invest in Takenote, forget the book (unless you want more depth), and you should do just fine.  Overall, great class!
awesome prof! :D

Super nice and caring prof, put in the work and show that you care and the prof will be super nice and helpful. Accessible outside of class & loves meeting with students to talk out ideas. A LOT of work and reading, the content is interesting but the workload is extreme for a 3cred course (many indicated this in eval's so workload may be decreased)

Ashley is the best professor I’ve had at Cornell. She’s very relaxed and class is a nice atmosphere and the time passes quickly. She also encouraged me and made me feel confident in my writing for the first time whereas teachers in high school would only criticize.

This definitely should have been a four-credit class. Professor Rich assigned so much reading and work for a class about sociology. I did really enjoy a lot of the content but the workload was insane. Tests were also really difficult to study for.
Extremely clear explanation, Willing to help students

Lectures are engaging and funny. 3 reading reflections (1 every 4-5 weeks), two exams. Discussions were chill. There is a lot of reading (most is skippable if you skim before discussion). its important to go to lecture and take good notes because the slides are outlines. explanation/essay based exams. focus on understanding concepts over details.

Incredibly dull lectures. Goes off on tangents, no clear point or outline for the discussion. Weekly reading of about 150+ pages and weekly 1 page papers, due at 9am Saturday. In-class discussion was also very dull, he would respond to one student for 10+ minutes without allowing for engagement. Very long research paper, graded harshly 50% of grade
a gem of a prof
He is a nice guy, but the material is incomprehensible, and he assumes you have a ton of background knowledge that isn't explicitly required by the prerequisite classes.
A bit unclear at times though in general he is great

Loved it! Lectures were always super clear and the tests and problem sets correlated strongly. Psets got tougher as the semester went on, but group work is a great solution. If you read the textbook and take good notes on the lectures, you will do very well in this class. Very straightforward, knowledgeable, and responsive professor!

This was by far one of the most interesting classes I have ever taken. My only critique is that the tests were HARD. If you have a photographic memory you will do amazing on them because you need to memorize all the slides word for word. Homeworks were very easy but tests were very difficult.

Overall the class is very arbitrary, especially when 60% of your grade is based on what decides to give you on prelims and your "class participation" . Overall he is a very tough grader with the average in our class being a B. You come in thinking he's a nice guy but there are easier classes to take for your concentration with a higher mean

One of the most interesting classes Ive taken at Cornell. It is essential that you thoroughly do all reading homework because she cold calls students in class. This can be stressful at first but is honestly not a huge deal. I honestly never felt bored, and as long as you show up to every class (attendance is huge) and take good notes you'll do well

Professor Bambha taught my favorite class of my freshman fall semester! Her FWS was fun and engaging by having the actual interests of the students being the main focus of the essays. The class in total was only 4 essays with a final project. Topics included reviewing popular media articles on play and reviewing toys!

She assigns 3+ journal papers with associated pre-recorded lectures that can be 1-2 hours long each every week. The in-person class is boring and isn't helpful for the exams. Your course grade is 100% exams. The content and exam questions are difficult. There is nothing to help you prepare for them. The class average was a B+. Would not recommend.

A truly great English Prof. at Cornell. Super approachable and with absolutely no ego or intellectual snobbery, which is rare with older white male English profs. Taking Decadence from his is truly a privilege considering it's what he specializes in. I'd recommend this course to all Cornellians perhaps as the most ingenious English class here.

The class content was overall interesting but the lecture and readings were very dense and covered a lot. Some of the grading seemed more subjective as it was graded more on analysis as opposed to knowledge of content. Overall a sweet person though - pretty approachable if you did need help. Attendance optional for some lecture days!

She is an awesome teacher. Her class is super fun and easy. There are two discussion posts every week and 5 essays to write. I spend on average 30 minutes a week doing work for her class (not including watching the movies). She really cares about her students and is very approachable. The workload is very doable and I 100% recommend this class.

Marysia is an absolutely fantastic professor! Her course, Histories of the Apocalypse, was interesting and filled with multimedia presentations of the course information. Moreover, her use of a forum, like the Apocalypse Now forum kept the course interesting and relevant. I highly recommend taking a course with professor Jonsson!

Clayton is really just a grad student, but he shows no interest in furthering your knowledge. Unprepared for class discussions, awkward, extremely unhelpful even w/ simple questions, gives B's to everyone b/c he doesn't know how to grade, and hands back papers over 5 weeks later. I would not recommend if you actually want to improve your writing.
Decent lecturer and a nice guy, but unfortunately isn't the most helpful and understanding on an individual level.
He's ok. Tests are pretty hard but fortunately there's a pretty big curve. Lots of reading required and all the hw is online.
one of the best history professors at cornell

Cornell should hire more professors who, like Professor Rebillard, actually know things and can evaluate and train professionals in the field. One of the few scholars in Cornell Humanities left who hasn't given up on facts for trendy theories and nonsense.

I agree with the first post, his lectures were extremely interesting and engaging.  If you are motivated and are willing to put in the work, I highly recommend taking his class, you will learn a lot.  He is really on top of things and does his best to help you if you ask.  He gives excellent feedback on your essays.
Lewis can not teach and makes concepts more difficult than it seems. I would not recommend and he yaps too much about his own life into everything on this course. He needs to retire.

Worst teacher I've ever had! Lecture consisted of him going on the same political rant every time and vaguely relating it back to the material. Not that I really disagreed with his politics much, but it was just a waste of time. Way too much reading, like 200 pages per week. The grade was based on only a few papers, seriously why do profs do that?

Ehsan seems really smart, but he is the worst kind of professor to have. He isn't helpful outside of class, and assigns labs and homeworks that take upwards of 10 hours to complete, every week. He is great in lecture, but makes the class unbearable.

Probably the worst class I've taken at Cornell. She was absent for an entire month of the semester and then did very little in terms of actually communicating what the nature of the assignments were, what she was looking for, etc. after numerous asks. Totally ambiguous expectations as she isn't looking for the content or quality of the poetry.

Professor Pulsoni's emphasis on participation may shy people away from the class, but he is definitely an amazing instructor and ensures that you understand the material to the fullest capacity. He always establishes that if any extra help is desired, he is available whenever we need it. He's also a funny guy and will treat you like an equal.
Very caring and reasonable professor. You will get an A if you attend class, do the readings, and complete the homework assignments, of which there are many.
David Powers is an expert in his field and you will learn so much through his lectures, but don't take his class if you are mostly there for an A; the medians in his courses are B or B+.

The class required a lot of work (over 60 pages of writing) but was interesting. She is always willing to communicate with students and leaves a lot of comments on written papers. Class discussions were productive and beneficial. The narrow focus also teaches how to pay attention to detail at all time. Great class!
Amazing class, topic and all presented by a super enthusiastic professor.
Really fair, open to discussion, and reasonable on homework and exams. Definitely would take again, and in fact I plan to. He will challenge you, but in all the right ways.

He does not teach at all. He just expects you to know everything by yourself. His prelim mean is around 60. He also doesn't have any rubrics and he grade everything for what he thinks is right. If you ask him any questions, he does not answer directly to your questions nor helping you solve the problems. Don't take his class.

Thompson-Spires is an awesome writing professor, and she really cares about her students. She will individually give you great feedback on your writing. She was a bit intimidating at first, but is nice when you get to know her. Lots and lots of writing, even for a creative writing class, but overall it's an easy A if you participate in class.

Great prof for a tough subject. I of course did not do the best in the class but still appreciated his way of teaching. He teaches well and uses analogies to make you learn better. This class 1st or 2nd hardest mandatory classes but Thompson makes it approachable and better
Shes sweet, but very direct. Will help you to the extent you allow her to. Tons of readings!!

Good communications class, especially for E&S/plant science/agsci majors. Assignments included: mock newspaper article, video, elevator talk, and final presentation. Carlyn was a riot (affectionate), and I thought the assignments were reasonably graded. Only con was that she was disorganized at times & expectations weren't always straightforward.
I took SOC1101, he was fantastic. Take this class.

He is amazing! An exceptionally caring man and he brings that attitude with him to class every day. Papers and the exam are graded fairly. About 30 pages of reading each night which was not bad at all, and if you didn't actually read on time you were still in good shape. Definitely would recommend.

Awesome lectures and lecture notes. Prof Rana is exceptionally articulate and clear and explains things very well. Very likely the best lecturer in ECE. Homeworks can be tough if you don't attend the sections. The course 4060 starts from the basics but quickly rockets itself into advanced quantum stuff.

Lectures were just her rewriting the previous professors notes on a Goodnotes page. The YouTube videos from the previous professor taught me more than she did in class. Everything in this class was covered in Entner's 3020 and he arguably taught it better. Its disappointing that such a integral class for the major basically wasn't taught at all.

Great professor, a much better choice than the other instructor who taught another session this semester. Professor Yang explains ideas behind multivariable calculus well enough for us to understand. Any questions? He is always helpful during office hours!

This class is strange. Pop quizzes are beyond scary and ask for niche memorization but do not contribute to much grade. Homework/final project are easy and make you walk though the material and are guided. Feedback received a little slow. Lectures are a bit dry. However, Professor Yang is very caring, you just kind of have to dig for it.
The most unreasonable and harsh grading system I have known in my life. Does something called grade

Prof. Yang, or Dennis, as he goes by, was a strong teacher.  His notes largely followed the book, but he seemed much more interested in us learning the concepts and theory behind the math than how to do the specific examples.  I only went to his office hours once, but he spent a long time with me making sure I understood a problem very thoroughly.

She's very nice and wholesome, and the course content is digestible and easily understood. Attendance counts, but exams are straightforward and generally graded leniently. Duanyi is super genuine and nice and helpful.

The easiest class of all time; genuinely a tragedy if you do not get an A+. All quizzes are online, asynchronous, and open notes, and attendance is not mandatory. Truly the easiest class you will ever take in the history of the universe.
Prof LaVigne is very nice and spreads her enthusiasm for communication well. The discussions are fun and the class isn't too stressful so I'd recommend it.
Very Difficult, Very Rewarding
Professor Michelle Kavall was great! Helped me understand the material and even took additional time to explain things.

Prof. Smith is very accessible and understanding. She knows that it is a bio class for non-bio majors, so she tries to give you opportunities to get points that aren't from tests. She is accessible via email and office hours, and welcomes feedback. Overall a very sweet and knowledgeable professor

If you want to think, take a class with Prof. Kosch. If you don't want to think, no judgement here, but don't take a class with Prof. Kosch. 

For me, the seminar was academically rewarding and personally transformative. I've never had seen a final project rubric be so clear, nor a syllabus so well structured. 10/10 class.

I absolutely loved this class. All lectures were online, and in class attendance was required for weekly quizzes on the lectures. Nearly everyone got full marks on all homework assignments and I found the class super interesting. Timmons truly wants his students to do well, although is full of very old fashioned humor.

Very practical class. Lectures are very helpful and clear so I suggest attending classes. There is a group final project in the end. It's possible to A+ even if you did mediocre on the final project but get full mark on everything else.

Prof Zhou is easily one of the most consistent and efficient lecturers I've ever had. He rarely makes even minor mistakes and his delivery of material is extremely smooth. Real analysis is not always the most riveting subject - there are a lot of tedious details to keep track of, but Prof Zhou manages to make it interesting.
Mediocre lecturer but has easy tests and psets. mainly just reads off the slides

The class was very difficult, so I wouldn't have managed without Prof's help. She's really amazing and is a great lecturer. Her office hours are very, very, very helpful (I went to almost every single one).

Dr. March is a great professor. He taught the second portion of ENGRI/BEE 1337. He taught us a lot about engineering ethics and scientific research. He assigned very little work and his prelim was open note and was very fair. He emphasized trying to give us an appreciation for the field. Would definitely take another class with him down the road.

Extremely easy, didn't even have to show up to class, but I still showed up most of the time because the topic was interesting. He 100% knows what he's talking about and is very well reserached, but isn't the MOST interesting professor and kinda tends to drag things out to be a bit boring. Not an exciting class or professor but still was happy.
Yikes, avoid this guy

Awesome grad student teaching my FWS last year.  I hate English and the FWS in the Department are some of the hardest out there.  I loved the class, learned a lot, and pulled out a great grade because he was always willing to help and let you revise papers and improve your work.
I feel really lucky to have had the opportunity to have taken a class with professor Diaz. He is very knowledgeable and provides very helpful information, and his lectures are always fun.

One of the easiest classes I've had at Cornell. Attendance isn't mandatory or really too necessary to do well, and online quizzes with unlimited attempts. Great class especially if you need to get your engineering distribution requirement out of the way.

Prof. Keller is so nice and very knowledgeable. He is clearly passionate about what he teaches. My one pet peeve of this class was that no A+s are awarded so in a way it can drop your GPA. However, it's super easy and lots of cool information. Guest lectures provide great insight.

Tests and HWs are tough and not doable unless you've gone to class. His grading policy is probably the fairest I've ever seen, albeit decently strict. Show respect. In the eternal words of Boris Kogan: "You disrespect class, you disrespect me."

I regret taking ANSC1500! Worst course I've taken! She appears engaged, but her knowledge seems limited (always reads off slides) When seeking clarification, she would say I'm not sure or it's in the reading, giving no guidance but expects us to know EVERYTHING though she claims that it's for non-majors. She takes points off without explaining why.

Marty is such a nice and humorous professor that he shows respect to all students and always be glad to offer help to students. He always leads great class discussions with a comfortable atmosphere and gives out few after-class assignments. Really enjoyed his class at the beginning of my college life.
Not involved at all. Communication messy, expectations unclear, and grading random. She is nice but what she says and does are contradictory. Disaster course design.

Love him, his prelim scared the life out of me during it but I actually did very well. Very clear lecture slides and is great at breaking down difficult topics. The class was VERY lecture heavy. Flipped classroom which sucks. Cool guy though

This was a fun class. I enjoyed taking the personality tests and learning about Freud. Grading is based on exams, but Professor Zayes gives quizzes throughout. I mostly enjoyed the lectures and the use of videos to illustrate concept. Although there is a textbook, the exams are based on lectures.

Dr. Lin did a great job presenting biology in the context of organic chemistry. The exams were straight forward, fair and followed the problems sets and lectures well. Although there is a ton of reaction mechanisms he presents them in an enjoyable, experiment based, and easy to follow manner. Excellent prof!

She's an awesome teacher, and really easy to follow if you're engaged in the material and are taking intro to neurobio at least concurrently. She breezes through topics that the 2220 class goes in depth into, and uses that knowledge as an assumed baseline. A very concept-based non-memorization class. One of the best classes I've taken at Cornell.

Professor Lindsay is very kind and has a lot of knowledge on important topics but is still very new to teaching. The class was very easy to pass if you just attended lecture and turned in assignments.
Amazing class and amazing lecturer

Daniel is the best teacher I have ever had. Lectures are incredible; the perfect balance of clarity while still making you think. I found myself excited to go to every lecture so I could learn something  new interesting. Also he truly cares about all his students and is always more than willing to spend countless hours even helping a single student

Very nice and caring professor. Grading is evenly spread out amongst exams, quizzes and homework. Workload is fairly light, quizzes are pretty long (1hr) and tests are slightly less than twice the length of a quiz. Highly recommend going to OH and working on HW with friends.
there really isn't any work besides the prelims and the week of the prelims is probably the most depressed and hopeless you'll feel.

Absolutely the best prof I've ever had. Engages you with the material, not simply because she cares about it (she does), but because you have realized YOU care about it. Every class was a pleasure. She generates great classroom atmosphere, and more than anything will make you write better. Great at teaching close reading of text. And hot to boot!

Great professor. the whole class is discussion and seminar based so be ready to talk. work and material is easy

The only gripe I had was the grading criteria was incredibly unclear. Ambiguous number of points would be deducted from papers and assignments with no real explanation. perhaps more of an issue with the TA than Kreps but still a problem

Sarah is just a graduate student for HIST 1200: Child Migrants in U.S. History but she teaches the class like a fairly seasoned pro. There are an averagely long amount of readings per week and much of the papers for this FWS are pretty straightforward. It is not a class where you will lay back and do nothing, but it's pretty rewarding I feel
Super disorganized and she canceled more classes than she attended. Doesn't grade until the end. Take someone less crazy for creative writing.
She will help you with all your writing as long as you seek out the help! As long as you work hard, you will become a much better writer!

Professor Hobbs is very intelligent and kind, but the class doesn't necessarily feel intro level. Weekly Perusall readings (Intro to R book) w/ a lot of code examples. Must spend >1 hour on weekly reading, and add 3 comments or questions. Do the readings and show up to class. And GO TO OFFICE HOURS. ask a lot of questions, he's there to help!
Energetic, huge ego, large classes.  Would recommend taking other science classes that have more practical benefit than this one.
I love Will

Took an IDSEM with Jenny at NYU this past fall and absolutely adored her class. She really values student input  and puts in the effort to know each student on an individual basis. She is always willing to meet with students outside of class time as well. Would highly recommend.

Best professor I have had at Cornell! Very clear with explanations and the audio recordings were helpful as well. Made me genuinely interested in the topics and the final grade I got reflected the work and consistent effort I put into the class, which was very rewarding.
Tough, but fair grader. Has a lot of smart, interesting things to say on the subject.

Horrible lecturer, poorly presents even interesting info.  His lectures dont connect so the best you can do is memorize tons of phylogenetic trees.  Quizzes are absurd, and the answers are posted only when many students complain.

Horrendous lecturer, very disorganized and poorly planned. Success in her class is completely contingent on reading the textbook, the lectures do not prepare you for the tests and homework in the slightest. Course was barely curved despite harsh grading on both exams and homework. No flexibility on due dates even with extenuating circumstances.
Very enthusiastic about the content he teaches. Do the extra credit for an easy A+
Cares a lot about student success, even for those he doesn't officially advise. Excellent prof.

Fashion, aesthetics, and society is a fun and interesting class w a minimal workload. We had projects (one group, one individual), discussion posts, and an attendance grade. The prof is so sweet and gives ample time to complete assignments
She was incredibly disorganized and difficult to understand.

Literally the kindest professor I've ever met. Explains labor economics very clearly and he truly cares about his students and wants them to succeed. Manages to make econ interesting and not too difficult for a humanities oriented student!
I expected much more out of this class :(

His lectures are interesting, but slides are only pics so you HAVE to go for notes. barely any homework, except the readings (optional). The projects are kind of annoying. For prelims, you get 10 questions ahead of time, 4 are on the prelim. The q's have part a,b,c and he looks for specific answers. Go to office hours! Its a minimal effort A.

He is literally the nicest person I've ever met. Super reasonable when it comes to work and he doesn't try and trick you on tests or anything. Understands that kids are busy and is willing to work with you. Listens to students' suggestions.

Content was interesting to analyze but made the material way harder than needed. Half the time, I couldn't understand what she was saying. Class involves discussions and busy work, which are easy but repetitive. Isn't very accommodating towards absences (only get two absences before your grade goes down, including illness and death in the family).
He's awesome! Really nice guy and truly a brilliant scholar.
just amazing.
The worst teacher I have seen at Cornell.

I took a few courses with Professor Cohn when I was at Cornell. She's an exceptionally gifted instructor and a real advocate for her students, always providing help and encouragement where it's needed. Couldn't recommend more highly.
Super kind, super understanding, very kind! Her syllabus is very well spaced in terms of course work, and she gives excellent 1 on 1 feedback for papers. Nice class overall!
If you take the time to complete all the lectures and assignments, the class is extremely easy
nice prof but a terribly useless class
Professor Cohn was wonderful, very easy to contact and was super flexible with deadlines. I loved her class so much. Highly reccommend.

Poe was a pretty good Professor. It is possible to do well in the class if you do the homework and go to office hours. Poe is also very friendly and truly cares about his students. I recommend this class if you are interested in econ.

Logevall is by far the best professor I have taken a class with at Cornell.  Unfortunately, he will not be teaching next semester because he is being appointed the head of the IR minor.  He is a real softy at heart. Questions are on readings and films, probably doesn't give below a B, and gives extra credit through optional sections.
shelley is very funny as a TA and knows a lot about sociology. Very passionate about the topic and fair grader for essays.

This was such a good FWS to take my first semester of Cornell. Dr. Green cares very much about both the content of the course and his students. He also takes feedback and makes changes to the course based on that. Grading was very clear and based on effort and improvement over the course. He also offered extra assignments for extra credit. 10/10

really nice and easygoing, contract grading system so you decide what grade you want and do the amount of work for the grade you want. no technology in class or slides only writes on the whiteboard so hard to focus on what is said a lot of the time

2200 was one of my favorite classes I have taken at Cornell. Not saying it's an easy A by any means, I put a lot of work into this class. The exams are very specific and you have to pay attention in class to get everything out of it. The two papers are graded pretty leniently though, so it is definitely doable. I would definitely recommend!

Amanda is a great instructor and is very patient with all her students. Cannot remember a class I looked forward to more than hers. Make sure you are prepared when possible and try not too miss too many miscellaneous points and you should be good!
I feel bad for you if you get stuck with her

So, she doesn't teach at Cornell, she teaches at Seattle U now. Anyway, she was an awesome teacher and I highly recommend her if you can get her at whatever school she goes to next year. she's awesome and is a boundless spring of information in the best way possible.

There was 8 papers, each had a rough draft, peer review, and final. Gives you the questions to the papers ahead of time so you can begin writing as you please. The final is an in class essay that he gives you the questions for. He knows his stuff and can be interesting.

This class is rough, BUT, if you really put in the work and grind office hours, you can get by pretty well without attending lectures. This was 100% the most time consuming class I took this semester, but if you just attend labs and OH (can't stress how important this is) you can end up with an A+.

Congratulations! If you're reading this, it's because you won Kyle for your instructor. Kyle is great at answering all questions, as in-depth as you want, and super clear and easy. Kyle could write his own textbook "Chemistry for People Who Aren't Good at Chemistry". He is super clear and cheerful. Prepare to work SUPER hard, though! He expects it!

This class covers incredibly difficult content but there are a lot of resources available. OH not mandatory but I don't think I would've passed the class without attending every week. Lectures move incredibly quickly so be ready - I don't recommend ever skipping lecture as not a lot of materials are posted online, it would be hard to catch up
take his fws!! great teacher, very fair grader and very approachable. Always willing to help and gives great feedback

Kyle Shen, while an accomplished researcher, can show immaturity when students ask him for help in his class. He has told students they should drop the physics major because they asked a question he didn't like. His lectures are clear and you will learn a lot, but he could be a lot more accommodating in helping students in his course.
You had to study a lot and had daily homework but the course was very easy if you praticed a lot.

Underrated. She is a very nice person, and her lectures are overall structured and organized. The proofs in the lectures may be a bit overwhelming at times, but they are good knowledge for you. Her exams are very reasonable with 1 easy 1 medium 1 hard question and the hard question has easy subparts. I'd say it's worth it to take her course.

I have never worked so hard to still get a grade that wasn't great. All the work has to be done by yourself, and you essentially read an entire textbook. He also talks incredibly slowly, so I always had his videos on at least 1.5 speed to make it feel normal.
My favorite professor at Cornell! Professor Dai really cares about students and gives amazing lectures.

Professor Tantillo is a great lecturer who covers interesting topics and subjects. He always has a ton of energy, which brightens our mornings. He is also very hilarious and gracious. Definitely recommend taking his class!

Love Jim!!! I took his Beginner's Dance Comp class freshman year and now I can't wait to take Intermediate Dance Composition with him next semester!! Such an awesome guy! The class is tons of fun and really opened my eyes to all types of dance!

Incredibly enthusiastic and inspirational. Does this guy ever have a bad day? Really interesting experience in the real estate world and genuine willingness to help students. Makes an effort to get to know all of the students. Dynamic presentations with a variety of media. Assigns interesting case studies. Cornell needs more professors like him.
Man, this woman kills your gpa. DO NOT take the online course. she's the harshest grader I've ever gotten at Cornell. Don't get fooled.
TA for Intro to Global Health Class
great, attentive professor. explains concepts well. very organized. a lot of homework, but not overwhelming. material is tough, but he teaches it well.

He is terrible at breaking things down and he expects you to understand the content well. You cannot stay to work through a problem set in his office hours because "it's not his job to guide you through it" but rather answer very specific questions. The assignments and exams were very vague as he is more focused on his research and academic drama.

Fall 23' - Lectures aren't bad but are fast-paced. He sticks to his grading policy and particular answers for prelims. If you want a practice test, he will NOT give you one. It would be helpful for the class, but he does not provide any information on past exams. I took the class for credit, but I would not take it again.

Here's very charasmatic, so it's hard to tell sometimes that he just goes on and on about nothing.  He'll keep you interested, but at the end of the day, you've learned about an average amount (which still isn't bad).  "Get them on Your Side" is a book designed for incompetent workers with loose pockets - I'm insulted he assigned it to us.
Tea is the best. Really nice, and also funny. Her comments are constructive and helpful.

Bad professor, doesn't teach. Only went to class to answer Poll Everywhere questions. TA's are good, make them your friends. Avoid this prof. at all costs, this professor has the potential to ruin your college experience.
Very easy to learn from and very down to earth. Also understanding and flexible with deadlines.

One of the first engineering classes I've taken where all the material and assigned work felt useful and applicable to real world engineering. He is a great professor who prioritizes making his class the least stressful possible. If you aren't interested in the material the lectures can be boring, but it is clear he really cares about teaching.
He's a nice guy, but his lectures are long and confusing.
Don't take his class if you don't need to. You can't really understand his lecture, especially on molecular orbitals.

I do not recommend this class. Random class attendance quizzes and lectures were not useful for the problem sets. The problem sets were beyond impossible and everyone used GPT for them (and still struggled). When it was time for the final project, everyone I know did very badly bc there were no guidelines. don't take this course, the median was B-

I had the pleasure of being a student of Professor Lujan for NS 1400 Intro to HBHS; her lectures are very interesting and she is great at explaining biological processes; there were 3 lecturers for this course and I liked her the best! She's also very nice
He is so kind and just wants his students to be as excited about BEE as he is. Big fan of this guy.

Extremely intelligent. Unique teaching-uses his own slides, can be difficult to follow sometimes. Exams have lots of math (fully agree with last poster, Econ Math 2.0), get one wrong and your grade can plummet.  10 total problem sets, some of which were extremely confusing. However, he is incredibly helpful in office hours, gives great life advice.

I read some of the reviews -- Whitehead speaks quickly, but he is raising different ways to understand the same point.  If you need to scribe everything -- don't.  If you listen and take down the key points -- it was pretty clear to me.
Class is well organized but Walker White himself can be so incredibly condescending to students lmao like relax bro you're not that guy
Great!

The poster below has no idea what they are talking about.  The class is designed badly, I have no doubt in Professor Chan's teaching ability.  He is extremely clear and interesting, and as long as you know your math you should do fine in the class.

Because the "French doesn't believe in perfection," the grading system is messed up and the highest you can on oral, participation and essays are 95, which bring your grade DOWN. With that said, if you put in some effort, you can keep up your grade on chapter tests. San San doesn't actually do anything, it's ALL with your TAs during discussion
Very knowledgeable and you can tell there is a lot more to learn from him beyond the beginning level ECE course I took.

Very interesting class - Reeves clearly passionate about the subject matter. Manageable workload - 9 labs during the semester and 3 in class prelims (no final). Could be more engaging during lecture but overall pretty knowledgeable and tries his best to engage. Good examples.

I stumbled upon his class by coincidence, but Professor Burrow turned out to be my favorite professor at Cornell. He is very in tune with his students' needs and cares a lot about everyone. His lectures were the highlight of my week--he is incredibly engaging and passionate while staying open-minded. Take his class if you can!!!
I loved his lectures and having the daily quizzes before lecture worked better for this class. Very engaging in class too!

AMAZING. The exams are fair but you have to study both lecture material and assigned readings. I love Professor Ong - I highly recommend. I have learned so much from this class it is crazy how relative and applicable material is to real life.

An absolutely amazing professor! He truly cares about his students and does his best to perfect all the course material. His lectures are so well put together and he always times himself to make sure that his lectures last just the right amount of time. He is always open to help students despite being kind of scary at first haha.
Reed lectures really fast and grades the papers unfairly.  The material is interesting but the class would be much better with a different teacher.
Didn't know a single thing about birds when I signed up. Left knowing so much awesome information about parrots. Really enjoyed the class.

Lectures and videos provide precisely what you need to know to complete the assignment. TAs were also nice with helping during class and office hours. I would say that grading could be harsh at times, and the cutoff for an A in this class was surprisingly high (95%). Someone that pays a little bit of attention to detail will have no problem.

To any freshman looking to take AEM2010 in the fall, please don't. Spring semester professor is much better, wait to take it with him (Reza Moghimi). Haeger never had office hours, only TAs. Overall pretty cold person.. seemed to not care about students. I only survived this class because I had a goated classmate that explained the hws to me.
Clearly is an expert in her field. However, she is not the best at teaching and leading a class. The grading is not that tough though, so I would recommend taking Labor Econ with Thomas

Really like Dr. Thomas as a person, a kind prof who goes out of her way to bring in awesome guest lecturers and give her students career advice. I do feel that the course was graded harshly by the TAs, and the fact that groups were randomly assigned did give high school vibes, but these are more of the nitty gritty details.

Prof McEnaney had great lectures. They were very interesting and combined the assigned reading with relevant text from different sources. There is a lot of reading, but the books were great and well-selected. I loved the course.
awesome
Mr.Patton is really enthusiastic about what he teaches :)
