He and Professor Hodge were fantastic for mushrooms and very caring and considerate. Fun but not too biologically in depth for this course if thats important. Great for distribution as a non bio major.

Dr. Juni is a great professor. He is easy to communicate with and flexible about accommodating extensions. He genuinely cares about his students. He wants to hear what you have to say about the readings. Make sure to do the readings and pay attention to the lectures, as both will be relevant to the essays.

Shawn is one of the best professors I have had the opportunity to take class with. This class was more like a walk through a really cool museum every week and then writing about whatever you felt most passionate about. 20/10 I would recommend the course and the professor. Essays and projects, you are able to chose your topic. Readings and music.
Strange attitude on international students and unreliable

Lexi was so great and really helped improve my writing. Participation was really important and made the class very good. The topic of the class is not what I am studying but Lexi made this class very enjoyable. I highly recommend taking her class no matter what class it is because she is an excellent professor.

Super boring lectures but the entire class is graded by two exams that he gives you review sheets for and spends a class before the examing going over. Going to class is helpful, and he gives extra credit quizzes a few times throughout the semester, but it's really not necessary. A median.

Took BIONB 2210 with Prof Dietz. While she doesn't give any of the lectures, she is the main organizer for this course. She's super kind and wonderful at explaining concepts. Discovered her office hours right before the final exam, and wish I had gone sooner!
Solid lecturer. Heavy accent, but teaches and covers topics well. Knows his stuff. Great class on urban/economic issues in developing countries. Easy A if you have a brain and some dignity.

This class was ridiculously easy as long as you show up to lectures and do the readings. The class itself is interesting but the textbook is super boring - just skim the chapters and you'll be fine. 2 exams and a non-cumulative final - all multiple choice with a few short answers. Lots of bonus/extra credit point available. Prof is really sweet

She is a great lecturer, very knowledgeable in her field, and really cares about her students. She's very friendly and easy to talk to. This class was not difficult (no tests)  and was mostly about getting upperclassmen to become very comfortable with critically reading scientific literature

Both TA's and the professor were extremely difficult to contact and typically do not respond: the class is kind of disorganized, and has the feeling of a mismanaged course. Anybody reading this will likely have to take this course no matter what, but it should be known that hard work, good study habits, and discipline will not get you far here.
Very helpful and clear. She tells you exactly what you need to study for tests, and if you study it, you will do well. Her class is a lot of fun! I always learn with her.

Super kind + caring professor, course was highly accessible. It is designed so that you can come in with no stats knowledge and leave with a decent foundation. Felt disorganized at times, and pacing (ie labs and content) was a bit slow. But Dr. Smith is one of the nicest and most caring teachers I have met. Put in the work, and you will be fine!
Super chill, had applied ethics FWS!

He was the best professor I had in my 5 yrs in Cornell, and my mentor. I even had the honor to help him grade one of his classes and edit one of his books (NOT because I was the smartest, but because I worked the hardest). When did Cornellians become lazy? Dr. Kelley, YOU are the BEST. Your student from '84-85 days.. Diana U.~~
Very uninspiring lecturer with unengaging TAs. Gave exams that were way too broad and overall, did not treat the class as the introductory course that it was.

Suman was one of the funniest professors I've had at Cornell. There were so many iconic lectures and even though the readings were dense, his personality and explanation of them were spot-on. The discussions were so much fun, and very creative on his part. He even incorporated some of his own readings into the class which were fascinating.

Took as my first FWS in 2022 and it was so overwhelming. Really confusing readings and demanding papers. He was funny in class but also a little intimidating. If ancient/old texts are your thing, you'll probably love but if they're not then this class will prob be a nightmare ngl. the readings were also really long and time consuming.

Dr. Lei in general is a good professor. This rating is more a reflection of the course in general. ANSC 4880 is a very disorganized class that in its current state, I wouldn't recommend. The TA has only graded 2 assignments and his expectations are unclear and unfair. You can learn as much or as little as you want, but you have to go. Study hall?

spent most lectures doing example problems on the board with no explanation as to what he was doing, and usually made so many mistakes he forgot what he was doing. Was often corrected by his co-lecturer.

I thought this class was wonderful and I would easily take it again. I highly recommend setting up a meeting with her to simply talk about course content or even an idea you have. She is a very insightful professor who is easy to talk to and she provides great guidance in your studies (if you ask for it).
Funny guy but talks kind of slow. Lecture wasn't worth showing up to because we barely covered anything. Better off reading the textbook on your own.
she does care about students! Dedicated to teaching, well organized and crystal clear. Exams are not that hard if you review previous ones. But 10 problem sets are way too much;
very pleasant class and professor - moderately challenging content, learned a lot. 10/10 recommend

This is the class you should take for both your A&S science requirements. It is basically like a very easy history class. There are a lot of little quizzes that you can take as many times as you like, an easy online midterm and final, and a research paper. Lecture is not mandatory but I liked to go because the professor is passionate. great class

HE 1150: The Afterlife of Fashion. Learned about sustainable fashion, the history of fashion, and the concept of fast fashion. Quite an eye-opening class. In total, had to write around 4 papers, and weekly annotations, were completely doable. Overall, do not regret taking this class and would take another class with Professor Roberts.

Professor Rogers' first duty is to her filmmaking. She started the semester with a good impression on her students, but the course did not proceed as it was stated on the syllabus. She makes you feel like she does not care about her student's creative aspirations, and she has stifled my passion of filmmaking and shut me down. I don't understand why

Professor Khatchadourian is a fantastic archeologist and even better instructor. Her own research is fascinating and worth exploring regardless of whether you are taking one of her classes. With that said, her courses will not disappoint in terms of content clarity, prompt grading, and depth of knowledge in archeology. You'll love it!

Dr. Leonard has wonderful lectures that are thought provoking. She is very engaging and encourages her students to speak their mind. There are no tests, just 4 essays total. There are no textbook requirements, but the readings are uploaded online. She also invites professionals from various organizations such as UN Women to guest lecture.
Amazing Professor.

Professor Smith's lectures were very informative and followed the assigned readings very closely. The readings were a must since every class began with a series of (sometimes overly specific) iClicker questions that were graded for accuracy. The few projects in the class were fun and really helped understand the importance of the material!

Instructor Smith is an extremely thoughtful lecturer, generous with his time with students and very approachable. His lectures are well organized and takes students' input and surveys very seriously. His class examples and tests are challenging yet stimulating and has excellent comand of the subject.

FWS: Word and Image. The readings each week were a bit much upfront. She's a slow grader but gives lots of comments . The grading system for the papers and course are really transparent. Class was more discussion heavy than lecture heavy, so attendence and participation is weighed heavily. Too much workload overall, but it was a good class.

She is so knowledgable and PUBPOL 3110 is an extremely interesting class and lectures were engaging even if not mandatory. There is a decent amount of homework for the drug project but it is graded very leniently and the tests are pretty fair. Also there are no readings so everything you need to know is on the slides.

I LOVED Professor Michener. Her personality is so enchanting. Her teaching is very comprehensive. She is understanding and gives each assignment a 72-hour extension. 3 fair pop quizzes on the readings, 2 papers, a midterm, and a final project. It was a lot of reading and the TAs could improve but overall a lovely class with interesting topics!
He is obsessed wiht shadow puppets and exotic spices. He is my only love for ever any always.

Prof Simons is definitely very passionate about what he teaches. He's a nice guy, but can come off as a little intimidating sometimes.  My only real issue was the fact that there were a ton of assignments in the second half of the sem and basically none in the first half. Just make sure to get feedback from him and TAs on your papers and go to OH.
If you're a minority student, run away.

Prof. Simon is, in a word, amazing. This was one of the most interesting classes I've taken in a while, and she really knows a lot about the material (health policy). She was extremely helpful, very responsive to email, and would always do whatever it took to help you understand the material. Tests are fair, and the research project is appropriate.

Prof Saccamano's class was my favorite this semester. Very knowledgeable in his field and knows how to articulate it to his students. VERY discussion-based class sessions that he uses next to none slides to stimulate, but he stimulates nonetheless. Books are interesting and cohesive, for the most part. I would strongly suggest taking this course.

Dr. Mattson is an exceptional educator. He understands his content, knows when not to take himself too seriously, and has curated an exceptional pedagogical experience in controlled-environment horticulture. If CEA/hyrdoponics interest you or you just love plants, I cannot recommend this course highly enough.

Most of the grading occurs at the end of the semester, so it is difficult to gauge your performance in the class. There are interesting concepts taught in this course and it is heavily discussion based, so participation matters. The professor believes his opinion is fact most of the time, so be careful with disputing.

Interesting class. Group project that meets throughout the semester and gives a good understanding of product development from start to finish. Could use clearer grading criteria - grades are arbitrary based on how much she likes your work. Lewis also is clearly not interested in students; she was pretty rude to me when I approached her after class
simply the goat ...
Mark Lewis is like Brock Lesnar. He looks good on the outside, but he sucks actually.

Great teacher.  Had him for into to physics.  He has a great way of making a copmlicated subject seem straight forward.  Would take any of his courses in a heart beat.  He sincerely wants you to do well.

Hes honestly not that bad! All lecture slides posted online which is super helpful and the content is very interesting. He may not be the most lively professor but its clear he loves communication and cares about students doing well. I would recommend him.
Prof. Goldberg must be one of the most patient professors at Cornell. His grasp of technical concepts is so strong that he can answer any question that you may and will ever have!

I love this guy, but I don't love the way he teaches. Super cool guy, but his lectures are unclear and he includes material that is neither in the textbook nor lectures in his prelim. I thought BIOG 1440 was easy, but he was by far the hardest out of the four professors. Still love him though.

VERY rude, sneaky, back-stabbing Prof. WATCH OUT for this lady. She'll give you attitude if you cross her, and she won't hesitate to put your grade on her guillotine. She cares WAY too much about participation, and even if you're shy and do your best to speak up here and there it's not good enough for her. If you're a native speaker she'll like you
One of the best professors I've had. Clear explanation. Well-prepared, structured lectures. Easy to follow. Too bad he's retiring from Cornell.

This is the worst class I have taken at Cornell. The professor doesn't care about her students, the feedback on assignments is unhelpful, the class is completely unorganized, and the content is pointless. When talking to her one-on-one, her tone is very condescending. This class is a requirement for the Global Health major/minor, so beware...
She's pretty fair and willing to explain if you fall behind. Overall, not that easy of a class for me

Krosch is one of my favorite professors at Cornell. She's understanding and accommodating (even providing multiple ways to get participation points beyond speaking up during class -- more than I would have expected from any prof!), and her line of work is super interesting and engaging. I really hope to take another class w/her in the future :)

Amy Voida's structure of the class made it very easy to do well in. It involved a lot of reading, but she told you exactly what was on the exams. The class used a lot of blogging. She has this obsession with groupwork during lecture, and having students bring up and discuss concepts in the readings, which only works if everyone in the class reads.

TERRIBLE! SOCIAL ENGINEERING CLASS! Newman feels Any challenges, failures, or misery non-white people face in life is not their fault. And, even worse, there is nothing they can do to change their circumstances. So, just stew in your bitterness and hatred for White males along with her and her like, so goes the leftist campus orthodoxy of the day!!

Paskell was a pretty chill professor. Five essays in total, with each being weighted more as the semester progresses. Attendance in lectures and discussions were mandatory, but going is helpful just to gain further clarification on the readings and insights to write the essays.
Good class, not as good as chirik but no complains

Professor is a true scholar and gifted professor who is able to take literary criticism to the next level through her engaging lectures. Finer shades and subtle beauties in the works we studied were revealed. A difficult semester, but delightful in what it achieved and the style of lecture and delivery.

She does not know how to use her technology, and she comes up with grades with no criteria. No one knew what to expect. She behaves unprofessionally with students, and has made some students cry. Randomly decides on projects, regularly changes deadlines... there is no structure to her teaching. BEWARE AND DON'T TAKE THE RISK!
She is friendly, clear and fair. Overall, good class with a nice instructor.

Very easy 2-credit class taken for a GPA boost. Just 2 papers and a final paper. Attendance required unfortunately. Her lectures are so awfully dry. It was an easy A but probably not the most worthwhile class I could take.

Went into the class thinking it would be an easy A-- it's not and I am fairly fluent in Chinese. Much of my free time was spent writing essays, doing hw problems, and preparing for the next class. I thought the lessons were somewhat interesting. Su laoshi is a strict and harsh grader. Classes reminded me of Chinese school from my childhood

Lectures do not cover the materials that the PSets and the exams are on. His lectures were also super boring and he sometimes moved through too quickly. It's a running joke among CS majors that 2800 is a hellhole that you somehow need to get through. Thank god there's a new prof this year; hopefully, she does a better job.
Professor Frantz is a very talkative and great professor who is more than willing to give you help. I will definitely recommend his interesting (and not very difficult )courses!
Prof Chigas is nice and I would take his class again

Genuinely the worst teacher I've ever had in my whole life. The average test grade across the course was a 61% but then he just made up everyones grades at the end with no rationale or feedback behind it. Genuinely didn't teach us anything and then expected us to know way beyond the class material. Wanted to scratch my eyes out. He should be fired.

This class is one of the best classes that I have ever taken at Cornell. For non-science majors, this class is not as easy as everyone says (but not hard, just don't expect an auto A). Prof. Hudler is passionate, clear, and really nice outside of the classroom. I wish I could take more classes with him! Highly recommend.

I absolutely loved the first half of the class! It was interesting and engaging and I felt like I learned a lot. However, once the group project started, the class took a downturn. We stopped talking about the readings as much in class and it felt much less organized. The group project was SO much work and took 4+ hours/week.

My favorite professor so far - laid back guy but will always give you feedback on your writing. Definitely approachable and eager to helpshow up, pay attention and if you struggle ask him for help and you will be fine. Interesting topics and had more fun than I expected out of an English clas

The guy's scary smart - definitely agree with the rest of the reviews! The class is super challenging and McAllister goes very fast, but he's always eager to stop and clarify. Despite what he says, the problem sets and prelims are anything but "trivial", so be prepared to spend some time on this class, but it's well worth it!

Prof. Clary is a very kind, positive, and interesting professor. His clear passion for the subjects he teaches translates to engaging lectures and an approachable attitude. His upbeat teaching style makes even an ancient language relevant and captivating for students. If you engage with the material, you will do great.
Class is a little disorganized but Professor Walter is great!
He is a great professor, show up to class, do the work and you will do just fine in the class.

Gerarden's a smart guy. He explains complex econ concepts in simple terms and is clearly passionate about teaching. Super accessible outside of class. It's one of the rare times I've walked out of an upper div econ class having fully understood what was presented to us. Tests are fair. OHs are very helpful for psets.
An amazing professor! Made topics easy to understand and always explained difficult concepts extremely clearly. I was dreading taking physics, but he made the subject very accessible.

Professor Marie Chazel was one of the funnier professors that I have had. She was very nice and usually got a good laugh out of me in every single class. It was tested heavy being worth 85% and homework being worth 10%. If you do the HW and show up to class you should pass. If you need to take Calc and more specifically Math 220 Take it with her.

Also confused by all the negative reviews. Don't listen to those people - they likely didn't ever go to class or OH. Seriously, Marie is so caring and loves what she does and wants her students to succeed. Go to OH with her for HW answers! Go to class and you'll do great! Marie is there to help you, put in the work and you'll get a good grade.

I had her in Spring 24. She is very approchable, and she gave me great advice for my intership during office hours. Lectures may be heavy, lots of content, but a great prof and a useful course if you want to work in the F&B industry.

She is such a sweetheart! The material itself is interesting, and her lectures make learning so much better. She is clear with explanations, and repeats key concepts several times. Her quizzes and exams are challenging, but fair, and she is very easy to talk to. She does her best to clarify any questions. One of my favorite classes and professors!

I wouldn't recommend this class if you're merely trying to fulfill the A&S bio requirement. This is an amazing class if you have strong interest in the subject matter. His office hours are very helpful. However, do not expect an "easy A" out of this class -- the medians for the exams were quite low, hitting the C+ and B- range (without a curve).
The best professor I had. Wonderful scholar.

Group projects are fun and engaging. The lecture itself is also interesting. Very respectful and open to students' opinions and thoughts. Assignments are not too heavy. If you at least read and pay attention, no problem at all. Highly recommended class!

The real deal in ORIE. Make sure to take his classes if you want to learn some good old mathematics. Took his 5370 back in 2016. Hope he still teaches the class. The guy has high level of seriousness in both teaching and research. Legend.
Literally the worst
amazing, caring, and extremely smart professor. passionate about what he teaches and is accommodating. content is also very useful

Honestly one of the worst professors or just teachers i have ever had. Acted like she knew everything but wound up asking students to answer questions cause she didn't know the answer. Super susceptible to ass-kissing and heavily picks favorites. Also, there were days she was just extremely rude when I asked questions or just super lazy to help us.

Prof. Jones is extremely knowledgeable, insightful, and even funny. She is incredibly flexible and a fair grader. She does come off as disorganized, but she is still very responsible. She always makes time for students. Highly recommended.

Based on her knowledge should not be teaching a class on Islamism, based on a poor attitude towards students and totally unprofessional behavior in class should not be teaching at all. Whatever I know about Islamism, I learned outside of class. Susan was a waste of my time and a (rare) horrible Cornell experience.

Very nice. She clearly understands that there are students in a single class that are coming at the language at many different levels. Beware though: the required "course pack" of textbooks, CDs, and leaflets is not useful at all, and will likely run you $200 for materials you do not need. Buy the main two books and the online code only.
Dr. Quirk ignores email purposefully(one of her teaching assistants told me), often makes disrespectful comments about students and other animal science staff + professors, and rambles during class

I took the fall online version and I thought the class was pretty easy to pass if you just copy the formulas used in the lecture and hw practices during the tests. Final presentation is graded pretty harshly. Not the most accessible.

Prof Merkel was the worst professor I've ever had at cornell. During a pandemic, she was not understanding, extremely rude, and did not take criticism well when a couple of students reached out to her with concerns over the ridiculously long quizzes. a bunch of stupid in class assignments were given and points get taken off left and right. avoid!!!

Professor Fussel was the worst professor I had at Cornell. By the end of the semester, I had no idea what my standing grade in her course was because she had failed to grade even one of the three papers I wrote for the class. Furthermore, the course content was boring and trivial. There was also a terrible group paper at the end. Avoid this class.
Study for her quizzes like you would a Prelim. Study lecture guides thoroughly...and only use the textbook when you need clarification...She is very helpful during office hours....

The class was largely structured like a conversation related to the readings. The readings, however, are heavily biased so conversation is limited to a mediocre regurgitation of an agenda. Not only this, but the conversation had little to no direction so it felt stagnant and useless.

Caitlin TA'd the course, but I decided to review her because she played a HUGE role in my understanding of the content. Clear lecturer, but even better at teaching the practice side of making community-based theater - I loved the exercises she led and found her to be one of the most effective of the 5 professors involved in the course.

Professor Ray is a really sweet person. Her lectures are very interactive and she tries to structure the class based on interests. There are only a few assignments but they're mostly graded on effort so it's easy to get a good grade.
Amazing time. class was so fun and a walk in the park HIGHLY RECOMMEND
She is the best professor I've had yet!! Too bad she's gone because HD is hurting without her. She was very considerate and helped me figure out my future!

Professor Bernstock is a wonderful teacher, enthusiastic and knowledgeable. I graduated some years ago, but I still remember and talk to people about what I learned in her art history class. She is truly inspiring, plus she is friendly and down to earth--very willing to meet with students.

Prof. Piotrkowski is a dazzlingly lucid speaker, and our class discussions were always exciting. Difficult for sure - lots of reading and some intimidating essay prompts - but she's a fair grader and extremely helpful if you approach her. Plus, she brought us candy for Halloween!

Really enjoyed this class! If you have no interest in music, then this class prob isn't for you; but if you have an interest in music, pay attention in lectures, and put effort into your papers, you do decent. Think people are misled to think it's a "no-effort A" - there's a fair bit work, but if you put in some effort, you should do well.

Very easy class to pass with Judi. She is super caring and nice and gives the easiest homework. Honestly the class is a bit of a joke where you play games and chat most of the time. You can very easily get an A if you show up and just put a little bit of time into the few pieces of homework that she gives. Big plus - she has no finals unlike others
this woman is not a very good teacher, all of her lectures are straight out of the book. will sometimes also test random bits of information too.

This professor is a menace. Her grading was completely unclear and she obviously gave students bad grades for no other reason than she didn't like them. Taking this course was like watching a child on a power trip every day. stay away
Great all over... Excellent.

Dr. Rossi is awesome. His lectures are engaging and is there to help you when needed. The workload is very light: 1-3 short readings a week, 3 short writing assignments during the semester, & a short lab report each week. Attendance is required and is a big part of your grade. But very easy class and tbh, most kids do other HW during the lectures.

An actual icon of a person! I'm so honored to have taken a course with an actual celebrity! Talks about his personal experiences in lecture and real-life applications that actually make us better people. Also read his new book that he had just published.
Great professor

Not sure where the negative comments come from.  I found this class to be excellent, and Prof. Schroeder was EXTREMELY accommodating and helpful.  Sometimes the lectures were a bit dull I guess, but overall I feel that I learned a ton from him and really enjoyed the class.

Wayno is a likeable instructor, but this class is really boring and ineffective. Readings are long and largely uninteresting (with some isolated exceptions), and the majority of the class, by their attendance, agrees that a 2-hour lecture MoWe at 8am was not worth it at all. Would strongly not recommend this course unless absolutely required.

When teaching a graduate course, Silva comes off as extremely knowledgeable and approachable. I really enjoyed taking 6110 with her and I thought her lectures were good. The course uses exceptional course notes (hence rendering lecture attendance a bit optional). Silva was quite accommodating and there was ample time to do both psets and exams.
Very friendly, extroverted, and easy to talk to. She is very good at explaining complex concepts in simple terms. Highly recommend taking any class with her, she's awesome.

Professor Dufresne has the amazing ability to take complex and difficult subjects like immigration and make them not only enjoyable to learn about but easy to digest. She goes out of her way to make sure both students who have a law background and those who do not are able to get the information they need.
Don't buy the book you will never open it. Class is really easy- she prints out the powerpoints and hands them out and the prelims are the same as her review sheets except different numbers.

This class requires your participation and I really enjoy digging and searching for artifacts in the state park :D Professor Baugher is so knowledgeable and also a nice person. Recommend this class if you're ready to do fieldwork.

Some of the readings are pretty interesting, lectures are sometimes decent, other times not. Too much work overall. Often she overcomplicates relatively simple things, midterm was an absolute disaster-- unclear grading expectations. I'm sure you can find a better and easier class that does the same stuff.

amazing teacher. made me feel really heard and included in class; i am personally not very strong in english, but she made me want to try and want to raise my hand in class. she makes sure that everyone is engaged in class (it is not difficult to be, at all!) and is super accessible & flexible in office hours (they were over zoom, by appointment).

I believe Prof. Valentin is just a visiting professor, but I hope that he will stay. I took HADM 6230 and HADM 6231 with him. Highly recommend both. RE statistical modeling uses Rstudio, but as someone who has never coded I can say it's light stuff. Also, it was pretty cool to learn. RE mkt analysis is very pertinent to analyst roles in RE.

Professoressa Fulginiti is a great teacher of the language. Very knowledgeable and kind. Expectations are clear and easy to fulfill - homework doesn't take long at all if you pay attention. It's a good way to boost your grade and reinforce la lingua..
Loved her lectures, very interactive and helpful!
We covered interesting topics, and the assignments were easy, just a couple vocab quizzes and some short presentations.

Homework is reading the textbook on your own and getting quizzed on it before going to class. Class is going over concepts and doing problems. There's weekly problem sets, which aren't super hard, but they are really tedious. He does really know the material however, and is more than happy to help if you have any issues!
she really micro-manages what you do. She is also unfair in her grading. She makes me hate photo so much that I don't want to even go near the photo darkroom.

Very accessible out of class, but the assignments were impossibly convoluted. The lectures were also super long and boring. Jean Hunter is a very kind and smart person, but unfortunately that does not always translate to teaching. God bless her soul, she is retired now.

Lectures are great as Prof. Schmitt stops were any questions or comments. Material overall is really interesting! The readings can be a bit heavy but nothing too bad. Discussion sections are great and interactive. Papers/prelims were not bad but make sure you know what you are talking about. Either way, Prof. Schmitt and the TA gave great feedback!

this is an interesting class if you're into agriculture/plant science. the prof is not the best at teaching but is passionate about her class materials and is a quiet, nice and sweet lady so book office hour appointments if you can. you don't need to understand all the difficult genetic stuff to get an A+ in the class since everything is open notes

HADM 4050 with Professor Anderson is a great option if you are looking for a Hotel elective. He has clear expertise in the field, and his course was a great introduction to Revenue Management. He's extremely laid back and an easy grader. The assignments can take a while, but he provides supplemental videos that are extremely helpful.

He's okay. He covers a ton of things in lectures which makes it pretty difficult to retain all the information. Exams were basically memorization-based, and there wasn't a lot of generous partial credit. Assignments were half easy and half confusing. He didn't really have a personality, so he was just a middle-of-the-road professor.

Hands down the best professor I've had so far at the Hotel School. Professor Lawrence is HILARIOUS and gives amazing, informative, and interesting lectures. One of the few classes I always looked forward to going to!

Bonassar is the man. He is brilliant and it really shows in his lectures. He is always prepared and knows the answer to everything. The prelims are pretty much impossible though and the homeworks can be a struggle without going to office hours.

Professor Kahn is a boring lecturer and gives unclear instructions. He provides study guides for the exams but says that none of the content on the study guide will be on the exam, making the study guide useless. The prelims are two essay questions that are 50 points each. There is also a group project that is a major portion of your grade.

Lawrence's class was one of the most engaging and thought provoking classes I have taken at Cornell. While essays were few and far in between, they required a close reading of the text and a well structured argument. Anyone with the slightest interest or prior experience with philosophy would enjoy his class. I myself am a ChemE maj and I loved it

I'll be honest, I didn't learn much E&M. His lectures are math-heavy and I often got lost. But in my time at Cornell, as my mental health & class grades tanked, Gibbons was the ONLY person to ever reach out and ask if I was okay, and he put me in contact with resources that saved my life. His class is tough, but he truly cares about your wellbeing.
Probably my favorite professor at Cornell.  Thanks Steve!

Incredibly boring and unstructured. Makes it hard to focus let alone understand material. Thankfully the material itself is not so hard so you can pick up from self-study. Lectures offer very little value. Slides are good but lack of other reference material is a challenge. Would not recommend taking his class unless you have to.

I actually enjoyed this class. Biggest tip: sit up front! It can be boring, but you will have a much easier time following her and staying interested. Papers (2) not too bad. Some very specific MC on tests — she likes facts, figures, and definitions — but fair overall. Annoying and pointless sections on Fridays, but my TA was extremely laid back.
Amazing professor. Sometimes we would get distracted from what we are supposed to do, but overall, a great professor.

Lives up to her first name. Easy class but can put you in trouble if you procrastinate everything until the end of the semester. She makes you think critically and makes sure you understand what is being taught, but she can come off passive-aggressive as a result of that. If you get on her good side, you should be treated fairly (I was not).

The most socially unaware professor I have ever had. I don't think she even knows what she wanted the students to learn from lecture. Doesn't create slides, speaks in a stream of consciousness manner. Would not recommend.

Prof.Bennett is a pretty good lecturer that asks a lot of thought-provoking questions. I took the writing intensive section, and dependent on your TA it can be easier or harder. The readings are interesting and she allows extensions on any of the four papers.
Very kind and caring professor. Discusses topics deeply and is willing to be flexible for the needs of the class.

Probably the best lecturer I've had at Cornell. Very engaging and passionate about what she's teaching. My favorite part of the class was when she explained the lore behind Hulk Hogan's sex tape. There are no exams which is great

I really enjoyed this professor - by far my favorite at Cornell. My very first class at CU was his biomechanics class; he was hilarious, witty, and so knowledgeable. Do the homework with other students and/or the TAs. Light studying. INCREDIBLE class!

Professor Makridis is an amazing professor. She is so caring and kind, and she genuinely cares about each of her students. Her class is a reasonable amount of work, and all of it is very creative and enjoyable. She is probably the kindest professor on this campus.

I enjoyed this freshman academy class. I do believe that academy coaches (basically upperclassmen TA's) were the reason for my enjoyable time. However, although Prof Valero-Cuevas was not the most exciting person at times, you can tell he is a smart and nice guy with a good heart who really wants to see you succeed. Also an easy A.

Obviously biochem is going to be hard, no matter who teaches it. I thought Prof. Qian did a good job of presenting the material in as accessible of a way as possible. He was also really kind and somehow kind of funny. I appreciated him even more compared to next professor who switched in halfway through the semester.

Course is very, very interesting and you learn a lot. However, the workload is high for 2 credits. If you put in the work, it's an easy A. Don't just take this course because you want a freebie with minimal effort. 

Great course for pre-health students or people interested in pharma/industry.

If you're NYU, don't listen to these Cornell reviews. Micro w/ Calc is tough in general, but she is the nicest and smartest professor I've had. Even when she had personal things going on and had to travel during the Sem, she would still hold class. Truly cares about her students. Definitely be prepared to put the time and effort in though!
The class is very applied, covers a wide range of topics, and introduces you to numerous tools. The homework can be long but is always rewarding. The lectures are good.
I had Prof. Andreoni for my montserrat so I don't know what she's like in a language class but she was always trying to be available outside of class and offers great advice for writing assignments.

Literally one of the best professors at Cornell. When a classroom's desk space was too small for an in-person prelim during COVID, he immediately moved the exam online. His lectures were engaging, and some of the few recorded ones that I looked forward to watching. Honestly the best.

by far the worst math teacher i have ever had. it was often better to tune out his teaching because it would confuse me with everything i learned from the textbook. he is still a really nice guy...but a nice guy is useless as a teacher if he can't teach.

He is not accessible outside of class for help and does not give ways to study for the test. He suggested to do questions from the book and take the practice prelim but they were nothing like the actual prelim. He explains concepts assuming that we have post-undergrad level knowledge or something. Lectures are a waste of time.
Very kind professor who seems to genuinely care about your learning! Pretty good lectures- somewhat difficult exams but extremely fair and generous curve.

Not a good class/teacher. Took all material straight from the book and its resources, cancelled OH often, and gave us homework and extra material for the final that he hadn't taught. Lecture devolved into him reading from a powerpoint written by the book's publishers.

Professor Pegues is an amazing lecturer and, even more so, a great course designer. She does an amazing job of engaging the entire class with the readings, and especially this semester, has done a great job of tying in many of the things we are reading with real-world happenings both locally and globally. Great professor, very reachable, 10/10
excellent!
A lot of group data science projects. Gives a great introduction to data science and you come out of the class with useful skills for internships.

Atrocious grading rubrics with no thought behind them. Consistently took off 20-40% from total homework grades for tiny errors like not writing dimensions on a Free Body Diagram or not writing "Given: Find: Plan" above the solution to a problem, even when all final answers were correct. Partial credit wasn't given on prelims, which weren't curved.

Sujata Ji ends up teaching almost all the South Asian languages, and I think a lot of students give her a bad rap. She is really friendly, disorganized, but CLEARLY overwhelmed. Yes, I wish she didn't miss class as much as she did but honestly, learning how many courses she teaches, it feels unfair to her to blame her.
tests do not reflect what was taught, grading is harsh
discussions felt really superficial.

Marc is extraordinary, and his class ranks highly among those that I've taken at Cornell. It's basically just guided frolicking in nature in labs and on field trips, and I learned so much about identification that I think about it at some point on almost every day

As both an instructor and a person, Prof. Kohlbry is awesome. The material, while niche, is incredibly fascinating and highly conducive to writing. The clarity of feedback on work is second to none. In-class discussions are run beautifully, operating at a perfect level of depth. I have never been more invested in a writing class in my life, 10/10.
This class is bad. Waste of time and money, do not take.

Professor Samuels's class offers a variety of text that is engaging to read. The readings are a bit long, but are always engaging. Class discussion is fairly relaxed, and papers can be tough so make sure to go to office hours! I would most definitly recommned anyone who would like to expand their reading reportoire to take this course.

Prof. Zhao is one of the best instructors in ECE. Although ECE 5640 is a relatively hard course which require solid mathematical fundamentals, prof. Zhao definitely made the lectures simple and interesting. The exam is not easy but fair enough. Prof. Zhao and the TA are both very helpful during their office hours.
grat prof

Amazing professor! Learned a little bit about coding and how to incorporate fashion technology into my designs. Very cool concept, good amount of work, but if you do it you will get an A, and different from other fashion classes I've taken. Highly recommend.

Didi's poetry FWS was designed to be a more laid-back and slow-paced class for those interested in the topic. He has extremely lenient deadlines and is flexible with submissions. I only did some of the readings, but class participation isn't strictly necessary for a good grade.

He is very bad to his PhD students. My gf was driven crazy by him. Every time they talk, she cries because he says extremely mean words to her. He is not helpful at all as a professor. The only comment that he gives is: your paper is not good enough.  
This professor gives us so much pain. Avoid him at all costs. unless you want to ruin your life.

Park's excitement over thermodynamics is palpable through his lectures. He knows Pchem isn't the most fascinating subject for many students and tries to insert short stories or interesting applications into lectures. His lessons are well organized, and a decent number of homework questions are a variation of textbook examples.
Dan Ralph is an excellent lecturer. He can make very challenging subjects extremely intelligible, and works very hard on his classes. He seems to care about his students, and is very friendly.
Took her Women's Studies class. One of the best professors I've ever had. She changed my life and for her I'm eternally grateful.

Amazing and entertaining lecturer! Kickass leader in her field as well, commands a lot of respect and much of her data on exoplanet discoveries she published herself with her team. Explains the content in a very visual way and gets the class involved as well. Definitely tries to ensure complete understanding and always happy for a chat after class

Laid back course that is not difficult. Primarily film theory; do not take if you are focused on production. Patti is a very knowledgeable professor though can be a bit verbose. Only necessary to do readings when writing assignments. Not necessary to buy textbooks.

Lisa's class is too much based on exact rubrics and sticking to a system of grading. While it's not hard and the case studies are fun, my TA was too attached to following her system word for word. She didn't have too much common sense. Also, in terms of application to the real world of HR, this class = a bunch of memorization and useless. HR is a q

I like the reading assignments - they are all important essays pertaining to the topic. However, her lecture was very confusing. She did not actively lead nor facilitate the discussion. Also, I felt like she was not even familiar with her topic and assigned readings. She had strong opinions on things, but some of them were wrong.

It appears reviews are positive for Pearo's time teaching at Cornell. Her performance at William & Mary leaves a lot to be desired. In addition to unclear feedback on graded assignments, Professor Pearo missed multiple mandatory weekend group sessions and failed to apologize or rectify her unprofessional behavior. A waste of time and money.

Horribly taught class. The class isn't difficult because the material is hard, it is difficult because Professor wickham doesn't understand how to conevy her ideas over paper, and thus the homeworks become excessively cumbersome. Honestly half the students in the class could probably teach the class better than her.
She's brilliant and kind.
You need to put in consistent work to do well, but the effort is worth it because you gain foundational knowledge. Prof. Pender is one of my favorite professors despite the difficult tests.

Note: the course I took is actually HIST 2931- Making an Empire in China. 
Prof. Du is very knowledgeable, caring, and hard-working. Her lectures are information-dense but well-structured. No need to do all readings, but read papers. Go to her office hours and you'll get great book recommendations, advice on writing, and even life lessons.

Sebastian was a good section leader generally, but went too slowly during class time, often causing us to be unprepared for weekly homeworks. His office hours were generally very helpful, but sometimes i would still get points deducted even after following his instructions exactly. Exam prep was little to none.

4430 has been a huge disappointment for me. From vague lectures, to badly defined assignments, to his insistence on mainly teaching his own research, I've been let down at every turn. He actually didn't know when the prelims are being held and a student had to correct him. Exams are also disproportionately difficult for a class with this content.
Miss. Kim is grading material wack, her eyeliner wack, her eyebrows wack, the way she is so passive aggressive wack, the way she acts condescending wack, me I still have a 50 on my research paper.

Professor seems like an extremely nice guy, but the material of the course is bone dry which can make it difficult to pay attention in class. Whole grade is based on one final paper which has very little to do with the content of the course and has no clear grading criteria.

Don's extremely knowledgeable and eloquent about film history, theory, and criticism - terrific selection of films and essaysreading. Combines cinema, philosophy, humanities. I took several classes as a major. Also great dry humor. Passionate about film. Loves discussion and discovery and debate. Be ready to learn and challenge yourself. Good guy.

This Doctoral level class had a lot of overlap from 5600, the instructor competence was also questioned as instructor wasn't able to answer class questions. There wasn't any clarity, focus in the lecture, if you aren't impelled because of your education you should run away from his class.  He is the one who directs 5600,poor material and delivery.

Does not care about students. "Lectures" are him reading slides copied from textbook. Does not understand the material he is teaching because he is unable to respond to any questions not directly answerable from his slides. He does not hold office hours and is unreachable via email. Does not publish grading criteria. His TA should win an award.

Like in the previous review, Ewan Robinson is a nice and kind professor and knowledgeable in his field of study. However, his class is very leftish-based and kind of biased, and he only really gives his opinion on topics. The essays are a major part of your grade and they are very hard and confusing to write (took multiple days to write). Good Luck

I did this class in spring 2024 and enjoyed it a lot. He is a very smart guy, and he is very caring too. ECE 2720 is offered both semesters but the instructor changes each semester. One of the best ENGRD courses I have done

Krishna seems to be well prepared for every lecture he gives, which is good but expects the class to remember some small details learned earlier in semester for almost every question he asks the class. Which reminds me, he is one of those professors that waits for students to answer a question he asks towards the class. Does that make sense??

Absolutely loved this professor. Very interesting class and he was amazing. However, it's important to say that the class is mostly led by TA's with the grading. I personally did not like that because my TA was a very harsh grader, but that's just my opinion and the only disadvantage of the class.
cool guy

Goes on long tangents about nothing for literal hours
There is no rubric to be found and grades completely arbitrarily
Openly admitted to grading every student on a different rubric
No clear project guidelines and never teaches you actually how to do anything
Most of the class is LinkedIn Learning tutorials
most inspirational, brilliant professor I've had at Cornell. Incredibly humble and funny too.

Do NOT take AMR. She is rude and condescending. Lectures are unorganized and unclear. When she asked a question and students didn't understand, she said "Really? No one knows? You'll have to figure it out then" rather than explaining! The workload is insane. She provided a rubric for the final report, but deducted points for items that didn't exist

Dr. Pritts, is such an awesome dude! This class, Hands on Horticulture, is a blast - literally the highlight of my week! He really strives to inspire a love of horticulture in each of his students. Side note: he makes really good vanilla ice cream and has an incredibly cute dog!

Definitely a great professor, his class really isn't hard and if you just show up and put a little effort in you'll definitely get an A. The classwork is comprised of two weekly one-page reflections, and the content is actually pretty interesting. Samson is the man and definitely the best FWS professor out there
Ethics is such an open ended subject and yet he grades the papers really harshly. For a 1.5 credit class, he gives way too much work...3 short papers and 1 long paper.

Honestly, Jiachen Wang is like the ultimate professor, yo! I'm telling you, this guy is on a whole 'nother level! Not only does he know his stuff, but he's got this magical way of teaching that just sucks you in.

First time teaching the class and it showed 

Not competent, many mistakes were pointed in her lecture material multiple times in lecture/office hours 

Wrote an exam that averaged 61% in a class where historical averages were ~80%, blamed her students

Dramatic discrepancies in prelim grading, >30% of class received prelim regrades

Avoid.

MIHARI 1110 is the most difficult class I've taken at Cornell thus far. But, also the most rewarding. Professor Wang and Professor Manthri evidently cared deeply for their students. Their consistent, reliable response time on EdStem are what made the content doable at all.
I would like students to know that Emily is a fair grader and get ready to participate.
He's so yummy.

Embarrassingly bad. If he taught any other subject you would litetally not believe it, you would think someone was pranking you. Inaudible past the 1st 3 rows, thick accent, and total disregard to how well the material is being understood. Class has more linear algebra than differential equations, and homeworks are impossible without TA help. F-.
Really hard to follow, doesn't really make a point when talking..

PPS304: Exams are multiple choice and short answer questions but there isn't enough practice for the short answer questions so you're basically left to fend for yourself. The problem sets just aren't a good reflection of what's tested on exams. Also leaves all the teaching to the TAs, who grade almost everything too. Get ready to memorize A LOT.

Professor Huyghue was incredible to learn from and clearly has incredible experience in negotiating in the sports world. The class can be tough if you are not accustomed to speaking in front of the class or being in a tough negotiation with someone else. He gives detailed feedback on your negotiating. Grade is mostly based on two live negotiations.

She's an amazing lecturer! The class was difficult but I learned so much! The problem sets were long (2-8 hours each week) and the exams were fair. The problem sets were graded very harshly and were worth 40% of the overall grade, so spending a lot of time on them was definitely worth it. I honestly wasn't expecting to like PChem so much!

Not a good professor.  Her comments on papers are not helpful.  I found her class to be the worst class I have taken at Cornell, and I wish that I would have taken any other class for FWS rather than this one.

She is good at introducing students to material they've never heard much about before - but assigns so much homework it gets annoying for a class people only take for the required english credit. She requires you to buy one book but you don't actually need it. Interesting class. Learned a lot about history.

She taught Section 103 "Arctic Dreams and Nightmares" this semester. Extremely professional yet super nice (and hot)! Was very generous about giving extensions  and was always willing to meet during office hours and also outside office hours if you asked. Gives plenty of extra-credit opportunities and lets you improve and re-submit assignments! :)
Danielle was my lab TA, and she was very decent, approachable, and very helpful. However, she was a pretty hard grader.
Ms. Eiseman made class interesting and made me more passionate about the subject. I think she grades more than fairly and I used many of her lessons in my actual line of work!

Took MATH 1910 with Prof Bernard in freshmen fall. Overall, lectures are very organized because he mostly follows the textbook. He answers every lecture question and tries his best to help the students by breaking problems down into simple terms. Furthermore, his lectures are always lighthearted and have a positive vibe in general. Best 1910 prof!

Allen is a super nice person and totally willing to help you. However, he is really busy (I believe) and it is hard to find him after class. Still, I highly recommend taking his course and enjoying the discussion there.

He made the number theory class really interesting. He used a combination of algebra with diagrams. An extreme clear and nice professor. If you were sick and need an extension on homework, he will mostly grant you couple more days to turn in the homework. He made all the notes available, and is very coherent when he teaches.Great professor overall.
One of the best lecturers I've come across. Gives thorough motivation and insight for theorems and what we're learning. Homeworks are pretty easy.

Professor MacNeill, preferably known as Allen, was a fantastic teacher! His class was so fun and interesting! He keeps the class focused with unexpected shouts, and goes on occasionally very funny rants. The work was not overbearing and he gave us plenty of notice for due dates. Only 3 essays and one final group project

Definitely a little dry at times if you are not passionate about the topic, but overall Professor Manning is a great guy. It's clear that he cares about students, and is lenient with extensions and grading. Not much homework at all, although there are a few group projects.

Orgo is a hard class in general, but the lectures were generally pretty clear. Exams were harder than lecture material. Take notes and READ THE TEXTBOOK! I found the textbook super helpful and did practice problems from the book after/in between lectures. It is easy to fall behind, so stay on top of studying bc it can be stressful to catch up.

Razor-sharp scholar with enough wit and personality to make things interesting and impactful. Very easy 2000-level English courses I'd recommend to freshman English majors or those outside the department without much critical background. Not super reading-intensive (just a handful of short novels and essays) but requires you to engage meaningfully
Wessels teaches a section of Film Aesthetics and Analysis at Ithaca College, and that's where I had her. She is a fantastic professor who clearly loves her job. Pick her section if you can!

Kenkel can be vague and confusing in hw/ test questions and in lecture. Asks a lot of true/false/uncertain questions and will basically accept any answer if you can justify it, which is a little annoying bc how can the right answer be all three choices?? The hw is graded harshly but TAs try to give partial credit where they can on the prelims.

Prof made lectures as fun and engaging as he could. Clear he cares about his students and enjoys teaching. Overall the workload was very manageable compared to the prior physics course I had taken but labs were a little long and felt a bit more tedious. The content was interesting if you enjoy physics.

Altschuler comes across as a very nice guy in class.  However, talking with him 1 on 1 he appears to be a really self-center SOB.  I think only his arrogant peers respect him.  All the other ones pretty much agree and talk about him behind his back.

Professor Trevino is a really funny guy. I can't really judge him on grading because we had 3 instructors and 2 TAs for the course who all graded the tests. Prelims were easy, but the final was a killer! Lectures were too by-the-book though some of his own examples were used occasionally. Extremely helpful during office hours! Highly recommended!!!
Can be challenging but is worth.

She's caring and understanding towards struggling students, but she's tough enough to uphold her policies before assignments get excessively late.  Be prepared for some interesting reading, which will be pretty hard to understand in some cases and ridiculously easy in others- but class discussion is always fantastic regardless of the difficulty.

He wasn't the head professor for Japan 1101 but he was a discussion instructor which played a huge part in learning the language. Really funny and nice instructor and truly felt like he helped progress me in learning.

The lectures are mostly about optimization problems to model effective contracts, so it ends up being math / theory heavy. I found it hard to keep up without reviewing between lectures. Workload was low though so it's easy to do well. The professor is a nice person and cared a lot about ensuring everyone understood what was being taught.

Frazier is very passionate about his work and clearly enjoys teaching. His patience is second to none, and he truly cares about his students. The class was a bit challenging with the nonstop problem sets but nothing unmanageable. The TAs are a bit unresponsive on Ed, but asking Frazier questions after lecture was always an option. Great professor!

I personally had a good experience with this class. If you are responsible and keep up, and don't take advantage of the leniency of Professor Nimark by getting lazy (ie, attendance isn't mandatory, won't quiz you, etc.) you will excel. Also, he is by far the most caring professor and clearly cares and understands that life is more than just a class
Lectures were poorly done. Case studies were very interesting.
WORSE TEACHER EVER
PK is a brilliant professor that makes you think about how the world operates

She is a model of scholarly dedication.  The life of the mind is a calling, not a job, and Norton models the passion, stamina, and excitement of living for ideas.  I was her teaching assistant decades ago, and I still cherish the memory of that semester.
Lots of work and quizzes, and can get really nit-picky about the aspects of Spanish. Grades are weighed by lots of assignments and tests. Go to office hours, put in some work, and you'll be fine.
She is usually available outside of class
Professor Richards is extremely helpful during office hours. The expectations are clear and the course is very organized!

if you want to transform your worldview and challenge yourself, take troy's classes. he's truly one of the most knowledgeable, patient, and skillful educators i've ever had the privilege of learning from. amazing human being (ask him about his life), and his classes are incredible. readings are long, but classes are transformative.

Professor Richards is extremely knowledgeable about the organ and her love for it was infectious. She truly cared about all of her students. She made sure to learn everyone's names and get to know them. I would definitely take another class with her!
Richardson is a fantastic professor. I took her Africana Studies class this semester, and had a great experience. I felt included, and definitely broadened my horizons. Absolutely recommend.
One of my favorite professors at Cornell! The workload and expectations for this class are reasonable.
Offers a unique insight relative to engineering that more people need exposure to, and tries to liven up lectures with interesting questions. Take his class if you are on the edge
Good teacher. Her assignments and her criticism are helpful. Total workload is fairly small.

Tripp is a lovely person and truly cares about the kids in his class and the subject he's teaching. Took this class as a requirement and I'm not the best at econ, but I ended up doing fine. The first two prelims were fairly easy, while the final was a literal nightmare (the median had to be ~60). Don't stress too much about it because it curves.

Hencey, while sometimes not the best lecturer, definitely makes up for it in office hours. His explanations are thorough, and he is willing to help you through a tough problem. The homework assigned at times was too difficult, but was rectified about 2/3 of the way through the semester.

Prof. Kirkland is quiet which can make staying awake hard but he's a great teacher with a good sense of humor. He's always around during labs and is friendly/non-judgemental if you need extra help. The labs for this class are tedious and grindy, though, so make sure you don't fall behind. Weekly homeworks are short.
Unfortunate to have a professor read off slides the entire time and not go in depth to specific topics. Cookie cutter format. Does not really care to be there.

***This is for BIOMG 2800, NOT the lab class 2801. Dr. Blake is an amazing professor. Genetics is tough but her lectures are so clear and make difficult concepts easy to understand. Go to study sessions to help understand things better and go to office hours when you are struggling. It is very possible to do well in this class.

I took 3040 with Prof Applegate, and it was a really interesting and well-constructed course. Readings were amazing, Prof Applegate is really smart and compassionate. Only lame thing was the other students-almost all of whom clearly didn't read or care much.

This was a really interesting class, Joe is a good lecturer and brought in several high-quality guest speakers throughout the course of the semester. However, the assignments were very long and difficult, and the multiple choice portion of the exam was excessively nitpick-y and tested pointless specific reading components.
One of my favorite professors ever

Prof. Muscalu is one of the best maths professors at Cornell university. His honor analysis series along with abstract algebra by Prof. Allen Knutson are the most challenging and rewarding maths courses I have ever taken at Cornell.
He's a good teacher. Focus and pay attention because he writes and talks fast. It's a good class and he will help you if you work for it or go to him. Too bad he's retiring from cornell :(

He is very passionate and knowledgeable. Amazing skills as an instructor and really encourages engagement and critical thinking. Really challenges you to think about material from the context of the real world and apply it practically. Cornell would be perfect if all instructors were like him.

Interesting guy, but incredibly boring teaching style. Expect to spend the semester yawning while he explains black-and-white diagrams. The information communicated is only useful if you have previous hands-on knowledge of agriculture. Those who grew up on a farm should have no problem, but those who don't havethat background will find it hard.
David is amazing! Be prepared to work really hard and dedicate a ton of time to the class, but also get ready to learn SO much.

Collin was consistently patient, supportive and encouraging this summer -- although I was definitely not the easiest student to put up with. He is an excellent lecturer, a talented research mathematician and one of the most social mathematicians I have ever met. There should be more professors like him!
good comments on papers

Doctor Behrens is an amazing professor! Each class had very interesting topics and he threw in some debates to spice up the class which made for a fun semester! There were a lot of readings and the class had a short quiz in place of attendance which had students have to pay closer attention to the readings. Overall a great class would take again!

Hard to contact him should you find yourself needing to, but his hockey class is so much fun. He's such a genuinely nice guy, and I actually looked forward to this even though it was early. He's reasonable and takes everyone where they're at (experience-wise). 100% should take this class, Dusty is awesome.

The class ultimately underwhelmed my expectations, and I came away not knowing too much more Business Law than when I went in.  The cases are interesting and fun to read, and Prof. Wagner is a good lecturer, but concepts are sometimes a bit vaguely explained and the tests are graded really harshly.  Decent class, but tough grading.
Paul Carr has an awesome background in the field of construction management and he isn't afraid to give students the real truth to make them the best future professional they can be.
No longer teaching. Professror Emeritus. Laid a good foundation for future teachers to model. His clarity of thought and instruction has helped me in my upper level courses. Master teacher

Lectures are just read ppts, except for guest lectures, which are the highlight of this course. The grading of the few assignments in the course is very strict and there's not much room for critical thinking. You are expected to recall lecture material near verbatim in exams, rather than original ideas (which will be marked wrong).

Professor Strebel is a real one. He's flexible with grading options allowing you to make up for mistakes. He's a very clear presenter and makes it incredibly easy to succeed in this course. Just show up every week and do the homework and you'll be vibing.

great professor, super super knowledgeable in his field, lots of fascinating experiences and overall a great guy, genuinely wants to talk about class topics and help you with assignments. lots and lots of readings but definitely manageable.
You need to take this class if you are at all interested in Chip Design. Prof. Batten is a great guy. Extremely well organized and fair with grading.

Dr. Deitcher is great. Overall the workload was pretty managable and he was very helpful. The class is very fexible. We are meant to meet 2x per week but are able to get everything done so we only meet once. There are no tests which is great. There are quizzes before each lab so be sure to read the protocol. Lab reports can be dense to complete.

Prof. Goncalo has such a great sense of humor- as others have said, such an asset to the Cornell (and ILR). Just a heads up, his name is pronounced "Gonsalo," not Goncalo. In other words, the C is soft. Take any class you can with him. He's so awesome.

Jack is an amazing professor for intro DEA course, he is passionate and easy going. It was a long semester, and everyone learned so much about design at the end of the year. Highly recommended if you want to look at the world from a designer's perspective.

Freed is, without a doubt, one of the worst professors at Cornell. His exams are completely unfair and frequently test material that he skips over in lecture/on problem sets. The textbook is almost completely useless, the lectures are boring and riddled with mistakes, and problem sets are verbatum from other sources. Do not take any class with him.

Really nice guy who clearly knows what he's talking about if you talk one on one, just isn't great at teaching it in lecture. You need to teach yourself most things to do well on quizzes. The final was unreasonably hard.

EDUC 2610 is an annoying and tedious class. Much of the curriculum and content in every 3 hour class period is pretty much spent on discussing how good and valuable the class and program (Intergroup Dialogue Project) is. There's lots of talk of changing society through education, but this class does nothing for that goal. Lots of work and it sucks.

I studied under Professor LaFeber back in the late 1970s as an undergraduate at Cornell.  He is the best teacher I've ever had.  He has the ability to inspire you with his command of and passion for the material.  He demands excellence.  The lectures were a high art form, organized around a 3-word outline and including great anecdotes. Magnifcent.
Excellent
Lectures were very entertaining and informative.  Tells a good story.
What a waste of money. Very insecure and needs to leave Cornell.
Easy A/A+. Makes accounting enjoyable. Caring and willing to work with students outside of class. If you are looking for an easy A for accounting, his class is best to take.

Profesora Hendrickson is an incredible instructor! Classes are very engaging, and she is very friendly and helpful. She genuinely cares about her students. There is a lot of HW, though, so be ready for that.

Your grade will mostly depend on two exams. He never specify what he really want to see on the exams, like graphs, examples, etc. The exams are not very relevant to the lectures. Avoid him if possible.
Ridiculously brilliant, even for cornell
Econ 3030 is a pretty straight forward class and Mitra explains the material well. The one thing is that he does repeat himself and lectures can drag on.

Reed lectures really fast and he had the TAs grade the paper pretty unfairly.  He assigns alot of articles to read... which he tests heavily on.  I found the subject really interesting but I would not recommend taking this class with Reed.

Professor Reed was the head professor when I took BIOEE 1780. He is an overall nice guy, and he is relatively funny, but he can be a bit condescending sometimes when you come to ask him for help. He was an alright professor, but not the best.

Really nice and useful materials! She rly knows what she wanna teach and how to deliver them clearly. No final exam but a big group project with clear grading criteria. Love the topic, love this class.

I don't understand why people below keep saying that the lectures for MAE 2250 is useless. And the people who say that sit on the back and don't even listen to the lectures. They only think lectures are helpful if they help you increase your grade. Well. the lectures are not helpful that way but it is very fruitful if you're going to be a meche.

Fan always brings awesome energy and a good sense of humor to lectures. She's great at presenting information intuitively. The class is also extremely well structured. I would highly recommend taking any course with her
Lovely and caring professor! But you have to actually work for your final grade

AMAZING PROFESSOR!!! I have never had such a caring professor that genuinely wants her students to understand the material and do well in the class. There is a lot of work but you learn more than in most classes, especially for a topic that is so important if you are pre-med/vet/etc and will use physiology again.
She's been a great professor, liked her more than others!
take turkish!!
He rocks!
Griffin Erich is amazing. He sure knows his paw-paws! Ask him about his strawberries.
I guarantee that he is one of the best professors in Cornell. Take his class if possible.

Lewis is great; he's personable, he moves the class slowly enough so everyone can easily keep up, and I learned a lot. His Intro to Yoga class is definitely more based on stretching and balance than it is on strength, which makes it very relaxing and a nice break during the day from other classes. He made doing yoga fun and easy.

Absolutely amazing professor. Freedom's genuine love for the material keeps the lectures fun and lively. Also, a superstar in his field. When the class is over you will have learned a huge amount of material- and in a strangely painless manner too. Bravo.
Such a sweet man- one of the most interesting classes I've taken at Cornell. The recorded speaking homework definitely piles up and was hard to find time to do in a quiet area, but great class!

Dr. Barrow had interesting content, but wasn't the most engaging lecturer. Her case studies and exams were also harder than Dr. Johnson's, and graded much more harshly. Honestly the worst part of this course was the disengaged TAs (they would be hiding amongst students during lecture, then speed out as soon as it ended).

One of the best professors I've ever had. Always able to crack a joke or link something back to his crazy family history. Super interesting lectures/content, and the Policy Wargame at the end of the class was honestly my favorite project that I've ever done. I would take him for literally every class if I could, I cannot recommend him higher
PhYZ 101 she is a good teacher take her , an easy A class , not tough grader

Prelims are based on lecture content, so study the slides. Make sure to manage your time well with the final term paper. Overall, the class was well put together and Professor Krewel is a very knowledgable lecturer.

Anita Racine is strict in her grading, and she will push you to be the best you can be. She will be honest with you, even when it is not what you want to hear, because she truly wants to see her students succeed. She makes herself available to her students and loves to answer questions.

He was very welcoming and didn't give much work. The whole class was surrounded by 8 though papers based on what we were reading, and a final project. Half the papers were interchangeable for art pieces which were easy and quick. not a stressful course in any means, although be ready to read.

Ken is a great guy. He's a little goofy, but very knowledgeable The class is very practical and a great way to spend a few hours in a forest every week. If you stay on top of the readings, the workload is pretty minimal. With a little effort an A is pretty easy to get.
Today I learned from my professor that Ken passed away. I am sure he was a nice guy and his contribution to the field of computer graphics (Specular BRDF Model) is covered in many textbooks.

Roberts is awesome. Sometimes he'll be describing a coup or rebellion and be like "yeah I was down there talking with the president when it happened." The book was actually really interesting. Posts all lecture slides online. The weekly 1-pagers are an easy grade anchor, research paper and final are not bad if you use notes and put in effort
Helping to create a post-Katrina New Orleans plan was a unique experience CRP 2000 offered. Professor Reardon has got to be the most helpful person alive. He makes an 8:40 am class feel like noon.

Very clear and organized lecturer. His lecturing skill noticeably improved as the semester went on since this was his first large lecture teaching, good became better. His exams are relatively straight forward, however no practice exams were posted. Problem sets were manageable.

Professor Zorn is amazing - Jerusalem the holy (arkeo/jwst 2666) is my favorite class I took at Cornell. It is definitely a challenging class, but he is so passionate about the content that it was so enjoyable. If you take diligent notes and memorize the slide IDs for the exams it won't be too difficult. 10/10 would recommend.

I really cannot sing Professor Marxs praises enough! I know next to nothing about start-ups, but Marxs lectures are always accessible and engaging. He really cares about his students and goes above and beyond to make sure we are assessed fairly. Has the most amazing guest lecturers!!
One of the best engineering teachers I've come across at Cornell
Brilliant and kind though he is hard to understand at first. His ideas are brilliant and rich and class conversations are generally very enlightening.
He's very fair and organized. As long as you follow the class materials, it is not difficult to do well. All about effort.

Professor Jura was great to learn from.  She is very interested in the subject matter and actively engages students in the classroom.  The homeworks require quite a bit of time and are group work, so pick a good group.  Grades are very exam based and also depend on the final project.  Nothing was too difficult to learn and help was available.

Jura made my first year at Cornell. Never have I looked forward to a class more. He's hilarious and seems like he's always on red bull, but at the same time, he's extremely dedicated, caring, and knowledgeable. If you put effort in he will make sure you do well. Can't recommend the class high enough, just make sure you can handle some teasing.

I love Katie so much!! She is extremely reasonable and relatable--which is hard to find nowadays.  She's also incredibly funny and uses her creativity and humor to make each class special and enjoyable. I took "Vindicating the Villain" with her and she was just so inspiring. It was BY FAR my favorite class of the semester!!! She's totally amazing!

Professor Roy is an excellent teacher. He is very knowledgeable in psychology and writing and displays this through in-class presentations as well as in his feedback for essays. As a professor, he is also very caring and understands the fact that students have other classes and activities. (He also knows CS and makes cool demos!)

Jodi's lectures were sometimes confusing due to an apparent lack of classroom control (sit in the front if you can't hear very well). In all, though, her class is very informative if you do the powerpoints and prepare for the speeches (I recommend going over each one beforehand with Jodi/your TA). Also, do the weekly discussion boards for an A+!

Dr. Cohen is a nice guy, but the class is run, and grading conducted, very rigidly.
