Lectures mostly recapitulate the readings, but you have to do the readings anyway because the tests include questions from obscure passages in the texts. He curves the tests, but only b/c students did so badly. It's an interesting course that could be much better.

Good class. The case competition can be difficult and ambiguous but its a good experience. He is very hands-off but very clear and grades easily. Study the slides very well and go thru the book to ace the tests. He can also be moody. If youre interested in business, take it. Good for networking too.
If you dont have to take a class with him don't take it.

The course is great in that it is removed form your typical euro-centric ARTH standpoint. It forces you to think about the various cultural influences on different works that were produced. The material is beautiful, diverse and interesting.

Worst class Ive ever taken across all departments at Cornell. Instructor and TA are awful at teaching grading and communicating. 1) endless mistakes in lectures and in office hours and not releasing homework sols 2) pointlessly harsh rubrics, slow grading 3) neither Prof nor TA reply quickly to emails unless they need something from you

Lectures were dry and heavy on theoretical information. Recitations were somewhat helpful but the many assignments were extremely difficult and required asking questions or stumbling through searching for specific coding functions. The class felt like it was preparing students for a PhD rather than just a masters level course. High expecations.

I did really well in his class so I have no complaints about his teaching style. However, I have never met such a rude, entitled, and narcissistic person before. I've worked in the hospitality industry for over 3 years, and he is still by far the rudest person I've ever met. If you have a choice, please choose another professor.

Many thought-provoking, dense readings, but Damien puts in extensive effort guiding the class in unpacking them. He is very passionate about philosophy, and it will show by the amount of work he gives you and the detailed feedback you will receive on it. He grades generously, especially if you show that you put in the time to improve your skills.

One of the toughest but also most rewarding classes I've taken at Cornell. By the end I had a rock solid understanding of the ins and outs of micro which prepared me well for macro and advanced electives. Lectures covered a lot of material so section with the TA is non-negotiable if you want to survive the tests.

Avoid this professor at all costs! The class was highly disorganized, and lectures were challenging to follow. Even though the professor made attendance mandatory, the slides are still ambiguous and unclear. Also, the TA/ professor never clarified assignment instructions until the very last minute, and exams tested the most random minor things.
One of the best teachers I've ever had. Really knows how to engage the students and teach about pertinent issues that affect students of color.
Lectures often hard to follow
Awesome lecturer. He actually made gen chem fun which I thought was impossible. Great guy!
Chip is a really cool guy. The lectures can be boring at times but the workload is very doable. I'm more interested in genetics just because of his class!
A clear example of how a professor can hurt a students experience at cornell. Had a situation where she bashed on essays and treated students unfairly. Not professional at all.

This class was not an easy A if you do not have a bio background. The prelims are oddly specific and difficult with a lot of memorization. Would not recommend it as a grade booster. Professor Hodge cares a lot though and teaches great. Quiz heavy class
SHE IS THE BEST

Although the course material was somewhat interesting (Medieval literature), she had a ridiculous amount of work required of us. She literally told us "you guys are doing grad-level work". My final project ended up being 27 pages long, consisting of a million questions and an 8-page essay, altogether took ~20 hours (FOR AN FWS!!! WTF)

A good lecturer, but the slides can be a bit lacking, with a bit too heavy an emphasis on theory at times. Tests can be difficult but graded fairly, and if you study you will do well. Problem sets are difficult but definitely reasonable. Overall he is a caring professor who takes feedback well and is willing to accommodate his students

It was an absolute joy to take CLASS2603 with Professor Platt and I'd recommend her to anyone! She is very open and accessible, organized, and gives excellent feedback; not to mention, she's a great person! The final project was very open and gave room for lots of creativity.

Professor Estad may have been one of my least favorite teachers I have ever had. He is the least personable human I have ever encountered. Talking to him is a chore as he demonstrates no emotion. He teaches the class like we are middle schoolers, but when it comes to grading he is obscenely harsh. Whatever you do DO NOT take his class.

Prof Fuhrmann is a wonderful lecturer and is very knowledgeable and passionate in her field. The way this course was structured was quite interesting as a bulk of it were guest speakers within the Asian Studies department and I enjoyed learning the course materials through those different perspectives. The workload was normal and pretty manageable.

Don't listen to the haters. They're right that this course has way too much work, but Bert himself is great. Only a few people can truly understand his comedic genius. He can be a little boring, but what can you expect from a professor who has been teaching the same course for 50 years.
nice

I took this class with Carrie for my FWS. The small class size means that attendance is mandatory. Class itself was interesting, but there were a few times when the lectures became somewhat dry. She tended to focus on content over grammar in the 6 essays we had to write. She is very kind and happy to help if you reached out to her.

Extremely friendly and approachable. Dennis is always willing to meet outside office hours and class time for as long as you need. Mostly clear though not always. Still, a lot clearer than Professor Barseghyan! Homework was always graded promptly and fairly.

Jasmine is the best!! She really challenges you with your development as a writer. The class itself isn't overwhelmingly reliant on grades, so as long as you make enough effort, you won't have much to worry about.

Literally such an amazing professor! Super enthusiastic about what he is teaching, always encourages questions. He really makes everyone feel really positive about coming to his class. His lectures are AMAZING, but somehow he always apologizes. 

My absolute favorite professor (+most humorous professor) at Cornell!
One of the BEST professors I have ever had! Class isn't necessary to get a good grade, but you should go anyways cause you learn a lot and cause he's so funny. Very approachable and helpful too!
she puts out for a better grade. I would've gotten a C.
Really chill professor and good teaching

This class is hard, though I think that its structure keeps you on pace as best as possible. O'Brien can be monotonous at times but it is very clear precisely what you need to know - the slides are really well made. NS 3410 with her is not for the faint of heart but it is absolutely possible to do well in it as long as you study consistently.

Very sweet woman, but so unorganized and unfocused in lectures it makes it so difficult to follow along with anything she is saying. She is a pretty easy grader on papers and assignments and tells you what you need to know for the one prelim in the class. Overall easy course, but hard to sit through her lectures.

No tests, just case studies and a final project (all group work).  Truly wants you to do your best.  Was willing to meet at any time to answer questions because I could never attend office hours.  Tip: you can answer the case study question any way you want, just USE EVIDENCE to back up your claim and you'll get As.  Great professor, great class.

DCP is a very manageable class since little outside work is required. Three prelims worth 25% each. If you are sick or unprepared, alternative test times are available. It would be helpful if you have a dairy background, but it isn't necessary since this class is mostly about dairy topics in general. Prof taught her first Cornell class well.
great professor. learned a lot in this class.

One of the best teachers I have ever had in my life, both at and outside of college. If you are interested in screenwriting or even just reading and analyzing screenplays, take this course. Amazing lectures that he puts a lot of effort into preparing, and he brings in great guests from the film world. Accessible outside of class as well.

First class I had with her, she said she "didn't know how to grade essays objectively." Years later, that is still the most bizarre thing that I have ever heard from an English prof. She picks favorites; if you participate a lot (even if you don't make good points) and come to office hours often, you'll do well. Very, very strange person overall.

She is interested in the topics she covered, but there wasn't much of a connection between all of them. She does give great insight into learning German better, and her feedback on papers is also great.

Prof. Bloomfield assigned a lot of reading at first, but lessened it when told by students that it was too much. Essays were graded fairly, always gave good feedback and was available to help. Towards the end of the semester became lenient and allowed us to do whatever we wanted for the final project, such as a group project on a current event.

Suzuki-Sensei is absolutely amazing. Class goes by so quickly with her and, despite always needing to do quite a bit of preparation and homework for class, I've never been stressed. She grades quite leniently for daily grades, but make sure you're prepared for written, oral, kanji, lesson, and vocabulary tests

Such boring class, barely has interaction with students. She just read the slides and didn't allow the use of laptops. No exam for this class, only one paper, so if you have bad teammates you are done.

Fun and intriguing professor. Exams can be a bit tough (even though they are open note) so listen in class and make a good study guide. He wants you to succeed and really is passionate about the class

Cliff is a great instructor.  He's more than willing to meet and talk over the material or current assignments.  He is very knowledgeable, and makes the material really clear.  You'll definitely learn how to analyze complex arguments and write a good philosophy paper.

Theo seems like a very strict teacher, however, he is very lenient on submission time and attendance. As long as you do the work, you should be fine. He does talk a lot in class and get off tracked, but his lectures are very interesting and thought provoking. When you ask questions though, he will question your thoughts harsly.
Lowi probably used to be a great lecturer, but now he just rambles all class and occasionally says something entertaining.  Everything depends on your TA, so try to get a good one.

Professor Case is without a doubt the best professor I have had at Brown. I took HIST0150G, History of Law: Great Trials. Her lectures have a beautiful arc, in which major themes are discussed, and the lecture ultimately culminates in grand realizations about themes of law, history, and human nature. Amazing class and amazing professor!

Dr. Seeley is one of the best, most inspirational professors I've had in college.  He cares so deeply for his students, and knew all our names before the second lecture of the semester.  Truly genuine, and the prototypical professor.  Simply put, one of the best professors at Cornell.

The class was a lot of reading that was very difficult, but she did really help me understand what was going on in them through discussions in class. It was more writing than normal compared to other German classes, which felt a little overwhelming at times, but I think that's mostly because of it being the capstone class.
too bad to have him as my professor. I am a master student in Cornell. This is the WORST professor in teaching I ever met, including my undergrad life.

Greg Sloan is one of my favorite professors I've ever had. It was obvious that he really cared about the content and made a point to share his passion with the class. He intentionally made the tests pretty difficult, but mostly so those who were going on to ASTR 102 were adequately prepared. Overall though the class was fun and super interesting.

I loved Professor Page & actually ended up taking two of his classes! He is such a caring and fun professor who gets very happy and excited when a good print is made! Is very knowledgable and is willing to share with you his experiences with printmaking and bookmaking! Will miss him very much since he recently retired from teaching.
Excellent teacher, clear instruction, helpful discussions in class, patient explanations, supportive, and passionate about the subject matter.

He got a lot better as the year went on and I was able to see how all the material connected. Definitely a hard class so would maybe benefit from an older professor with more experience but not bad nonetheless.

Class is eh. Professor is passionate big whoop! 2 credits and you need to memorize 600 slides worth of content for each prelim, for 5 prelims + a comprehensive final. No structure to exams, they ask random obscure details. If you want a good grade, don't come to this class unless you have photographic memory.

She seems like a nice person, however, she is not. There is little homework but she is a boring lecturer. She does not offer much help when preparing for prelims and finals and was rude when I asked for help with something (so be careful). The class sounds interesting so I recommend if you want to take it, then do so with another professor!
He was a great instructor. The class is a lot of work if you want to do well, but he is clearly very invested in the students and makes the discussions interesting. I would definitely recommend him!

Great professor!  Best language course I've ever taken, hands-down.  She gives great feedback and is very approachable, always friendly, and happy to clear up anything that might confuse her students.  As she said at the end of class, she will not be teaching any more classes at Cornell, but her clear and engaging style will surely be missed!

Strange class. Probably unnecessary. Covers basics of finance, very little on sustainability. Papers graded harshly by TA Branham, who is very nitpicky and does not offer constructive feedback. Final group project to develop financial instrument highly dependent on assigned group, some of which are carried by MBAs while others don't even have MBAs

Fantastic professor - his class on epistemology (Knowledge and Belief) was fantastic, with an eclectic, interesting reading list. He grades very reasonably, with clear expectations outlined in the paper assignments and the (comprehensive and helpful) review. Just do the readings, pay attention in class, and study with his guide, and you'll get an A

Took in SP22. Great professor! I'm not a huge fan of the flipped classroom but found the pre-lecture videos to be helpful in thinking about topics before lecture. His exams were fair given what we covered in class and he weights heavy on tests (60% of grade is one midterm and one final). GO to study hall for Psets! Super helpful b/c they are hard.
GREAT Prof. Is you are lucky enough to take his class, you are lucky enough. He is a crystal clear, excellent teacher with a kind heart to boot. Take his class!

He is not very good at time management. He spends almost the whole class period on simple concept questions then the last 8 mins he derives something new. The lon capa questions can be very difficult and he just doesn't give us the tools to complete some assignments. I got 100% on the first test then I got a high C on the 2nd. it's a rollercoaster

A very enjoyable professor, very knowledgeable and open to questions. All material is on printed notes handed out each class, but not much of it will make sense if you don't go to lecture. I did have his class in the late afternoon and his voice put me to sleep several times, but not for lack of interesting content.

Dude's a legend. Takes time after class to explain anything you had problems with. Yeah the homework is a lot for sure, and he expects some basic fluency in excel/python for some assignments, but he's really good at introducing Differential Equations. Handled the online transition like a champ too (Spring 2020)

He leads classes with multiple ways of stimulating discussion, generally allows it to be run by student comments, and he often adds the etymological history of pertinent words. His grading of papers is realistic, plus he allows rewrites, and is helpful in office hours. You're likely to end up a much better reader and writer because of this class.

Impossible to gauge if she likes you or not. Takes any question as a challenge of her knowledge and will ramble rather than just saying "I'd have to look into that." Relies on precolonial, colonial, and post-colonial framework for entire class which is reductive. Take a class from Judith Byfield instead and you will learn more and enjoy yourself.

Dr. Fiddler clearly knows what she is talking about, and makes herself available to students outside for anything whatsoever (whether that be personal, to answer questions from lecture, or to engage in a topic you are interested in). Her passion for the subject is clear and motivates students to do well and also have that same excitement.

The French grading system here of 95% is top score, no 100% is absurd.  This automatically lowers the grades. Professor Luks teaches French as an advanced English teacher would teach the origins and semantics of English. While interesting, it does not help learn the language much. Sections are where you learn everything, and grading varies by TAs.
She should go home and not come back to Cornell ever again.

For HIST 1951. Class isn't hard and I an easy A if you put in the work (which is a lot). However, there's a lot of reading so if you don't want to read a LOT, do not take this class. Attendance is mandatory for lecture and discussion sections. Lecture is incredibly annoying because the assigned readings tell you the same stuff in more detail.
Jón rules.

Prof. Lennon is the best. I took his ENGL 1134 True Stories FWS and it was amazing. The readings were all interesting, and there were 6 (3-5 page) papers due throughout the semester, only three count for your grade, and can be revised up to an A. Prof. Lennon is very caring and creates a creative and kind class environment. TAKE THIS CLASS
Made a boring subject interesting. Funny and relatable; very good professor!

Prof. Field's lectures were really interesting and actually made me want to go to class. There's a long list of sample problems given before each prelim, and if you study those you'll do well. Definitely recommend this course!
awesome awesome awesome
He is a very interesting, intelligent, and fun professor who cares about his students and inspires and creates educational growth more than any other professor I've had the privilege to encounter.

Talks about the university the author went to, the random people the author met, the random Russian authors the author drew inspiration from, all leading to 40 minutes of lecture that really adds nothing to your essays. On the other hand, course load is very chill, participation is almost optional, and essays are mainly graded on grammar.

Professor Kent is the literal best! He explains concepts clearly - it's obvious he wants to see his students succeed. Even for someone who sucks at coding his class has been great as he really dumbs it down for you. I will note he does have an off-putting dry sense of humor ... regardless TAKE HIS CLASS!
He is terrible. That sums it up.
Great professor

If you need an easy A, take this class (just talk at least once a section and stay off your phone). Professor Carter is very kind and down to earth, but his lectures often felt bias and it was not entirely comfortable for students with the minority opinion to share their thoughts. For this reason, I probably would not take another of his classes.
Extremely rude and condescending. Don't bother asking questions on ed discussion if you aren't ready to be criticized and patronized

Professor Hilgartner is excellent! cCass was very interesting and made me rethink science and technology's impact on society. Attendance is mandatory as there are in-class activities, and he doesn't post slides on Canvas. Also, quizzes and exams cover material in readings that aren't covered in class, so make sure to read and review those.
He's so friendly and helpful! Definitely take his FWS on free will!!

The semester began and I was not a fan of the conversational style of the course. Lengthy readings would be discussed all class. Eventually, I found Cosgroves teaching style to be great. I valued the lessons he shared regarding interpreting and understanding historical lit. His course really taught me how to back up arguments! Best class! Take it!
One of the best professors on campus by far. Helps manage stress really well. Love how all exams are open notes however you still need to study because of the time crunch. Hope he gets tenure soon.
run

Make sure to take Intro to management with Rogers in the spring semester. He is a good lecturer and really wants the students to do well. The median grade for the class was an A. The most important thing for his exams is to memorize everything on his powerpoint slides.

Easily one of the kindest and most genuine professors I've known at Cornell. When I needed an extension, he never said no. His courses were well organized, and his lectures were always a joy to attend. I think he legitimately loves what he does and wants to share it with his students. Often went to his office hours just to chat, which was fun.
HORRIBLE. Unbelievably bad. This is what I'm spending 200K on? How shameful...

This semester with Prof. Liesching was amazing. She genuinely cares about her students' work and is enthusiastic about the content. It was a great intro course. It taught the basics of darkroom and digital photography, and the entire semester was spent developing a portfolio (the final). Some reading homework but nothing terrible. Highly recommend!

Great teacher and a very interesting person. He is really good at establishing authenticity with students. He cares more about understanding than about bs politeness (e.g.  saying "don't nod your head just to be polite, do you understand this or not?). Oh, and he's also a great physics teacher :)

Erik is great. He is really passionate about what he teaches and enthusiastic about sharing his knowledge. Took an fws with him and he assigned 5 papers totalling summing up to about 25 pages in the end. Very flexible prompts that allow you to explore what you are interested in. Always willing to meet after class and is generous with extensions.

Professor Florea is a great lecturer. She approaches difficult topics through literature, primary sources, and a good dose of humour. The selected material is very interesting and contributes to your growth way beyond her class.

She genuinely cares about your progress and you as a person.

She talks to the board and asks if everyone understands her, she uses the most complicated way to explain the SIMPLEST of problems. She teaches to the smartest student in the class and forgets that we're not all math majors. For tests, she'll give you one thing to study, then put something completely different or ten times harder on the test.

Makes stats interesting. If you have to take a stats course, take this one. Also, not sure if he still does this, but if you have an A for the semester, you don't have to take the final, which was nice.

No matter how hard you try, he will still be super harsh on your grading and will leave you no room to improve. DO NOT TAKE AN FWS WITH THIS GUY. I would say that I am proficient in writing and still got a B+ even though I displayed writing feats that deserved higher.
amazing lectures, and the research proposal assignment changed the way I think about economics

This guy might actually be the worst human being I've encountered at Cornell. He goes out of his way to make his student's lives miserable. He tried to fail as many kids as he could when I took the class, despite how well they understood the material. He would grade arbitrarily and inaccurately. He singlehandedly ruined my interest in Mech-E.

Took this class out of curiosity. At the end, I was filled with nothing but deep regret.
The prelims are designed to ensure you make mistakes. The problems you have to solve in prelims do reflect examples from psets, but are intentionally made more convoluted & tricky. On top of that, you get very little time to solve them. DO NOT TAKE!

Professor Byrne truly cares about her students and not just their success inside of her class, but their outside involvement as well. I was fortunate to have her for COMM 1101 in person this year and she was so understanding about all the struggles of online learning for the kids who were not on campus and pushed back assignment dates to help.
Professor Byrne is the GOAT. I loved his Sports COMM class and I think any student interested in sports would too! The topics are very interesting, and it was a pleasure to attend class.

Extremely confusing. Never sure what is going on in class and have no idea what I'm supposed to write for the essays. She's not a bad person but she speaks too quietly in class to hear her and everything she says makes little sense. Essay prompts are confusing and she is little help when asking for clarification.

I took Alice's class a few years ago and I really liked her FWS class. It was really easy and I liked that we had creative freedom for our final assignment. She gives really good feedback on our papers and she is one of the kindest profs I've ever met. Would definitely recommend taking one of her classes :)

Took Medieval writing FWS; she gave a ton of freedom for paper & presentation topics and definitely wanted everyone to do well. Also had interesting guest lecturers & multiple extra credit opportunities.

Excellent and lovely professor. I took the Asian-American Food Writing and loved it! A great way to talk about the challenges Asian Americans face and the cultures enjoyed through food. She is very understanding and receptive to the mental health of her students. The grading is effort-based (not based on capabilities), too!  Would recommend!
Nice and very helpful if you ask

Just average. Good person, lectures were pretty boring. Grading rubrics were not straightforward, even for some prelims. Still, not difficult and final project instead of exam. Doesn't give A+ (even for students who earn 98-100), annoying for ENGRI course

For each lecture there is a quiz the questions seem much more like word games. The course has everything to rate, including quiz, prelim, projects and written homework. The lecture is no more interesting than the reading materials.

He is very sweet but fails to realize that he is teaching an introductory course. He will present information in lectures which has never been touched before if you did not read the book throughly. The content itself is interesting and the exams were not terrible. It is probably possible to get a perfect score in his class if you put in the time.

Professor Brann is a very memorable professor. Islamophobia, Judeophobia and Racism was an incredibly interesting course that I looked forward to attending. His lectures always had different formats which kept class interesting, and he always included snippets of current, relevant news which made the content more applicable!

Heidi is really so so sweet and is so understanding but she does not understand how a cornell student functions. She does not give A+ when they matter so so much here and she is heavy on group work to the point where our final project was a group project. She is so kind but a LOT of work
I loved the course structure and knowing the professor's expectations. The depth on the discussion boards was fantastic.

Taught Quantum Mechanics II. Very unintuitive course material, but this professor makes the course as intuitive as possible and has great explanations. Clearly knows what he's doing and makes this course tolerable.
Great Lecturer, CS4220 is a HARD class with a lot of linear algebra

Would not recommend taking this class with him. I took it when COVID first started but think about it to this day. He's nice and it's an easy A, but I learned the literal bare minimum of linear algebra. I occasionally am embarrassed that I don't know linear algebra concepts I need in my STEM classes now. If I could go back I would take ENGI linalg.

I took Professor Netravalli's class as a non-FSAD major and personally found the material a bit difficult to digest. However, Netravalli was very understanding and was available and enthusiastic to help during office hours. He even held a review session for the final exam. Overall very fair testing and wonderful professor!!
Has a cool accent! Shows us random videos, and comments on work a lot.

I took this course with her and Sachse teaching. Originally in Sachse's section, I found him too fast and difficult to understand. I found Van der Meulen great. She explains quickly, but she gets to the point and is great, especially in office hours. She's actually quite understanding and just plain awesome, imo anyways.

The class is straight-forward but the professor dislikes dissenting opinions. If you agree with everything he says, he'll give you an A/A- on assignments; if you try disagree or present a different theory, he will penalize you with a B/B-. If you're okay with compromising your personal opinions, you'll do well in this class.
Interesting lecture but the equations that you need for homework were never mentioned in lecture, don't take this if you really need the 1 credit.

He is knowledgeable of the content, but doesn't seem to care much about the class itself. Many times, his lecture slides are incomplete in class. Course material is covered in lecture once, and never again. Prelims are incredibly in-depth and he provides no additional material for studying. Pop-quizzes are on very specific details from the readings

Genuinely my favorite class at Cornell. He's funny and its really clear that he is passionate about the topics he teaches. His classes are fun and interesting and he actually care for his students. He always asks for feedback on what to teach and essay feedback is also really clear (if you do what he tells you, you'll get a good grade).

I absolutely love Professor Manne. She is extremely respectful & caring of her students and is careful in covering the complex ethical issues explored in this course. I have truly never encountered a professor who seems to care as much about her students as she does. In addition, she is a brilliant, brilliant academic whose class is engaging & fun.

The textbook is used heavily, and tests are hard, but neither is her fault. She helps you as much as you need it, and facilitates the class well. She will give you breaks in the form of good grades on projects if your grades are lacking in other areas.  Overall, a good, caring prof. Would recommend.

She is well known in the community and places students well and knows her stuff. But she is kinda bitter to talk to. She isn't the most pleasant easy going person who is happy with her life. She can be acerbic and rude.

Professor Royer lectures very well and is very considerate of students and student requests. I would definitely recommend going to his office hours. He is very clear on his expectations, but might occasionally miss one or two topics that you should read in the textbook or go ask about in office hours or after class.
Cool dude, fun stuff

Strauss is legendary. Seriously, look him up. One of the best lecturers I've had at Cornell. Wish we had more like him as they are a dying breed. Sections are optional (still get 4 credits). If you take good notes in lecture, you don't need the readings. You'll do well if you can write well.

Professor Mao is a nice lady but is super hard to understand during her lectures. She reads directly off of the slides so class gets slightly repetitive. There was no attendance taken but she does do WSJ discussions at the beginning of class. There were 3 prelims in class and a final so lots of tests.

Professor Li is newer, so there are definitely some small organizational kinks to the course. However, the course as a whole is very interesting, and the professor is clearly very passionate about the subject. He does a good job of letting students know what content is emphasized on each prelim, and cares a lot about the success of the course.
Really helpful and caring guy - he reached out to me individually when I wasn't doing well and it meant a lot.

Professor Pereboom made what I thought would be a really dull class engaging and thought-provoking - he really cares about all the students and class discussing, which makes you actually want to go to lectures. Great class!

The GOAT of ORIE himself. Very engaging lecturer. Participation is based on how much you answer or ask in lecture, and he tries to remember everyone's names. Clear and concise lectures, will explain until students understand. Weekly graded HW and python lab sessions. Overall, not the easiest ENGRI but definitely learnt a lot.
The difficulty is progressive. The final outcome is impressive. ANSYS and MATLAB in the pocket.

BTRY 3090 not 5954- I'm not sure how to add another course. I took this class as a statistics major elective and I was close to dropping it before the first prelim but I'm SO glad I didn't. This class was possibly the most useful and applicable class I've taken so far, and Professor Das was so caring and invested in us. It was hard but so worth it!

Great professor, very enthusiastic and knowledgeable about inequality, poverty, race, and families. Classes are mostly discussion based, so participation based on readings (which is pretty heavy, but very fascinating) is crucial. This was easily one of my favorite classes at Cornell, and one in which I learned a ton.
Unbelievable professor. Easily the best lecturer at Cornell. She is a humble genius who steers the conversation in such a way as to be unimposing but also critical

Shes not for everybody, as you can see by the sharp divide on reviews. Shes very strict with deadlines and making sure you are in class and engaged, which rubs many people the wrong way. But she truly does care about the students and will go out of her way to make sure you are doing well.

One of my favorite teachers/professors ever! It felt less like a formal student-teacher relationship, and more like a relationship of mutual respect. She's happy to meet with you for office hours, which you might need, since she's picky about thesis. She is very eccentric, but it creates an incredibly lively and entertaining in-class environment.

John Bunge knows so much about the statistics field and isn't afraid to spend entire lectures discussing an interesting hot topic, such as the replication crisis. He followed the textbook pretty closely, and exams and HW were reasonable. You should definitely go to lecture because he was so funny and gave great advice to stats majors.

Get there early. He starts writing notes before lecture that you need. He makes mistakes-just writes the wrong stuff, and has gotten corrected on concepts by students. He starts 3-4 sentences at once and finishes 1. He does everything he can to avoid answering questions. Hes very smart, but get ready to teach the class to yourself. Tas r chill

Elizabeth Meyer clearly cares about printmaking - but her feedback is semi-helpful at best and downright demeaning at worst. I frequently was given better direction by the TA and technician. At least for introduction to Print, the workload was intense.

Course is essentially an extended research project. Fairly self-guided, invest a lot of time in learning about and finding a good research topic. A lot of opportunities for feedback on your writing and you learn a ton about how to conduct and present research. End up with a nice writing sample useful for internships or senior thesis apps.

Pretty clear in his lectures, only 2 prelims & 1 final so exams are 85% of your grade but he gives 2 options. Class avg is a B+ I think, he gives practice final pdfs, & lots of office hours. Lowest 3 hws are dropped, pollev for participation each class. Exams are fair but can be tricky so study the slides. At the end, he offers extra credit

Genetics is one of the hardest classes you'll take as a bio major because you can't rely strictly on memorization - you have to consistently practice and engage with the material. Dr. Barbash is an excellent lecturer and made the concepts really clear and understandable! Prelims were difficult but nothing unexpected.

Gave good lectures but the class was very disorganized. The labs were taught by the TA's and didn't always relate back to the lectures. Homework and labs were often very confusing and required a lot of time at office hours for clarification. Tests were much too difficult for an ENGRI class. Would not recommend this class.

My experience with professor Xu was average. His lectures had the most quantitative material, but were all very basic and easy to understand. He likes plants, numbers, and graphs about plants, so you'll like him if that's your thing.

this is my first rating on this website but I had to give a shoutout to professor sampson. he made a very difficult class (cs 4110) that was FAR out of my comfort zone palatable and enjoyable. he truly cares about his students' learning and the class topics, 10/10 professor.
Prof. Lewis is super intelligent but the material is a little dry. Lectures can be really hard to follow.

Nice professor that discusses pieces of text in class. There are presentations from a student group each week. The weekly readings could sometimes be a bit much. Total of 5 pieces of writing for whole semester. Only negative is the no technology during class policy which will lose you lots of points if you don't follow.

Took this class as an easy distribution and it did its job. Prof. is nice and very clearly cares about what he's teaching, though the lectures were painful to sit through. Almost felt bad that people weren't taking notes/paying attention. Essays were easy, skimmed readings, and final was exactly like the review sheet he gives.

He is not an engaging speaker and lectures are incredibly dry. He reads off of his slides most of the time and teaches by reading 3 or 4 discussion questions to the crowd without providing answers to them. I think his style of teaching can work much better in a smaller discussion-based class. Tests are easy if you do all the readings

Great professor. Readings were insightful and informative. He expertly covers a plethora of interesting topics, including workplace cultures and discrimination. Papers are really easy (just have to reflect on the readings), and tests were graded fairly. I enjoyed the lectures, but if you aren't interested in the topics they may be boring. Gives EC.
Benissimo

This was easily my favorite class at Cornell. Dr. Zacher is super sweet and will always help you, as long as you're proactive about asking for it. Super fun person to be around too, she's funny and even brings her dog into class sometimes! Highly recommend.

Literally just do the assignments and study the slides (which he provides) for tests and you'll get an A. He is such a gem of a teacher, I took 3 of his classes while at Cornell and loved them all. Great content, perhaps not the most dynamic presenter but he has a heart of gold (brought cookies that his wife baked for us at the final).

Took a FWS class with her: Diverse Voices of the American Identity. If you think you are going to take a writing class just to satisfy the requirement and can breeze through the course, then this class is not the one for you. However, if you do take this class, you will be introduced to a caring teacher who devotes much of her time to her students.
he is very likable person

A decent professor, definitely knows CAD, sometimes went a bit fast with the demos. Computer lab format helps. The assignments were OK but a bit long for 1 credit, especially the 2 larger projects, and he usually took the whole 75 minutes leaving no in-class time for homework.
Favourite Professor of all time. Super understanding and caring of students. Exams are all very fair pretty similar to practice and homework.

Gilly is extremely unaccommodating. She never adopts students' feedback. TA's grading criteria is also very confusing. They always deduct points on assignments somehow. There is a lot of outside class work that is time-consuming but you won't learn anything in the end. Never take the class with Gilly. The worst professor I have ever had at Cornell.

Easy Class, not to much work, expect 2/3 essays tops, and maybe a 300 word assignment every week/other week. You can resubmit assignments to replace your grade.
Prof is super sweet and caring. She's passionate about our lives and wellbeing. Super chill with extentions. Classes are rich, and interesting.

Julianna was our print studio manager and TA for our print class when Professor Page taught print media. Julianne is like everyone's mom in the art department! We love her so much and I'm so happy to see that she is now officially teaching print media! She is so knowledgable and is always willing to help outside of class especially on weekends!

I was part of Hunter's first class at Cornell (Fall 2023), and he was great! Hunter did his very best to meld our interests in medicine with his interest in medieval literature. There was LOTS of reading, but as long as you get a feel for the class and who participates, you don't always have to read 100% of the material.
Easy going professor but not a good grader.

Professor Chen really cares about her students and is always accessible outside class. She also provides students with strong support for application of jobs and graduate schools/MBA. The workload of the course is fair and the materials are educational and interesting, but you need to be prepared before class.

At first was did think I would click well with Hascup. He has his set opinions about architecture, I have mine. Yet he is very experienced and great for the comprehensive design unit. Knows his stuff. But be prepared for a lot of white models, parapets and retaining walls
Really take a course with her! Fantastic professor

Easily messes up his notation and makes mistakes in lecture. Problem sets and exams are generally fair, although exams tend to be longer than what a normal person can finish.  Overall really nice guy.
My favorite professor. So kind and passionate. SUCH a fun class, best class I have taken at Cornell.
The lectures were fine for the most part.  The material wasn't that bad but the tests were tricky and not straightforward.

It may not seem like it at first but she really cares about her students and wants them to become good professionals. She's pretty funny but gives tough,realistic critiques/feedback. Even though its a four hour studio she usually leaves within the first two hours for meetings so a lot of the time is just work time. Lenient with deadlines.

Helen is GREAT! She is a great masseuse that really understands the stresses of being a Cornell student. She shows you step-by-step the different techniques. This class, Swedish Massage, is a fantastic class to take, it really helps relieve stress, it feels great, and it gives you some new massage techniques to impress your significant other.
Dr. Nivison is nice, practical, and knows her stuff. This is a great course. Very well designed. And youll have lots of time to interact with the instructor(s).
What can be said? Carl Sagan is the best!

Math 1910 was tough for me honestly. Carl was a great professor however do not rely on the lectures. I made that mistake and realized to really undertand this course read the textbook along w attending lectures. Class is not impossible but exams were not easy. If you can do textbook problems you should be fine but you need a good understanding.

Carl really wants students to succeed. He loves it when people come into office hours. He is clearly incredibly passionate about neuroscience - even though it is his last semester here, he is still teaching Journal Club and encouraging students to find him with any questions.
The class is merely okay.

He's really nice and means well, but he can't teach. Grading scheme is unclear, tests don't always reflect the content, & lectures aren't helpful in any way. His strategy is to look at the slides he took from another teacher for the first time in class, which means he has no idea what he's doing and sometimes gets his own iClicker questions wrong.

Prof. David Bindel is very good at explaining concepts. He also has exceptionally good lecture notes that go into a lot more depth in every topic. However, he does really like Partial Differential Equations, so a lot of his examples and homework problems come from that area.

He is extremely knowledgeable and smart, but he frequently used large words that were kind of ridiculous. He's a harsh grader, and not a very nice professor. He very obviously takes himself too seriously, and that translates to his ability to have a respectful and thoughtful conversation with his students.
She is amazing and well-experienced!
Just awful. Lectures are horrible he can't teach for his life. Prelims are impossible. The difficulty of the quizzes between discussion sections is honestly embarrassing.
Samir was my study group leader for biog1440. He required everyone to talk and pointed out quiet people a lot, but overall I gained a lot from the discussions and collaborative learning
very unfair and if he doesn't like you, he'll do everything in his power to make you fail

Peggy is a tough grader, but is very knowledgable about her subject area. I enjoyed being in her class, and I would personally rate her a 5, but I know that she wasn't well liked by many others in the class. It was her first time teaching, and I think she did a great job. Her feedback was always articulate, and she was always very direct.

Very difficult to understand. Lectures were dry, boring, and scatterbrained. I fell asleep almost every lecture that I went to. If you read the book though and go to Section, you can easily get an A like I did. Just pray for a good TA.

I had him for an FWS in Wierd Fiction. He is very nice, a fair grader. As long as you do pretty good to decent work and you show effort in the essays and revisions then you should expect an A grade. We watched a few movies in class, and he ended up dropping some of the readings from the syllabus. Would definitely take again.
Great Professor. Makes learning a new language fun and engaging. Exams were easy and final was online at home. As long as you go to class, should be an easy A.

One of the best professors I've ever had! This class is really interesting and, at times, complex material. There is a decent amount of reading, this is a literature class, but you can get away with being behind.

He really cares about his students and is passionate about the material. He often goes off on lectures, but he usually has some pretty interesting things to say. He is a tough grader and expects quality essays, but overall, I think I gained a lot from the experience, even if I didn't quite get the grade I expected.

The teaching methods he employed were not helpful. I think he mistaked us for elementary school students. The course could be better taught. Don't take the class to satisfy the language req. Take the test instead, if you can.

Not enthusiastic, somewhat boring, but tries very hard to be concise in lectures. Sometimes it is hard to see why lectures are important, or how things connect. Grading criteria are fair and well-documented.

Interesting, challenging, fun, understanding, but most of all relevant! I am currently on vacation in Costa Rica and I have been able to communicate with the locals and participate in all kinds of conversations. The feeling is amazing! Before taking his class I could only mumble a few words and phrases in Spanish.
Lena is great! I love her!

If I could describe this course in one word, it would be hell. There's no textbook and limited resources to help you understand the upper-level engineering topics presented. Despite this, it's a really interesting course. Prof. Kourkoutis makes lectures engaging and she's the best person to teach it. Prepare to be outshined by ECE/AEP kids though.

Pretty good professor. Really knowledgable about what he teaches. Probably one of the most knowledge in the dairy science field to be honest. Courses are not content heavy and easy. But grading is mediocre.
Dr.  VandenHeuvel is very strict and her lectures get very boring. She does pour as much wine as the other professor and they aren't as good.
Justine is a great professor to learn from. She is stern, but forgiving if you make a consistent effort

Very down-to-earth and genuinely cares about her students. Sit-down discussions are very interesting but slide lectures can be a little dull. She is super supportive outside of class for a STEM student like me who is not familiar with college history courses. Would definitely take again.

Maxson is a super funny guy. As much as I didn't really like the nature of having to do a lab class like this, he was a great professor and you could tell he was enthusiastic about everything he was talking about, which made the class a lot better.
Great Professor one of the best at Cornell
Professor Harel is a really nice man. He's a great poet and teaches the art well. I love his relaxed and easygoing personality.

Professor Pepinsky is AWESOME. I learned a ton about Southeast Asia despite being from there myself, and even with the abundance of information I still felt like I had agency in forming my own thoughts. I looked forward to every lecture despite them being held during sleepy afternoons because they are that good, and I'm not even a government major!

Phenomenal professor. I learned so much (sp22). He is so so so passionate about teaching and mechanics. Despite his quantum gravity research, he absolutely adores all of physics. He was almost bouncing on the balls of his feet from excitement when we got to SR. Come to him with all your unrelated physics questions, he loves thinking about physics.

Professor Silva is the nicest guy. This semester, I TA'ed for him for credit, which involved reviewing articles for his PLBIO 2400 course. Very very little work, but he wants all his TAs to register for 3 credits regardless. Easy A+ and he's nice to talk to. Take PLBIO 2400 with him, and then TA it for him the following semesters, nice GPA boosts.
really nice

I really enjoyed Annie's FWS class. Her lectures were really eye-opening, as I came in loving nature but with little knowledge of music. Workload was very reasonable. Guest lecture component of the class consisted of many outstanding speakers and helped keep the class engaging, although Annie's lectures are great too.

Took the class for my final semester as an undergrad. Would not recommend, he is just very selfish and full of himself. I was dealing with a serious disability and he refused to provide any accommodations even when prodded by the SDS. Dropped after he told me it was a "farce" for someone like me to be taking his class.

This professor was absolutely terrible. Even though it was not the easiest of material, he made class far more complex than it needed to be to explain the simple concepts. He is the worst professor that I have had here at Cornell, and he should be put out to pasture.

she has to be the most disorganized person of all time. her lectures are scattered, tangential, and non-structured. she'd be better off using verb lists and other language resources, with grammatical practice. the lack of rigor and precision on her part leads to an even greater sense of disorder. she also makes you do more work than syllabus says.

From the amazing reviews here, I was really excited to take her global warming class at UT. However, she is a lazy professor who doesn't care to see her students succeed. She gives you very few resources to study for the exams. In her office hours she was very rude and told me I needed to learn how to study better when I was genuinely asking help.

An absolute gem of a professor. If you don't do the readings you'll definitely strugglebut then again, why take a theory class if you don't want readings? Prof Makki is incredibly knowledgeable and helpfulif class discussion isn't great, go to his office hours for some super engaging conversations. 11/10

Professor was super chill and understanding, and the class was fun and full of interesting material. I think we had great freedom/flexibility in essay topics. There wasn't a lot of work, but you need to put in some effort (it was nothing compared to my other FWS). Just show up prepared, and you'll get a good grade.

Professor Chun's online marketing course was easy. She explains everything concisely & before the prelim & final releases a study guide for you to fill out. If you fill out this study guide well, you are set. Tests are very easy if the study guide is completed in full. Other than that, participate in weekly discussions and you're set.

Loved this class. As someone with very little law/govt background, I found this class to be very easy to understand; prof. Chutkow explained things very well and provided great examples. You dont really need to do readings to do well in the class. Very important to take good notes and go to lecture, as problem sets rely on stuff she says outloud.
Easy class. Great professor.

Professor Rzeszotarski is the best professor I've had at Cornell so far. He is witty, and cares a lot about certain things, but is a great professor that cares for students. I would take any course with him again

Nice professor. She is really knowledgeable. She is helpful during office hours, but she asks you to think hard instead of giving you the answers straight out. Exams were tough, material is extremely tough. But a great class to take if you are interested in analog IC design.

Heckathorn is AMAZING! He's monotone so it's easy to zone out, but the slides are posted. You can tell he really knows what he's talking about (apparently actually a big deal in his field) and his anecdotes during lecture are the best. A lot of readings, but you can figure out which parts you really need to read based on lecture and section.

The class subject is interesting, but the lectures tend to be a bit dry. He's very kind-hearted and gentle, so overall he's a wonderful person who really does care a lot about food security in the midst of climate change. Just know that classes and discussions tend to be very quiet. Content and homework easy, and average is A-.

Decent FWS class. She is a great instructor and very helpful. She is also incredibly understanding of circumstances and always will give you the extension if you ask. The readings are pretty long and hard to follow, though, but she appreciates any level of understanding and doesn't look for perfection, knowing the level of her readings.

The relevance of the assignments to the exams was relatively low. The practice exams didn't really match the real exams, I feel like that the exams shouldn't have been as nitpicky as they were, but nonetheless he was a funny guy and tried his best. I heard Professor RVR in the spring is much easier if you want to just take this class to get an A.
amazing

Prof. Cho is good. Like any other class, your dedication is a direct factor of your success. The semester started only a couple weeks ago, but I can tell he really knows his stuff, and his course policies are reasonable. You really have to focus in class to follow along, but as long as you do the pre-class activities, you'll be fine.

When I took the course I will admit, the class was fun! Learned a lot! Unfortunately he is definitely not the most accommodating guy, especially if you have an emergency/physical health issue. I missed class due to an emergency and when the makeup slot conflicted with a class he refused to offer me another. He also declared such to the whole class.
Called the cops on a student who wanted to work quietly in the back. Enough said.

Very caring about his students and pushes his students to do well. Kind of a quirky guy with sometimes a weird class structure, but his teaching is extremely effective. I'm really bad at econ and he made it very easy to understand.

Shelley Feldman's approach reflects a dangerous trend in academia. She is a postmodernist ideologue for whom there is no objective truth, except for hers of course. For her science is a tool that allows western white males to maintain power. This is what happens when you're so open minded your brain falls out. She is not a teacher, she is a bully.

Professor Wong has truly brilliant insights into American history and literature. While she teaches in the Asian American studies program she also has substantial knowledge of African-American literature.
Professor AI is a great person. He truly cares about his student, and has a kind heart for grades. Would like to take his class again.

6 Papers, three of which can be on any subject matter relating to social activism. Last three revolve around a research assignment that he is helpful in coming up with. Always very clear on his expectations and is a relatively easy grader. Too much reading but it is easy to get around it.

Professor Griffiths was an amazing and engaging lecturer, open to questions and clear in her explanations. She provided lecture notes to fill in during class and posted them on Canvas. Homeworks and projects were easy, with ample time to complete. Prelims were straightforward, and the final was slightly tougher but still fairly easy.

Nora has been super chill this sem, TWA work is optional, much kinder on grading than I heard she was in the past. She just really wants everyone to participate. I mean, its ASL 3 at this point you should do more than stare at her blankly all class... but I know some people don't like when she gets upset we aren't participating.

Had such an amazing time with Professoressa Nora! Not only did I have her for ITAL 1202, but 1201 as well, and she is one of the most easygoing, approachable professors I've ever had. She will grant extensions when asked for them, will meet with you outside of class whenever you need to if something needs to be discussed, and is a fair grader!

Professor Prior was one of the best English department professors I have ever had the privilege to study with as a student at Cornell University. Yes, I did take his class of the Personal Essay to fulfill an advanced sophomore writing credit, but even so I learned so much from him. It's a bit sad to know that he will be leaving Cornell :/

Honestly incredible. One of my favorite professors. He comes from and incredible background in spacecraft engineering, and can elaborate to insane depths on almost any question you have for him. There's a reason he was CTO of NASA

I love Professor Hay. He is very established in his field, communicates points well, and has a great sense of humor. Antitrust Economics was challenging but not unreasonable for someone looking to get a taste of a law school class. I only wish I didn't skip lecture so much haha.

Great course! I`ve learned a lot about space and missions. The professor and guest lecturers are NASA members. I can definitely tell that my writing has improved, and I`ve learned how to prepare for and give the presentation. Grading is clear and fair. Your grade reflects your work and improvement. You are not competing with others which is great.

Extremely boring lecturer and repeats himself a lot BUT nice, fair, considerate guy and he'll take the time to give you really thorough and detailed answers to questions you have. Class and material are not hard if you pay attention (go to OH), exams are easy if you memorize his slides (no surprises).

Pelliccia was one of the most accessible professors I had. He was more than willing to meet with me and help me in this class. That being said, this class was hard. I really struggled with it. Homework every night, and lots and lots of quizzes and tests.
Hes fine. Thats all.
Terrible professor
Teaches an extremely complicated subject like its 8th grade English. Truly an unbearable class to sit through.

Great class. No prelims or final. Most of the lectures are really interesting, especially the guest lectures. TAs are great. Not a particularly hard class. Prof Streeter really cares about students and wants you to do well. Just go to class and you'll do great.

I have had one and heard from several other people that he has inappropriate conversations regarding taste in partners.He is disorganized and late getting papers back, and leaves poor comments.He picks favorites in class, if he doesnt like you he will cut you off, if he does he will help you.Despite the course title class will be on Jackson Heights

Professor Kuruvilla is the best. His phenomenal lectures make a very complex subject fascinating. If you work hard and seek out his guidance, he will help you succeed. One of the most deeply passionate and genuine professors I've encountered in the ILR School. Highly recommend taking his course!

Cider is twice as much work as Wines and Vines in half the amount of time (seven weeks). Grade depends on weekly quizzes which may cover complex topics not mentioned in class. Two papers around sensory evaluation, apple production, cider fermentation, and/or marketing/economics graded (harshly) by peers. Very fun daily tastings and guest lectures.

This class was pretty tough for me personally. I found lectures difficult and unclear and the textbook was not great at explaining certain topics. Quizzes during discussions were alright since they closely followed the homework, however, tests extremely difficult and unfair. Also ed-discussion responses were a bit mean sometimes.

Had her for HADM 1650... she talks all the time and is just really annoying. Very very strict on grading so be careful to make sure you follow her rubrics to a tee. Also even though you are only "required" to go to 1 professor meeting, 1 meeting with writing center, and 2 with CA, you still get points deducted. Just try and not take her if possible

Shambhu is an excellent professor who really cares about his students and wants them to do well. I took four semesters of Nepali and loved it! It's a challenging language to learn, but Shambhu is very patient and good at breaking down concepts.

Great lecturer, super accessible for help outside of class. Class is tough but no textbook or work really outside of biweekly problem sets that are a bit tedious. graded on 1 prelim, problem sets, final robot competition.

14 years of Spanish and I never looked forward to class until Brisa. One time she gave everyone the option of writing an imaginative essay or a thesis essay. Everyone was going to write a thesis, then she gave an lecture on how to use your imagination. By the end, nearly everyone was going to write an imaginative one. I got an 100 on it. She's GOAT

Prof. Osborne looks out for her students and ensures that they actually learn instead of worrying about grades all the time. A lot of homework is given, but it helps you pick up the language quickly. Make sure to study for tests and practice for presentations.

Has a weird savior complex where she will drown you in work all semester only to magically give you a good grade at the end. No indication of curve before that. Additionally, OH are a waste of time where you may get insulted/berated for not knowing concepts. Avoid if you can.

Very clear explanations for difficult material. Only one (relatively optional) reading each day and two tests. No problem sets at all. Very helpful with questions even though there aren't always concrete answers.

This is an incredibly talented and intelligent professor. Her class is really, really fun and you will learn so much. It was such a pleasure for me to be in her class. Madame Watson, vous etes admirable.

Stanley is great at her job. It's a feat to teach this many people about wine in a way that's accessible and interesting. The lectures are long and definitely drag a bit after a glass of wine, but overall I'm glad I took the class. She tries to make it fun (not hard, it's a class where you drink wine), and I definitely learned a good amount.

Unfortunately I would have to say Prof. Vacanti was one of the worst lecturers I've had at Cornell. He didn't seem like he'd had much experience teaching and kind of just read off of his slides. I didn't think he presented the material in an accessible way. He was also quite rude when I tried to ask clarifying questions outside of lecture.
He is very well versed with his field and is super helpful!!!
He was an amazing professor. So kind and caring and inspiring.

She stirred conversation like no other professor, everyone loved her. Readings were very well curated and reasonable length. This was my first philosophy class. Go to her office hours; no matter how hard the text got, everything Karolina said was interesting and made sense. Perfectly dry humor and fair grader. Maybe my favorite class at Cornell.

In his first year teaching he was awesome.  Great guy.  Real funny in lecture at times.  Very good at explaining the basics.  Learned a lot.  Not much room for error though on tests as they are not out of many points.
This man decided to do his 80 minute exam with the class and it took him 75 minutes to finish. Let's remember that he wrote the exam and is a world renowned mathematician. Funny guy though.

The course content is interesting, but the course was not enjoyable due to the professor's teaching style. Her presentations were insensitive. She was unnecessarily strict about deadlines and missed exams. Assignments were unrelated to the course content. I'd recommend taking one of the many other good developmental courses offered by CoAS or CHE.
Enjoyed her portion of 2350 the most. Very clear lecture slides and teaching. If you do the readings and pay attention in class it is easy to do well.

I was in Desert Monasticism. Workload is fair. Weekly discussion posts and a capstone project. A lot of reading but discussions are led in an insightful way and Haines-Eitzen changed what we did each week (not boring). She is approachable and kind. Her own research is super interesting too. One of my favs from RELST.

Pass is the man. It takes a lecture or two to understand his accent but once you've got that you have one of the best professors at Cornell teaching a subject he's a master at. He's incredibly nice and great at explaining things.
Overall decent teacher! Very approachable and helpful. Could be a little clearer, but overall he's definitely a solid teacher. =)

While this professor's German accent can be a bit strong at times, Professor Stoye is clearly passionate about teaching, and this becomes especiallg evident as we learn more material applicable to the frontier of research. What I appreciated the most is his continual presentation of current/past papers that would tie course material very well.
Feel no respect & tough grading.
Professor Feathers is one of the most amazing professors I've ever had. He's genuinely concerned about his students' well-being and the material is very reasonable.

Great lecturer excited about the material that tries to make sure that all students learn in his class. It is an interesting and enjoyable class if you stay engaged, as he also definitely cares about participation. Class doesn't have too much homework but requires paper reading. I and other friends all had positive experiences
Interesting instructor, but unclear on assignment criteria. He was a harsh grader, and did not reply to all emails in a timely manner.

Each lecture was based on a short reading from the night before of relatively few pages and a few short readings in class + discussion. The class essentially boils down to a final paper and some reading assignments.
Loved the class, Professor Van Oyen loves her work and makes learning about ancient economies very interesting

I really enjoyed the topics. Lectures therefore were not boring for me, but you need to take a lot of notes during class. Exam questions often about small things he said in lecture, so take good notes. He is very nice and approachable. Need to study a lot though. Exams are fair and workload isn't too large, so overall great class and professor!

Seriously the best professor I have had in my 3 years at Cornell. The topics are great, he's very engaging, he makes you want to go to the class every week. The discussion attendance is mandatory, but also pretty interesting. Everyone should take this class. I'm taking two of his classes next semester. He's great.

Amparo is an inspirational and very nice lecturer. She is very caring for her students and shows equal respect for every student. I love how the lectures structured and the book we read in the class. Highly recommended!

Abruña clearly isn't all that interested in teaching at this point in his career. His lectures are dry, the course homework doesn't prepare you much for the exams, and the exams are brutal with targeted 50% medians. During the Chegg scandals, he did nothing to find cheaters and instead canceled the final exam. I'm so lucky I got to take it S/U.

Grandpa figure, very passionate but bias with his material. The TA (Jorge) completely re-did the tests for the course though. Interesting facts scattered into the material. Felt more like a history or gov't class than an actual sociology class. I'd say take it if you want to learn some interesting things. Good lecturer.

Great Instructor. Once you get past the accent you can really learn a lot from him. His lectures were very organized and he focused much more on the theory behind organic reations rather than simply listing them like in 358

Lmao these bozos below me probably didn't even show up to class. Professor Parmenter did a great job with the material and as long as you show up, participate, and do the work, you'll do fine. Would recommend.

Jon is too smart to be teaching this class. His brain runs at a billion gigahertz, so I feel like he's malfunctioning when he has to explain introductory statistics in INFO 1260. The way he speaks is so robotic it's hard to pay attention. I think he could build an AI model that teaches this class better than him.

McCobb is very intelligent, it makes interactions with him difficult, but once you realize this and begin to talk with him more, he is extremely helpful. Class was great, emphasis on the readings, participation is a must. Go to his office hours and get to know him, and you'll do great.

Excellent Professor, he's very kind and caring towards all students equally. Remebers small details about everyone. I learned a lot of concepts from his class, and the guest speakers he brought in were great, interesting cases and decent slides. Overall a great mentor and resource to have for the future, too.

Professor Parker is definitely enthusiastic about the material he teaches, however he looks for specific phrases / words and some of the things he tests you on is overlooked during the actual lecture. He makes the material more convoluted than it needs to be.
John McCarthy is a really nice guy with a lot of heart. He made exams open book and made review sheets with everything that was going to be covered on the exam listed. He deserves the world!

Rather than inspire his students to do better and aim higher, this professor will actually make you want to not care about the subject material and simply not bother. You will find that the reading is not at all worth your time, since the professor will mostly just ask you to read aloud in class, and he will not encourage you to do much thinking.
She was a pretty ok teacher I prefer the TA Alex Esposito better though. I took this over the summer pretty good class.
professor of finance, economics and engineering

He is a super nice guy, but the work we did in this class was time consuming and useless. Instead of actually designing for senior design we completed a bunch of awful homeworks. I dreaded this class every week. If the type of work he gave us is what being an engineer is like, I think I may get an MBA instead.

Best prof ever!!! This was my favorite class this entire semester, and she's so personable & well spoken! Yes the seminar once a week was long, but she baked us brownies & bought snacks every week :') The course was also extremely interesting and helped us imagine what a world would look like when technology gets more predominant. Take her class!

Hands down the most boring professor I've ever had. She just reads off of the slides. She doesn't appear to know the material very well as she is unable to answer many questions. STAY AWAY if possible. Worst class I've ever taken.

This is one amazing person. I cannot stress enough how egalitarian and open this Professor is. Knows her material, comes to class prepared and is always willing to answer questions, stay after class or attend study groups. Incredibly humble and quiet. Fantastic Instructor!

Brad is the professor I recommend to anyone needing to take an FWS. He is an amazing storyteller and seems to know so much about everything! He was pretty flexible with deadlines especially if you have a lot going on. He is always available for office hours and will work around your schedule, so take advantage of that. Your grade will thank you!!

It was a really hard class for me, but Professor Ramshaw was very helpful and accommodating through the entire semester. I scored around the median for every assignment and exam and still somehow ended with an A? I would strongly recommend this Professor though, super chill guy.

Dr. Brown's Comparative Nutrition class has been my favorite at Cornell so far.  The lectures are entertaining and informative and the labs are fun in the typical Animal Science way.  There is no textbook but rather handouts to read.  Tests are mostly take-home.

Everything that would be done in the class was dictated in the syllabus. Quizzes were exactly what was described they would be leading up to them. The class itself was extremely engaging with a lot of guest speakers. Attendance is not mandatory, but it is highly recommended not to lose pace.

Generally, I would only recommend to take this if you already had some tennis experience beforehand (at least JV ish level). Pretty chill guy, actually found out that he knew some of the kids who played tennis back home. Class is a little bit of a workout (definitely kept me in shape haha). Overall, definitely recommend.
Probably the greatest person I ever met

Professor D was great. The online part of the class was convenient and the lectures were very engaging. If you have any background in any physics this class will likely be way too intro level for you, but sometimes you're looking for that easy class. 5 tests felt like a lot but you end up dropping one. Material is a bit random at times but is easy.

This was the worst class I have taken here and I did not learn anything at all the entire year. The class was boring and the prof unhelpful. Also none of the assignments were clear and the readings were dumb.

One of the best lecturers at Cornell. The class requires discipline since the homeworks aren't mandatory, they're just prep for the tests. Our final grade was fully determined by 3 exams, but they were fairly easy. The questions were almost all extremely similar to the problem sets.

I absolutely loved this class. She was by far one of the most knowledgeable history professors I've had at any school. She explained things very thoroughly, and had the ability to add depth and complexity to subjects other teachers might gloss over or simplify. IMO, a class with her is a must for history majors at CSUCI.

Verdie is great and loves to hear student feedback and participation. She can be a little tough at first but as long as you take her comments it will be just fine. Not the most approachable but definitely always willing to help. She is definitely passionate about her work in order to get students engaged

One of the best GOVT professors at Cornell University. I personally think that her Political Institutions Under Autocrats class was difficult BUT it is one of the most interesting and useful classes I have taken at this school! I would gladly take another class with Prof. Rosenfeld.

Professor Rosensaft is an incredibly storied orator. All students ended up with some type of A grade (per the professor), and 70% of the grade is based on one 5 minute presentation at the end of the semester. The other 30% is "participation," though this is not really monitored or tracked. Would take this class again.
Truly the most amazing person
Incoherent.  That pretty much says it all.

Prof Hinestroza is honestly amazing! He genuinely cares about his students and their learning and appreciates hard work. Sure, there is a lot of work in this course, but he specifies time and again that if you're not up for the work, the course isn't meant for you. Learnt a lot of new, cool things in the class.

I've had two classes with her and she's wonderful! Can be tough at times but is seriously dedicated to helping you learn the material and is often quite funny. You obviously have to participate a lot since its a language class so I don't suggest taking it early in the morning or you'll be too tired to want to do the exercises
Very relaxed class on very interesting topics. Would definitely recommend taking his class for an interesting and relatively easy advanced chemical engineering elective course.

Really enjoyed professor gourdain's class, he made the class entertaining and while he sometimes wasn't perfectly clear you could tell he was passionate about energy and fusion. Not too difficult of a class based a lot on relatively simple transformations, though grading may seem tough at first the final project really helps out in the end.

He is one of the best professors I ever met in my life. His feedback on my research work is so valuable and helpful. Every time, when I start new research, I always get back to the comments this professor gave to me previously, as these comments really help me improve my research work.

Professor Volman is super nice and caring. If you need an extension, he will give it to you as long as you tell him a valid reason. He's really passionate about anthropology and really does care for his students. I would take any class with him again.

I had a great experience with Emi. Everyone in the class loved her as well. She genuinely wants us to succeed and become better language speakers, even if that means cold calling and pushing us to our limits to improve our language understanding, it pays off! Take her class if you want to learn Spanish.

Professor Nee is an ok guy and attempts to bring humor to his lectures, but ultimately his lecture style is extremely boring and unorganized.
