He changed the class structure and test structure twice and ultimately utilized extremely esoteric language without really explaining what he was trying to teach. I would avoid him if you have a better option

The class material in itself is very interesting but the 1.5 hour lectures of him standing up front and reading pages of information got boring.  He provides outlines for the lectures which are important but don't study last minute since the amount of material on the midterm is hefty.  No extensions on papers or assignments but knows his stuff

Cindy was a great professor, one of the best so far since being here at Cornell! She is willing to work with students if they have any issues, etc. Lectures can be engaging and brain breaks are the best! The TAs for the class were also extremely helpful and understanding. GO TO OFFICE HOURS! Cindy and the TAs are both amazing when you need help.

This class changed from the previous years. The grading is quite harsh if you don't put every detail or exact keywords. The nature of the exam doesn't allow time fro you to add to your answers from the study guide because you will run out of time. I was disappointed in how it was run this semester.
Such an understanding and engaging prof, workload is minimal and fun. Take this class :)

Super laid-back. Classes with him are really relaxed. He points out great writing advice and tips I never knew about. He's just not too into fantasy writing and head's up: not good with remembering names. I've taken two classes with him.

Professor Bassi is an expert in trade history and globalization. I studied with him when he taught in Spain. If you are interested in these areas, I recommend his class. He loves "flipping the classroom".

Great professor and great class. Professor Nelson is a genuinely kind man that enjoys his students and what he teaches. Meets with each student 1-on-1 at least once a semester. This is the easiest 4 credit class Cornell offers, and I would recommend to anyone. You will also probably enjoy it and do well if you manage to make the night lectures.

Stay away for your sanity. The only positive thing about him is he cares about Linguistics and puts a lot of work into his lectures. He shouldn't be a professor at Cornell he has no empathy for students who added the class late making their participation and homework grades lower. He will fully ignore and not respond to emails. He has a huge ego.

Aye was the most amazing and inspirational professor I ever had. Her material is very hard - I struggled immensely with her material, but at the end of it all, I felt rewarded with a wealth of knowledge that has forever impacted the way I understand chemistry and biology. I felt that I had learned, rather than studied, organic chemistry.

Professor van de Walle is a well organized and thoughtful lecturer who wants to make sure that his class is interesting for everyone. As of Spring 2023 he does not teach this course any more, but if you are interested in African Politics/Studies, then I would recommend you take some of his courses. He is very easy going, and a great professor!

I had the pleasure of being in Professor Buchon's class this semester, and I must say it was an absolutely enriching experience. I highly recommend their class for anyone interested in physiology, especially if you plan on taking the MCAT

EVERYONE shows up to class. Is this because its an honor's course with people who truly wish to learn? to some degree probably. However, his lectures are wildly helpful and informative - Brilliant guy who clearly knows his stuff, the enthusiasm will transfer on to you if you're open to it

He is a great teacher! His lectures are engaging, and you can tell he is really passionate about the subject. The exams are fair, and the exams are based solely on lectures! The class isn't curved, but there are a couple extra credit opportunities. I would def take a class with him again. If you work hard and study, you'll get the grade u want.

He is very hard to reach out. Did not respond to any of my emails and doesn't really take the class seriously. Always give yourself an A in the portfolio as he never bumps up the grade for getting it "right". Funny guy and gives decent feedback but it seems he does not care alot about the class. Ironically, not great at communicating.
Good class to take. Learn a lot of important skills regarding communication. You will enjoy this class and the professor.
She was very unhelpful and not nice when seeking advice from her as a student.
Grades harshly, but still is the GOAT

He is an awesome teacher and lecturer, not to mention an awesome person. It's kind of sad because students (myself included) feel awkward answering questions cause everyone's so spread out, but he still makes it an extremely interesting class. Nerd out with him after class; he will act as if he doesn't know 1,000 times more than you do, but he does

Such a nice guy, I learned a lot and he made me excited to go to lab. He really makes an effort to connect with his students and appreciates when his students show an interest in the subject. I will definitely take another of his courses in the future if possible.

Probability theory is not an easy topic and Raj does an excellent job teaching an introductory class while gauging interest in upper level proofs. Everything is proved in class but the test are mostly based on applied cases. Vert few professors can make you better at mathematics and that's why Raj was excellent because he made al of us better!
Good professor-- easy to do well if you go to class and occasionally office hours, but she was pretty rude and had a big ego. The TA's were cool though

Attendance policy was completely wack, no drops and will count against you unless you tell them way in advance (even if you become sick). The course was very difficult but worth it. Professor was sweet but also biased in her content. I enjoyed the content and the flow! Discussions were boring though

Ping was an amazing professor. Anyone who complained has never taken a rigorous math class at Cornell before. I believe that Ping gave me a strong foundation in the fundamentals of statistical theory.

This class is the most favorite and inspirational class I've ever taken. Carl is so chill, and he is also a genius with his mindset and lectures. Working in this course healed me a lot, and it helped me go through the first tough and struggling semester. I highly recommend taking this class.

Shalom is incredible, probably the best teacher I have had at Cornell. If you have any interest in learning Hebrew, I 100% recommend you take his class. He is extremely understanding and plans his lessons so well to ensure you really are learning. I looked forward to his class everyday, and you can tell he also enjoys every class.

Raguso is definitely passionate about what he teaches but his passion can be overwhelming since there's so much he wants to teach to students. At times his lectures are disorganized or jammed with too much information that leaves you scratching your head as you try to seep through the information. Overall, okay lecturer but brilliant biologist.

She is the best FWS teacher I have ever had. Although I never asked for one, she is willing to give extensions to anyone based on their circumstance. And she grades all of the work through completion, only if you are on the right track of the goal of the assignment.
cares a lot about her students. Fair grader but you have to put efforts. Probably a bit more workload than other FWS at Cornell, but it's worth it. Take her class, Darlene is great

Everything is through Aplia, so international students need a US VPN. Every assignment was time-consuming & nothing like the test. The textbook was useless. 70% of the grade was based on 2 tests. Multiple choice questions were ok, but the essay questions were time-consuming & tough. The comments he gave on them were vague, unrelated, & confusing.

Peeva is generally a good lecturer and is not only clear on the course material, but also on what we need to know for exams. Lectures could be a bit dry and I had trouble relating the topic material to outside topics, but all in all I learned a lot.

Anke Wessels is a very good professor! She comes up with great group discussions and the final project was executed in a very fun and engaging way! Sometimes it was hard to pay attention during class and sometimes she graded papers a little rough but overall she was super nice and the workload was manageable!

There were videos of class, so you didn't have to go, but lectures were definitely mandatory to watch in some way. The homework was unreasonably difficult given the percent each problem was worth. I'd say that for ever .01% of your grade, you had to put in around an half an hour of work. That's a LOT of work. Lectures are super boring too.
The course covers important materials in financial statistics, which is very useful for interviews. Professor Ruppert definitely knows a lot, but his lectures are quite dry.

The best, most enthusiastic, entertaining, lecturer; every lecture feels like it's story time. Its definitely hard if your'e not a huge history person but I still stuck around. Weekly assignments with several lengthy readings each, and a final project. Grading is fair but she really values when people to engage with her and lectures. No exams.

The class itself seems awesome in theory, but to me it turned out to be mostly useless and she will not teach you a lot. However, she is the nicest, most genuine person in the world and will go out of her way to get to know you and will even say hi to you when she sees you out of class. Extremely easy A as well, you don't have to do any readings

My favorite professor at Cornell. Inspiring, caring, and so so hilarious. Loves teaching, and loves his students. Classes are very hard and work-heavy, but it is so worth it and being in his presence inspires putting in the work. If you show up ready to be challenged and to learn, you will and you will do well in his class.

This was a good class and it is possible to get an A if you get good at the grammar (sort of hard grammar/tests and no curve but written tests on Canvas). I got B- on some tests and still got A-. 30-60 min homework. Monica is clear about her expectations, teaches well, and seems like a nice person. The class was interesting.

Professor Herter is a very kind-hearted man. Posts slides online a day before lecture. Gives two opportunities for extra credit (course eval. and presentation). Some lectures can get kind of dense but he makes sure to recap a bit each lecture.

Worst class ever. He is sneaky when it comes to justifying the material. This class is literally middle school level and so boring to sit through, even the lab TAs agree. His assignments are set up to confuse people and lower their grades to keep everyone from getting an A. DO NOT TAKE IT UNLESS YOU HAVE TO.

Andy is the best!! His lectures are so clear and he tries to make the class as engaging as possible (and we all know multi can be a bit boring). He is super kind in OH and always wants to help his students. If you ever get the chance to take a class with him: do it!

As if the book wasn't bad enough, his lectures were disorganized and unclear.  At office hours, unwilling to help unless HE thinks you have spent enough time on a problem, whether you've spent 5 hours or 5 days on it.  Arrogant, rambling, and unsympathetic.  Doesn't give a * whether you understand or not. His advice: 'Do the work.  Try harder.'

He made sure everyone in the class knew each other which was great, but grading criteria was vague and feedback/reasons for point deductions where not given. Grades were not posted until the end of the term making it difficult to improve writing. The class was interesting, but the policies made it difficult to enjoy.

Professor Bosteels is genuinely likable and has a clear passion for the material. For a FWS, the lectures are truly optional and the writing that you have to do is quite short.   You have to ask for help and such, but if you can't help yourself or reach out, you don't deserve to succeed anyways.   Comes across as a no-nonsense paternal figure

He is an absolutely amazing professor who not only knows the material, but is helpful and willing to answer questions. His lectures are funny, engaging, and if you are at all interested in the material, very interesting. He also makes it very easy to do well in his class if you attend class and put good effort into your papers.

Julie Carmalt is very kind! Grants extensions freely without you begging and she is very open to knowing her students. The flow of the course content started out strong, but it started getting confusing and disorganizing after the first topic. The projects had some unclear grading (talk to TAs), and tests were just annoying as well.

Distant and cold. Did not enjoy her as a professor. Hardly taught. Was unnecessarily difficult in accepting my final project and assigning a grade. Caused my degree to be withheld. I do not recommend.

Prof. Dubreuil is an amazingly well spoken teacher. He will grade you fairly so long as you attend class and show that you are interested. You don't have to participate that much, he tends to lecture on for the whole length of class.
Lectures were a bit boring but gives a nice overview of a lot of topics. Grading was very lenient, and you didn't really have to go to class to do well. Overall a pretty nice professor

Compassionate and brilliant lecturer; be sure to hit up office hours, wish I would have more often. 2218 was a bit rough around the edges because it was his first time teaching it, but for a pilot class he handled it fairly.

provides a lot of the lecture ppts which is helpful but makes attending lecture not really necessary. problem sets by far are the most important tool for doing well on exams. not too difficult to do well but not really sure how much you end up learning.....

Great professor and great class! You have to read a lot of case studies and write alot of case analyses, but they are all really interesting and during class w talking about our thoughts and ideas. Super lenient on deadlines as well.

Very helpful and generous to students.  He was extremely flexible with homework, and often spent entire classes going over it if the students had problems.  Class more useful than I initially anticipated, albeit very easy.  I recommend it if you need a math credit, but don't want to take something challenging.  John Mayberry's a cool dude.
The class is an absolute waste of time.
Kris is SO smart. He's also fun, laid back, and great at explaining new concepts. 10/10!

Highly recommended class. Mark's overview covers what you've learned in other classes in a different way. Some of his one day overviews of NTRES topics were actually better than the weeks long covering given by other professors. I don't know who called Mark "hot," but the class is great. Case studies are also great. No textbook. Pencil this one in.

Jim West is very kind and intelligent, but I fear that since he was been an expert in "Geometric topology and infinite-dimensional topology" for so long, he no longer really is able to teach undergraduate classes. That means he usually is unable to explain the material in a way that someone new to the material can really understand it.

Prof Rickard is very passionate about subject matter and always very engaging lectures. Somehow knows everyone's name by end of first week and encourages active participation. Omits certain slides and info from lectures posted online so attendance is mandatory to do well on exams (as well as daily pop quizzes). Lengthy homeworks once a month.
Didn't like it. Lecture material versus test material didn't match too well.
I found this prof to be unapproachable and smug.  Can't recommend.

Exams are graded harshly with no partial credit. Very strict grader and very condescending in Ed discussions. Always has to tell the class to be quiet and pay attention due to not being able to get the class active.
The professor makes a boring topic very interesting and engages everyone. Class isn't mandatory, but all of the tests are based off what he says in class.

He is a nice guy but will kill your GPA if you don't give him exactly what he wants. He will give you feedback on your paper and then give you a low grade because he will find other mistakes that he didn't find before. Avoid taking his class. If you don't have EXACTLY what he wants, expect a C or lower on your assignments.

Dr. Lau is knowledgeable in AA history. The textbook is free. The course is straightforward with one a quiz a week (only 5 questions). Final is you interviewing an Asian American over the age of 50 about a topic of your choice. Comes across too professional, not very warm or relatable. Weird class with good content. TONS of reading.

I liked writing before going to class and I grew a lot as a writer because of it. Excellent, honest feedback that was not harsh. She encouraged all kinds of writers, including students who lacked creativity. Assignments were fairly light and fair, and the class had a good pace.

He is extremely passionate and it shines through with the effort he puts into his classes. He gives very detailed & rigorous notes for his lectures; with 4060 this was incredibly helpful since it is a complex topic, however it was definitely a lot harder. Only professor I've ever had that reminded/validated us how mechE is hard & we're doing great
I agree with the previous reviewer. In my last semester at UC Merced that was my experience. I will be talking with Dean about this professor.

Selvaraj is very kind professor and always helpful in office hours. His lecture videos are extremely long and dense - it is very easy to get lost in them and I would have to set aside a good 2 to 3 hour time block just to get through. His exam questions are VERY similar to the in-class questions, HIGHLY recommend going to lecture for neurobio.

She was a great lecturer, she was very nice to her students and took into account when people made suggestions of things that might make it easier for us (i.e. posting slides before class). She posted all the lecture notes and opened a discussion section on Canvas for everyone to ask questions. She was awesome, wish I had her in more classes.

Professor Lawlor is the best professor I've had. She is passionate, enthusiastic and has a great sense of humor. Despite longer readings, as expected in history, her discussions in seminar are thought provoking and she's not afraid to follow the interests of students in what they read. I want to be an academic and she is no small reason why.
Professor Chekitan was absolutely great in bringing the class attention and engage the students with group projects and case studies.

DeVault is one of my favorite professors at ILR. Super knowledgeable, engaging and interesting lectures, very helpful during office hours, fair grader. If you do the readings you'll do well in the class and leave knowing so much about labor history. She also answers to emails quite well and writes good comments on essays so you can improve

Her lecture covers a wide range of topics regarding diversity. You're graded in participation (attendance is mandatory), 3 open note take-home quizzes, and 2 projects. If you're even slightly interested in Human Resources, she's an amazing professor.
Best history professor at nyush, he basically knows everything
Ok so everyone says its an easy class, but it does require a lot of work, so be really prepared for that. Treat it like another class not an easy one.

Tells fun and relevant stories in class. She is a good lecturer and teaches interestingcool lab topics, but oh my god don't get on her bad side. She will dislike students for really petty things sometimes, which sucks. Just try to come to all classeslabs and do things the way she wants them and she will be really nice and helpful

Riley is the best professor I have ever had. I loved his teaching style and he always went through the homework and quizzes before we had to take them. He explains things in a way that is so easy to understand and will always answer questions. He is quite funny as well and knows how to make the class atmosphere light. I'd take any class of his.

NS 4600 is awful. Pelletier spends most of the lectures speaking about random things; the homework is unclear; literally no one has any idea what is going on at any point; no feedback is provided; he makes up arbitrary rules , etc. For a class designed around planning, the lack of a plan in this class is truly terrifying.

Prof Rooks was one of the worst professors I've had. She made it pretty clear that she did not care about the class and rarely showed up. If she did come, she was hardly ever prepared. Some of the grading was really frustrating and we had pop quizzes that asked really specific questions that were just to see if we read, not if we understood it.
Excellent class, encourages students to explore some difficult works. Read and participate actively.
Not only is Professor Ajunwa a great lecturer, she also cares about her students and is easy to connect with beyond class.

I took Fish Physiology course and I felt the course really useful and fulfilling. Besides lectures and guest lectures, we have group project "build a fish" and one presentation to enhance our learning in an interesting way. Dr. Won's powerpoint is well-organized, but you should take notes. The average grade in the course is pretty high (A-).

Dexter was a class act. Despite his incompetencies and lack of class structure. His accusations of students' behavior were egregious. He constantly harassed me and almost ruined my entire semester. He was inaccessible outside of class and used lectures as a way to verbalize his personal frustrations. Moreover, he is biased toward women and Gen Z.

Just reads his own notes.
Really interesting course though!

Decent lecturer, though the material is pretty dry. Hard content, but no curve. Grade is based almost entirely on exams and a final project. No practice problems given which made it extremely difficult to study for exams.

Steve is a fantastic instructor. I have had two semesters of Chorale with him, and he's amazing. He teaches music theory and notation very clearly, and always strives to have everyone do their best. That being said, he will tell you when you are not performing up to par, but he does that to help you succeed. I love him!!

Newton is hostile to students. If you miss a class, good luck trying to get the material (his slides have max 15 words). Assigns an unreasonable amount of reading, and is suspicious of students that ask for help or come to office hours.

Very Very helpful with material and technical sculpture applications. Always sharing ideas, artists, and anecdotes. Bertoia pushed me to think about every step in my process and I am thankful for the tough love.

Senior here, I've taken a lot of neurobiology courses at Cornell and this is definitely one of the most interesting ones. Prof. Ophir is hilarious and probably the best professor I've had. The class isn't stressful at all because it's broken up into small assignments like quizzes that are graded fairly.
I liked the learning through application, I felt like I learned a lot better by doing assignments and a challenging, creative project than by preparing for exams in other classes

Sent a very condescending email about participation grading when it very clearly did not match up with what the syllabus stated. Pretty sure this is an academic violation. Also very ironic considering she would constantly show up to lectures late, one time even 20 minutes. Very dry and dull lecturer. Do yourself a favor and avoid her
One of the best teachers I've ever had. I would take this class 1023 times if I could!

Great professor overall. You do need to do the readings and outline every week if you want to do well on the final as he is a tough grader. The lectures are amazing so make sure you come well prepared and caffeinated so you don't miss anything!

Closed-minded, passive aggressive, dismissive, unhelpful, and condescending. Made me feel like all of my effort amounted to nothing. I did not find her approachable or willing to account for students' personal issues. Felt defeated, unheard and not respected. Wholeheartedly would not recommend.
Lectures are soooo boring. Exams, homework, and papers are relatively easy but everything is worth at least 10% of your grade so if you do badly on one thing you can drop a letter grade.
Labs require a decent amount of work that has likely gotten easier with improvements to R software. Winkler was a great prof and I'd gladly take another of his courses.
Great

Honestly one of the worst 'teachers' I've had here at Cornell; very self-righteous, arrogant, and unapologetic with his opinions and never seeks to understand his students' point of view. I do not recommend taking ANY classes with him, unless you want to be continuously discouraged the entire semester

This man is my inspiration my shining light my idol my everything. He's secretly a genius but so humble. I was honestly skeptical about taking a class on water treatment, but he makes it so wholesome and fun. I can now see myself pursuing a career in water because of him.
Prof Nair really cares about her students and goes out of her way to make a difficult class something that everyone can do well in. Truly loved her course and teaching style. Engaging and caring.

He is the best professor I have met so far! His lecture is very organized with clear notes. He really cares about students as he remembers each student's name and he even grades the exam paper himself. (The class is around 80-90 people) The homework questions are very helpful for a deeper understanding of the topics. Definitely take his class!

Is condescending when students ask questions. When they don't understand after his unhelpful answer, he doesn't care and doesn't bother to help any further. Reads off the slides so his lectures feel pointless.

I had Dr. Kim for autotutorial physics. It's a LOT of work. This class should be 5 credits. Nonstop notes, practice problems, & tests every week. If you're not good with time management, I don't recommend this class. Test questions are confusing & worded weirdly. The labs take hours & have no reason to be as such. He is nice if you reach out though

She explains things very well, and often gave valuable insights, especially when contextualizing things we learnt in class. She left very good comments on the homework sets and was willing to fill any holes that the professor left during lectures. Her discussions were well organized, fun and balanced. The class was much more complete thanks to her.

Despite my poor performance (compared to the median grade), This was by far my favorite and most difficult class. The lectures are amazing, but be prepared to be assessed at every discussion on your in-class performance. I believe my grade reflects my proficiency in Japanese, and I look forward to 5 more semesters of Japanese!

I signed up for Prof Robbins's Shakespeare FWS to impress my significant other, and I left with my own appreciation for the surprising relevance of WIlliam's work (he parses WS like a surgeon). Agreed. Robbins places a huge emphasis on individual growth - essays become personal explorations. There are few profs more patient and caring.

Professor Rickard tends to go on lecture tangents that are unrelated to class and not particularly interesting. All presented ideas seem partially unfinished or unclear. The prompts for papers and vague and the grading criteria similarly vague.
No Comments
Chemistry was hard, but an amazing lecturer, super nice, funny, and wrote fair exams.

Lecture was so boring and actually impossible to follow. First 1/4 of the class was really easy but then had to teach myself everything and was basically guessing. Weekly quizzes/problem sets. Section with TAs was only saving grace.

It's her first year teaching, and that hurts her ability. She goes over a lot of info in class that is presented in a very dull way. She improved from when I took her for 2101, which is good. She is also a much better teacher one-on-one but struggles to present complex topics in an engaging way to a large class. Most stressful class I've ever taken
Lectures were a little disorganized, but Prof. Hess is a super nice guy and is always willing to meet with students outside of class to help.

Best class at Cornell. Material is incredibly well organized- handouts he used are like a bible, absolutely necessary for his course and incredibly helpful for other physiology courses. He genuinely wants you to learn and love the material, his enthusiasm is contagious and you can't help but be amazed by the content of lecture every day.
He is a typical Wall Street type of guy - everything is about money!
Great TA for discussions. Open to offering help and is not a tough grader. Is definitely there to make sure the students succeed.
He's a great lecturer and the readings are very interesting. He truly cares about the content.
Great man!

Miller is the most intelligent and knowledgeable professor I've encountered at Cornell (he knows not only philosophy but also economics, history, law, and a lot more). He frequently had fascinating insights to offer. Lectures are focused, extraordinarily eloquent, and easy to follow.

Would give us long readings to do each week that we would have to do a powerpoint presentation on it in class. Was basically what lecture was, us reading off our slides, and then him going back through the slides and giving his take. Only thing you learn is from your own reading. 1 graded paper at the end, but 20 pages, which is ridiculous.

Slava is an incredibly caring and passionate teacher. He made my first semester of learning Russian incredibly enjoyable, and was lenient in letting me make up work due to extensive sickness. Slava took creative liberty in constructing funny scenarios for us to work off of in terms of dialogues, so class was engaging. He is big on grammar rules.

I enjoyed being in Dr. Pirozhenko's writing seminar. The grading system was clearly outlined in a labor contract at the beginning of the semester. She gives constructive feedback on essays and offers in-person conferences throughout the semester.

Clearly a knowledgeable prof who constantly pointed out various interesting themes in the readings, which were truly fascinating. You knew that he was truly reading our assignments closely as he gave detailed comments on all our answers. On the minus side, no clear grading system was provided, and his thick accent is sometimes hard to understand.

Lectures are pretty cringe-worthy. Bad jokes and excessive cold calling make it painful to sit through. Three problem sets are due each week which get repetitive. His homework and practice problems (even exams) are riddled with errors which makes it hard to prepare for exams. Exam questions are always random and the multiple choice is so hard.

TAMU not Cornell. All I have to say is good luck. Get ready to cry a lot. He taught an Intro to OChem class like its Biochem 2. Final is 50% of the grade. Do ANYTHING to avoid his class. Get ready to learn from drawings of structures with little to no explanation. The first week he makes it sound like the class is going to be easy, but its TERRIBLE

Professor Falk is the worst professor I've had at Cornell. He enjoys bullying students in discussions and is also immensely passive aggressive. He refuses to answer questions and doesn't know what it means to give constructive criticism. On top of this, he is a harsh grader. If you value your time and want to be respected DO NOT take his classes.
Professor Turgeon is a nice professor; lectures are long and stale. Tests lean heavily into random points from the lecture but study from posted PowerPoint, and you're good.
Don't, just don't
He is so amazing!!!! I was ready to give up bio but he made me fall back in love. SO GOOD AT EXPLAINING CONCEPTS. He should teach more classes.
Super knowledgeable and personable professor. Helped me to read and write better, especially within the field of planning, but could have provided more foundation on the final project.
She was able to convince the prof to give us more time on our term papers since we all felt like not enough time was given.

Professor Yu is nice, but his class is terribly organized. Homework assignments are graded on a genuinely subjective scale consisting of a "wow factor" that is entirely dependent on how generous a TA is feeling. You may therefore never receive a 100 for completing an assignment correctly. Core concepts are hand-wavey and unfounded in data or logic.

One of the best Professors I've had in the Physics department. He truly cares if the students are successful, and is willing to go over concepts and exams one-on-one with students. Also, lectures are a bit hand-wavey, but he is willing to explain the bigger picture/give resources for deeper understanding to those who ask. Overall, a great Professor
She is a wonderful instructor. I loved her freshman writing seminar
GOOD PROF

This was by far one of my favorite classes that I have taken at Cornell. I took it my freshman year, spring 2022 and I learned so much from the class. There were no tests, two debates (which you just had to show up to and you got full credit for being there), weekly psets, and a final project. I learned a lot from this class with no stress.

The sweetest professor I've ever had. Her French accent is beautiful and she's so enthusiastic about the class! The lectures are a bit repetitive, but basically you know if you see information multiple times it's important. There are very few homeworks and the prelims are easy. 10/10 would recommend taking Biomaterials for the Skeletal System!

There are 6 final essays in this FWS. Each essay has one draft. There is basically one piece of writing due each week (she assigns them Thursdays and they are due on Sunday). Essays are short (1-2 pages, double spaced).

She edits them, but if you write a bad draft, she will deduct points off the final essay. Attendance is 10% of the grade.

Sin duda, Prof. Troconis is the BEST professor I've had at Cornell. Besides being an unquestionably brilliant intellectual, every class she teaches is filled with energy, enthusiasm, and humor. She truly cares about her students' well-being and is flexible and adaptable to their needs. If you don't take her class, you're missing out!

Interesting class material and professor has ample relevant experience on social campaigns. He is a great guy - kind, understanding, energetic, etc. The class is based around a major group project campaign, which can be hit or miss based on the client.
caring, nice

Class started a little tense but he eased up as time went on and the course was relatively enjoyable because it didn't require too much work and tests had the right amount of challenge imo. I wouldn't say lectures were terribly interesting, more of dump from his notes. Overall ok class and chill professor while a good lead into to math stats.

Nice guy, but runs through lecture slides far too fast. Speaks too quickly to write your own notes but hands out power point slides. The slides aren't clear and the information is incomplete on the slides. Don't take his classes.

I'm not an usual reviewer since I've only been watching his lecture online on Youtube. I'm not a student so I have no expectations about grades. What I can say is simply that I very much like his approach and style of teaching. He helps having a good understanding of the inner mechanisms of plans before learning chemistry formulas and such.
worst professor at cornell

He is god awful at explaining concepts remotely harder than the basics of physics. Never shows his work on how an equation was derived (e.g glosses over/ skips steps for a derivation but will spend 5 minutes explaining P up is V down in PV=nRT), uses unconventional signs, and reads off a script when recording his lecture videos. Overall useless.

Don't take this class if you are trying to learn anything. Prof was nice but the content was terrible, painfully simple and in some cases not even updated with modern widely accepted research. Memorizing will get you in the A range easily.

Pretty good lecturer, knew what he was doing and always tried to relate the material back to real-world applications. As someone who took more econ courses before this the class felt a little too easy/slow at times, but I still enjoyed it. Easy hws, no tests, just a group project that Rudik gives a lot of freedom for. Good early elective for econ
Not knowing what he is doing.

I love this prof, the topics were so interesting and we read/watching things about love so most of our assignments were a response to them. The final project was ab a topic of your choosing, it was so helpful to have meetings with her ab the essays and she's super understanding. My new fws doesn't compare-also the community in the class was on top

Super flexible, friendly, and accommodating as classes are small. We even get to pick the topics we learn about for the semester. Decent amount of work, especially since there's a 6 page final paper, but it's nothing compared to Spanish or the East Asian languages. Gives great feedback as well and cares about the well-being of her students.
Easy summer class, the only annoying thing is that the class is not asynchronous. Revelins himself is pretty chill and the quizzes can be tough if you aren't paying attention and taking notes.
Worse class and Professor. Never care about well student learn.

international monetary theory and policy- Has a great, sort of understated sense of humor. Can come off as a bit aloof and intimidating but actually cares about his students quite a bit. Tests are pretty tough, manages to create some difficult questions from pretty straightforward material. Lots and lots of graphs.

Lot of information to process. Fairly interesting lecture with movies. Very few tests but stuff from the tests are mentioned in a random few of the many readings. Pretty picky when he grades his long papers but you have plenty of time to do them. You can skip class and forgo the grade bump but make sure to not get lost.
Shrokrieh sometimes made me feel stupid for asking questions, and I didn't feel like he cared very much about students who were struggling. That being said, he was a great lecturer.

Professor Patel is my favorite Government professor at Cornell! I took his seminar (International Politics of the Middle East) and I am currently taking Issues Behind the News this semester. He is a fantastic professor with lots of knowledge of the region. He treats students as equals and has no problem with providing background knowledge.

Professor inaccessible outside of class. Never responds to emails. Final project not reflective of content covered during course. Very disappointed. Please note that this course features heavy coding assignments, which was not on the syllabus.

Prof. Woubshet is a really nice, good teacher whose earnest passion for the material is palpable. And the books are  quite good. My only criticisms is that he agrees with everything students say, so it stifles discussion. He also had us read some LGBTQ material in an African-American lit class and he didn't connect the two. I recommend him overall!
Worth attending his lectures

I took NS 1400 with Professor Hoddinott and his lectures were mostly interesting, sometimes boring; he taught the economic portion of the class and the exam questions were pretty lecture heavy; overall, its a good class and he's a good professor
Silvia does not respect students or this job, just teaches the course as a job, not motivates students to learn at all. Will never take her course again.

Silvia is honestly a great professor, but her class is really hard. I had to drop it because it was taking too much time and was going to lower my GPA. Lots of work and a really tough grader. She cares about your learning deeply and wants to see you improve your Spanish. I am retaking with a different prof.
One of the nicest people you'll meet. Knows the stuff really well and is hugely committed to ensuring that students understand the material. Clear, excellent lecturer. The class is easy, too.

John is one of the best professors I've had at Cornell. He has literally sent me on a brand new trajectory in my career. He has a really interesting perspective as a regional scientist, and one that isn't explored anywhere else in the department. He wants you to succeed, and is willing to give second chances. I've never learned so much.
Such a queen I love her. Class can be somewhat stressful cuz you have to participate a bunch but generally very doable and all the tests and formal presentations are online

I took Ottoman Empire 1800-1922 with Minawi. Really cool guy, passionate, inspired speaker who enjoys making his students think, ask questions, and engage. His classes are very reading-heavy and attendance is mandatory. His lectures are more a formality and discussion forum tbh. Exams are extremely reading materials-based. No papers but book report

Quincy has to be the worst professor I've ever had. He would often get confused by his own questions, struggle to explain basic concepts, and nitpick every HW calculation to his exact standards. Be prepared to teach yourself. He is a horrible professor, and a rude person who tries to make you feel dumb instead of helping you during class.

I took this class as a freshman, and absolutely loved it. Prof. Casasola was incredibly helpful, detailed, and really pushed her students to succeed. The bulk of your grade comes from two research projects - you'll learn a lot about academic writing, APA formatting, and the scientific method. Highly recommend!

One of the better professors I have had here at F&M. Relatively easy grader, but forces you to seriously engage with the material and to participate in class.  If you do that, you will thrive in her class.
Prof. Haghtalab did a great job of lecturing 170. She made everything super clear and had a great lecture style. Would highly recommend.
Material doesn't make sense and complicates assignments..
Shilo helped me learn a lot, and she's a great professor!

Professor Robson is the absolute best! Her lectures are so informational and she is just amazing in her field. She made the grading criteria clear and I never got confused in this class. Although lectures are not mandatory, it is highly recommended that you don't miss them. They are fast and cover lots of information not showing in the slideshow.

Worst professor ever. Just be nice to yourself and don't take his class if there is an alternative to it. He is NOT open to new ideas and if the work you made interferes with what he "likes", his comments during critique will be very mean and judgmental.

An excellent professor! He can be a little quirky sometimes, but overall very caring and chill. Grades come easy as long as you go to the lectures (which notes are available online, so not a note-heavy class). Assignments can be time-consuming but were graded mercifully, and there are ways to get half of the lost points back.

She is extremely nice and is great at explaining concepts and answering questions well! She is also very understanding when it comes to assignments, attendance, etc. She always makes sure students understand concepts. Go to her office hours!
Mike is a tremendous leader in the dairy industry. Actually being able to know and work with him in classes is truly amazing.

Really good at making you a better writer, it turns out instructors like him are actually pretty rare. Gives a bit more reading than most FWS's, but it's still hardly anything and usually pretty interesting. Always helpful if you approach him after class and respects your opinions.
This professor was terrible: No grades until the last day, zero instruction, and then horrendous grading system where the median grade was a C
the GOAT language professor, and genuinely one of the best teachers I've ever had. Probably forever influenced my view of Italian
Quizzes were very specific on material and lectures were not very engaging at times. The discussion sections were interesting in the class because we got to talk with other students.

Weekly 9:05am Friday lectures and is an easy A+ if you do not forget to turn in more than 2 assignments (like I did). Will not curve if you're on the edge of a higher letter grade. Easy assignments and not harshly graded by any means.
She's a great resource for ansci majors and wants the best for the department's students.

She is very knowledgeable of the material but teaching style wasn't for me. Most of my good grades in the class were from material I learned in AP calculus ab. If I had no prior calculus understanding I would have been screwed.

This professor has no interest in assisting you develop your own vision for the project. If you don't do exactly what he wants (which is quite unclear anyway), he actually takes it out on you in a personal way. Further, all desk crits and reviews will be a means for punishing you for trying to work he does not approve of. Unprofessional.

Dr. Carrol is fantastic! funny and brilliant; he really makes the students think about the real-world applications of econ. but he does speak quickly. even though the text is rarely used, it helps to read through the material if you're having trouble. Go to TA office hours. the tests are hard with random questions mentioned maybe once in class.

Prof Gaskins has changed the way I think and view our world today. Furthermore, he has also changed the way I write, for the better. One of the best professors here at Cornell. Take his class, it is seriously life changing.

Interesting class on sustainable business. Bill tends to go off on tangents, but he has a super interesting background and is able to provide insights regarding his own research to topics brought up in class. Super easy A, take home midterm, and final presentation is simple and he allows you to choose your own groups. Take this class. Easy A
she is awesome, the way how she is teaching and makes you remember history is very easy. quizzes are not hard. I learned a lot and understand American history without stress.
He's a king and a bright man all around. keep on rockin Dr. C!

Amazing class and professor. You learn a LOT about the world, and the topics are interesting. The class requires a specific style of writing (different from other classes) that you will learn to meet/adapt to over time, but it definitely takes a bit. The undergrad TA's are also incredibly friendly and always accessible.

Gives great writing feedback which I really appreciated. Be careful, I thought this was a blow-off class but it's not. 10 500-word reading responses, 2 5-page papers, a take-home prelim, and a 10-12 page final paper. It's definitely manageable, but more than I was expecting. Paulo is a great professor and tries to make class engaging. He's chill.
terrible
She makes the class stress free but I will choose another more interesting writing seminar.

Professor Duffy is a true gem. It looks like a lot of work, but she's flexible and caring. She recognizes when her students are struggling with school and life in general. She's also a great lecturer and gives many opportunities to earn points through different things (essays, tests, discussions).
Jaeun was great as an FWS teacher. Papers are graded on completion/ensuring all essay components are there. If you don't read and pay attention during lectures, you'll get an A on your essays.
I agree with the other low ratings. Switch to another prof for business writing if you can.
It's so so. Sometimes it's hard to follow because he usually does proofs.

She talks ALL THE TIME, and wouldn't let anyone else put in a word edgewise. She can also be really sarcastically sweet with the students she doesn't like. If given a choice, I would not take another course with her.
Best class ever at Cornell. Too bad he is leaving Cornell for Michigan. It's a big loss for Cornell.
Terrible teacher. Flipped class. All you need to do is copy what she does in the videos and you don't have to learn anything about statistics. She doesn't make her grading guidelines clear either.

Qi Li is the goat! There are quite a few homeworks but they are reasonable in length and are done in groups. Exams were fair and she would always provide a comprehensive review beforehand. Qi Li cares a lot about her students and her office hours were always helpful.
The class is flipped style and the assignments are reasonably done in class. The exams can be difficult but Professor Slaugh added some opportunities for re-weighting which helped grades a lot.

Best professor ever!! Really fun, makes class interesting, tells good stories, and the time flies by. I looked forward to going to lecture. An enthusiastic lecturer and funny guy. After taking this class, I'm on track to be an Architecture minor.

LOTS OF MATERIAL!!!!!! Exams are super long and meticulous, so you really need to know your stuff. Levistky is an amazing professor, and knows how to make the material more interesting. Would recommend taking very detailed notes!! Do not take for an easy A, but otherwise a fascinating class.
Definitely the best professor I had at Cornell - his lectures are amazing, makes the concepts very easy to understand. Super nice person as well!

Horrible lecturer. Was little to no help in office hours. Microbio was taught along with Professor Winans, who was MUCH better. Zinder couldn't explain the material and made exam questions extremely specific. Take this class with another prof

Horrible handwriting. Quite disorganized. Writes chunks of the sporadic rambles on the chalkboard. Patronizing. TA's are very helpful and down to earth. I've taken 2 philosophy classes at Cornell and both professors have been so unorganized and constantly go on tangents...it can be frustrating at times. Thank God for TA's

Munther is so brilliant and kind, highly recommend taking Arabic with him! Be prepared to learn and do the homework every night - if you miss more than a day or two, it will start building up. He's happy to help and has really interesting stories if you can get him to talk about his own life. Definitely not a class to slack off in but so worth it.
NICE CURVE. Although you might not do well on your exams, so does everyone and you end up with either an A or B. Got A-. Knows how to teach.
Very interesting lectures. Great experience of take-home and oral exams.
Good professor: consistent, caring, full of information. Agree that he helps to clarify your thinking and improve writing. Solid professor.
PHIL 181: Philosophy and the Science of Human Nature

Professor: I am watching these videos online and reading the material.  She makes it fun and easy to understand.

Review_Example: Pretty good professor. She says things like "come visit me in office hours, I'd love to meet all of you", but then she doesn't ever seem particularly friendly or interested. Overall though it's a fun class, the textbook is good, and prof Kushnir is solid.

Good lecturer. Class content is super interesting, only time I've ever been disappointed when a class gets cancelled. Exam and final paper are easy. Discussions were on some level a waste of time, but I will admit it was really cool how the results figured into lectures. Rare class that is both an easy A and academically valuable.
Awesome awesome awesome! Best professor at Cornell!

class is generally just prof waite talking for an hour straight with some interjections for questions. the class is ridiculously easy for an fws, and homework is entirely self directed based on what you want to write. as long as you write something, you will get a good grade and good feedback.

No office hour, had to schedule appointment. The appointment was unhappy: the meeting was supposed to be 3pm but he had a previous meeting that didn't finished. I waited for 1 HOUR and then was allowed to come in. Professor Fisher told the student next to me: I will finish this meeting within 10min. I didn't see him respect me as a student.
Sometimes his explanations for things are long or ambiguous. Emphasis on sometimes; other times, answers to questions or feedback are straightforward and helpful.
WORST CLASS EVER.

Prof. Weatherspoon is a brilliant scientist and a kind professor. He is, however, not very good at explaining concepts. So confusing and keeps talking in circles. I stopped attending lectures after the first week because I learned more from reading the slides on my own. There is a lot of content covered in this course but exams are straightforward.
Amazing Arabic teacher

Kadhim is an incredible instructor. As other reviewers have mentioned, he genuinely cares about his students and works hard to create a friendly classroom environment. His Arabic class was the best hour of my day, because he strongly encourages students to work together outside class. Also, he has a great sense of humor.
A great professor.  She really knows what she's talking about and makes it interesting.  Got behind in lectures, but she took stuff off the final to make up for it.

Jeff is a great professor, who makes the class exciting. He utilizes acting games and vocal exercises to improve beginner actors. Take his class if you want to have fun, have minimal homework/tests, and play improv games. Don't take this class, if you have a lot of experience and expect to become a stronger actor; this class is for beginners.

This is my second class with Prof. Bronfenbrenner and I have to say that I'm ride or die now. She was increadibly patient and caring with me and other students. She knows what she's talking about more than nearly every other faculty member at ILR. Highly recommend.

Professor Kate is amazing! She takes the time to engage with each student and is very accommodating. I have tremendously improved my writing skills because of her and would highly recommend to take her class.
pretty straightforward class and professor. do readings, understand readings, tested on readings. grading was a little vague but whatever
Ugh

I generally wouldn't recommend this class because the professor seems indifferent to their students and unnecessarily complicates the material. Strategy is typically a straightforward subject, but it was made more challenging by the unreasonable exams and unclear grading system. The professor enjoys ridiculing student's final presentations.

He's an amazing lecturer. Easily approachable outside class to continue class discussions. Gives good feedback on class work and genuinely cares about his students. A very understanding lecturer. Emphasizes on learning more than grades.
Clear grading, prelim questions directly from assignments and lecture slides.

Started out boring (I mean it is econometrics), but then he got into the  application of the statistics and was an amazing professor. You can pass without going to the lectures, but I highly recommend going to them because they help a lot and he makes the subject a lot more interesting!

Prof Debbie is a very caring professor and is very organized in her material and assignments. The exams weren't bad if you stayed on top of readings & lectures, and were mainly synthesis questions so you had to really understand the material. This semester she allowed exams to be open note which was incredibly helpful. Readings took alot of time.
One of the best course that I took at Cornell. Great way to learn how to design and build technology for underserved communities in different parts of the world. Strongly recommended.
Professor Manthri is an exceptional teacher, truly one of a kind. I highly recommend this course: life-altering meowing experience guaranteed.

Alisa is one of my favorite Spanish teacher. Her enthusiasm for the language is infectious, and her teaching style is helpful to get better at the language. Rather than getting hung up on the details, she wants students to get the language. Great sense of humor. Grades very fairly (and quickly!) and work assigned is reasonable for a language class.
Cool dude

Dawson REALLY CARES and goes out of his way to make the material more UNDERSTANDABLE if you show INTEREST. An EXTREMELY DRY lecturer, but posts all USEFUL notes online. 4140 will mostly teach data analysis & solid mech theory used in ACTUAL EXPSIM. REALLY COOL projects, but VERY time consuming. Often gives extensions. No exams in this class.

Very hard class. First paper had a median of around a 25%. If you want to do well on the two papers, you basically have to spend two straight weeks in the library before you submit them. If you don't want to do that, and you care a lot about your grades, try to avoid this class. Curve saves you; upper 80s turns into A+.

Mike is a good person, but as a professor, I disliked him. He yaps all class, is disorganized, won't post slides in advance, a tough grader, threatens to take off more points with regrades (so that people won't ask), is inconsiderate (made an exam and exam review on an important, well-known holiday), does not respond to emails, and grades slowly.

I went out of my way to take a second class with Professor Hutchinson. He helped me elevate my poetry and was more than willing to meet one on one with anyone to discuss his comments in greater detail. He wants to see his students succeed. I asked him to be my advisor because of this dedication he has to his students.

I'm a bit surprised that all the other reviews are so negative. She maybe wasn't the best professor I've ever had, but definitely not terrible. She seems to know a lot about the content, she just comes off a bit monotomous and unenthusiastic. The tests are really pretty easy, everything is from the slides. I actually kinda enjoyed the class

Compared to other language courses, this is definitely one of the best options. He is EXTREMELY disorganized which makes the course much more stressful, but he ultimately gives everyone a good grade (A- or above), unless you are really bad then maybe he'll give you a B+. Much less work than other language classes. He acts a lot and is fun also!

Gerald Dugan is my hero. He really cares about his students and frequently stops during lecture to make sure everyone understands. He's quirky and charming and he makes the cutest little self-deprecating jokes in class. He's the man.

Daniel is the man. Super chill, great sense of humor and informal style of teaching. Doesn't make you feel too pressured when you're talking, which is good because talking in class is definitely emphasized. Have to study on your own a lot for the tests but he makes class as fun as it could be.
Such a great prof. Would take another class with him in a second.

Oceanography is a good class. You learn a lot from it, and the content isn't that difficult if you study, like everyone at Cornell will tell you since this class is famous around campus. Other classes I'm currently taking are much more difficult than this was. This TA showed she cared for students and was always willing to answer any questions.

I really enjoyed this course with professor Bitar and it made me interested in pursuing the American Studies minor. The class had a decent amount of readings that were all very accessible and posted on Canvas in a reasonable time frame. Additionally, the TA Patrick was also great and he was very educated and knowledgeable about the course. Easy A!

She's a great professor! The grading for homework assignments and exams is very fair. Most of the assignments were done in groups; lecture attendence is vital. The textbook is needed for the assignments, but not so much for learning the content.
Fine

I had Sally for Intro to Creative writing and I learned more from my high school classes than I learned in this one. I was very unimpressed and she doesn't know how to facilitate a discussion properly.  One-on-one, her comments are helpful, but in class, no one learned anything.  Overall, the CLASS has good reading requirements and is fairly easy.

Following his lecture is like watching thriller movie. You think you know something, but it turns out that you don't. He teaches us a theory (or model) and its strengths very clearly, and in another session, he will open up the theory's weaknesses, and ask us to be wise in using it. A highly recommended course.

Professor Nishikino is an occasional teacher for JAPAN1101 and 1102. She is very kind with students and often gives you hints. Larson and Ichikawa sensei are the main professors, but the rundown: daily grades based on participation and accuracy, two lectures a week, occasional quiz/test.

Professor Dietrich makes an effort to know your name and care about you as a student. The homework's kinda suck and are graded harshly, but the professor and TA's are super accessible for office hours. The tests are very straightforward and she won't throw surprises at you. If you put in the effort, you can easily do very well in this class.

Brenda is SUPER sweet. She is easy to understand which is insane because she's speaking in a different language. She's probably the best ASL 1101 prof to have because she is the person who signs for the tests, so you'll be used to her style and speed while other students are not, even though other profs grade easier than her, go with Brenda!

Taught by case method. Readings (articles and cases) and prep are important. Material is interesting and class discussions/lectures are often brought beyond theory by interesting real world experience of professor. Great guest speaker

I really liked Professor Golestaneh. She has an interesting background and anecdotes. She is kind, caring, and has reasonable expectations. Her lectures are interesting and seeking her out outside of class, she can give you interesting perspectives on current events. I would like to take more classes with her.

She is clearly very passionate, however not the most interesting professor I've had. The subject material is usually really interesting though, including up-to-date assignments that require actual creative thought to try to pose solutions to modern day policy problems. Overall I'd say the class and material was more interesting than the lectures.

Lectures were all over the place, he YAPPS about irrelevant information 80% of the class. We had 6-7 different books to read almost at random across the semester. Papers are extremely long for an intro class, and even longer readings. Extremely boring lectures, only survived because of my TA! (They are 100% responsible for all the grading).

Sayama Yu Yu is fantastic. She has instilled in our tiny class a genuine love for the Burmese language. The class uses worksheets rather than an expensive textbook and moves at a manageable pace, so long as you dont slack off.

I loved CEE 5420 with Gadikota. The team projects and in-class interactions were amazing. She is very accessible outside class and cared deeply about my progress. She brings a unique perspective to decarbonization, sustainable energy and resource conversions. I feel well prepared to tackle these topics outside the classroom.

Soloway is a very caring professor, and readily easily accessible outside of class. He was always willing to help and discuss course content material. His lectures though were very dense and confusing at times, which led to frustration sometimes. However, he tried to break the content down through analogies and examples which was always helpful.
great
Irrelevant to todays musicology.

The lectures were difficult to understand, irrelevant, and quite useless. The class itself was very unorganized. Homeworks were graded very harshly, Office hours weren't that helpful either. The exams were do or die and you get a single problem even slightly wrong, your grade goes for a toss.
Really enjoyed his class - definitely a great teacher.

Took in Fall 2020. Best econ class I took at Cornell. Best mix of interesting content, fair hws and tests, and great teaching. Class is more "mathematical/theoretical", but Ted is fantastic at explaining it all. He's also very approachable and caring, and I appreciated that he put in extra effort to deal with the circumstances at the time. GOAT.

Most COMM classes give A-range grades if you work hard, at least by far for all the classes I have taken in last 2 years all ended up with solid A's EXCEPT FOR THIS ONE. There's one prelim and a final (60% of the grade), and the class average for the first prelim was a C+, kinda ridiculous for an intro-level COMM concentration class

A very lovable professor that is personable, has quirky style, and a straight-forward lecture. Her powerpoints are usually included on blackboard so that is helpful. Review the slides, readings and handouts exclusively to do well.
She is an amazing teacher who helped me with my writing skills and really cares about her students. I loved this class.
There were no assignments outside of class, the only grade was at the end of the semester and it was a group project. Barely any work, but very boring.

I chose to take this class after attending the first lecture. Professor Legunsen was engaging right from the start. I struggle to focus through lectures but he found the perfect balance of in class activities and asking questions to keep me engaged. Fantastic professor who clearly puts the effort in and cares about his students.
Super nice professor, class was graded by 3 homeworks and 1 group project that are all covered thoroughly by class lectures.
Excellent class. Prof rocks.

Professor Giles may seem clueless, but if you actually get to know him and ask him for help in office hours or one on one settings, you'll find that he really knows what is going on and he knows his stuff.

Christopherson is an excellent instructor. She's incredibly knowledgeable and engaging, and stimulates very interesting conversations and debates. I went into this class knowing nothing about economic development (and not being all that interested) and left with a thorough understanding of economic development issues and a desire to keep learning.

This was a very tough class - definitely take an intro programming course first. The lectures moved super fast and were confusing, but Giles was super helpful in office hours and on Piazza. The problem sets took a long time but weren't too difficult once you understood all the directions. Past knowledge of every math course is expected- be prepared

Finest professor at cornell.  Had him for civil pro, evidence and trial tactics.  Went that extra mile for me and concepts from him are still with me after 30 years of practicing law.  He and Clermont were in a league by themselves.
There was a quiz for every class so that she could know how well we learned. There were three reports but no exams. The course isn't easy and has lots of work to do, but it's worth it! Not that hard!
Moisey is a great professor and he makes the course content interesting and engaging. The teaching team is very accommodating. Would definitely recommend to people even if they are non-majors.

Dr. Ahmann is incredible. Not only is she a knowledgeable, and passionate anthropologist, but she also is relatable and approachable. She even took the time to speak to me about my interest in switching to anthropology as a major. Her lectures were Ted Talk quality and grading was more than fair. Couldn't recommend taking her classes highly enough!
Dr. Bauerle was pretty laid back. The classroom wasn't the best I agree but that has nothing to do with the class. We got to actually handle plants and I liked the hands-on approach.

Loved his lectures. He answers questions if you have any (even though only few people asked). Homeworks are very representative of the exams and also gives enough practice exams. Not hard to get a good grade in this class. Office hours are also good.
Very witty and entertaining lecturer.  Doesn't hurt that his material was some of the most interesting of the class.
Louge is a good guy, but often can be hard to follow. If you don't do the reading you wont understand the course-- and thats not his fault.
Super chill and nice, doesn't reply to emails but enjoyed his class and learnt a lot in the three sessions

Incredible, engaging, and charming lecturer. She likes to use visuals to draw you into the historical context. Readings are dense, and you memorize lots of facts to do well on the exams. No extra credit. 
Also, the historical narrative she offers is conspicuously feminist and Marxist (but not communist).

It was a paper-based class, so will need to get textbook and print stuff. I personally liked the Friday lectures in language learning center because she showed us some fun materials besides textbook, such as music, opera and interviews.

Easy class but she is one of the least sympathetic professors I've ever encountered. You could be in the hospital or be incredibly sick and she will still penalize you for an assignment not being handed in on time. No sense of humanity or understanding whatsoever. Easy material but merciless attitude makes the class awful. Avoid at all costs!

She rambles A LOT. The entire class consisted of her "lecturing" which wasted the opportunity to discuss in the small class. She was fun and interesting at times, but usually very boring. Worst of all, there were very few grades. I never knew how I was doing in the class. I feel that my grade did not reflect my knowledge of the material accurately.

You will get yelled at and might get hit by Nero, but keep asking questions and don't give up. She's definitely there to help you but you need to put in the other half of the work. She'll push you and is not afraid of whacking you on the head to get you to learn.

Professor Codera is one of the most caring professors! I could not have developed my writing without her support. The classes are fun, unique, and wholesome. I would recommend taking her class without a doubt!

I took his Exploration in Engineering class and it was fairly easy if you work well in groups. There are quite a bit of textbook readings, and I don't recall taking a single test. Overall, although it could sometimes get quite boring, I really enjoyed this class.

Lectures can be a bit dry but he's a good teacher. Answers any questions or concerns you have and is very helpful if you need any help. Problem sets aren't necessarily hard but take a lot of work. Pretty heavy on conceptual knowledge so make sure you actually understand what your equations mean

She's often unclear and contradictory. One minute you do this; the next you do something else. There's also a lot of busy work assigned. She demands you meet 4-5 times a week for class and hands out repetitive assignments almost every day. The grading can be very arbitrary as well.

Such a nice Professor, I absolutely loved this class. Such a fun and great FWS. She priorities you above all else and does not punish you for taking mental health days. My favorite class I have taken in Cornell so far. I can imagine someone disliking her, such a great person. Gave us snacks for Halloween and Christmas to celebrate the holidays :)
Prof. Cosgrove is one of my favorite professors at Cornell BME! He is super organized, willing to help, and cares about his students. Highly recommend taking his class.
I've never seen anyone pour so much effort and passion into teaching a course than this man did. He was loving every minute of it, and so were we.

Fantastic, passionate teacher with a great deal of real-world experience in death penalty cases.  The socratic method is a little scary, but once you get called on, it won't happen again.  The Death Penalty in America remains the best class I've taken at Cornell.

Explains why we are doing things such as processes and not just make us do it. He goes above and beyond the understanding of the course which helps me make connections to concept in biology, that I finally get my aha moment.

Majority of class dropped out. The exams were fair but the problem sets were atrocious. Only two questions (out of 4-6) in each PS (which takes 4+ hours to complete) are graded but never mentions which ones. Lectures and textbook are almost never enough to be able to complete PS thoroughly. Avoids answering questions in office hours. Steer clear

Genuinely funny guy, lectures are long, but he makes sure to make it worth your while. There are books required, but I just checked them out from the library instead of buying, they were pretty easy find since they're all classics.

Lectures are hasty and it feels like they rush through some parts while wasting time on unhelpful derivations one could find in the textbook. Prelims + final quite straightforward with non-strict rubric, but no practice exams provided which is annoying. Problem sets a bit lengthy, but not too bad. Would want better lectures, but not bad overall
Easy grader, but but her class is so boring

She gives good lectures generally, but her tests are absolutely ridiculous for an intro class. You pretty much need to know every single detail on the lecture slides. The answer choices are very tricky. You could "defend your answer" but you need to give her a very very detailed explanation. She seems like a sweet and caring professor though.
Lectures give exactly what you need. Honest about the material - which one is really useful and which one you just need to know to pass the exam.

Very clear course, knew the expectations from the beginning. She prepares a lot for classes & students. Learnt all fundamental concepts in 1 course. She's a very sweet person, & gives great feedback. She is very accommodating, empathetic and passionate about what she teaches, & I respect her tons.

He is a the type of professor who tries hard to be idolized by students. This is not a good thing. Nor should he take philosophical disagreements so personally. When people disagreed with Sturgeon, I got the sense that he disapproved of them in a moral sense---and that he kind of got off on it. Steer clear.

Prof. Tebbe is my favorite professor this semester. He makes very difficult topics much more accessible, and he does it in a fun and lighthearted way. He's also very encouraging and is the kindest professor when it comes to cold calling. All in all, 10/10.

Heavy workload! Doesn't understand meaning of homework and assigns month long assignments. Is extremely delusional in what it means to teach NLP and teach MLE. He thinks his homework assignments are reinforcing course topics yet so much isn't taught. It's so telling when half the class drops after the first homework.

She writes everything on the board, so if you miss lectures, don't expect any slides to be uploaded on Blackboard. She is very helpful and friendly in office hours. Exams are super hard, but class is curved to a median B+.
Do not take his foundations of robotics class...
Cornell made a huge mistake. Our loss, Iowa's gain. She is a wonderful professor.
Too focused on details.  She'll spend a class focusing on a paragraph and over-analyzing every single minor flaw.  Every single thing she harps on is her own personal preference.  Do not recommend.
Tough Course, but Awesome Professor. Archer really knows his fluids and is kind. I do not like Denn's Book. Let me repeat, I do not Like Denn's Book. I hope this causes Archer to choose another text.

Professor Friedland's lectures were great and he is very knowledgeable about the French Revolution, which was the topic of the class.

Throughout the semester, he managed to draw a clear, logical line from the beginning of the Revolution to its end.

I would definitely strongly recommend this course.
Professor Welker is very nice. Her lectures were very engaging. The readings provoked deep thought about global problems. The discussion sections were also very insightful.
great class, bees are cool!

Good class. I learned a lot, but we didn't focus on writing skills as much as an FWS maybe should and the class was lecture heavy most times. Still really enjoyed it and Libby is a great philosophy teacher & person.

Larry is great, he does make it obvious that 1585 is meant to lure people into taking more history classes so if you think of it like a baby history class it makes more sense. Lots of reading, but if you do them and show interest in sports history you'll probably get an A

Professor Lakhani is one of the most flexible and easy-to-understand professors in the Hotel school. She is clearly knowledgeable in human resources but always willing to learn and be open-minded. HR has a very light workload and easy/fair exams, making the learning experience enjoyable and stress-free.
A legendary genius who cannot talk to anyone below his level of intelligence.
Not well organized in in terms of the lecture, and difficult to get his email reply

Really good professor who makes a difficult subject (digital signal processing) clear and easy to understand. Especially Spring 2020, with coronavirus, he has been extremely understanding and flexible in making the course help accessible and with changing deadlines and such. Wish there were more profs like him who care so much about the students.

I took Mr. Najemy's 'Machiavelli' course in Fall 2006. He truly inspired his students,  and was clearly passionate about history. He had an unparalleled knowledge of early modern Europe, particularly Florence. He may speak a little softly, but this fits well with his unassuming character.  If you like European history, you must take this seminar.
chill professor but tries too hard to be funny. does not provide any practice for exams other than the 4 easy homework we had that did not help on the exams. take the hotel version instead.

Professor Silver's networking class is a must-take for anyone looking to master networking. Her engaging teaching style and guest lecturers provide invaluable insights into building and managing professional connections. I gained timeless skills that will last a lifetime, making it one of the most valuable classes I've taken at Cornell.
Gave clear and organized lectures even though the topics are difficult.

Homonoff's not bad - behavioral econ is an interesting subject and she was pretty straightforward in explaining concepts and gave a lot of interesting policy examples. However, she has a horrible habit of asking questions while the answer is ON THE POWERPOINT SLIDE. One group proj at the end, which wasn't too much work but was kind of annoying.

Would NOT recommend. He treats students like little kids. He should understand students only pay attention in classes because professors talk about interesting things, not because phones and computers are banned. For a class that repeats industry leadership jargons and outdated concepts, 2 cumulative prelims and 1 final are more than excessive.
Horrible Professor!  Never had class, graded very hard, no feedback whatsoever

To be fair, I was really excited for this class before I took it. You just talk about paradoxes the entire course. It's great. Just show up to lecture, minimal readings that are basically optional. Very helpful lecture notes distributed in class. Class makes you think. Explains things clearly and thoroughly. Grading was kind of tough though.
He made exams unrealistically hard for what we learned and graded them entirely too harshly.  I think he also assumed that people generally had more knowledge than they actually did.

Wonderful person, brilliant philosopher, terrible professor. His lectures were boring to the point of being painful, and so complicated and rambling that if I zoned out for even a second I was lost for over an hour.
