I didn't do any of the work for his class, wrote a half-assed paper and got an A. The real challenge was giving up my Tuesday nights.
He is the best!
One of the best and most respected professors at Cornell.
That's toooo hard for the course, and the test avg grade is 40+ even after curving up. That's crazy.
The readings are super interesting and she always allocate time to meet with you if you schedule an appointment with her. Very enjoyable class overall!!
Awful professor and person for that matter.  Does not care about students and is demeaning in class.

Professor Danko is a very good speaker, and her presentations are always interesting, even though the material is sometimes repetitive. The assignments for the class, however, are very specific, time consuming, graded harshly, and can be difficult without photoshop or illustrator.
Prof. Hemami does not have much knowledge about the subject in general. Look at her research papers and decide for yourself.
Great professor, one of the best, if not the best, in the cs department. fair tests and homeworks, very knowledgable about the industry. His textbook is also great and easy to read.

The information was incredibly dense, and had us learn a software with little to no help. The class is definitely designed for grad students, and you shouldn't take this class unless you either already know a lot about genomics or are required to.

This class is not for the faint of heart. Plan to go to office hours for the majority if not all of the assignments. With that being said, Karthik is a good lecturer and the class is a thorough introduction to learning theory. The class was also curved, but I am not sure by how much.
every lecture has a quiz but super useful and almost exact same as exams

I like how 20% of the grade was based on participation because the Professor was rlly nice and super easy to talk to. When I took it in the summer, he was willing to meet us outside class to discuss about our assignments. Briefly reading the assigned textbook pages outside class would do. However, heavy memorization for prelims/finals.

I love Professor Sengers and her lectures are incredibly enriching, but 4240 frustrated me. There is a lot of busy work, the miniprojects don't deserve to be called "mini" (big time commitment), and you're at the mercy of the TA graders who fluctuate between lenient and excessively picky. There's a difference between challenge and blind luck.
The professor is extremely monotone and lectures can get very boring. However, they want to see students succeed and are willing to assist outside the classroom.

Very inspirational and helpful lectures. NO HOMEWORK!!! Weekly discussions to reinforce material. No required attendance. Straight-forward class with a great professor. Exams held all of the grade but they were weighted towards your best 2 of 3 and had fair, not too hard questions. Can't ask for much more. Great class and professor.

I would strongly caution against taking a class with this professor. Projects are unnecessarily difficult, especially given the low teaching quality, with unclear grading schema. Slides are almost purely formulas with no explanations of what variables mean, and prof zips through them in class. I'm a CS senior and this class took up all my energy.

Lectures were clear and very useful. Online help sessions were spectacular - highly recommend viewing them. Amazing professor and very caring and dedicated - I had no idea he is also one of the worlds best scientists!

Fascinating class on political violence and international relations. Genuinely easy prelims and writing assignments if you pay any attention at all during lecture. Passionate about teaching and easily accessible outside of class. While the topics are heavy at times, I highly recommend this course!
Server error. Please refresh and try again.Server error. Please refresh and try again.
This is the best class I've taken and recommend it often.
Great Professor. Very accommodating and understanding of personal circumstances.
Fantastic professor!

This class is definitely not enjoyable if math isn't your thing, but you can make it through with TA and Prof help. If you're kind and confident in approaching Prof. Nussbaum, he will help you in office hours and will accomodate you very fairly. Don't be afraid to reach out just due to his seriousness in lecture. He's a nice person.
Stuff is substantial but EXTREMELY confusing. Ridiculous Hw grading.

super kind and helpful professor despite my lack of knowledge in Persian. Really patient in teaching and never shames anyone for their less-than-perfect participation or attentiveness. Strives to create an equal learning opportunity and doesn't exclusively pick up good students.
It's the same format and style as 2800, but with more advanced topics. It feels like he walks us through recent papers, and you really feel like an expert by the end.
Great professor. The material was on the harder side but she is a generous grader on the final exam.

One of the worst classes and professors Ive ever had in my life. I honestly dont think he is In teaching to actually teach. The entire class was just pushing out as many assignment as possible. And then when I got something wrong, he would not teach and let me know why, he just said this is not correct, like gee thanks TEACHER

Dr. Loss really enjoys teaching. He has such a passion for food. From his culinary background to his food science expertise, he is the perfect man for the job. In addition to this, he trusts his students a lot and gives them a lot of authority. As a food sci freshman, this was a great opportunity to expand my knowledge and independence.
Lots of work. Very passionate about Gulf. Learned a lot but the readings were very dense. Also not the most approachable.

I heard from a friend that Professor Odato would be an easy grader, and at Cornell I feel like that is hard to come by. Im a double major so I needed an easy fill for my premed degree, boy was I wrong. When I tell you Prof. Odato was the hardest grader with biggest work load I've ever received. He gave pop quizzes on chapters we hadn't learned yet.

The project has no rubric no matter how much you ask. Then you get a random grade a few peers come up with. Your project can be better than others but you will get a worse grade. learn very little in this class. Avoid this class please. Its just a shame there are not other good ML classes available. it forces people to take this class.

He's a great guy. He was extremely helpful in office hours and his tests/quizzes were very fair. He made it very clear what material would be on the exam. However, his exams are difficult, but if you put in the work, you will succeed.

I took this class in 2009. I felt comfortable with the material up to the last paper. I emailed for guidance but got no response. He basically wrote on my paper that he was being nice by giving me a C- and that is the nicest thing he could say about it. He later modified my grade to a C as a courtesy. Very unprofessional. I do not recommend.
He doesn't really lecture, lets the students teach the class so you don't learn much, but I got a decent grade and it was easy so I can't complain too much
Shes very kind and engaging and approaches the material in a really unique way. Made me actually not upset to wake up early to go to class. Highly recommend.

I didn't really attend a lot of the lectures (cause they were optional) but I thought I'd nevertheless leave a review for the class. The class content was extremely interesting and insightful. Just download the textbook "Networks, Crowds and Markets" and read through some parts. You'll know whether you wanna take this class.

He made the learning outcome thing much better this sem. I liked it a lot personally. His lectures were pretty solid. Kinda rushed at the end a little since content was harder. Course is generally smooth sailing until u hit Fourier Series/PDEs. Just practice PDEs a lot b4 the final and use the inner product shortcut when u can, it makes life easier
Draga was always available for help outside class and was patient with all my questions. I found it a little hard to follow along in class, but her helpfulness made up for that.

The professor teaches the content with enthusiasm, which makes the class more engaging. Getting cheat sheets is a huge plus for exams + finals, and with enough time and effort, all content is understandable, just a bit time-consuming.
GOAT. Interesting topic. Class is mainly paper-based with one group final project. Prof. Ellis is great.
This guy always go on tangents during lecture so we never know what shows up on the test and what doesn't.
Really interesting lectures, easy class.

Took a very long time to grade. Lectures were not engaging or adaptive and she was a very strict grader on top of that. Gave contradictory advice for writing and tests were not as advertised. Worst prof I have worked with in the department as well. Not sure why Cornell admin assigned her this class as it's her first year and this is a req class.

Jerrie is an amazing professor and extremely kind and helpful, especially to pre-vet students. She has an enormous wealth of experience and knowledge that she is happy to share, and does so frequently through her courses and while advising students. Definitely take her class, and get to know her!

I had Diane for PE 1100 (beginning swim) and she made learning to swim super easy and not scary at all. I think the entire class ended up passing the swim test by the end of the semester. I really appreciated her feedback and thought she was kind, but also knew exactly how to help students if they were struggling.
Worst professor yet at Cornell. Does not care about students well-being and has horrible TA's that grade very unfairly and erroneously.
Comrade Gross was a chill Professor. The class is one big paper and participation. Be prepared for lots of conversation. The man speaks truth to justice.
Great Professor.  Classes are straightforward and the assignments are challenging but doable.  I found his material and perspective to be useful in the engineering field.

Professor McKee is a great lecturer and professor. They always try to make the class interesting and applicable to the real world. The work can be a lot but he is always willing to help students when needed! I would absolutely take another class with him!

Great professor who is very accomplished in Graphics. A class is hard with a lot of work and a median grade of B so make sure you have time and are interested in the subject. He is monotone in lecture but very knowledgable and descriptive. Fair and easy to access.

Great professor, makes the tuition more worth it. Knows his stuff and explains material clearly and effectively. Provided some of the best lecture notes I have ever seen, could almost form a textbook. 4670 required a decent amount of effort but not extremely difficult by any means. Overall a good balance between workload and learning.
Professor Saleh is a great professor, and is super caring. We did 2 discussion posts, and two main projects. They were all interesting, highly recommend.

Kevin is the GOAT. Responds to emails within minutes, is super patient, and never makes you feel dumb. There are weekly pass/fail quizzes, but he mainly just wants to save the procrastinators a mountain of trouble later on with these. Prelims are fair, proportional to the amount of effort you put in.

The class is interesting but really easy to get an A in if you go, take notes. I never study for the exams and I get As because its really straight forward. Dont need to read/textbook stuff but I would go to class. Extra credit if you do business simulation. Also really easy homeworks every week. Participation pollevs and stuff so go to class.

shouldn't be too difficult to get an A if you do ALL the assigned work. Final paper is unnecessarily long an a total nightmare.  professor honestly doesn't do much but is generous with iclicker points and extra credit at the end of semester.
Shao Laoshi is demanding, but her classes will teach you a lot, and she is always willing to help. She's also very funny. Make sure you keep up with the work - she has many small assignments.
Definitely one of the best Professor who can make things crystal clear, make everything in class interesting and make everyone so involved! Love this guy!

Aleks is an outstanding professor, expanding the imagination of the discipline, and a necessary voice in the department. The studios can be a little directionless sometimes but Aleks always has a number of directions in mind and student input is welcome. Thought provoking prompts and very fun/generative representational strategies

He is the NICEST professor ever! He is very fair with grading, respects our occasional need for an extension on an essay due to exams/prelims and gives easy quizzes to reward anyone who attends. There's no reason to skip this class, it's interesting and Prof. Scherer is awesome.

Among other students taking this class, I think Professor Pollock is considered the best for Math 1910. His lectures were good because he explained things well and worked through many problems on the board to SHOW students how to work through problems instead of just explaining a concept and assigning hw. Not heavy on the theoretical side of math.

Lectures were often difficult to understand and follow. Regarding papers, she would often deduct points for errors and not explain how to correct them, even if you already tried to correct it. Grading criteria was not always easy to understand.
Sometimes attending her class is like getting bullied.

Professor Stapp loved providing unclear guidance and ignored many emails sent by students. He talks about his life too much and does not really know how to personally help each student. Please take the class with any other professor over him.  As a communication prof, he does not know how to engage students effectively.

Miloje is such an amazing professor! The concepts are difficult (especially in the syntax portion of the class) but he explains everything very clearly, and the homework and exams are straightforward. He is incredibly kind and wants everyone to do well. Plus his accent is awesome!

Over-assigns reading but goes slowly through it in class so its okay to be a bit behind. Really fun Socratic dialogue and class discussions. Makes you think through the tough questions and constantly makes you argue for the other side of what you think. This is what law school is about.

Bland is an amazing guy. He is so nice, caring, and down-to-earth. He is an ok teacher, but you must go to class and take notes during lecture to do well.  One problem: his voice is monotone and he speaks softly so it is often hard to understand him. He really, really cares about his students. He's Inspirational; just such an amazing person.
Interesting class, Velazquez makes class interesting. Class consists of presentations and participation mostly. Take him he is also a DJ on the side, chill professor

Lectures are basically reading over the slides and does not communicate information effectively or in a logical manner. The assigned reading gets very dull and jargony which really makes doing the reading reflections hard. Only stayed in the course for minor requirements, which I think is the reason 90% of the class is there for too.

Overall chill class as long as you keep up with it. Weekly quizzes are open note but it helps to be familiar with readings ahead of time. TAs are super generous graders. Landon is super accomodating, but as others have said he does have a limit. He makes lecture content relevant and interesting

You would think that for a class with such intuitive content, this course would be simple but Prof. Sternberg/TA's grading made it excessively difficult. Given his tendency to ramble off & base the quizzes almost completely on his tangentially related stories, my consistent efforts to attend class every day were wasted. Funny though so props there.

Intermediate Narrative Writing with Professor Viramontes has been on of my favorite classes at Cornell so far. Not only does she have great knowledge in the subject as an accredited novelist and short story writer, but she also takes the time to connect and impart this knowledge on her students. I know have improved as a writer with her guidance.

Participation was graded based on a few questions during lecture and was 10% of the final grade. Weekly problem sets make up 40%, while exams make up the rest. Professor was nice and lectures were fairly engaging. Sections were a bit boring. You don't need to buy the textbook.

Your ideal FWS teacher - caring, knowledgeable, well-spoken, and engaging. There were times where some lectures could get a tad long-winded, but you won't need to listen to every word in order to succeed in the class.
Professor Campanella's lectures are inspirational and amazing. He is always well prepared and passionate about everything urban planning and architecture
KV is awesome! Very chill , nice , caring, smart and responsive guy. Learned a lot on the IARD trip over winter break.
She does not care.

Worst class every. Course not curved. Not given a single point to extra points problems, even if you did all of them and received full credits. No response in email and failed to answer students questions during classes. Told you quiz tested on a certain chapters, but actually tested on different chapters.
Great class, interesting and supportive professor.

Uber hard prep sheets (30% of grade and 5 pages each in great detail) each week about an article. High amounts of papers to read that are 5 years old and ancient in the field added on. Only plus is she gives out prelim questions before the exam and you can drop a prelim (out of 3). Lectures are decent and relevant to the week's paper.

I've learned more in this class than any other class at Cornell. If you're at all interested in cell biology, this class provides the context you need. Don't fall behind in lectures, the material is DENSE and plentiful. Put in the time and effort and it will pay off. First time a prof will give you helpful practice/feedback on scientific writing.

Dr. Allred is very kind and extremely intelligent. She is warm and helpful with any questions. She knows the material so well and her class is enjoyable. The final research paper and story map are hefty and lack clear guidelines, but that was the only downside of this course. The material is interesting. Make sure you ask clear questions.

I feel like Professor Whitmore is about as good a professor as you can get for biz comp. In class he's always willing to help you and his TAs are helpful as well (and hold lots of office hours). Regardless of who you get, the class has a ton of work, so just do it and you should be fine. The excel prelim and final are easy imo, only word was hard.

Professor Constable may be a brilliant researcher but he is anything but brilliant as a teacher. Lectures were extremely disorganized, he repeated himself many times during lectures, and our entire grade was based on one paper, for which we received no guidance.

Professor Cowie is genuinely amazing. He is the most captivating lecturer I've ever seen. He's so smart and witty and he has his his finger on the pulse of what matters and makes his class relevant and enlightening.
dope professor

His lectures are always very informative, and he's very passionate about the subject matter. He teaches everything very clearly, and he's always available outside of class for more help. The homework is challenging, but interesting, because it forces you to think about problems in a different way. Overall, he's a great professor.

The most responsive professor I've had here. Responds to email super fast and is always on time. His analysis is hard to follow at first but once you get the skeletons everything starts to make so much sense. Will take his class again.
She is the best. Super knowledgeable and super friendly. A tough grader in writing assignments but she gives you the chance to improve your writing skills and your grade.
He's really helpful. Emphasizes participation in class and staying engaged.

Carolyn is clearly passionate about acting and wants each of her students to succeed. As long as you show up to classes and participate, you'll do well in this class. Most work is done in-class, and there is very little homework/assignments.
take this class. meet with him.

I took Marxism and Liberalism with him and really enjoyed it. His lectures are super engaging and I loved the way he connected ideas. He graded the essays pretty easily and gave good feedback on what to change. I'd definitely recommend him if you are looking for an FWS to take.

Great course, I would 100% recommend taking it with Dr. Mozafari as he responds and addresses any concern related to assignments as fast as he possibly can. Just make sure to reach out with any questions with hw submissions as early as possible so he can act as leniently and understandably as he can.
One of the most enjoyable learning experiences I have ever had.

Dr. Kassam is very wise and knowledgeable. Pushes his students to their fullest potential and gives great and specific feedback on the essays, as a grad student. He makes the lectures very personal and intimate, and deeply connects the information to spiritual wisdom. I would have given 4.5 because lectures often are rambly and anecdotal.
Professor Hirschl is disorganized and he presents his theories like they are facts. While he claims to be objective, his lectures and essays are anything but. Easy class though.

I did not like this class at all. He was all over the place most of the time and he made zero sense the majority of classes. I went to every class and had no idea what was going on. He grades super weird like I had no idea what was going on. Also hes really bad with tech and that just made it worse.

Professor Bachminska is an exceptional instructor! I took POLSH 2034: Intermediate Polish II with her and there was never a time when I did not feel supported, heard, or respected. I highly recommend taking any class with her :)
great guy, easy exams (can easily get 100s), doesn't matter if you show up to class, take him for HR over the other profs, very chill class, one final group project and 2 prelims.

His sense of humor is definitely better than your avg prof, he makes the lecture fun. But he has so much non relevant advanced math on his slides that are just there 'for fun' and aren't tested on that the slides are difficult to study from. The lectures were nice, but i found them rather useless because of the 'extra stuff' everywhere.

Professor Gleeson is incredibly knowledgable about the topics she presents. She provides many guest lecturers to give real insight on the topics and events she speaks about. Her lectures are pretty helpful, but half the class doesn't pay enough attention, or reads anything at all to realize that. If you do the work, and don't bs you can learn a lot

Calc II is pretty standardized so you get the same work/grading regardless of prof, but Sen has to be the worst. Some lectures he would just start writing on the board and not look back at the class until it was too late and everyone was lost. Stopped going in favor of just learning from the text myself and looking at another profs online notes.
Just amazing and so inspiring. He KNOWS what he is talking about

A great professor. He is clearly very knowledgable. There wasn't a lot of feedback regarding what you got wrong on the Problem Sets and exams, but he was very easy to get a hold of in office hours. He has a great career ahead of him. Make sure you keep up with the material and ask questions and you'll be fine.

Most students who do not do well typically don't show up to class. The prelims can be difficult, but the class is curved. Also, the prelims have parts that are very similar to some problem-set questions. If you show up to class, do the problem sets, and study or go to review sessions, then it is reasonable to get an A/A-
Best CS professor at Cornell. Hands down.

Notes are very hard to follow. Homework for this class will make you want to gouge your eyes. This class is a guessing game. The math learning center and random Youtube videos will have to carry you. Good luck, you will need it.

Horrible teacher. This class is supposed to be one of the easier ones in the ECON major, but he made in unnecessarily complicated and difficult. He didn't allow calculators, and was generally not very approachable. The material on the tests seemed to be from a different class a lot of the time. I regret taking this class with him so much.

Good person, has a tendency to get distracted during lecture, but that didn't hurt my ability to do assignments. A ton of readings, but you can really just cherrypick certain chapters/concepts if you can't fully commit to reading everything. Always happy to engage and answer questions.
Professor Gu is very passionate about wastewater and engaging!

A great professor with excellent juggling skills! I had no prior ML experience before this class, and Kilian explained so many tough concepts in ways that just made sense. He is a master at finding helpful analogies and has a great sense of humor. I would highly recommend this class if you are someone that wants to learn the foundations of ML.
Razak is hands down one of the best professors/instructors currently at Cornell. His discussions and instruction were very clear, precise, interesting, engaging, and challenging. Loved class with.
Gave me a B+ even though I scored higher than my peers with As because I was a sophomore. Not saying he is biased but be careful when taking his courses.

Very dry and boring lectures, doesn't engage with students at all during class. If you've read Harry Potter, this guy is essentially professor Binns. There were no regular quizzes or assignments, but there were two really difficult exams and one group project that make up the majority of your (uncurved) grade. I wish I hadn't taken this class.

An excellent lecturer who pulls from a wide range of personal and professional experiences to make this class a cohesive picture, alongside Prof. Diaz and her work. He has written across may genres and provides great insight and feedback. He is someone you want to impress!

Professor McCouch is the best teacher I have ever had in my life. She is so bright, approachable, and knowledgeable. She has had experiences impacting the world, yet is modest about her work. Highly recommend.

This isn't the worst course I've ever taken, but it's definitely up there. The material is interesting, but the course is so disorganized that it makes it hard to care about the material. Almost all of our assignments were shoved into the last month of the course. Lectures are boring, slide decks are not organized, and staff support is non-existent
Mid

Of the three professors teaching 1440 during the FA 20 semester Shapleigh was not the best. That being said, he was very accessible outside of class with extra office hours and his test questions tended to be drawn from lecture. His lectures sometimes strayed from relevant content but his inclusion of pathophysiological studies was interesting.
Nava makes Hebrew fun and enjoyable. You are going to want to make aliyah after her course...

Interesting material in the class, but the professor was extremely boring and the 1:15 lectures were painful. 1-2 page and a half response papers are due every week after a lot of reading, so if you're looking for a class with little work this isn't it. They also grade the papers and exams pretty hard - I studied a lot and got B+s...Not an easy A.
Really smart guy, and kind of funny.
She is great at teaching writing.

He has a reputation for not caring about classes since he's near retirement, and can be a bit ethereal and touchy as someone else mentioned. He's really brilliant, but during the pandemic kind of lectured about something off topic. Kind of moody, and maybe even rude at times

Professor Corpis is THE professor to whom I compare all my other teachers. He is engaging, enthusiastic, an amazing lecturer, funny, friendly, and an overall wonderful man. His lectures were always filled with interesting tidbits, and he has such a deep knowledge base. I absolutely loved his class.
A really nice professor who wants his students to exceed. I would take again

Zia is one of the best professors I've ever had at Cornell. Her lectures were clear, easy to follow, and helped with the homework and exams. She was helpful outside of class and provided fun and useful extra credit activities. She really wanted to make sure we learned the material and enjoyed joying so.

Phyllis Janowitz, besides being a world-renowned poet, is a gifted professor, able to give the deeper shades of critique and understanding to a fiction writer's work. Highly recommended for serious writers.

Prof. Hammer is an amazing lecturer. His lecture is clear, organized, informative, and engaging. He's also very friendly, approachable, and he actually cares about students. The class itself can be hard tho - especially for someone who doesn't have a solid background in physics, but he's very helpful in office hours and outside of the class.

Took this class as an elective and don't regret it at all. Professor Hafeez is one of the best people out there. He cares about his students understanding the material well and doesn't assign readings unless it's absolutely necessary. I appreciated his flexibility with deadlines and resources. He also delivers amazing lectures - 100/10 class!!

While he has sadly moved on to Tufts, Spurlock was one of the best professors I've ever had for Spanish! He went beyond teaching and really tried to connect with each and everyone of his students. His OH were very helpful and he'd always explain things in an easy to understand way. I have no doubt that you'll learn great things from him.

Had him for a lab supervisor this year.  He will give you an A if you just run people like you should.  He's famous for being a solid experimental designer in the questionable field of parapsychology.  Doesn't teach regular classes anymore but I highly recommend his lab.

He makes you think critically for the required discussions. His lectures are taught well and it was obvious that he was really passionate about Astronomy. I looked forward to the labs and lectures because they demonstrated interesting things about space. The assignments and quizzes were manageable, and the essay was the only difficult thing I did.

I haven't taken a class with Prof. Scur, but I wanted to write a review. She's extremely charismatic and knowledgeable. Nevertheless, she's humble - she admires, congratulates, and compliments other economists, while she, clearly, is a very good economist too. Inspirational.

Highly recommended!! If he is teaching any topic on quantum or many-body physics, take his course - he connects his lectures to current research and provides his own insights that you can't get in any textbook.

Are you trying to get rid of your passion for architecture? Look no further! This professor will "provoke" you with such single minded feedback, that you will not only hate your project but even yourself! There is a reason why 95% of students put him on the "especially not him" box, on their ballots for Thesis advisers.
Great course. Great professor!

Bi Happiness is one of the best professors out there! She is so nice and caring! I took this class as a language requirement, but it ended up being my favorite class of the semester. For learning a new language, Bi Happiness makes it super enjoyable and easy. Weekly assignments help you improve both speaking and writing. 10/10 would recommend
Awful. People were failing a one credit class.  100 points QUIZ every class. and gives you 5 minutes.  Being mauled by a bear would be a better chose than signing up for this class.
Made a difficult class better than it could have been

Dr. Alano is an excellent instructor who really teaches you how to write historical analysis. I had never taken a history class before, so this was definitely a bit tough for me, but with her feedback I was able to improve drastically throughout the course of the class. The class requires a lot of reading and writing, but the work is worth it.

Excellent professor. He's a little disorganized, but his lectures are fantastic and he's very helpful. The class was fairly easy, to the point where you could skip all the time and get an A, but I went daily because he made lecture enjoyable. You want to take a class with this guy, trust me.
She's really nice and grades based on personal improvement and completion. Classes include a lot of close reading, working with peers, and discussion.
Great!!  Very helpful with extra sessions when needed.

Nanotech was pretty fun with him. He was pretty good at breaking things down for us, since nano gets way more in depth than this class, I feel. Nonetheless, if you had a decent chem background, you can follow along. And the homework wasn't too bad. Pay attention,  and you can do ok. The lectures were good, and anyway, there was no textbook...
Lots of novel vocab in her speaking makes her hard to understand but good for learning. Ask nicely what stuff means and she'll explain. Wants to make class fun

I took BIONB 1220 (FWS: Evolution on Islands-Islands Unique Species) during the Fall semester of 2022. In my opinion, The class was well-paced and appropriately challenging. Its a significant amount of work, but I think the work Raunak assigns is worthwhile if difficult. Raunak is very chill and pretty funny, I would take a class with him again.
Material is taught very well, he is very easy to contact for help, and testing was done to actually test material you learned rather than trip you up. He wants you to succeed.

By far the best professor I have had in my (albeit short) time here. She is brilliant; she provides excellent explanation, and very clear guidelines for grading. The seminar can be a bit intense as an undergrad, but definitely worth it. I want to take as many of her classes as I can!

POOR slides, with only few images and words and no definition, which makes it useless. Also, quiz is OBSCURE and could have multiple right solutions; you're NOT allowed to review the quiz after submitting because of the so-called "anti-plagiarism" that actually HINDER students from learning on their own. LOW QUALITY course recordings. DONT COME .

Excellent & inspirational professor. Dr. Leak is v busy as a researcher but takes considerable efforts to connect w students & is genuinely invested in their success. Her advice is actionable and specific. Go to office hours, don't be late, and if you can, ask specific q's about her/public health. Grading is tough but realistic to the industry.

Dr. DeFaria was a great teacher, he clearly cares a lot about the material he is teaching. He tries to pack a lot into his lectures and they can drag on near the end, when he isn't going by the textbook anymore. The material is interesting and useful, and the weekly readings take about 30 min to 1 hour and are online.

Shoemaker is great. The class feels hard as you're going through it, but there are many opportunities to accumulate points. I got a C on the final (harsh grader) and still got an A+ in the class. He asks a lot of you, but if you try, it will pay off. Fascinating subject taught by a passionate and dedicated professor. Would definitely recommend.

Definitely take a FWS with Nick if you can. Nick is really good at facilitating discussion and helping with papers if you have problems. You learn a lot and it's mostly discussion. The class wasn't too difficult, but as with all FWS, you have to be prepared to write. 9/10.

Bruce is so smart, and it's clear that he has a love for what he is doing and teaching. I took this class as a freshman and went into it not expecting to love it as much as I did. He inspired me to continue looking into the justice system and continue working as an advocate for change.
Great teacher, easy/fair essay grader, very approachable. Discussion can get a little dull but there isn't much reading. I'd recommend taking this class

Joe is a nice guy and a solid thinker. You read some interesting texts in the course but his grading of essays and participation is a bit harsh. The class isn't really about robots and is more about abstractly thinking about what makes things mechanical or humanlike.

This is NOT a stats class, it's a financial mathematics class. You will be plugging in formulas - start memorizing early. Multiple new concepts are introduced in every lecture and there is no discussion, just a three 75 minute lectures and office hours. Homeworks are not like the lecture examples but do reflect the difficulty of the exams.

Professor Besharov is the best professor I've had in the ILR school and maybe Cornell. She is really passionate about the topics she teaches and gets students involved and excited as well. She brings a wealth of academic and real world experience that makes the lectures interesting. Essays are straight forward, fair, and practical.

I think the other rating on this page could not be more false. Shami is a GEM, the grading criteria is very clear in the syllabus along with all the due dates, lecture is amazing, there are lots of assignments that go into your grade, so one bad paper won't hurt you. A great class for non science people, its also very possible to get 100
The nicest instructor I have run into at AAP - however, this does come with his feedback being infrequent and frequently not very helpful.
Amazing professor for Greek & Roman Philosophy! Easy to talk to outside of class, readings are dense but interesting, and papers are graded fairly.

LaunchPad is miserable and a waste of student and faculty resources. It is vapid, filling time with  unhelpful and unnecessary info; they hold the class just because it has always existed. Prof. Gaulke is not a pleasure to have leading this course. Graded by attendance, with annoying HW taken too seriously. I'd rather watch paint dry. Or eat paint.

I feel that Professor Burkhauser gets unfairly criticized by people who feel that any introductory social sciences class should be a walk in the park. Yes, his final is very difficult, but doing well on it is extremely rewarding and cements your knowledge of the material covered over the 15 or so weeks prior. If you take his class, be prepared to think about all of the concepts he goes over and be wary of some trick questions, but also be prepared to learn more about economics than most other professors at Cornell could ever provide.
Excellent professor.  Constantly encouraged us and engaged us intellectually. I really enjoyed going to class.  Perhaps my favorite Econ class.  She's really nice too, if a bit shy.

This professor sucked. He would not answer all questions, and he was really a smart alek. As a humanities major, this class was recommended because it was said to be easy and this was not at all the case... don't take this class unless you're ready to put 5-10 hours a week on homework that was always unclear.

Professor Parpia was my discussion leader for PHYS 1112, and I feel that he really helped me understand this subject and provided me adequate help for the course. Super approachable and a great person, he is best suited for students who have seen physics before at some level because he likes to go in depth with some of his material. Awesome prof!

Professor Fromme is very knowledgable, passionate about biology, and enjoys teaching and coming to class each day. He's easy going and very accessible either after class or during his office hours. He explains concepts well and calculates your class grade 1 of 3 ways in order to give you the best score possible.

Super easy class. We had 2 short reaction papers, midterm and final. Tests were mostly multiple choice and about 10 questions total. Don't need to do the readings and fine to skip lectures/ not take notes. Lectures are a little dry/ not much is added beyond what's on the slides. Final exam took me 22 minutes and I got a 100 on it. Would recommend

Clearly cares so much about the topic of the class. Tough quizzes, lot of random information. Homework was very straightforward, especially if BEE 2510 was previously taken. Exams were very similar to the practice ones he gives out.
Great professor and easy going for sure.

Lecture not very clear, but can be helpful if you actually attend. Main learning happens during the discussion sections. The discussion TA does all the grading, too. She's just there to summarize the readings (there are a lot of readings)
Super unprepared to teach, and super disengaged. Made many errors in class.

Material is fairly boring, and sometimes the slides or assignment questions are vague or not thought through. Matt is kind and generally explains things well, takes his time, gives reasonable tests/assignments, and is flexible when you need it. The lab/discussion period is extremely boring and usually useless --if that were revamped I'd give 5 star

while the content is interesting the homework is intended to be answered by a 4th grader. there is just too many works where he asks the same questions time and time again 
i would not take this course again nor would i recommend it

Teaches 1st 1/3 of the course. Review the material covered the previous class for 15 minutes before the start of the next class and you'll be ok. Do not take creative liberties on projects. Good at teaching the material, but project discriptions are poor. Ask him for clarification of instructions 2-3+ days before the project is due. Fair workload.
Nice person. And exam is easier than expected.

Clear lectures that really take care to walk through explanations and give examples. Tough exams, but the lecture, homework, and discussions try to prepare students for them, so they are always doable.
really cool guy, pretty fair grader and interesting discussions

This professor's employment makes the school look like a joke. The most disorganized and unfair professor I've ever encountered. Our entire grade was based on a singular research paper that was given to us 2 weeks before class ended, without any instructions and a misleading rubric. The presentation we worked on for 3 months had no weight. Fire her

Prof. Rubenstein goes on a lot of tangents. It took a few weeks to get used to the idea that you can't prepare for what she'll end up discussing in class, but once I accepted that, I actually really enjoyed the course. She knows what she's talking about, and she grades fairly. She was also available to go over papers in depth outside of class.

He's a good professor and the class was pretty interesting overall, especially for people looking to get into entrepreneurship. The grading criteria could have been clearer. Lots of guests lectures, some of them were misses.

Professor Silins is invested in the course material and carries that joy into classes - the classroom atmosphere is fun and laid back, which makes the complex concepts a lot easier to get through. Really enjoyed his class!
Best professor at Cornell. Very patient and supportive. You can learn a lot from his class

He is pretty good at lecturing, but the structure of this class is bizarre. This class was probably the least organized class I've taken. It's not a hard class in any way, but it feels like this professor genuinely is not aware of certain expectations of what a class should be, which results in a frustrating and disjointed experience.
The worst professor in Cornell's MSE department. (2 other students in the same class also agrees). Lectures are ill-prepared and she seems to put no effort into teaching and HW assignments

The psets are really hard and you basically can't do them without going to office hours, attending the lectures, and reading the "lecture notes." The prelims were very rough (45% median on the first and 62% median on the second), but the final was a lot more fair. The labs and reports were disorganized and stressful but easier than psets.

Could really tell she cared about her students. A lot of reading/discussion posts and mandatory attendance was a pain but ultimately worth it because the take-home exams and essays were bearable. Hated the group project, but that wasn't her fault. She took student feedback to heart and tried to make lectures more engaging.

Prof Bischoff is a pretty good lecturer. The course is broad and covers a lot. There were 3 assessments and a final paper and a good amount of readings. The prelims are doable if you skim the readings + look at the slides. My TA was very helpful too!
He is a very nice professor! The class is fun, and the contents are not difficult to understand.

I really hate the way he lectures. It was never clear what we should actually write down, and he moved on from slides too quickly to write down any graphs or jot down what he was saying. The class is generously graded, but in my opinion, it's not worth the hassle. The class has a lot of busy work with pre and post-quizzes for lectures and sections.

Amazing in every way, always willing to chat and discuss with interested students. The class covered the field of classics through many perspectives. Discussions were varied and always interested, would recommend to anyone interested in classics.
Good guy. Had him for my FWS. Interesting and thorough lecturer. Will work with you and help you succeed, but does expect a certain amount out of his students. Wrote a couple of papers, no tests.

Hutchens gives engaging and smart lectures. He doesn't answer emails or respond to criticism of material. Doesn't seem like syllabus has changed much in the last two decades, or since the great recession, which is pretty lame for a labor econ course. Other kids made a big deal of things being very hard but they just don't read - that'sILR for you.

Overall good, very very knowledgeable and an interesting lecturer. You should make an effort to stay on his good side, he can be quite lenient but also quite strict. Thorough and helpful in his feedback on papers. Lots of reading but its interesting stuff. Cold calls on people to answer questions/offer insights.
He was not lecturing a lot. We had to do all the work. It was hard to understand what he preferes. He doesnt grade well.

She's tough but she's got her own quirky charm and humor. She may not be the best teacher out there but she knows her stuff and are very passionate about fostering students' curiosity in the antiquity period.
No Comments

I really enjoyed the Six Books course, and would put it in my top 3 classes at Cornell. Macy and Ceci are not the most exciting lecturers, but they really know their stuff and are very personable if you talk to them outside of class. Would highly recommend this course or any others Macy teaches.

In my entire time taking this class I only saw him on lecture days with the exception of one pin-up where I received vague and confusing feedback.

I wish I had more time to create a rapport with him, and maybe get more project clarification from him, but he was gone for a significant part of the year.

The class felt a little disorganized, but overall was still really enjoyable. We covered a lot of stuff that was genuinely funny, and then class was just discussing it. I also took it during the start of the pandemic, and she was genuinely concerned for all of our wellbeing, and that felt really nice to have at that time.

He spent way too much time focusing on the mathes and very little classtime actually adressing the beaucoup des animaux dans la fenetre. He also encouraged us to learn through the Wheel of Fortune game, which only served to distract us even more.

Participation required for the core class is quite ridiculous. 

He is not approachable and would say that 9 out of 10 individuals have more negative than positive things to say.
Language classes can be kinda difficult at Cornell but she made the material even the difficult parts readily understandable. Would definitely recommend her as a professor!!

Uncaring, unreasonable, sloppy exams. Despite making tons of errata on the exam, he defends himself vehemently and refuses to give any points for missing the errata. Also praises himself for pointing out all the errata he makes thinking that we appreciate it. He also decides on point allocation after grading the exam, so that's not very fair.
Not bad.
Love every part of this class. It is one of the harder FWS in terms of work load but the content you gain is so worth it. Made me consider a minor in FGSS

Dr. Huntley (Dr. not Prof) for calc 1 after having taken calc in high school, so none of the concepts were new. Attendance was mandatory and there are ungraded preview assignments before each class along with HH and webworks weekly. Those were annoying and surprisingly hard, exams straightforward and fair. Time in class was intense and no phones.

Professor is amazing, and the workload is light with only 6 homework and 4 small quizzes. Homeworks sometimes require a bit of thinking. Overall, although not too hard, this is a decent course to take if you want to satisfy the probability requirement but don't want to waste credits on trivialities like MATH 4710.

Archbold's style may not appeal to everyone, but it certainly did to me. Hilariously dry yet passionate, he deepened my love for German. Archbold provided me with an in-depth understanding of the language and always brought in unique anecdotes and tangents to keep lecture memorable.

I entirely expected to hate this class considering it was an English FWS, but it turned out to be my favorite class the entire semester. Dr. Lears is very flexible with meeting and also when it comes to extensions. Criticism is very helpful and constructive. Highly recommend!
Great! I'm proud to have had him as a professor and he continued to amaze me with his knowledge and depth of the subject.
Very nice professor.
he's mean. super snarky. igor was rough. he was always unnecessarily vague.

This would be an historical evaluation. I was a student in Prof. Schatz's Math 191(I think that was the number) class in the fall of 1985. During my 27 (and counting) year career as a High School Mathematics instructor, I continue to reference my experience in that class. He left a significant and positive impression that lasts to this day.

Fishing class with him is a great way to relax and relieve the stress of Cornell! He is a very knowledgeable fishing captain from around the area, so he knows the ins and outs very well.  He is more than willing to help everyone with anything, from the beginners to the experienced. He's a little dry personality-wise, but still a good guy.

He is the best professor and an appellate advocate in patent law I've ever seen in Cornell Law school. He has some insight to advocate in front of the court of appeals and the supreme court. If you want to see the real stuff from both the history of the supreme court and the context of the supreme court's decision. This is the class!

Intense class with a fast pace. He's an easy to understand professor and is much nicer than he seems. He's very helpful and understanding about the difficulty of his class and provides a lot of support resources. He's strict on deadlines and you don't get much of a break when you're in his class. Worthwhile if you want to do real estate.

There is a tacit understanding that your race and gender play a significant factor in determining your success. Ask any student who took his class, and unless they benefitted from his deep rooted biases, they are likely to say the same. Most don't blow the whistle on his appalling behavior in fear of retaliation in another required core class.

Literlaly one of the worst professors--expectations are not clear for deliverables. He can be rude and refuse to answer questions from students for no reason. I would avoid taking any of his classes at all costs
Time for him to retire
Buehler? Buehler? I had and have no interest in economics. But miraculously, the guy is able to create an inkling of interest for the sole purpose of killing it. Absolutely mind-numbing.

While the professor is nice in person, this is an introductory physics class that is VERY deflated. It functions by "anything that can harm you will harm you."  The subject matter is pretty interesting, but this is not a well-structured class.
Tough class, but Prof. Belk really cared for his students and explained the content really well. You could see the passion while he taught, making students engaged. Awesome Prof.
disorganized, lab 2 grades released after last day of classes.
Lectures were a bit bland, but he tried to put in lots of interesting examples to better explain concepts like research cases or planet earth videos. Seems like a nice guy, though.
He's very invested into our learning, and you can definitely tell. Great teacher and also a great person with a sense of humor.

Essay based that's graded by him and the ta. He's a little monotone but is super nice, lectures definitely help with writing out essays and as long as you go to discussion and put effort into your papers, you'll get an A.

I had Prof Lunine for the second half of Astro 1101 after Prof Kaltenegger. He was a letdown from Kaltenegger's enthusiastic, inspirational teaching -- Lunine certainly knows his stuff, but his lecturing was boring and there was no life in his voice. Overall though, the class is a very manageable A and TA's are great. Reading textbook is critical

Bonter is just the best. If you have just the slightest interest in birds and are an undergraduate, talk to him. He is supportive, caring, and very knowledgeable about research. Gives great constructive feedback. Class is easy, but teaches you so much. Don't slack off tho, or your grade can slip. Note class has long waitlist.

She's nice and the class is interesting, but her lectures are a bit dry and her power points aren't great. There were prelims that were memorization heavy and two essays that were graded generously, along with a completion paper that was easy. The discussion sections were interesting and I liked the discussion that was had.

Avoid this class at all costs. They recommend to take this class for a requirement if youre not a STEM major. Do not do it. If you know nothing about physics, dont do it. Even if you think you know something about physics, dont do it. Gen physics is easier. The homework assignments are ridiculous. This is not an April fools joke, Im serious.
Similar to Previous years, Compilers is just as difficult and rewarding as ever. Group wrote over 15k lines of code. Well worth the grind!

Fahmy is the goat. It's a super easy class that will teach you a lot about the Middle East. Readings are "mandatory" but not super necessary. Study Fahmy's PowerPoints and you will ace the exams. There was a midterm, paper, and final exam. The median for Spring 2023 was an A-.
Awesome class.
wonderful discussions and intersectional curriculum. incorporated the struggle for justice into everything we read and learned.
No qualms. Average amount of papers, tests are straight forward short essays. About 200 pages of reading a week but maybe thats because its a history class.
legend

Proff. Hairston is a genuinely good standard professor. He teaches exactly why you SHOULD know for the exam. It's probably not his fault that whoever writes the exams has issues with constructing a clear question. It seems like they give a group of grad students shots of tequila and then make them write the exam from memory, and then no one revises
He's chill. Def the best Labor Law professor. Follow his twitter. He is quite a nice guy, and you learn a lot. Would take for sure.

Took the class in 2018, loved every minute of it, even though it was at 8:40 am. The class was majority derivations of physics equations applied to chemistry. Be prepared to use office hours and lots of math!

Definitely a gem. One of the most passionate people I've ever met. Not a professor to take if you're expecting a lot of concrete, foundational skill teaching. But a great one to take if you want inspiration, artistic insight, laughs, interesting discussion and critique. Overall, leagues beyond anyone else in the department that I've had. HE CARES.

Failed swimming lessons twice growing up. I finally learned thanks to Susie. She is incredibly caring and understanding. You'll be swimming in no time. She'll make sure you become comfortable in the water and wants to see you succeed. If you have to take swim, take it with her (and don't be embarrassed--everyone in the class is in the same boat!)
Overall excellent low-stress one credit course. Great lectures, easy grading, couldnt ask for much more.
I love coming to class. Anum is so kind. You can tell she puts time into reading and grading your assignments and it makes you want to be better at writing. Id take anything she taught!

She is an incredibly smart scientist/researcher who definitely has so much to teach. That said, she should not be teaching an intro level class. Her lectures were given at the speed of light and were extremely dense and confusing. Prior exposure to difficult physics concepts is assumed.

Had him for fws, very philosophy based try your best with the reading and if you don't do them at least google them beforehand he likes it when you discuss in class. Grading papers is sometimes unclear but he is always helpful in office hours. Overall an easy going prof lenient with deadline if you have a valid excuse. Very flexible.
So hot and so nice.
AWESOME.  ONE OF THE BEST SPAN TEACHERS I HAVE EVER HAD.  REALLY NICE AND PERSONABLE AS WELL.  COOL ARGENTINIAN ACCENT.

i adored ASIAN 1111: Haunting and (Still) Living: Spectral Body in Asian Cinema! while our class was dead, she was fantastic. most of our papers were open-ended and, while there was reading, it was interesting, especially if you like film. she was also really open to feedback + had a lot of office hours if you needed help with anything.
Extremely passionate and understanding. One of the best professors I've had at Cornell.
Amazing lecturer, caring, simply one of the best professors Cornell has to offer. Take this class!

Professor Monger is incredibly passionate about the topic, and this course is an easy overview of interesting material. If you review the slides before the exams, you will be fine. His lectures can sometimes feel dry and repetitive sometimes. Overall would recommend.

Professor Sipple can be a bit scattered at times, but he was always super attentive and went out of his way to show he cared about his students. He was easily accessible outside of class and even held extra office hours before big projects. Instructions were sometimes vague however, it's because he did not want to restrict/limit exploration.

Bateman wasn't terrible, but he tended to go over content in lectures extremely quickly, making coming to class feel useless even though it was mandatory. Assignments, in-class quizzes, and exams weren't terrible, and Bateman is very approachable, though, so this class isn't entirely awful.

Arch Analysis is my least favorite class that I have ever taken. Ruben is really nice but he is very particular and he grades hard. He allows resubmissions but there is so much work for this class that it's hard to find time to do these. He overdoes his lectures in this class. They are hours long and could be summed up in 10 minutes or less.

One of the best and most engaging lecturers in the AEP department.  He provides insight where the book lacks and creates engaging, yet challenging problem sets.  Of course there were hiccups with course management, but students have 3 drops (for 14 assignments).  Exams ranged in difficulty (1st Avg: 86, 2nd Avg:50).
Brilliant professor, great guy. Highly recommended. An absolute expert on negotiations and conflict resolution.  Incredibly clear, very willing to help, you will leave having learned TONS.

Boring lectures, cool content.  He repeats things a lot & inquires about weird things like "hows your blood pressure?"  Can quickly turn rude if you anger him, like not paying attention in class or skipping class.  VERY harsh grader, will make u think you're stupid.  Despite getting a raw 78% in the class, I ended with an A. Get on his good side;)

Definitely not an easy class, but it is fairly simple to do well as long as you take good notes. The information is interesting and Professor Dear presents it very professionally. The readings aren't necessary to do well but can help give additional info on topics covered in class. Was very accommodating on the transition to online classes.

Super kind and caring professor, which I appreciate. Wants his students to succeed. Unfortunately his lectures are very, very poor and so the TA's will probably end up teaching you the material. It's curved generously which was the only saving grace.

discontinued PLBIO 2490 after this semester but Scanlon is a great professor. Def seems a little rough and impersonable at first but throughout the semester he gets better. Class paper grading criteria was super unclear until grades came out which was annoying but he is light on grading for quizzes and the exams.

In MSE, you will probably have to take a decent amount of classes with Umbach. He is pretty not well-received as he doesn't teach well and tends to give grades arbitrarily based on personal feeling. You probably won't learn much in his classes and will def feel frustrated, but if you BS effort and try to seem nice, you'll get a better grade...

Super duper dismissive of a person. You have to play the game exactly his way man; there is not creativity or individual discretion allowed... which is weak for an architecture prof. Worse prof I ever had. Do not take this dude's class my friend. Not sure about this, but he seems to not like Non-American or Asian students.

I love Remus! I took M&P with him in the summer, and surprise, it was a very interesting class. It took me two days to get used to his accent, but he is very funny and helpful. He relied heavily on the textbook, but prob because it was a 3-week class. Provides all the materials for you to do well if you put in some work, and monitors your progress.
No Comments

Attendance is now mandatory and lectures are boring, dull, and kinda pointless unless you want to listen to Prof Kraft rant for 55 min at 9am. There was a paper each week which aren't hard to write, but require SO MUCH READING. On numerous assignments there were over 4 readings. Weekly discussions are mandatory and use group work for activities.

I try so hard on my papers but I cannot still get A's on them. She really, really makes you work for your grade. We have like 3 writing assignments due every week, and it is SO tiresome. She holds her students to a very high standard when it comes to writing. Her feedback is good but it's not worth the amount of time and energy this class demands
Very caring instructor with a true passion for teaching. Grades entirely on attendance and completion.

Robert Turgeon was a terrible lecturer. Seems like a smart guy, who's involved in very important research, but he could not lecture for the life of him. Extremely boring and covered zero material. Just read off of his slides. Class was not worth it, but he did say some weird stuff that could be included in prelims and the final.

Prof. Ke seems like a competent researcher and overall nice guy, but he's a terrible teacher. Lectures are dense and boring. The course was directionless and disorganized. Exams were essentially test of memorization, which seems like a waste of time in a course level this high. I regret taking his class.

Went a little too quickly during lecture, but class was very fair. Exams were challenging and there were never any surprises. Office hours were great and Prof. Olbricht was always happy to help improve student's understanding.
Bad professor
Cancelled several classes then changed attendance policy without notification, pretty much everyone was failing after that. Did not learn anything until weeks into the course.
The most boring class I have ever taken. Mandatory attendance taken but no takeaway at all. The professor makes you want to sleep in every session.
OG cat dad data scientist, mimno is the best

The topic fluctuates between being interesting and boring. Reading is required to some extent, but you don't need to spend hours on it. There's a solid amount of writing on it, and she's not the easiest grader, but she is fair, provides thorough feedback, and typically allows you to submit early to get feedback before turning it in. Compassionate.
She's very professional and smart. Gives fairly good lectures, clear agenda, and grading.

Bishop is a very intelligent and passionate man but he is an ineffective professor. He is extremely scattered, cannot answer questions directly, and goes off on meaningless tangents. EVERYONE in class is confused and irritated, class size started at 30 and ended at around 10. I'm a 4.0 student not afraid of work and I would not recommend this class

PHYS 1110 lab section. Most people did not go to lectures because attendance was done through a poll. One time he did tell everyone present to not answer the poll to catch the people who were skipping lecture, but he only did that when talking about ethics in science. Quite monotonous lectures, but chill guy
Great Professor.

Amazing professor! Only downside is that classes can get a little boring. Little work, but Professor Hackle is very caring of all students and never has problem pushing due dates back. TAKE HER CLASSES

Knew the material, how to teach, and kept students engaged with engr applications of material...decent courseloads, exams not impossible, and very fair with the grading. About all you can ask for a good prof.

Trent is a nice guy, but his Grand Challenges Writing course is incredibly hard. For the love of God, do not take his class. Compared to other grand writing courses, this is so much more work. Please do not do what I did. Save yourself and take another class.

She is beyond sweet and this class is so easy! If you're looking for a Professor that understands that life happens it is definitely her. She is truly an amazing person and the class is easy. Weekly discussions, midterm and a final. There is one project.

Great guy, wants you to learn (and wants you to get exposure to lots of weird percussion literature). Energetic. He's reasonably proficient/helpful in percussion. I have him for lessons too, and he's finding a good balance between teaching what you want to know, and teaching what you should know. Practice enough and show up, and get an A.

Szpiro is an amazing professor. He lectures at just the right pace, making some of the more complex material accessible. I especially appreciated his use of Panopto recordings, which are a valuable resource for reinforcing learning. Both the exams and homework assignments are fair.
Marseille was very good at demonstrating the tricks needed. He always made everyone's lecture much better and was the reason the front row in lecture was so highly desired.

I would've liked a little more freedom in the class but I was overall excited by the material covered. I learned so much about film and darkroom printing, artist books, and how to read a photograph. I was pleased that the material ranged from the basics/technical work to real-world applications and conceptual!

I used to be bored and not pay very much attention in my past math lectures, but Sanjay's engaging and clear lecturing style allowed me to pay close attention in lectures for the first time. This also deepened my interest in the course (differential equations) and made me enjoy it overall. However, the prelims were not too easy. Final was alright.

Blalock-- what a guy. Very nice and approachable, teachers an easy writing course. I actually did learn about writing, but he goes off on a lot of tangents, which make lectures pretty fun. Sometimes he seemed like a mess but the class was bearable, essentially participation-based, and very casual
Intelligent, insightful. Great class and made it more interesting than I thought it would be.

Professor Moghimi is quite good. His explanations about the concept and code is super clear. It is a nice course for business students who want to get more knowledge about python programming, especially its application in finance related field.

Reza is a great person. Not only is he extremely smart but he is very helpful and is always ready to clarify. He is also very friendly and approachable, so ask for help if needed. Some of material is pretty complex but it's very interesting. The essays can be a little hard but they are definitely doable with effort. I highly recommend the class.

She was available to meet one-on-one help with difficult topics. Working on the case studies in group were very involved but also the most fun part of this course. I recommend not skipping lectures to keep track of what is going. She also gave several hints during lectures about exam questions.

I gave Professor Booth a 4 based on how much I enjoyed the class; he is full of stories that he sprinkles in throughout lectures. If dry humor is your thing then he's the guy for you; hilarious but I never saw him smile once. I probably could have gotten a better grade by going to office hours, but even so my C+ was hard earned.

Professor Markell is passionate about political theory but fails to communicate information clearly and concisely. His lectures are rife with tangents and are accompanied by unbelievably unhelpful slides. He assigns hundreds of pages of readings per week whose core concepts are rarely mentioned. The assigned papers seem daunting but are manageable.

Useless. Teaches nothing about design or engineering. Insanely strict formatting on technical papers, while  ignores substance of paper. Do not receive any grades during semester, only get surprised at end, with no way of knowing how you're doing and how to improve. Tries to embarrass people during presentations. Lots of work for 2 credit class.

Dunning knows how to deliver a captivating lecture. He doesn't just read his slides - he presents it in a way that makes the material stick. Probably the best lecturer I've had. He's clearly very well-read. He goes over interesting topics in the class. Very engaging and approachable. One of the best Psych classes I've ever taken.
He is the worst teacher I have had at Cornell. Harsh grader and hard to learn anything other than prior knowledge.

Hilarious professor. Knows his material well, and the lectures are super clear. The labs are great fun, and the grading is quite easy. However, the course itself has little structure, and can occasionally get very tough. There is no textbook and assignments and exams are mostly research-based.

In class, he highlights and points out exactly what will be on the prelims and final. Although the class is called Intro to Ancient Rome, it primarily is about Roman literature, not an intro class about the civilization itself.
Clearly the most exceptional professors in the department. Goes the extra mile to explain complex issues to the students. Really fortunate to have studied under him.

Plamen is one of the best teachers I have come across.  Unfortunately he is quirky which can be distract attention away from the material.  One also sees him running around campus so often that you would think Cornell was being invaded by an army of light infantry text book wielding Romanians or a croatian cross country team.
that himeself ,rubbish classcm1029.
Him, one of his past students Devika Pokhriyal, and other students of his are cyberbullying students on facebook.

He cares about his students' learning but struggles to teach them effectively. The theory behind the course material is very relatable to real life, but the testing includes material that you may not cover in class. Attendance is graded, but you'd likely be better off (for your knowledge and achieved grade) learning the material outside of class.

We were the first elementary class Katagiri had taught in a number of years, and she was still fantastic. She's great fun packed into a cute elderly lady, and though she takes what she does seriously it doesn't get in the way of bringing humour to every class. The course was not difficult, and thanks to her I looked forward to every section.
Solid

This man really made my day.  He was so into teaching the material, even if it was simple, that it really got me interested too! All the exams are open book, and he pretty much tells you what to know anyway.  Really a fun class and an easy entry into some basic entomology

Delchamps has been my favorite lecturer at Cornell. He exclusively uses the chalkboard and explains complex subjects in an intuitive way. He also has a great sense of humor. I thoroughly enjoyed his lectures and would absolutely recommend the class to ECEs.

The exams are graded strictly, with highly specific questions. Although the lecture readings are intriguing, the instructor's poor teaching skills made it difficult to learn and made attending lectures a challenge. I don't feel like I learned much, and if I did, it wasn't due to the instructor.

Professor Petru Petrina is an amazing professor. I've had him for MAE 2120 back then and now MSE 5150. He really knows his course content, and will admit when he doesn't know enough and will do research to learn more. He was thought to have retired when he stopped teaching MAE 2120, but has since returned to teach a smaller MSE 5150 class.

I wouldn't recommend to take this course becuz the professor gives you quiz, essay, reading comprehension, prelim, and oral exam every other week. Homework every other day.  Even though this is an intermediate course, he expects us to be fluent and write fluently. You also have to work with your partner during class and participate A LOT in class.
I took elementary arabic II with him and had an alright experience. learning through doing the textbook readings is critical and content on quizzes/exams will be related to the textbook readings
Incredibly unhelpful and demeaning. Absolutely no redeeming qualities. I implore anyone reading this to stay away from Bowers.
Chill dude, not too overbearing. Unfortunately he will also try to push his anti-vax agenda onto you occasionally, but still a great guy overall.

He was the TA for a lasers course I took. His accent was a bit hard to understand. The course was really tough and he seemed willing to help. But it was difficult to understand him. He showed care and good command of subject though.

Tara Holm is easily the best math teacher I've ever had. Her lectures are rigorous, comprehensive and digestible and by the end I had a really strong grasp of calc 1 and its applications. I was able to coast a little bit having taken AP Calc but even without that it wouldn't have been overwhelming.
He does not cover enough material correlated with the textbook. Very unresponsive to emails. Tests are inconsistent with lecture material.

Professor Hammer is one of my favorite professors at Cornell. She cares about the students and is committed to helping everyone understand the material. The course was not too hard; there were a nice amount of readings but you can get away with skimming. 10/10 professor.

Professor Barbasch was incredibly available, staying after lectures to help students (constantly), and treating all with respect. The course gets a bad rap because it is difficult, but that is not something in his hands. The support system in this class is strong, just make friends in class and ask him questions or email him/your TA. Good luck!

This class was easy to do well in, but hard to get an A if that's what you're shooting for. The prelims are a bit knit-picky and the online textbook can be a bit boring but I would otherwise totally recommend this Intro. to Bio course for non-majors. The lectures were the most dynamic ones I've seen considering the size of the class.

Great lecturer, makes the material easy to learn through powerpointsin-depth lectures. Easy to get at least an A- if you have a tough semester, easy A if you have more time. Dont get the textbook its only used for a few problem sets. Oh and if you want to learn how to become a ForEx trader from a crazy Armenian then this is the course for you.

The class is easy, but really boring. I fell asleep in every class even though I tried to stay awake. Take notes (tested on things not on the PowerPoints because the other professor is there taking notes on what he says.) The tests are hard-- need to know too many specific details to do well. Don't take it unless you need to.

I did not think Professor Carvell was a good professor. His lectures weren't informative and he did not give a lot of opportunities to practice. The exams were heavy on open-ended questions that were not practiced beforehand. His TAs were awful and could not answer questions that were asked, and seemed just as confused as the students.

Professor Hostetler genuinely cares about his students, and it comes through in every facet of the course.  I have genuinely grown as a person and scholar through his instruction, and I would highly recommend his course to anyone who aspires to see leadership in a new light.

I really fell in love with anthropology through Prof. Ramberg. Courses are reading-heavy, but nothing more than would be expected for someone dedicated to the humanities. Her class discussions were rewarding when you did participate. One of the few classes as well where I felt I got a fantastic variety of readings, some of which I would read again.
Favorite professor

Great Professor and solid lectures. The class can be a little dense at times but that's very likely due to the subject matter and not the Prof. The lectures are well organised and the assignments aren't too demanding and tend to be graded rather generously. The class is about Germany and the Prof is German so you can tell he's well informed!
Had her for my FWS, I really enjoyed the content and the creative writing essays gave us a bunch of freedom. As long as you submit decent essays, youll be fine.

Okay class and fair Prof. In person lectures are pointless because the class is flipped with video lectures, which were convenient. Weekly quizzes were a fair way to test learning but unfairly graded on R syntax for paper quiz.  Learned more about R which made me like R less, niche, and redundant material if you've taken other programming classes.

Professor Rooth is very smart (look his papers up they're cool). However, I simply have never been able to follow lectures; I always had to go back and read the haphazardly posted notes. We only had one exam as the second one got canceled because we were behind schedule. I legit could not have finished his class without his amazing TA.

Cody is fabulous. I took intro to creative writing to fulfill my fine arts requirement and had a great time. Everything was graded on completion, which was a relief as a mediocre writer. She always had something good to say about your work.

One of the lowest grades I received in an ECE course at Cornell yet I felt like I learned so much more in this class then any other. Professor Molnar is truly a genius. His homecooked homework problems are very difficult, and the labs are harshly grades, but if you apply yourself through and don't worry about your grade here you will learn so much.

Professor Fleming is the best professor I've had at Cornell, and Critical Theory is possibly the best course I've taken. His lectures are super engaging and interesting.
